Further comparisons of the instantaneous velocity, streamwise force history, and representative wake structures reveal that the HT mode corresponds to a more distributed thrust-generation process, a smoother streamwise response, and a more regular wake organization. As a result, the HT mode shows clear advantages in both propulsion stability and energy performance. The present results indicate that the two intermittent gait patterns have limited influence on the time-averaged propulsion capability, but play an important role in energy utilization and motion stability, with the HT mode showing better overall performance than the MT mode. These findings can provide useful guidance for gait selection and control design of biomimetic underwater robots.

title: Robust Force Control for Omnidirectional Mobile Robots in Wall Grinding Tasks
authors: Junyu Fan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Automated wall grinding and spraying require precise contact force regulation despite unknown surface geometries and stiffness variations. This paper presents a robust adaptive impedance control framework for omnidirectional mobile robots with Mecanum wheels. We propose a first-order spring-damper interaction model to reduce computational complexity while maintaining compliance. The geometric configuration employs an inverted L-shaped arm positioning the tool away from the robot's center of mass, with coupling effects explicitly accounted for in the dynamics. Stability analysis confirms asymptotic force tracking error elimination, and simulations demonstrate low overshoot and zero steady-state error, validating the approach for labor-intensive construction tasks.

title: Explainable Deep Reinforcement Learning for Scalable and Trustworthy Autonomous Decision Systems
authors: Lalit Sachdeva, Utkarsh Anand
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Autonomous decision systems are increasingly being implemented in the critical fields of transportation, industrial robotics, and intelligent infrastructure, where reliability, safety, and transparency are of primary importance. One of the most powerful approaches to these systems has been Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL), which allows an agent to acquire the best behavior by experiencing the environment. Nevertheless, the conventional DRL models are frequently characterized by low interpretability and low safety assurances, which restrict their usage in situations involving safety concerns. To overcome these challenges, this article introduces Scalable Explainable and Trustworthy Deep Reinforcement Learning (SET-DRL), a novel framework that would bring together transparency, trustworthiness, and computational scalability. The main goals of the research of SET-DRL are to: (i) understand the interpretability of DRL models by giving us human-understandable reasons behind choices, (ii) guarantee safe decision-making methods by safety constraints, uncertainty-conscious decision-making, and out-of-distribution detection, and (iii) facilitate scalable implementation in edgecloud systems by distributed training and real-time inference. The evaluation has been the most important as it indicates that SET-DRL performs better than baseline DRL models, providing an opportunity to increase the success rates between 82% to 88%, and explainability is greatly increased. Perturbations of fidelity and stability of explanations are increased by more than 10%, and safety constraint violations decreased by 75%. Results of scalability testing indicate a nearly linear increase in throughput with an increase in the number of workers concurrently, and confirm the scalability to large-scale, real-time operation. These findings indicate that explainability and trust frameworks can be incorporated into DRL to offer clear, credible, and scalable autonomous systems solutions to real world scenarios.

title: Modeling and Measuring Redundancy in Multisource Multimodal Data for Autonomous Driving
authors: Yuhan Zhou, Mehri Sattari, Haihua Chen, Kewei Sha
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Next-generation autonomous vehicles (AVs) rely on large volumes of multisource and multimodal (M<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) data to support real-time decision-making. In practice, data quality (DQ) varies across sources and modalities due to environmental conditions and sensor limitations, yet AV research has largely prioritized algorithm design over DQ analysis. This work focuses on redundancy as a fundamental but underexplored DQ issue in AV datasets. Using the nuScenes and Argoverse 2 (AV2) datasets, we model and measure redundancy in multisource camera data and multimodal image-LiDAR data, and evaluate how removing redundant labels affects the YOLOv8 object detection task. Experimental results show that selectively removing redundant multisource image object labels from cameras with shared fields of view improves detection. In nuScenes, mAP50 gains from 0.66 to 0.70, 0.64 to 0.67, and from 0.53 to 0.55, on three representative overlap regions, while detection on other overlapping camera pairs remains at the baseline even under stronger pruning. In AV2, 4.1-8.6% of labels are removed, and mAP50 stays near the 0.64 baseline. Multimodal analysis also reveals substantial redundancy between image and LiDAR data. These findings demonstrate that redundancy is a measurable and actionable DQ factor with direct implications for AV performance. This work highlights the role of redundancy as a data quality factor in AV perception and motivates a data-centric perspective for evaluating and improving AV datasets. Code, data, and implementation details are publicly available at: https://github.com/yhZHOU515/RedundancyAD.

title: IoT Enabled QR Code System for Automated Patient Health Monitoring
authors: S. Prabavathi, Saran S, Varun S, M. SentamilSelvi, Iyyappan M, Prajith Aswin S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The growing demand for efficient healthcare monitoring has highlighted the need for real- time access to patient information, especially in hospital environments where doctors must quickly review multiple cases. This project introduces an IoT-enabled QR code system designed to automate patient health monitoring and simplify data accessibility. Each patient is assigned a unique QR code that securely links to their individual health records. IoT-based sensors continuously collect vital parameters such as heart rate and body temperature, updating the data in real time. When doctors scan the patient's QR code during ward rounds, they can instantly view the latest medical information, enabling faster diagnosis and improved treatment decisions. This system enhances hospital workflow, reduces manual record- keeping errors, and promotes seamless communication between patients and healthcare professionals.

title: Evaluation of Sidewalls Properties in HZO-Based 3D Ferroelectric Capacitors
authors: Nicolò Giovannelli, Jean Coignus, Simon Martin, Julien Borrel, Carine Jahan, Antoine Salvi, Fabien Grimaud, Melanie Louro, Romuald Blanc, Corinne Comboroure, Christelle Boixaderas, Mehdi Oujanba, Stéphane Minoret, Julian Sturm, Damien Deleruyelle, François Andrieu, Laurent Grenouillet
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: Hafnia-based ferroelectric devices have gained more and more attention in the framework of emerging (nonvolatile) memories over the past few years. In particular, ferroelectric capacitors (FeCaps) are the elementary building blocks of Ferroelectric Random Access Memories (FeRAM), Ferroelectric Metal Field Effect Transistors (FeMFETs) and Ferroelectric Tunnel Junctions (FTJs). In order to increase FeCap density, the vertical direction can be exploited to increase the switched charge per footprint. However, the ferroelectric properties in a 3D configuration have yet to be understood. In this paper, 3D ferroelectric capacitor (3D FeCap) structures are fabricated using a simple 300 mm maskset specifically designed to decouple the remanent polarization coming from the sidewalls <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2 P_{r}^{3 D})$</tex> and the one coming from the planar areas <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2 P_{r}^{2 D})$</tex>. Extensive electrical characterizations demonstrate that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 P_{r}^{3 D}$</tex> increases with 3D FeCap aspect ratio, suggesting that 3D FeCap ferroelectric properties can surpass planar FeCaps' with no detrimental impact on reliability.

title: Design Architecture Trade-Offs for Hybrid Bonded Array-Periphery VBL 3D-DRAM
authors: H. Oh, N. Rassoul, A. Belmonte, G. Sankar Kar, M. Garcia Bardon, D. Biswas, F. Garcia Redondo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: This work presents hybrid-bonded vertical-bitline (VBL) 3D-DRAM architectures to alleviate the pitch-critical “bond-each-bitline” array-edge bottleneck that limits periphery connectivity. We propose two architecture alternatives: (i) sBiW (Sequential-BL and Interleaved-WL), which groups sub-bitlines (SBLs) into global bitlines (GBLs) and uses interleaved/merged wordlines decoding to relax HB pad density, and (ii) BLS (BL-selector), which inserts per-SBL selectors to enable single-GBL-to-single-SA sensing under a more relaxed BL-side HB pitch. Both are evaluated with single/dual-side section-wordline driver (SWD), to quantify HB pad budget/pitch and read/write behavior using a bottom-up 3D PEX + SPICE flow with HB and WL-staircase parasitics. The proposed architecture relaxes the baseline HB pitch from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.17 \mu \mathbf{m}$</tex> (BLP) to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.36 \mu \mathbf{m}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.44 \mu \mathbf{m}$</tex> for sBiW and BLS, respectively. Consequently, the number and area of SA per sub-MAT(SM) decreases by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$N$</tex> (multiplexing factor), corresponding to the number of shared SBLs per GBL.

title: Research on Dynamic Modeling and Control Strategy of a Novel Planar 2-DOF Parallel Robot
authors: Chaohui Wang, Jianjia Liu, Dong Liang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To meet high-precision demands in smart manufacturing, this paper investigates the modeling and control of a novel planar 2-DOF parallel robot. First, its kinematic solutions are derived via the closed-loop vector method. Subsequently, a complete dynamic model is established using Kane's equations to capture complex coupling mechanisms. Finally, a Non-singular Terminal Sliding Mode Control (NTSMC) strategy is proposed to overcome nonlinear dynamics and external disturbances. Comparative simulations demonstrate that NTSMC outperforms traditional PD and CTC methods by ensuring finite-time error convergence and superior robustness, significantly enhancing the high-speed trajectory tracking accuracy.

title: Automatic Detection of Cervical Cancer Cells via Residual Network
authors: Sai Kalyana Deepthi Chintapalli, Pravallika Chinthala, Anusha Eragadindla, Anusha Peram, Sandya Meghavath
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: One of the leading causes of cancer-related deaths among women worldwide is still cervical cancer. The disease is still a serious public health concern despite the existence of screening programs, particularly in low- and middle-income nations. Improving patient outcomes and reducing death rates depend on early detection. Conventional screening techniques, such as manual cytological examination and Pap smear analysis, require a significant amount of time and effort. Additionally, they rely a lot on professional interpretation, which can result in inconsistent diagnoses and human error. This study offers an automated cervical cancer detection system that employs the Residual Network (ResNet-50) architecture and a deep learning technique to address these issues. Even when trained on small medical datasets, the model successfully extracts significant features from cervical cell images using transfer learning. Weighted random sampling is used to address class imbalance, extensive data augmentation is used to increase dataset diversity, and CutMix regularization is used to improve learning efficiency and prevent overfitting in order to increase the model's robustness and generalization. The results of the experiment demonstrate how well the ResNet-based framework classifies cervical images as benign or malignant. The model's accuracy, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-scores reach up to 95 %, demonstrating its dependability and consistency in forecasting. Additionally, the system demonstrates its ability to generalize to new data with training accuracy of 91 % and validation accuracy of 95 %. The results indicate that the suggested automated detection system is a viable and effective choice for early cervical cancer diagnosis. In actual healthcare settings, it could help physicians and improve screening accuracy.

title: Torque Reallocation Strategy Considering Thermal Derating Constraints for in-Wheel Motor EVs Under Extreme Conditions
authors: Ruihang Zhang, Huirong Hao, Xiaogang Wen, Ronghuang Chen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Aiming at the severe thermal overload risk of inwheel motors (IWMs), multi-objective optimization imbalance of traditional torque allocation strategies, and abrupt power mutation from separated passive thermal protection for distributed drive electric vehicles under extreme track conditions, this paper proposes a thermal safety-embedded multi-objective quadratic programming (QP) framework for torque allocation. Its key innovation is breaking the traditional “torque allocation first, thermal protection later” passive architecture: it integrates IWM thermal derating hard constraints and thermal equalization objectives into the core torque allocation solution process, and constructs a multi-dimensional collaborative optimization strategy with a smooth torque switching mechanism. Co-simulation results via MATLAB/Simulink and CarSim show that the proposed method effectively suppresses IWM thermal overload, significantly improves power output continuity and energy efficiency, and ensures no deterioration of vehicle lateral handling stability. It thus possesses favorable engineering application potential for the thermal safety control of IWM-driven electric vehicles under extreme conditions.

title: Imw 2026 Committees
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)

title: Logical Scaling Beyond 4 Bit/Cell: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Muhammad Masuduzzaman, Xiang Yang, Deepanshu Dutta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: In addition to mainstream physical scaling, logical scaling offers an alternative path to increasing bit density in flash memory. This paper examines the feasibility and requirements for extending logical scaling beyond the state-of-the-art QLC technology. We analyze key trade-offsincluding performance and reliability-and identify the cell-level improvements needed to support additional bits per cell. Our results indicate that, unlike the TLC-to-QLC transition, scaling from QLC to PLC cannot be achieved through performance or reliability trade-offs alone. Instead, we quantitatively identify the key cell parameters that must be improved to enable viable PLC operation, underscoring the need for targeted materials and architectural advances. In the near term, NAND density gains are therefore expected to rely primarily on physical and overhead scaling while these enabling improvements mature.

title: A Unified Adsorption Performance Framework for Selecting Biochar-Nanoparticle Systems in Heavy Metal Water Filtration
authors: Dev Srivastava, Aashka Doshi, Jim Egenrieder
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Heavy metal contamination in water is a rising problem as it poses serious health concerns. This study explored different biochar-nanoparticle pairs on their efficacy for filtration, factoring in sustainability, reusability, and cost efficiency. The Metal Adsorption Performance Index (MAPI) was used to rank filtration techniques. This index was tested on 18 entries from 15 peer-reviewed articles. A program was implemented in Python for the calculation of the MAPI. This code produced recommendations for suitable filtration methods for specific contamination issues. The MAPI is an efficient method to compare filtration methods, and its parameters can be adjusted based on research goals.

title: The Ethics of Generative AI in Anonymous Spaces: A Case Study of 4chan's /pol/ Board
authors: Parth Gaba
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: This paper presents a characterization of AIgenerated images shared on 4chan, examining how this anonymous online community is (mis-)using generative image technologies. Through a methodical data collection process, we gathered 900 images from 4chan's /pol/ (Politically Incorrect) board, which included the label “/mwg/” (memetic warfare general), between April and July 2024, identifying 66 unique AI-generated images. The analysis reveals concerning patterns in the use of this technology, with 69.7% of images including recognizable figures, 28.8% of images containing racist elements, 28.8% featuring antiSemitic content, and 9.1% incorporating Nazi-related imagery. Overall, we document how users are weaponizing generative AI to create extremist content, political commentary, and memes that often bypass conventional content moderation systems. This research highlights significant implications for platform governance, AI safety mechanisms, and broader societal impacts as generative AI technologies become increasingly accessible. The findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced safeguards in generative AI systems and more effective regulatory frameworks to mitigate potential harms while preserving innovation.

title: Probing English and Spanish Syntax in Language Models
authors: Caroline E. Witty, Chen Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Language models must handle syntactic complexity in multiple languages with different sentence structures to be useful in real-world domains such as medicine, law, and education. Prior studies show that models tend to encode syntactic roles, but this work focuses on English only data so it cannot be applied to languages with different syntax rules. We created a Spanish minimal-pair dataset based on previous benchmarks by translating English captions and reordering them from Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) to Object-Verb-Subject (OVS) using GPT-4o. We verified the dataset is comparable to previous benchmarks using a compositionality benchmark test. Then, we probed word embeddings from a range of text encoders using logistic regression to predict whether a word is a subject or an object. On embeddings from monolingual models the classifier achieved average F1 scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 4}$</tex> for English SVO, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 0}$</tex> for Spanish SVO and 0.80 for Spanish OVS data, but dropped to 0.47 for combined Spanish SVO and OVS data. Contrastingly, given embeddings from models trained on multilingual data, the classifier achieved average F1 scores of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 6}$</tex> for English SVO, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 9 6}$</tex> for Spanish SVO, 0.92 for Spanish OVS, and 0.87 for Spanish SVO and OVS combined. Our research aligns with prior work demonstrating that language models encode syntactic roles, while also expanding this work to multilingual contexts. Specifically, we add a metric to ensure syntactic roles are encoded rather than word order, supporting the applicability of language models in languages with freer word order.

title: Design of a Triple Band-Notched UWB MIMO Antenna with Mutual Coupling Reduction Using H-Shaped DGS
authors: Elijah Joseph, Pradeep Kumar, Thomas J.O Afullo
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: With the growing demand for compact antennas capable of operating over wide frequency ranges, ultra-wideband (UWB) systems continue to face significant interference from narrowband services such as WiMAX, WLAN, and X-band radar. In this work, a compact dual-port UWB multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) antenna is presented with integrated triple-band notches to suppress these interfering signals. An H-shaped defected ground structure is employed between the two radiating elements to mitigate mutual coupling and improve isolation. The proposed antenna consists of two orthogonally arranged circular Minkowski-shaped radiating elements on an FR-4 substrate, each loaded with rectangular slot resonators to achieve notched responses targeting WiMAX (3.2–3.5 GHz), WLAN (5.1–5.8 GHz), and X-band (8.4–9.96 GHz) interference. The H-shaped DGS, etched in the common ground plane, effectively suppresses surface currents, resulting in improved isolation while maintaining a wide impedance bandwidth. Simulated results demonstrate an operational range from 2.79 GHz to 11.96 GHz with isolation exceeding 18dB, peak gain of 5.8dB, and an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.012 across the passband. The radiation patterns remain stable, with near-omnidirectional E-plane coverage and complementary H-plane response, while the notched bands effectively suppress undesired radiation. These results confirm that the proposed design is compact, interference-resilient, and suitable for portable UWB MIMO applications.

title: Noisy Pixels, Credible Decisions – A Robust, Edge-Ready Neuro-Symbolic Architecture for Rural (African) Clinics
authors: Christian Kutabuna Kubakisa, Kyandoghere Kyamakya, Witesyavwirwa Vianney Kambale, Isaac Lukusa Kayembe, Divin Kayeye Kabeya, Hima Deepthi Vankayalapati
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Deploying robust and interpretable medical-image analysis in real-world settings remains challenging, especially in rural African clinics where imaging devices are low-cost, maintenance is irregular, and connectivity is intermittent. In such environments, images are frequently corrupted by noise, motion blur, low resolution, and other hardware- and workflow-induced artefacts that conventional deep-learning systems—typically tuned on clean, curated datasets—do not handle well and can rarely explain. This paper introduces a deployment-aware hybrid neuro-symbolic architecture that couples (i) CeNN-inspired convolutional modules for localised, noise-tolerant feature extraction, (ii) a symbolic reasoning layer that enforces anatomical and pathological consistency via rule-based logic, (iii) a Bayesian trust estimator that quantifies predictive uncertainty and triggers safe fallback pathways, and (iv) an anomaly-handling component that detects corrupted regions, models device-shift artefacts, and initiates self-adaptive compensation. The full stack is explicitly designed for edge execution under tight latency, memory, and energy constraints, while exposing human-readable rationales aimed at clinicians and community health workers. We detail the system-level design, edge-oriented optimisation choices, and an evaluation roadmap based on public medical imaging datasets, robustness and interpretability metrics, and ablation studies comparing individual components to the complete pipeline. This conceptual contribution is intended as a blueprint for future quantitative validation and sustainable deployment in mobile radiography, tele-ultrasound, community retinal screening, and low-cost dermatoscopic workflows in low-resource health systems.

title: A Decentralized Proof Framework for Digital Authorship and Ownership
authors: Rohith John Jacob, Shane Jacob Sebastian, Varsha S Panicker, Vishnu Satish, Jerrin Sebastian
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Digital art marketplaces have been expanding rapidly, resulting in a greater focus on issues of ownership verification, copyright infringement, and content tampering. Current blockchain non-fungible token (NFT) systems only keep ownership data on the blockchain, with the media files stored off the blockchain, resulting in a lack of trust in the ownership of assets secured by NFTs. This work proposes a framework for verifying the ownership of digital art in a decentralized fashion, using invisible frequency domain watermarks, cryptographic hash functions, and artificial intelligence (AI)-based semantic checks. This results in a method for embedding ownership directly into the image being protected by an NFT, with the cryptographic fingerprint of the image stored on the blockchain for easy access. An AI-based semantic verification layer provides assurance that ownership can only be confirmed by performing consistent content checks. As has been verified through experimentation, the framework developed will allow owners of digital images to preserve ownership of their images and to securely and decentralized verify ownership.

title: Nimbusort: Advanced Detection of Ionospheric Disturbances Using EM Signatures
authors: Maxwell Mamishev, Zheng Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The detection of ionospheric transient signals is critical for applications such as environmental monitoring, aerospace operations, and national defense. This paper presents a framework for the automatic differentiation of several types of events, including meteors and meteorites, orbital Falcon rocket launches, and space debris. A family of spectrogram analysis algorithms based on short-time Fourier transforms was built and used to process 24 TB of data accumulated during field experiments. The algorithm suite, termed Nimbusort, derives its name from the ‘nimbus,’ a nod to the halo around glowing objects in the sky. Comparative feature analysis revealed several distinct features in spectral morphologies. The spectral morphology and temporal dynamics were analyzed with phenomenological characteristics in mind. Higher significance weights were assigned to frequency content, duration of select spectrogram bands, and energy shifts and modulation structure within the VLF/ULF bands. By characterizing the signal morphology of known event types, we established a baseline against which unknown or ambiguous signals could be compared. This study lays the foundation for automated, data-driven detection of ionospheric disturbances, providing a scalable methodology for both scientific investigation and aerospace anomaly detection. A comprehensive table of features based on numerous observed signals is presented. The paper lays the foundation for a more granular analysis of already collected data and the construction of future observation systems.

title: Archaeology Meets Materials Science, Chemistry, and Statistics: A Multidisciplinary Analysis of Ecuadorian Greenstone Beads
authors: Darby Ko, Natalie Chen, Zachary Ciappa, Jack Ecklund, Abigail Frey, Lucas Horowitz-Kurtzberg, Serena Lorenzo, Julianne Ma, Andie Park, Alexica Perez, Luke Shao
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: This study investigated the composition of greenstone beads from suspected production sites in pre-Columbian Guangala and Manteño settlements in the southwestern coastal region of Ecuador. To determine the raw materials used in the crafting of greenstone beads and to locate and assess the rarity of the minerals present, a multidisciplinary analysis was performed on a variety of samples. It was found that the tested artifacts were composed of various minerals, predominantly from local and common rock formations. The results suggested that the greenstone beads served as an essential domestic export in a greater coastal trade network. By integrating traditional historical and qualitative approaches with modern quantitative techniques, including chemical, geological, and statistical methods, a novel, nondestructive approach to archaeological research was demonstrated.

title: Queue Monitoring and Occupancy Estimation Using Image Processing
authors: Manjula Gururaj Rao, Priyanka H, Moulya N Shetty, Neel Patel, Sarthak R Gouda, Shriya Alva
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Crowd queue management is a serious matter in high-flow areas such as schools, transportation centers, and public-service junctures. Traditional queue-management methods widely depend on human observation or paper tokens, which face huge challenges in crowds with drastic changes, causing lengthy waiting times and lowering user satisfaction. Herein, the authors propose a vision-based queue monitoring system with automatic estimation of waiting time.The proposed methodology combines real-time human detection with frame-level analysis; for object detection, YOLOv8 is used, while image processing is done using OpenCV. It is further optimized with frame skipping and filtering of the region of interest to reduce the computational load. The waiting time is estimated from the observed queue length and the average service rate. The system implemented at a college canteen gave an accuracy of around 90% under varying illumination and cluttered background conditions, hence showing ways compact AI- enabled vision systems could improve flow and user experience in everyday scenarios.

title: Transformer-Patch Networks for Accurate Remaining Useful Life Prediction in Electromechanical Systems
authors: Elizabeth Lin, Roy Ma, Janice Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Predicting the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of complex electromechanical systems, such as modern turbofan engines, humanoid robots, and autonomous vehicles, from multivariate sensor streams is challenging. This paper presents a new method for RUL prediction based on the Patch Time Series Transformer (PatchTST)[1] self-attention architecture. Our method was developed and tested on the NASA CMAPSS[2] turbofan engine data sets, and it demonstrated notable performance benefits compared to state-of-the-art methods (e.g. CNN-LSTM) and classical machine learning approaches (e.g. Gradient Boosting), in multiple data sets with varying operational conditions. Our approach is evaluated using RMSE (Root Mean Square Error) against ground truth RUL, along with various plots of model performance. We also analyze key performance indicator plots across operating conditions to highlight when our model is most effective. Our results indicate that transformerstyle patching networks can achieve lower estimation errors than LSTM type state-of-the-art recurrent models for RUL based machine failure prediction.

title:
California PM
                    <sub>2.5</sub>
                    : A Ground- and Satellite-Based Data Analysis and Policy Investigation
authors: Lennon Chrisman, Ayan Dalmia, Clare Patchett, Uriah Sacdalan, Sneha Verma, Zaina Merchant
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Particulate matter, namely PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf>, is an air pollutant linked to widespread respiratory health concerns. This study analyzes PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> trends in California using policy review alongside Python-based analysis and visualization of EPA ground-level monitor data and the USHighPM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> comprehensive dataset. We identify a long-term decline in PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> from 2000 to 2024, despite notable outliers caused by extreme wildfire events, supported by both data sources. Our results also show various temporal, geographical, and seasonal PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> trends, integrating multiple datasets in the analysis. We show that PM<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2.5</inf> reduction requires targeted policy approaches that focus on specific emission sources.

title: Autonomous Vision-Based Smart Ramp in Urban Infrastructure
authors: Viraj Kope, Anisha Mulinti, Celia Tombalakian, Fiona Wu, Xian Wu, Benjamin Lee, Megan Paonessa
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Cities worldwide are beginning to adapt to their rapidly growing populations by implementing innovative and smart technology to address common urban issues. A significant challenge that evolving cities face is accessibility, particularly with urban infrastructure. The shortcomings in accessible accommodations have caused issues for individuals with physical disabilities, especially wheelchair users. The purpose of this study is to develop an efficient system that allows individuals with wheelchairs to navigate through urban settings. The proposed solution is an autonomous accessibility ramp that is deployed in the presence of a wheelchair. Using object detection and machine learning algorithms, the system can effectively deploy and retract the ramp once a wheelchair user is near the stairs for on-demand accessibility. The use of this ramp allows people to access restaurants, stores, and buildings that may not have the space to build a traditional ramp. An important factor in designing the autonomous ramp was performance. Although realtime detection can vary under different conditions, this system was able to detect wheelchairs and individuals in both bright light and darkness. The results of this study could be used to measure the feasibility of low-cost AI-driven accessibility solutions in urban infrastructure.

title: Prompt Engineering for Competitive Programming: Improving o4-mini's Accuracy on Codeforces Tasks
authors: Sunmay Padiyar, Aaroosh Kurchania, Daivya Singh, Mihai Boicu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Generative AI tools such as OpenAI's o4-mini are increasingly used to solve programming tasks, yet their effectiveness in competitive programming remains underexplored. This study evaluates four prompting techniques—No Prompt, Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought, Prompt Chaining, and Structured Chain of Thought—on 90 randomly selected Codeforces problems rated 1600–2400. These problems were released after May 31st, 2024, the knowledge cutoff date for o4-mini. Code was submitted to the official Codeforces grader, and accuracy was measured based on the number of problems accepted. Structured Chain-of-Thought achieved the highest accuracy (56.67%, p = 0.0139), showing a statistically significant improvement over No Prompt (44.44%). Prompt Chaining followed closely (55.56%, p = 0.0249) and showed strong potential, though its improvement fell just short of statistical significance over No Prompt. Zero-Shot Chain-of-Thought (42.22%) did not improve from the baseline. These results demonstrate that carefully structured prompts can substantially improve LLM performance on advanced, reasoning-intensive programming tasks.

title: Optimizing Data-Driven Solutions for Power Quality Management in Africa: Addressing Unbalanced Datasets with Machine Learning
authors: Lavenda Shipichira, Opondo Broono
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Energy security is a critical issue in Africa, where reliable power supply is essential for development. This paper discusses optimization techniques for processing power quality data using machine learning (ML) to enhance energy security. By addressing unbalanced datasets and improving the detection of power disturbances, we aim to ensure stable and reliable electricity in African power systems. In recent years, ML applications have garnered significant interest from power system researchers, with their success heavily dependent on extensive and diverse datasets for training and validation. However, power systems typically operate in quasi-steady-state conditions, resulting in measurements that offer limited novel information for ML development. This paper proposes a data mining approach utilizing optimization techniques to filter root-mean-square (RMS) voltage profiles and identify unusual measurements within triggerless power quality datasets. We create balanced datasets with equal representation of event and non-event observations, allowing ML algorithms to extract valuable insights from rare but critical events. The proposed framework is validated using both synthetic signals and field data measurements, demonstrating its effectiveness in enhancing power system stability and energy security in Africa.

title: Challenges in 2D Hybrid Non-Quasineutral Hall Thruster Simulation
authors: Matthew Fang, Weiping Li, Aditya Kaul, Efthimios Gkatzimas, Ashita Pant, Rhea Haridas
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Hall thrusters (HTs) are the most widely used electric propulsion systems in development today. However, accurate modeling remains challenging due to complex kinetic effects and poorly understood plasma-wall sheath interactions inherent in HT plasmas. For the past two decades, the standard has been to prioritize computational efficiency over sheath resolution by assuming a quasineutral plasma. In this work, we review the assumptions behind the standard model, assess the computational trade-offs of reintroducing electron inertial effects, and present early qualitative results from a 2D hybrid-PIC simulation that utilizes 5-moment Euler electron dynamics and a full Poisson solver to resolve sheath dynamics more comprehensively than existing codes.

title: AnoDoc: Robust Anomaly-Based Document Forgery Detection
authors: Aboudramane Diarra, Tegawendé F. Bissyande, Pasteur Poda
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Forged official documents threaten institutional trust, especially in regions lacking robust digital verification systems. While supervised methods depend on large labeled datasets and struggle with unseen manipulations, the unsupervised Doc-Patch approach proved the feasibility of detecting anomalies from authentic samples only. Building on this foundation, we propose AnoDoc, a robust anomaly-based framework that enhances unsupervised document forgery detection through adaptive self-attention, document-specific data augmentation, Focal Loss, and dynamic thresholding for improved adaptability and precision. The integration of t-distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding (t-SNE) further supports interpretability by visualizing the separation between authentic and forged embeddings. Experiments on a real-world dataset show that AnoDoc achieves 98% F1-score and 0.987 Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic (AUROC), surpassing Doc-Patch by over 6% in precision and generalization. These results demonstrate its effectiveness and scalability for deployment in administrative, legal, and financial authentication workflows.

title: Implications of Higher Voltage Levels for Electric Vehicle Traction
authors: Michael Gleissner, Mark-M. Bakran
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: The increasing demand for high-performance electric vehicles (EVs) has led to a focus on optimizing traction systems, particularly through the exploration of higher voltage levels beyond the conventional 400V DC. This paper presents a comprehensive analysis of the feasibility and characteristics of higher voltage levels for EV traction, considering factors such as component availability, efficiency, weight, cost, and compatibility with charging infrastructure. The study evaluates established voltage levels in various industries, the impact of higher voltages on battery technology and traction motors, and the implications for onboard network topologies. The findings suggest that while higher voltages offer certain advantages, such as reduced cable weight and potentially higher efficiency, they also present significant challenges, including increased insulation requirements, higher component costs, and limited compatibility with existing infrastructure. This paper aims to provide a detailed understanding of the trade-offs involved in adopting higher voltage levels for EV traction systems.

title: Zero-Shot Image Classification in Low-Resource Medical Image Diagnosis Using Fine-Tuning Transformer Models
authors: Manjula Gururaj Rao, Pratheeksha Nayak, Mrudul Mascarenhas, Vatsala G. A, Priyanka H, Ahamed Shafeeq B M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Deep learning-based (DL) medical picture identification frequently relies on huge annotated datasets, which are hard to come by in actual clinical settings. Significant domain mismatch prevents transformer-based vision-language models from being directly applied to medical imaging, despite their strong zero-shot performance on natural images. This paper analyzes the usage of pre-trained vision–language transformers for brain MRI tumor classification under zero-shot and low-resource scenarios and highlights the limits of direct zero-shot inference and prompt-based adaptation. To address these issues, a lightweight few-shot adaptation technique based on partial unfreezing of the vision transformer is developed, enabling selective domain-specific learning while keeping pre-trained knowledge. The suggested technique considerably improves classification performance utilizing only 20 labeled samples per class, beating both zero-shot and frozen few-shot baselines. These findings suggest that in clinical settings with limited annotation, minimum and regulated transformer fine-tuning offers an efficient and data-efficient method for brain MRI categorization.

title: Experimental Validation of a Novel Smart ASIC for X-Ray Spectroscopy
authors: Amehja Williams, Soumyajit Mandal
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Radiation sensors employed in high energy and nuclear physics experiments often suffer energy and data rate limitations that worsen power consumption and constrain streaming capabilities. Power requirements can be reduced while maintaining desired performance standards by decreasing the sensor's average output data rate. A promising approach for reducing output data rates relies on extracting characteristic features of sensor data prior to transmission, thereby mitigating the computational load for downstream operations. A novel multi-channel application-specific integrated circuit (ASIC) has been designed by the Instrumentation Department of Brookhaven National Laboratory (BNL) to demonstrate how an artificial neural network (ANN) can be employed for feature extraction. This research project endeavors to experimentally validate the analog front-end (AFE) and digital back-end (DBE) of the ASIC, as well as train the ANN on experimental data. Validation results show alignment with expected simulation behavior for pulse amplitude linearity, shape, and impulse response.

title: Explainable Brain Tumor Classification in MRI Using VGG19 Transfer Learning and Grad-CAM Visualization
authors: Mridul Sikder, Sayon Chakraborty, Himanshu Kumar Sah, Anindya Rudra
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Brain tumor classification from MRI is critical for diagnosis but remains challenging due to visual complexity and class imbalance. This study presents a VGG19-based transfer learning framework for four-class classification (glioma, meningioma, pituitary tumor, no tumor) using 2,443 MRI scans. The pre-trained VGG19 convolutional base serves as a feature extractor with a custom classifier head for task-specific learning. Extensive data augmentation and class weighting improve generalization and address imbalance. Unlike prior work, this approach uniquely combines strategic layer freezing, comprehensive augmentation, and integrated Grad-CAM explainability. The model achieved 96% test accuracy (AUC: 0.99, p < 0. 001) with 0.96 precision, 0.95 recall, and perfect recall (1.00) for glioma and pituitary tumors, with test performance exceeding validation accuracy, confirming robust generalization. Grad-CAM visualization confirms that the model focuses on clinically relevant regions. These results show strong potential as a reliable decision-support tool for radiologists.

title: SPHINX: An Agentic AI System for Automated Scientific Discovery in Computational Biology
authors: Aditya Sengupta, Manolis Kellis
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The rapid growth of biomedical data and analysis techniques has created a critical research bottleneck, leading to inefficient discovery workflows. We introduce SPHINX (Scientific Progressive Hypothesis Iteration and Organized Novel In-silico eXperiments), a multi-agent AI system designed to accelerate discovery by automating computational biology. SPHINX integrates specialized agents for literature synthesis, hypothesis generation, data analysis and visualization, and in-silico experimentation within a “Lab-in-the-Loop” framework. Through rigorous case studies in Alzheimer's Disease and immunology, SPHINX replicated findings and performed complex analyses by autonomously reasoning, writing code, and self-correcting errors, demonstrating its end-to-end capability to accelerate biomedical research.

title: An Experimental Analysis of Data Augmentation and Pretraining for Mammogram Image Classification
authors: Sharmila C, Chidananda Murthy M V, Sunil S. Harakannanavar, Keshava Murthy T G
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Breast cancer continues to be one of the major health challenges for women worldwide and detecting it at an early stage greatly increases the chances of successful treatment. Mammography remains the standard imaging technique for identifying tumors at their earliest development. In this study, an attempt is made to classify mammogram images as benign or malignant by applying deep learning-based methods. The MIAS dataset is used for model training, while the larger CBIS-DDSM dataset serves as a source for pretraining to strengthen feature learning. Since MIAS contains a limited number of images, several augmentation and preprocessing techniques—such as rotations, horizontal and vertical flips, Gaussian blurring, CLAHE contrast adjustment, and median filtering—are incorporated to enrich the dataset and improve image quality. Two backbone architectures, ResNet18 and ResNet50, are examined for the classification task. The experiments indicate that ResNet18, when paired with MoCo-based pretraining and the enhanced augmentation pipeline, reaches a validation accuracy of about 65%. In contrast, the same model without these improvements performs noticeably lower at around 52%. The study highlights that effective preprocessing and representation-learning strategies can substantially enhance model performance, even when only a small amount of annotated medical data is available.

title: Uncertainty Estimation and Risk Analysis in Hybrid Self-Attention LSTM Models for Insider Threat Detection towards Cyber Risk Management
authors: Akampurira Paul, Maninti Venkateswarlu, Bashir Olaniyi Sadiq, Buhari Muhammed Dahiru, Bala Gangadhara Gutam, Katuri Keerthi Naidu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Insider threats continue to present a formidable challenge to cybersecurity operations due to the inherent difficulty of detecting malicious actions performed by trusted individuals. This paper presents an Uncertainty-Aware Hybrid Self-Attention Bidirectional LSTM (U-AB-BiLSTM) model that integrates Monte Carlo Dropout for principled epistemic uncertainty estimation. The model captures complex sequential dependencies and dynamic behavioral patterns in user activity logs while quantifying prediction confidence to enhance interpretability and trustworthiness. Experimental results demonstrate superior calibration (Brier Score = 0.0068) and predictive performance (Average Precision Score = 0.9882). Analytical validation confirms perfect precision (1.0000) on low uncertainty samples, enabling reliable automation for high-confidence threat predictions. Moreover, uncertainty-aware risk profiling and user micro segmentation enable proactive, risk-adjusted decision policies that reduce analyst fatigue and missed threats. The proposed framework demonstrates how uncertainty quantification significantly strengthens insider threat detection and operational cyber risk management.

title: A Hybrid QGWO–PSO Based LET-Bounded RSU-Assisted QoS Routing Scheme for VANETs
authors: Sowdagar Fowzia Sultana, Karamtot Krishna Naik
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Vehicular ad hoc networks, have a lot of mobility, which makes links fail a lot. This means that the routes need to be figured out again and again, which lowers the quality of service (QoS). This work suggests a per-flow routing framework that limits routing choices using a mobility window based on analytical Link Expiration Time (LET). This makes sure that paths are possible and stable. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) are reformulated to operate under LET-bounded routing constraints. A LET-adaptive Hybrid Quantum Grey Wolf–Particle Swarm Optimization (QGWO–PSO) algorithm is proposed to balance path stability and QoS performance. The routing objective jointly considers end-to-end delay, jitter, recomputation overhead, and packet delivery ratio, while selectively utilizing RSU-assisted links. Simulation results show that the hybrid approach achieves higher throughput and packet delivery than PSO, ACO, and GWO, with comparable delay and jitter. While GWO attains higher minimum LET, the proposed hybrid method provides a superior QoS–stability trade-off under dynamic VANET conditions.

title: Broadband and Wide-Angle Conformal Active Metasurface for Continuous and Dynamic RCS Modulation
authors: Haiqing Chen, Tonghao Liu, Linying Wang, Gu Liu, Weihan Li, Chaoqun Ge, Dongshu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Metasurfaces have attracted significant research attention due to their exceptional capabilities in manipulating electromagnetic (EM) waves, which have achieved a wide range of groundbreaking applications. Active metasurfaces represent an emerging class of reconfigurable platforms that can dynamically tailor functionalities in real-time, especially in radar cross section (RCS) modulation. Nevertheless, conventional designs for metasurfaces are not only difficult to conform to curved surface structures, but also suffer from narrow bandwidth and strong angular dependence, which tremendously limit their practical application. In this article, we propose a conformal active metasurface (CAM) that enables continuous and dynamic RCS modulation on curved targets. By strategically adjusting the bias voltages of regionally distributed p-i-n diodes to achieve adaptive impedance matching, the CAM can efficiently absorb EM waves across a broad frequency spectrum and a wide range of incident angles. Through theoretical analysis, optimal design, and experimental validation, we demonstrate that the proposed CAM achieves an average continuous dynamic RCS reduction of more than 10 dB, with a maximum reduction of 27 dB in simulation and 26 dB in measurement within 8–15 GHz, which is well maintained across an incident angle range of 0°–50°. This facilitates continuous and dynamic modulation of the target’s RCS in adaptation to variations in the surrounding EM environment, allowing the target to merge seamlessly with conventional platforms in the reflection state, preventing the detection of anomalous scattering signatures, while enabling active stealth functionality in the absorption state. This work provides a feasible strategy for designing highly adaptable, broadband, and wide-angle CAMs, with strong potential for applications in radar stealth, wireless communications, and integrated sensing systems.

title: Breast Cancer Classification from Medical Images Using AI Models: A Comprehensive Review
authors: Swathi K S, Hareesh K N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Breast cancer continues to be the most common and deadly cancer among women around the world, causing illness and death for a lot of cancer-related. Early identification and diagnosis are very important for making survival rates and success rates in treating patients. The medical imaging and artificial intelligence (AI) has integrated over the past decade has transformed research by facilitating automated and image interpretation across multiple modalities of breast cancer images. This paper provides a thorough examination of recent progress in breast cancer classification utilizing ultrasound, mammography, and histopathological images, emphasizing machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) techniques. We look at both traditional methods that use handcrafted feature extraction and classical classifiers and modern architectures like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), Vision Transformers (ViTs), attention-based models, and hybrid multi-branch frameworks. The review also talks about how transfer learning, segmentation-assisted classification, and explainable AI (XAI) are becoming more important for making things easier to understand and building trust in the medical field. A thorough examination of publicly accessible datasets which are BUSI, CBIS-DDSM, INbreast, BreakHis, and BACH were represented to promote reproducibility and benchmarking. Comparative analyses demonstrate that transformer-based and hybrid architectures surpass traditional CNNs, providing improvised robustness, generalization, and interpretability across various imaging modalities. The paper concludes by pinpointing significant research challenges, including data heterogeneity, restricted cross-modality integration, and insufficient interpretability, while delineating prospective avenues in multi-modal data fusion, federated learning, and precision oncology. This review paper acts as a single source of information for researchers and doctors who want to help make AI systems for breast cancer diagnosis which are reliable, clear, and ready for use in the clinic.

title: Joint Channel and AoA Estimation in OTFS System with Fractional Doppler Channels
authors: Lin Litao, Boyi Tang, Zhen Chen, Jie Tang, Kai-Kit Wong, Chan-Byoung Chae, Xiu Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Orthogonal time frequency space (OTFS) modulation is a promising technology for high-Doppler-effect mobile communication. However, fractional Doppler shifts cause inter Doppler interference, degrading the channel estimation (CE) performance, particularly at low signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs). Previous approaches have framed Doppler shift estimation as a direction-of-arrival problem but have limited accuracy because of the finite number of available Doppler points. To overcome this challenge, we propose a delay-staggered pilot scheme that leverages the abundant resources in the delay domain, effectively increasing the number of points for CE and enhancing its accuracy. Additionally, we introduce a novel angle-of-arrival estimation method tailored to the unique channel characteristics of OTFS modulation, reducing the antenna array requirements. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior NMSE performance compared with existing approaches, including embedded pilot-based CE and grid evolution-based CE algorithms.

title: Emotion-Aware Multilingual Sign Language Interpretation Using Deep Learning
authors: V. Inbarasu, J. Shalini, S. Bharathi, J. Rakshita Bai, M. Sakthivel, G. Nagarajan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The barriers to communication between hearing and speech impaired people and the general populace still exist because of the lack of knowledge with regards to the sign language. The current sign language recognition technologies are mostly limited to alphabet recognition, are non-emotional, and only write the results in English. In this paper, I am going to introduce an Emotion-Aware Multilingual Sign Language Interpretation System based on Deep Learning which allows interpreting signs in real-time with both emotional and contextual meaning behind it. The presented system tracks hand movements and facial expressions with the help of a regular camera, recognizes gestures with the help of Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and interprets continuous stream of gestures with the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. It also has facial emotion recognition, which is used to determine the emotional status of the user (happy, sad, fear, neutral), and multilingual translation, which is used to generate English-to-Tamil. The read text is also transformed to speech to ensure that the communication is smooth. Experimental scoring has shown high level in all modules with gesture recognition of 94.2% accuracy, sentence level interpretation of 92.5% accuracy, emotion detection of 93.1% accuracy and multilingual translation with voice output has scored 96.0% accuracy. The system is a low-cost, convenient assistive system that can be implemented in hospitals and educational institutions.

title: EEG-Based Depression Detection with Temporal-Spectral and Graph Cross-Attention in a Sensor Signal-Processing Architecture
authors: Yongxin Wang, Yuan Sui, Haimei Yu, Zhihui Li, Haijun Lin
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG) provides a noninvasive basis for depression detection. However, building robust models remains challenging due to the nonstationary temporal-spectral dynamics, subject-dependent inter-channel relationships, and weakly constrained fusion across heterogeneous EEG representations. This paper proposes a Temporal-Spectral and Graph Cross-Attention Network (TS-GCAN) for EEG-based depression detection within a sensor signal processing framework, and develops a structurally constrained collaborative analysis paradigm. The proposed framework integrates multi-scale temporal-spectral encoding, prior-constrained adaptive graph learning, structure-aware cross-domain attention fusion, and prototype-based aggregation into a unified pipeline, thereby enhancing coordinated modeling across complementary representations. Experimental results on two public resting-state EEG datasets (PRED+CT and HUSM) show that TS-GCAN achieves 94.52% accuracy (Acc), 93.73% specificity (Spe), 94.56% F1-score (F1), and 0.948 AUC on PRED+CT, and 91.89% Acc, 91.54% Spe, 91.62% F1, and 0.970 AUC on HUSM. Paired statistical hypothesis testing across folds further confirms that the proposed method achieves significant performance improvements over competing baselines (p < 0.05). In addition, TS-GCAN maintains a moderate model size and computational cost while achieving millisecond-level inference latency. It should be noted that the results on PRED+CT are obtained from a subject-level balanced subset, with labels defined by BDI questionnaire thresholds. In contrast, HUSM provides complementary evidence under clinically grounded DSM-IV diagnostic labels. Therefore, the reported findings should be interpreted in the context of the current subject-level evaluation protocol and differing label definitions.

title: Decomposition and Successive Decomposition Methods and Algorithms for Nonconvex Optimization
authors: Yiqing Zhai, Ying Cui, Danny H. K. Tsang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Existing results on decomposition methods and algorithms for nonconvex problems are minimal. Parallel decomposition algorithms do not exist for nonconvex problems with coupling nonlinear equality constraints. Besides, decomposition structures (i.e., coupling variables and constraints) are not fully exploited in designing decomposition methods and algorithms. In this paper, we consider nonconvex problems with decomposition structures that are more general than those handled in the existing literature. We propose primal and dual decomposition and successive primal and successive dual decomposition methods and algorithms for these nonconvex problems, which exploit decomposition structures, allow for parallel and distributed implementations, produce the original nonconvex problems’ stationary points, and offer good opportunities to achieve superior tradeoff between convergence performance and computation time. Finally, we compare the proposed methods and algorithms and provide examples and numerical results to demonstrate their respective values. Notably, the proposed decomposition and successive decomposition methods and algorithms generalize the basic ones for convex problems, and the proposed successive decomposition methods and algorithms extend the existing ones for nonconvex problems, together enriching the decomposition theory.

title: Site-Specific Channel Modeling and Optimization of RIS-Assisted Multiuser MISO Systems
authors: Ziqi Liu, Wei Yu, Sean Victor Hum
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a physics-based channel modeling and optimization framework for reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS)-assisted downlink multi-user multiple-input single-output (MU-MISO) communication systems in site-specific environments. A hybrid ray-tracing (RT) and full-wave electromagnetic analysis approach is developed to construct a deterministic channel model that explicitly captures multipath propagation, RIS scattering behavior, and mutual coupling effects through a non-diagonal load impedance representation. Based on this model, an alternating optimization scheme jointly updates the base-station (BS) beamformer and RIS load impedances to maximize the minimum achievable rate under a total transmit power constraint and practical capacitance limits. The objective of the proposed framework is to provide a reliable initial assessment of the system-level impact of RIS deployment in realistic propagation scenarios. To evaluate this capability, the RIS is operated in a column-paired 1-bit control mode that enables exhaustive evaluation of all realizable configurations in both simulation and measurement. Performance is compared at the distribution level through achievable-rate histograms across all configurations and further examined under small user-location variations. The observed agreement between simulation and measurement demonstrates that the proposed framework reliably captures practical performance trends and provides useful guidance for the design and deployment of RIS-assisted MU-MISO systems in site-specific environments.

title: Reconfigurable Magnetic Field Shaping via Current-Controlled Relay Coils for Wirelessly Powering Capsule Endoscopy
authors: Chen Gao, Shiyang Luo, Haotian Shi, Hao Zhang, Yongxin Guo
publish: IEEE Journal of Microwaves
abstract: Wireless power transfer (WPT) for wireless capsule endoscopy (WCE) extends the operation time by replacing the capacity-limited on-board batteries with a receiver for a continuous power supply. However, the magnetic field pattern is usually fixed once the transmitting (Tx) coil is determined. Due to the uneven dwelling time of the capsule along the gastrointestinal tract, the desired charging region may vary in different practical scenarios. Under existing Tx configurations, such variation is mainly accommodated by adjusting the overall field intensity rather than the spatial field distribution, which may cause unnecessary magnetic exposure outside the target region. To address this limitation, a relay-based reconfigurable magnetic-field-shaping method is proposed to enable switching between the axial-dominant mode and the radial-dominant mode without rewinding the coil. Specifically, an analytical relay-based current-control method is formulated by parametrizing the Tx coil array, and the desired field pattern is realized through joint optimization of the array variables. In addition, field uniformity and electromagnetic exposure are compared with representative existing designs under unified conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed design achieves measured current ratios of 0.705 and 0.509 for the axial-dominant and radial-dominant modes, corresponding to measured field uniformities of 88% and 75.3%, respectively.

title: Analysis of China's Dual Carbon Control Progress and Its Impacts on the Power System
authors: Yuan Chen, Qing Yang, Jinyu Wen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Achieving carbon peaking and carbon neutrality is a broad and profound transformation across economic and social systems. The Chinese government has officially initiated a dual-control system for carbon emission. However, as the key component of low-carbon energy transition, China's power system has not yet taken a systematic analysis of the impacts of the dual-control mechanism, as well as its corresponding impact pathways and mechanism. To address this issue, the paper first reviews the policy background and motivations for China's transition from the previous dual-control system based on energy consumption to the current dual-control system focused on carbon emissions. It then conducts a comparative analysis of the key features of both approaches and summarizes prominent domestic and international research hotspots related to this topic. On this basis, considering China's own overall institutional framework for carbon emission dual-control, it explores and analyzes the implications of policy implementation for various components of the power system—including power generation, transmission, and demand loads. Finally, the paper concludes by identifying the challenges faced by the power system in terms of planning, dispatch and market, and proposes corresponding technical support requirements and recommendations.

title: YOLOv8-EfficientViT for Robust Personnel Detection in Substation Operation Scenes
authors: Ming Xue
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Accurate and real-time detection of on-site personnel is essential for safety supervision in intelligent substations. However, personnel detection in substation environments remains challenging due to complex structural backgrounds and frequent partial occlusion caused by transformers, steel frameworks, and other equipment. Conventional convolution-based detectors mainly rely on local receptive fields and may struggle to capture long-range contextual information under such conditions. To address these issues, this paper proposes a YOLOv8-EfficientViT model for substation personnel detection. The conventional convolutional backbone of YOLOv8 is replaced with an EfficientViT-based feature extractor to enhance global feature modeling while maintaining computational efficiency. The proposed framework preserves the lightweight one-stage detection architecture and supports real-time deployment on edge devices. Experiments conducted on a visible-light substation personnel dataset containing 6004 images demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior detection accuracy compared with the baseline YOLOv8 model, particularly under partial occlusion scenarios. Meanwhile, the model maintains competitive inference speed and reduced computational complexity. The results indicate that integrating an efficient transformer-based backbone is an effective and practical solution for robust personnel detection in complex industrial environments.

title: Early Anomaly Detection of Wind Turbine Main Bearings Based on Multi-Head Self-Attention Network
authors: Junshuai Yan, Can Wang, Peng Hu, Yan Du
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: An early anomaly detection method for main bearings of wind turbine based on a Multi-Head Self-Attention neural network is proposed in this paper. Firstly, the Quartile Algorithm and Grey Relational Analysis are used to perform data cleaning and feature selection on the data. Secondly, based on the MHSA network, a normal behavior model for the main bearing temperature of wind turbine is established to deeply mine the spatio-temporal features contained in massive high-dimensional SCADA time-series data. Finally, anomaly indicators based on the Kernel Density Estimation and sliding window algorithm are constructed to realize the automatic identification of early anomalies in main bearings. Validation and analysis are conducted using actual operating data from wind farms. The results show that the proposed method has excellent detection performance and can detect early anomalies in main bearings an average of 291.3 hours in advance.

title: Research on Synergistic Optimization of Source-Load-Storage Based on Integrated Energy
authors: Jinghong Zhou, Zhewei Cao, Jiangkai Jia, Xin Zhang, Yifan Zhao, Bin Zhang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To resolve renewable integration challenges, we propose an integrated energy system deeply coupling multienergy storage and flexible loads. MATLAB simulations confirm that compared to the unoptimized baseline (Scenario 4) with a cost of 8666.63 yuan and 757.33 kg emissions, Scenario 1 reduces operating costs by 2.1% to 8486.54 yuan, cuts carbon emissions by 152.97kg (20.2%), and boosts new energy utilization by 5.13%

title: Harmonic-excited Closed-slot Brushless Wound Rotor Synchronous Machine with Reduced Torque Ripple
authors: Zuhair Abbas, Ghulam Jawad Sirewal, Jin Hur
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a brushless wound rotor synchronous machine (WRSM) employing a self-excitation method based on harmonic field interaction and a closed-slot stator design for torque ripple reduction. In the proposed configuration, a harmonic magnetic field is established and regulated in the air gap through voltage induction in a rotor-mounted harmonic winding, providing self-excitation without brushes and slip rings. To further enhance electromagnetic performance, a third-harmonic component is injected into the armature current to introduce a controlled third-harmonic magnetomotive force (MMF) component for brushless excitation and achieve a higher air-gap flux density. The closed-slot stator topology contributes additional field smoothing by minimizing permeance variation along the air gap. Analytical modeling and two-dimensional finite element analysis (FEA) demonstrate that the proposed closed-slot B-WRSM achieves approximately 49% reduction in torque ripple compared with a semi-closed slot configuration, while maintaining comparable average torque and efficiency. Analytical modeling shows that the reduction in slot-induced permeance harmonics directly suppresses torque ripple components associated with both fundamental and harmonic MMFs. The combined approach of closed-slot geometry and third-harmonic current injection provides an effective means to improve torque smoothness and excitation stability, making the design suitable for high-performance and precision drive applications.

title: Integrating Network and Attack Graphs for Service-Centric Impact Analysis
authors: Joni Herttuainen, Vesa Kuikka, Kimmo K. Kaski
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Cyberattacks on enterprise networks exploit complex dependencies among infrastructure, services, and applications, which challenge traditional analysis methods that focus on attack paths or network topology in isolation. In this study, we introduce a novel probabilistic multilayer modelling framework, based on influence propagation in networks, that integrates attack graphs with the communication network topology, enabling a service-centric impact analysis of cyberattacks. Our method captures both the vulnerability exploitability and network connectivity, allowing us to assess the likelihood of attack propagation and cumulative impacts across interconnected services. By integrating standard vulnerability metrics (such as CVSS) with the network-level connectivity probabilities, the framework provides a cohesive view of the dynamics of cyberattacks. We validate this approach using a realistic case study of an enterprise network, demonstrating its ability to determine critical nodes, vulnerabilities, and service dependencies that significantly influence attack outcomes. Our findings show that integrating network and attack graph perspectives offers more actionable insights into risk assessment and mitigation planning, advancing the analysis of cyberattacks in complex networked environments.

title: Reliable Multi-Modal Object Re-Identification via Modality-Aware Graph Reasoning
authors: Xixi Wan, Aihua Zheng, Zi Wang, Bo Jiang, Jin Tang, Jixin Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Multi-modal data provides abundant and diverse object information, crucial for effective modal interactions in Re-Identification (ReID) task. However, existing approaches often overlook the quality variations in local features and fail to fully leverage the complementary information across modalities, particularly in cases where features are of low quality. In this paper, we propose to address this issue by leveraging a novel graph reasoning model, termed the Modality-aware Graph Reasoning Network (MGRNet). Specifically, we first construct modality-aware graphs to enhance the extraction of fine-grained local details by effectively capturing and modeling the relationships between patches. Subsequently, the selective graph nodes swap operation is employed to alleviate the adverse effects of low-quality local features by considering both local and global information, enhancing the representation of discriminative information. Finally, the swapped modality-aware graphs are fed into the local-aware graph reasoning module, which propagates multi-modal information to yield a reliable feature representation. Another advantage of the proposed graph reasoning approach is its ability to reconstruct missing modal information by exploiting inherent structural relationships, thereby minimizing disparities between different modalities. Experimental results on four benchmarks (RGBNT201, Market1501-MM, RGBNT100, MSVR310) indicate that the proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance in multi-modal object ReID. The code for our method will be available upon acceptance.

title: Autonomous Causal Discovery: Evaluating LLMs' Priors and Constraint Strategies for Reliability_supp1-3689960.pdf
authors: Xiangyu Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Expert-guided Causal Structure Learning (CSL) incorporates prior knowledge to improve the accuracy of causal discovery, yet the acquisition of such knowledge is often restricted by the availability of human experts. While Large Language Models (LLMs) provide an alternative source of causal priors, LLM-derived knowledge can be inconsistent with the true causal structure due to hallucinations or contextual misinterpretations. This paper introduces a structural constraint measurement framework, which defines constraint strength and constraint quality to describe reliability and effectiveness, enabling a systematic evaluation of LLM-derived constraints. Using this framework, we evaluate five categories of structural constraints: Edge Existence (EEC), Edge Forbidden (EFC), Path Existence (PEC), Path Forbidden (PFC), and Order Constraints (OC). Our theoretical and empirical analyses demonstrate that while EEC offers high constraint strength, it exhibits low quality when derived from LLMs; conversely, PFC and OC provide a balanced trade-off between search-space pruning and reliability. Building on these insights, we propose a two-level CSL optimization framework that partitions the search space by node order and refines the structure using global path constraints. The results show that this framework provides an effective way to incorporate noisy LLM-derived priors into CSL, particularly in settings where expert knowledge is limited.

title: Mitigate Catastrophic Remembering via Continual Self-Paced Dual-Knowledge Purification for Noisy Lifelong Person Re-Identification_supp1-3693671.pdf
authors: Jiahuan Zhou
publish: N/A
abstract: Current Lifelong Person Re-Identification (LReID) methods focus on tackling the data streams with accurate labels. When the data streams with noisy labels are given, their performance is severely degraded since the model continually remembers erroneous knowledge induced by the label noise. Moreover, the well-known issue of catastrophic forgetting in LReID is exacerbated by noisy labels, which disrupt the retention of correct knowledge from previous models. Such a practical Noisy LReID task is important but challenging, and few works have attempted to address it. In this paper, we pioneer the investigation of Noisy LReID and propose a Continual Self-Paced Dual-Knowledge Purification (CSDP) method to address the catastrophic remembering of erroneous knowledge, catastrophic forgetting of correct knowledge, and limited acquisition capacity, simultaneously. Specifically, both local distribution information and global classification results are jointly leveraged to enhance the recognition and correction of noisy labels. In addition, a self-paced learning paradigm is introduced to facilitate the progressive accumulation of reliable knowledge. Finally, a Noisy LReID benchmark is constructed to support research on this task. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed CSDP method, under comparable computational overhead, outperforms state-of-the-art methods by a large margin.
