Our code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/zhoujiahuan1991/TPAMI-CSDP</uri>

title: BAPA-Net: Cross-Domain Few-Shot Object Detection in Remote Sensing via Background-Aligned Prototype Aggregation
authors: Chao Li, Qi Zhang, Xiao Liang, Yuhang Xiao, Shenzhi Li, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: While large-scale supervised learning has catalyzed remarkable strides in remote sensing object detection, its real-world efficacy is frequently stifled by the formidable costs of expert annotation and the inherent scarcity of high-quality samples. Cross-domain few-shot object detection (CD-FSOD) has emerged as a strategic paradigm to alleviate this dependency by facilitating knowledge transfer from label-rich natural domains to data-starved remote sensing environments. However, the pronounced geospatial domain shifts and intricate background interference often lead to suboptimal generalization in existing frameworks. To address these impediments, we introduce the Background-Aligned Prototype Aggregation Network (BAPA-Net), a consolidated framework meticulously crafted to enhance the invariance of cross-domain representations. Specifically, BAPA-Net integrates three pivotal mechanisms: (1) Cross-Domain Background Fusion Alignment (CBFA), which decouples and synchronizes source-target background distributions through an innovative sampling-clustering- aggregation pipeline, effectively mitigating low-level feature interference; (2) Soft Mask-guided Prototype Augmentation (SMPA), which employs dynamic probabilistic Gaussian masks to construct a continuous spatial attention mechanism, thereby filtering peripheral noise and refining prototype representativeness; and (3) Prototype Retrieval and Knowledge Aggregation (PRKA), which leverages a cross-domain Cache bank and attention-weighted retrieval to aggregate multi-source information adaptively, ensuring persistent exploitation of limited labeled samples. Extensive evaluations across diverse benchmarks, including high-altitude, underwater, and deep-space scenarios, demonstrate that BAPA-Net consistently outperforms state-of-the-art (SOTA) methodologies. Specifically, our framework achieves substantial performance gains, improving the mAP by 5.0% to 10.8% relative to the baseline across 1, 5, and 10-shot protocols. Our results validate the framework’s robustness in bridging the semantic gap between natural and remote sensing domains. The code is open source: https://github.com/JackLeeAI/BAPA-Net.

title: A Variegated Look at Direct-to-Cell Satellites in the Wild
authors: Wei Liu, Jingyi Lan, Yuanjie Li, Bowen Sun, Yekai Dong, Yufeng Wang, Hewu Li, Qian Wu, Zeqi Lai, Jun Liu
publish: Abstracts of the 2026 ACM SIGMETRICS International Conference on Measurement and Modeling of Computer Systems

authors: Zhuo Zhang, Jingyuan Zhang, Jintao Huang, Hui Wang, Yue Yu, Hongzhi Zhang, Xun Zhou, Lizhen Qu, Zenglin Xu

title: Modeling and Experiment of Horizontal Vibration Isolation for Series-Connected Inverted Pendulum and Single-Chamber Pneumatic Spring
authors: Qiang Li, Dengfeng Xu, Baolong Yu, Gang Wang, Wenhui Zhang, Liekai Su, Guoting Huang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To improve the horizontal vibration isolation performance of pneumatic springs, this paper proposes a novel structure that serially connects a single-chamber pneumatic spring with an inverted pendulum. Taking the pneumatic spring piston as the research object, the compressed air pressure is calculated, the horizontal restoring force and stiffness of the spring are deduced, and the horizontal vibration isolation dynamic equation as well as the analytical expression of horizontal natural frequency is established. Parametric analysis identifies the inverted pendulum length as the key factor governing horizontal vibration isolation performance. For heavyload and low-frequency vibration isolation engineering demands, a four-unit experimental vibration isolation system with a 10-ton bearing capacity and a designed horizontal natural frequency of nearly 1 Hz is developed. Experimental tests show the actual horizontal natural frequency is 0.75 Hz, and the horizontal transmissibility reaches −10 dB at an external horizontal disturbance frequency of 4.5 Hz, validating the theoretical design. This system realizes heavy-load bearing and low-frequency vibration isolation simultaneously, and has broad application prospects in the horizontal vibration isolation of bases for precision manufacturing, optics, life sciences, medicine and other fields.

title: Best First Search based Control of Residential Appliance Load Demand
authors: Anoohya Runkana, Kandala Prapthi, Nagaraja Chitimi M., Rashmi M. R., Hoong Pin Lee, Rachana M. Hullamani
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The increasing use of rooftop photovoltaic (PV) cells in residential buildings requires effective demand management to utilise the available power effectively. This paper presents a household appliance load demand control method based on the Best First Search (BFS) algorithm, implemented in MATLAB. The proposed approach schedules household appliances by considering the available <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> power, demand by the appliances, and user-defined priority levels. The demand control problem is solved by using BFS algorithm, which explores possible appliance combinations and selects the best combination that operates within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> power limit while considering a greater number of appliances and its priorities. To represent realistic household time of the day-based priority variations are also included. Simulation results show that the proposed BFS based method effectively utilises available PV power with maximum number of appliances being supplied at all the times.

title: Machine Learning-Based Analysis of Lithium-Ion Battery Ageing in Electric Vehicles Under Dynamic Conditions
authors: Karthik Chandu, Satish Bolli, S. Gayatri
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Lithium-ion batteries are the primary energy source in modern electric vehicles (EVs); however, their performance and lifespan degrade over time due to complex electrochemical ageing mechanisms that are intensified under dynamic driving conditions. Accurate prediction of battery ageing indicators, such as State-of-Health (SoH) and Remaining Useful Life (RUL), is essential for ensuring safety, reliability, and optimal energy management. Traditional empirical and physics-based models often fail to represent nonlinear degradation behavior arising from variable loads, temperature fluctuations, regenerative braking events, and diverse driving patterns. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive machine learning-based framework for analyzing lithium-ion battery ageing under realistic EV operating profiles. The proposed approach integrates advanced feature engineering with classical machine learning and deep learning models to extract meaningful health indicators from voltage, current, temperature, and capacity data. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks and hybrid ensemble architectures are employed to capture complex temporal dependencies that are not adequately handled by conventional models. The framework is evaluated using dynamically cycled lithiumion battery datasets under diverse load conditions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms baseline regression, tree-based, and standalone neural network models in SoH and RUL prediction. Model performance is assessed using standard metrics such as MAE, RMSE, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex>, along with degradation trend visualizations. The results confirm the effectiveness of machine learning techniques in capturing battery degradation behavior under highly dynamic operating conditions. Overall, the proposed framework offers a scalable and flexible solution for predictive battery ageing analysis with strong potential for integration into real-time Battery Management Systems (BMS). The model was evaluated on over 600 dynamically cycled charge-discharge cycles using a 70:30 train-test split with 10-fold cross-validation, demonstrating robust generalization across ageing stages.

title: Message from Program Chairs

title: Novel “S-Cut” Flows for Highly Uniform Multi-Tier Integrated Oxide Semiconductor DRAM
authors: Ziheng Bai, Hao Shi, Zhou He, Zhao Liu, Jianqi Chen, Zhiyu Tian, Huan Liu, Jiahan Ke, Jiajia Wu, Gangping Yan, Wenjun Chen, Zehuan Hei, Tongshang Su, Baodong Han, Xueli Ma, Xiangsheng Wang, Yuke Li, Jinjuan Xiang, Weihan Fan, Hongbo Sun, Guilei Wang, Hao Meng, Mingyuan Liu, Kanyu Cao, Chao Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: We propose “S-Cut” (S-shaped Cut) integration flows to achieve five-tier oxide-based DRAM transistors and a 64 × 256 array without parasitic channels on a 12-inch manufacturing line. Two “S-Cut” approaches are investigated: the “outer-cut” flow, which removes the parasitic channel from the bit line direction, and the “inner-cut” flow, which removes it from inside the transistor hole. After flow and process optimization, including bit line/oxide channel contact optimization and common bit line/staircase parasitic resistance reduction, the “inner-cut” flow demonstrates good uniformity both across the five tiers and across the entire 12-inch wafer. The <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{on}}$</tex> of a single transistor reaches <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$77 \mu \mathrm{A} / \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{off}}$</tex> is less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1 \times 10^{-18} \mathrm{A}$</tex>/cell, and I-V hysteresis is nearly 0 mV. Remarkably, the uniformity (Std./Mean across the 12-inch wafer) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{th}}$</tex> among the five tiers ranges from 0.10 to 0.22, while that of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{on}}$</tex> ranges from 0.14 to 0.51. The survival ratio for both <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$V_{\text{th}}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$I_{\text{on}}$</tex> exceeds 90%. This study is the first to demonstrate that “S-Cut” is a promising integration pathway for oxide-based multi-tier DRAM architectures on a 12-inch manufacturing line.

title: Toward Robust Autonomous Driving in Rural and Off-Road Environments: A Perception Based Approach
authors: Griffen Agnello, Shaikh Tanveer Hossain, Ishparsh Uprety, Xinghui Zhao
publish: 2026 IEEE 4th International Conference on Mobility, Operations, Services and Technologies (MOST)
abstract: Autonomous driving in rural and off-road environments remains challenging due to irregular terrain, limited infrastructure, and the absence of consistent visual cues that urban perception systems typically rely on. To address these difficulties, this study integrates a segmentation-based perception model, YOLOv11-Seg, into an autonomous driving pipeline to improve environmental perception in unstructured settings. The model was trained using a combination of real rural imagery from the GOOSE dataset and synthetic scenes generated in the AWSIM simulator, then optimized with TensorRT and deployed within the Autoware framework as a full-frame segmentation module. Simulation testing demonstrated that the system can run in real time and produce meaningful segmentation outputs suitable for downstream mapping and planning tasks. Our experimental results show that segmentation-driven perception holds strong potential for rural environments, while also highlighting the ongoing need for improved dataset balance, refined labeling strategies, and further optimization for real-world deployment. We expect this work will pave the way toward deploying robust autonomous driving applications in rural environments.

title: Demonstration of MultiVis-Agent: Interactively Generating Reliable Visualizations via Logic-Rule Agents
authors: Jinwei Lu, Jiawei Lu, Chen Zhang, Yuanfeng Song, Raymond Chi-Wing Wong

title: Section-Weighted Hybrid Approach for Legal Case Retrieval
authors: Rajith Arulanandam, Nisansa De Silva
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Finding truly analogous precedents requires capturing legal reasoning beyond surface word overlap. We present a two-stage, section-aware framework for legal case retrieval that first segments raw judgments into facts, issues, decision, and reasoning using a deterministic large language model (LLM) offline. In Stage 1, we combine parallel lexical (BM25) and semantic (dense ANN) whole-document searches via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) to form a high-recall candidate pool. In Stage 2, we perform fine-grained, like-for-like comparisons (e.g., query reasoning vs. candidate reasoning). To address the scale mismatch between unbounded lexical scores and cosine similarities, we apply query-wise Z-score normalization before aggregating signals with learned section weights. For the top results, the system returns the relevant section text with a concise, grounded rationale and party-stance labels. We evaluate on a jurisdiction-scale benchmark, demonstrating consistent gains over strong lexical and neural baselines while maintaining high candidate coverage.

title: Mechatronics-AI Collaboration: A Flexible Automated Testing Framework for Smart Home Devices Based on Multimodal Large Language Models
authors: Bizhi Yu, Chaoxia Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Modern smart home HMIs (Human-Machine Interfaces) are highly complex. Traditional testing methods depend on predefined coordinates and rigid fixtures. Consequently, these approaches suffer from poor flexibility and long setup cycles. This paper presents an AI-integrated mechatronic framework for flexible testing. At the perception layer, a multimodal large language model (MLLM) is utilized. A coarse-to-fine visual localization algorithm is also proposed. This setup achieves zero-shot, precise UI localization using unstructured product manuals. At the execution layer, a visual-to-physical mapping model is constructed. Additionally, a Hooke's law-based biomimetic touch mechanism is developed to prevent screen damage from rigid robotic hits. Tests indicate up to 90.0% localization accuracy on thermostat displays. The proposed system realizes a complete closed loop: cognition, perception, flexible execution, and visual feedback. This framework significantly enhances testing adaptability and provides a new method for product quality control.

title: A Survey on Multilingual Fact-Checking
authors: Yanpeng Ou, Bao Wugedele, Xiaobing Zhao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: With the acceleration of global information dissemination and the cross-regional penetration of social media platforms, misinformation detection and fact-checking in multilingual environments have become core challenges in the fields of computational social science and natural language processing. Multilingual fact-checking aims to develop technologies capable of automatically identifying, retrieving, and verifying factual claims across multiple languages, which is of significant importance for fostering a healthy information ecosystem. This paper systematically reviews the research progress in text-based multilingual fact-checking, providing a comprehensive survey covering tasks, datasets and methodologies. It aims to offer researchers a systematic overview of the field and to promote the advancement and application of multilingual fact-checking technologies.

title: PLL-XO Codesign for Energy-Efficient and Ultra-Low Noise Frequency Synthesis
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)

title: Climate-Driven Disease Dynamics: A Scoping Review of Machine Learning Applications in Predicting Vector-Borne, Tick-Borne, Airborne, and Waterborne Diseases
authors: Vanitha K, K.B. Jayanthi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The increasing global incidence of vector-borne, airborne, waterborne, and tick-borne diseases is strongly influenced by climate variability, environmental factors, and human activity. This scoping review analyzed 34 studies published between 2019 and 2024 to assess the role of climate data in predicting and managing these diseases. The findings show that increasing temperatures, changing rainfall patterns, and extreme weather events significantly influence disease transmission by expanding vector habitats and altering pathogen survival. Vector-borne diseases such as dengue and malaria are strongly affected by temperature and humidity, while airborne diseases like influenza and tuberculosis are influenced by air pollution and humidity changes. Waterborne diseases, including cholera, are associated with flooding and contaminated water sources, and tick-borne diseases show geographic expansion due to warmer climates and longer activity seasons. Although climate-based forecasting models, IoT sensors, and remote sensing have improved early warning systems, gaps in regional surveillance data remain. The study highlights the need for interdisciplinary approaches combining climate science, machine learning, and public health to strengthen outbreak preparedness and prediction.

title: Ensemble based Driver Behaviour Analysis and Accident Mitigation using CNN and LSTM
authors: Mohan S T, Sreekanth Rallapalli
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The proposed system is a new method for improving road safety by launching intelligent accident mitigation on smart highways that are deep learning models, specifically Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks, as those are among the most effective tools. Thus, the system here has taken it one step further by combining visual data with the temporal deep learning behavioral patterns of the drivers to be able to detect and interpret the possible hazardous driving behavior accurately and in the course of time. The CNN module is employed to gather significant spatial features from the video or the images of the driver's face or the scenes of the environment part that is consistent with the signal of facial expression, the eye movement, or even the traffic signs around. These features, which are continuously updated, are further processed with LSTM that is best suiting in the recognition of time-dependent patterns of the driver. LSTM then analyzes the driver's actions and recognizes the instances of fatigue, distraction, or aggressive behavior if any. The ensemble of CNN and LSTM is a hybrid framework that makes it possible for the system to digest comprehensively the behavioral part of its input in an anticipatory mode to enable it to forecast the accidents that are going to happen before running into them. The method is quite new, but it is still a major input to the field of intelligent transportation because it is actively engaged in the early detection of accidents and responses to emergencies hence cutting down the number of accident occurrences and boosting the overall safety and efficiency of highway systems.

title: Rolling Bearing Domain Generalization Fault Diagnosis Method Based on CWT-MTNet
authors: Peng Li, Chenyang Lu, Lixiu Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the failure of traditional deep transfer learning when target domain labels are completely unavailable for key equipment under extreme working conditions, this paper proposes a domain generalization fault diagnosis model (CWTMTNet) based on Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) and multi-resolution feature fusion vision Transformer. CWT converts 1D vibration signals into 2D time-frequency diagrams to enhance non-stationary feature expression. An enhanced multi-resolution feature fusion (EnhancedMRF) structure extracts multi-scale local features, while a lightweight Swin Transformer performs global association modeling to extract domain-invariant features. Experiments on the CWRU bearing dataset show 99.75% accuracy under single condition, 91.5% average accuracy in crossload transfer without target labels, and 91.2% accuracy under 6dB noise, demonstrating excellent generalization and anti-noise capability.

title: eBeeMetrics: An eBPF-based Library Framework for Feedback-free Observability of QoS Metrics
authors: Muntaka Ibnath, Mohammadreza Rezvani, Daniel Wong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: Many system management runtimes (SMRs), such as resource management and power management techniques, rely on quality-of-service (QoS) metrics, such as tail latency or throughput, as feedback. These QoS metrics are generally neither observable with hardware performance counters nor directly observable within the OS kernel. This introduces complexity and overhead in instrumenting the application and integrating QoS performance metric feedback with many management runtimes. To bridge this gap, we introduced eBeeMetrics, an eBPF-based library framework to accurately observe application-level metrics derived from only eBPF-observable events, such as system calls. eBeeMetrics can be used as a drop-in replacement to decouple system management runtimes from QoS metric feedback reporting, or can supplement existing QoS metrics to better identify server-side dynamics. eBeeMetrics achieves a strong correlation with real-world measured throughput and latency metrics across various latency-sensitive workloads. The eBeeMetrics tool is open-source; the source code is available at: https://github.com/Ibnathism/eBeeMetrics.

title: Self-Supervised Representation Learning for Data-Efficient Crop Yield Estimation from Multi-Temporal Remote Sensing Data
authors: M. Jeyanthi, SK.Piramu Preethika
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Crop yield estimation is an important part of agricultural planning, food security and policy making. Traditional methods of predicting yields typically draw on a lot of ground-truth data which are very expensive and time-consuming to collect especially over extensive areas. This study handles this issue of lack of data at higher resolution for estimation of crop yield by contributing to develop a framework called Self-Supervised Representation Learning (SSRL) which makes use of Multi-Temporal remote sensing imagery to learn rich spatiotemporal embeddings without need for huge datasets for their trainings. The framework combines 3D Convolutional Neural Networks and Temporal Transformers for extracting features from the data, self-supervised pre-text problems such as Temporal Order Prediction, Masked Patch Reconstruction, Contrastive Learning for self-supervised pre-training and few-shots learning together with MAML algorithm for fine-tuning with limited data. Further, the embeddings are combined with ancillary agronomic data such as soil, weather and irrigation variables using a multimodal attention mechanism. Experiments on Sentinel-2 imagery for maize fields in India show Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.82 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex>, Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.31 \mathrm{t} / \text{ha}$</tex>, R2 of 93.4 % and Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of 4.9 % which are better than five state-of-the-art models namely Random Forest, LSTM, GRU, 3D-CNN, and Transformer based models. The results show that a self supervised learning approach coupled with multimodal fusion and few-shot adaptation is highly effective to perform accurate, scalable and data-efficient crop yield prediction. This methodology gives practical insights to the uses of precision agriculture, regional planning and sustainable resource management.

title: IoT-Enabled Smart Helmet for Real Time Rider Safety Monitoring and Automated Emergency Response System Technology
authors: P. Rajeswari, D. Kishore, M. Manoj, T. Logeshkumar, R. Dhanushkodi
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In this case, two-wheeler accidents are a serious issue, as it takes a long time to respond to them, and no one can pay attention to a rider in real time. The proposed project suggests the use of an IoT-Enhanced Smart Helmet that will monitor the rider's real-time safety and automatically respond to emergencies to enhance road safety and minimize deaths from accidents. It has developed the system around the ESP32 microcontroller as the core processing unit upon which the data is received, data is analyzed, and communications are realized. The helmet will be smart and include a number of sensors, including an accelerometer and vibration sensor, to notify of unexpected impacts, unusual movements, and possible accidents. The alcohol sensor that measures the rider's alcohol concentration monitors for unscrupulous riding. It is also sustainable and energy-efficient because it uses a solar panel, a charging controller, and a battery, which means it can be applied in the long term. It has a GPS module that enables it to get the rider's geographical position in real time, as well as in an emergency. The sensor data, along with the location, is sent over GPRS to the Adafruit Cloud, so one can always monitor the sensor even without being physically present. The system will automatically email the programmed emergency contacts with important information, such as location, in case of an accident or when alcohol consumption exceeds the required mandate. The presented smart helmet system can contribute to rider safety by enabling the detection of accidents in time, emergency notifications, and real-time monitoring. It uses less energy, is a renewable source, and supports IoT architecture; that is why it is a dependable solution for intelligent transportation and next-generation road safety applications.

title:
Contact-Length Scaling and Carrier Injection in ML MoS
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    FETs with Hybrid Sb S/D Contacts
authors: Szu-Huan Hsu, Wen-Chia Wu, Chao-Hsin Chien, Yiming Li
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this work, we investigate monolayer (ML) MoS2 field-effect transistors (FETs) employing hybrid antimony (Sb) source/drain (S/D) contacts. Experimentally validated density-functional-theory (DFT) calculations combined with device simulations reveal that stronger metal-induced gap states (MIGS) at the edge interface enhance Fermi-level pinning (FLP), resulting in a higher Schottky barrier height (SBH) compared to the top interface. When the contact length (L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</inf>) decreases below the transfer length (L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</inf>), vertical carrier injection is suppressed and transport becomes edge-dominated, allowing the hybrid contact to achieve a lower contact resistance (R<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</inf>) in the short-L<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">C</inf> regime. These findings underscore the critical importance of S/D contact interface engineering for achieving scalable and low-resistance 2D FET technologies.

title: Comprehensive Evaluation of Techniques for Autism Spectrum Disorder Detection: A Deep Analysis
authors: Patan Naazle Firdos, B. Sabeena, Shaik Yaseen, Damam Vaishnavi, K. Radhika
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder that impacts communication and social interactions. Early detection is critical for facilitating early intervention; however, traditional screening tools rely extensively on behavioral analysis and expert opinion, making them time-consuming and less accessible. This paper proposes an automated screening system that employs deep learning algorithms to analyze facial images. ResNet-18, EfficientNet B1, and EfficientNet B2 CNN models are modified by transfer learning to identify patterns linked to ASD. The screening system employed a dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2, 9 4 0}$</tex> labeled facial images for model training and testing. Among the models tested, ResNet-18 reported the highest accuracy of 86 %, followed by EfficientNet B1 with 81 % accuracy and EfficientNet B2 with 78 % accuracy. To enhance model interpretability, the Grad-CAM algorithm is used to visualize facial areas that contribute to predictions. The trained model is integrated into a Django web application that allows users to upload images and receive screening results instantly. The proposed system can be used as an assistive screening tool that aims to facilitate early awareness and promote clinical assessment.

title: Detection of the Upper Surface Pose of Yarn Cones for Robot Gripping
authors: Jian He, Yujie Chen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: To address the challenge of accurately measuring the center position of the spindle during robotic picking operations, this paper proposes a 3D vision-based algorithm for detecting the upper surface of yarn cones. This algorithm can accurately estimate the center position and orientation of the spindle, thereby guiding the robotic arm to insert it and complete the picking task.First, based on the image data captured by the 3D camera, the circular contour of the top surface of the yarn cone is extracted and optimized. Next, the least squares method is used to fit this circular contour, yielding a mathematical expression for the top surface's circular contour. Building on this, a pose estimation model is constructed to calculate the normal vector of the circle's center and the spatial center coordinates of the top surface using local point cloud data. The experimental results demonstrate that the detection model proposed in this paper exhibits good robustness, and its detection accuracy meets operational requirements: the average detection error for the yarn cone's axis vector is less than 1.3°, and the average detection error for the distance from the center of the upper end face to the axis is less than 1.24 mm, ensuring that the robot can reliably perform yarn cone grasping operations. Importantly, this model can detect yarn cones in any orientation with greater efficiency than point cloud processing; it not only offers a new perspective on yarn cone center detection but also provides valuable insights for related object detection.

title: Optimization and Simulation of a Broadband Vibration Energy Harvester for Self-Powered Transmission Line Applications
authors: Jie Yang, Lihan Wang, Qishuai Liang, Chuan Zhou, Hongxiao Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Traditional vibration energy harvesters face limitations such as narrow operating frequency bands and low power density under micro-wind excitations of transmission lines, which restrict their use in self-powered monitoring systems. This paper presents an optimized design method for a broadband piezoelectric-electromagnetic coupled vibration energy harvester. The approach combines cooperative tuning of the cantilever beam length and proof mass to achieve broadband resonance, alongside coil parameter optimization to maximize electromagnetic conversion. Finite element simulations and numerical analyses were conducted to evaluate the dynamic response and energy harvesting performance of multiple structural configurations. Results demonstrate that the optimized harvester operates efficiently across a frequency range of 16.5 Hz to 49.2 Hz, effectively covering the typical spectrum of breeze-induced vibrations in transmission lines. The output power remains stable between 3.84 mW and 5.57 mW under broadband excitation, confirming the design's suitability for self-powered operation of online monitoring equipment. This work provides a practical solution for enhancing the energy autonomy of intelligent power grid monitoring systems.

title: Design, Modeling and High-Performance MTPA Control of an Oil-Submersible PMSM
authors: Yunlong Peng, Tingting An, Liangfang Zhang, Yanliang Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the design, modeling, and high-performance maximum torque per ampere (MTPA) control of a unit Oil-submersible permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) for electric submersible pump systems. First, the stator and rotor topologies, pole-slot combination, stator slot geometry, and permanent magnet materials of the oil-submersible motor are studied, based on which the design scheme of the unit motor is developed and the corresponding motor parameters are presented. Then, considering the operating characteristics of the oilsubmersible motor, an improved nonlinear mathematical model is established in which the inductance and flux-linkage parameters are influenced by core saturation and cross-coupling effects. Finally, a simulation study on the high-performance MTPA control method is carried out by implementing the improved motor model in simulation software, thereby verifying the effectiveness of both the motor modeling approach and the highperformance MTPA control method.

title: An Explainable ML Framework for Predicting Booking Cancellations in the Hospitality Sector
authors: Nidamanuri Srinu, Yakkanti Jyothi, D. Bujji Babu
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Hotel cancellations is one of the primary pain points of the hospitality industry because it distorts demand indications and leads to revenue losses approximated at approximately 20 percent. However, prediction of cancellations in booking has been an understudied field especially with regards to the behavioural forces behind cancellations. The gap in this paper will be filled by proposing a novel method of predicting hotel booking cancellations based on stacked generalization and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI). In particular, the integration of linear, tree-based, non-linear and deep learning schemes into one meta-model led to the improvement of the accuracy rate up to 96 %. Furthermore, this article concentrates on interpretability, where the driver aspects of behavioural factors that lead to cancellation are found to be location, type of room and consumer segments. Such a strategy may offer hoteliers not only with very precise forecasts but also with marketing insights that will enable them push strategy to reduce loss caused by cancelling. The findings of the study can be effectively used as a solution to the problems associated with predicting booking cancellations and at the same time achieving a balance between forecast prediction and the capacity to make practical implications. The primary objectives of this research are to develop an accurate booking cancellation prediction model and to provide interpretable explanations for managerial decision-making. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed stacked ensemble model achieves an accuracy of up to 96%, outperforming individual machine learning classifiers. Explainability analysis using SHAP reveals that lead time, room type, customer segment, and booking channel are the most influential factors driving cancellation behavior.

title: AI-Assisted RFIC Design —State-of-the-Art and Challenges
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)

title: Design and Performance Analysis of an Axial-Flux Permanent Magnet Motor Based on Halbach Array
authors: Lin Feng, Huanhuan Song, Xiaolong Xu, Yanliang Xu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper proposes a single-stator, single-rotor axial flux permanent magnet synchronous motor (SSSR-AFPMM) to address the requirements for high torque density and compact structure in humanoid robot joint drives. A 3D finite element model based on Maxwell was established to simulate and analyse its electromagnetic performance. The results indicate that the motor has a rated power of 890 W and a rated torque of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.5 \mathrm{N} \cdot \mathrm{m}$</tex>, thereby meeting the design performance requirements. By analysing the influence of the auxiliary pole angle in the Halbach array on the electromagnetic torque characteristics, it was found that when the auxiliary pole angle is 4°, cogging torque is effectively suppressed and the motor's operational performance is significantly improved. The research results indicate that this motor has good application potential in terms of high torque density and high power density, and can guide the development and improvement of humanoid robot joint motors.

title: Hybrid Transformer-Statistical Framework for Explainable Anomaly Detection in Time-Series Data: Enhancing Operational Monitoring and Security
authors: Naeem Akhtar, Anurag Rana
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Anomaly detection in operational time series data is an important part of intelligent monitoring systems in the modern business and industrial environment, where the need for reliability, performance management, and decision support via temporal data is great. This paper proposes an adaptive and explainable anomaly detection system which combines Transformer-based deep temporal modeling and classical statistical deviation analysis. The proposed hybrid approach works in semi-unsupervised mode by incorporating reconstruction-based anomaly scoring algorithm along with zscore and interquartile range (IQR) measures by a unified fusion approach. To provide the robustness of calibration on heterogeneous datasets, a quantile-based thresholding mechanism is used. The framework is evaluated using both synthetic and real-world data sets from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark, and standard evaluation metrics used to detect anomalies (along with operational alert measures, e.g. false alarm rate, alert density and mean time between alerts). In addition, an embedded Explainable AI (XAI) module offers perturbation-based temporal attributions for discovered anomalies in order to determine the cause and location of the detected anomalies. Experimental results show stable performance in different operation scenarios, and it points out the trade-off between the detection sensitivity and alert stability.

title: Evaluating Logical Reasoning Ability of Large Language Models
authors: Emunah S-S. Chan, Aldar C-F. Chan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: It is observed that large language models exhibit reasoning abilities when they are sufficiently large. A key open question is whether these models carry out reasoning or simply recite memorized texts encountered during their training. We propose a new dataset comprising of unseen reasoning questions requiring semantic and deductive logical reasoning skills to evaluate the reasoning ability of large language models. The proposed evaluation framework has several desirable properties, including resilience to training data contamination, ease of answer verification, extensibility, and automated test case generation. We found that large language models exhibit reasoning ability, but this does not match the level of human intelligence. Besides, their reasoning ability is algorithmic in nature.

title: A Compact 3T1F FeRAM Compute Cell for In-Memory MAC Using Partial Polarization Switching
authors: Vikrant Singh, Anand Bulusu, Girish Pahwa
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In-memory computing (IMC) alleviates data-movement overhead in AI workloads by performing multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations inside memory arrays. Ferro-electric capacitors (FeCAPs) offer non-volatility and analog charge response, but conventional FeRAM readout is destructive. This work proposes a compact 3T1F FeRAM compute cell that uses partial polarization switching to enable quasi-non-destructive readout during MAC. A nucleation-limited-switching (NLS) FeCAP model captures pulse-width-dependent switching behavior. A pre-conditioning pulse establishes a stable ~100 mV differential at the internal floating node for opposite polarization states, enabling multiple MAC cycles before weight refresh. A 4-bit weight is implemented using four 3T1F cells, and SPICE simulations on a 4 × 4 array confirm correct MAC operation and stable partial-switching readout.

title: Deep Learning-Based Sign Language Classification with IoT-Enabled Emergency Alert Support
authors: Sunad Kumar A N, Likhith Gowda S, Poorvika M S, Jeevan H M, Uma H R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The difficulties that people with speech or hearing impairments have communicating highlight the increasing demand for assistive technology, such as systems that can recognize sign language. This paper, Deep Learning-Based Sign Language Classification with IoT-Enabled Emergency Alert Support, presents a real-time framework that uses computer vision and deep learning to detect, classify, and translate hand signs. For creating datasets, CNN-based model training, live gesture prediction, multilingual translation, and voice conversion, the system uses a Tkinter-based interface. It incorporates an ESP32-powered Internet of Things module that can send emergency SMS alerts in order to address safety. The suggested model improves accessibility and security by fusing artificial intelligence and the Internet of Things, providing a comprehensive solution for the community of people with speech and hearing impairments.

title: Unified AI-Driven Multimodel Framework for Real-Time Lecture Understanding and Study Support
authors: P J Kiruthiga, Kolaparthi Sri Venkata Harshith, Koya Sahith, Mandhava Lakshmi Kavitha, K Lakshmi Deekshitha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The significant increase in the use of digital learning resources like online lectures, recorded classes, and digital study materials has led to students facing a very large amount of information, thus making it difficult to get the main insights quickly. The authors of this paper introduce an AI-Powered Multimodal Lecture and Study Companion that aims to make the learning process easier by integrating automatic text extraction, smart summarization, and uninterrupted content delivery all in one platform. The system is built on a web interface that is based on Streamlit, and it takes inputs from two main sources: (i) YouTube video links, where the platform first captures and then transcribes the audio into accurate text, and (ii) PDF documents, where text that is part of the document is extracted directly. The application uses the Google AI Studio API for sophisticated natural language processing, which results in the generation of brief and contextually relevant summaries of content that has been extracted while retaining the important and specific concepts and terms that are needed for proper comprehension in academia. The users are given access to the produced text and can also get the summarized document in PDF form for studying offline. The system like structure gives priority to processing in real-time, very little interaction with the user, and that it is ready to take on the future multimodal developments like question generation and topic clustering, in a large scale way. This platform, which provides a digital companion that is effective, produces better understanding, encourages active learning, and supports the usage of modern educational methodologies, by shortening the time and mental effort to be spent on reviewing long lectures and study materials.

title: Heart Disease Prediction with Personalized Diet and Exercise Recommendation using Machine Learning
authors: Gyanendra Mishra, Raghavendra Reddy, Prabhuraj Metipatil, Sunil Manoli
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cardiovascular disease is one of the most common cause of mortality globally due to delayed diagnosis and treatment. If risk factors are identified early, it is possible to prevent major cardiac events through timely intervention. In this paper we propose a machine learning method that includes recommendations tailored to an individual's diet and/or exercise regimen as well as a prediction model to identify those at risk of developing heart disease. We present several models that have been used for classification of heart disease based upon clinical and/or lifestyle information (Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, XG-Boost). While all were successful models; the XG-Boost model was the most effective. Beyond prediction, our system also addresses prevention by providing action items. The diet recommendation module uses K-Means Clustering to recommend suitable meal options consistent with nutritional guidelines. The exercise recommendation module utilizes rule-based medical knowledge to provide safe physical activity recommendations based on the level of risk associated with each individual. Ultimately, the proposed framework provides support for long-term cardiovascular health management through accurate predictions coupled with tailored lifestyle recommendations; thus promoting an integrative approach where both individuals and healthcare professionals can make informed decisions to decrease their risk of heart disease and lead a healthier life.

title: Fail-Operational Architecture for ADAS-Integrated Lighting ECUs: Achieving ASIL-B with Triple Modular Redundancy
authors: Abdul Salam Abdul Karim
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Intelligent lighting Electronic Control Units (ECUs) have been increasingly integrated in Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) to improve visibility in the night and provide reliable perception and general road safety. Because the lighting functions become safety-relevant, especially when dynamic driving and fault-prone mode is applied, maintaining fail-operational behavior with standardized compliance with functional safety is of critical importance. This is the architecture of a fail-operational ADAS-integrated lighting ECU architecture, which sets the goal of Automotive Safety Integrity Level-B (ASIL-B) on Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR) usage. The suggested architecture uses three parallel lighting control channels, which use a majority-voting mechanism to identify and mask single point faults in real-time. A detailed time-correlated data set at a fixed sampling rate of 20 ms of raw sensor data, redundancy channel data, voter data, and diagnostic data is produced in control and injected fault conditions both with computation faults, sensor dropouts and temporary bit-flip errors. The overall accuracy of supervised fault classification over the generated dataset is 97.4 percent and the class-wise Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) is of 0.97-0.98, showing the presence of strong discriminative capabilities in operational and fault condition. The diagnostic coverage of all the analyzed fault types is more than 90, which meets the requirements of the ASIL-B and proves the strength of the offered fail-operational design. The findings confirm the usefulness of TMR and redundancy-based data monitoring and safety monitoring to next-generation ADAS lighting ECUs that have been designed under safety-critical conditions.

title: Light Weight MAMBA Based Deepfake Detection Model
authors: Sruthi Biju, R S Prasanth
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The rapid growth of deepfake generation techniques has raised concerns regarding digital misinformation, fraud and privacy breaches. The majority of deepfake video detection models currently in use rely on resource-intensive CNN-Transformer architectures, which restricts their practical implementation in real-time or edge environments. In this paper, a lightweight spatiotemporal detection framework based on Mamba-driven temporal learning and MobileNet-based feature extraction is proposed. By compressing high-level feature representations, a Vector-Quantized Autoencoder (VQ-AE) improves the ability to distinguish between authentic and fraudulent video patterns. Spatial artifacts within individual frames and motion inconsistencies between sequences are captured by two parallel VisionMamba branches. For the final prediction, a lightweight classifier processes the fused spatiotemporal representation. The suggested method is ideal for low-power deepfake detection applications since experimental evaluations show increased inference efficiency with competitive accuracy.

title: Leaf-Based Health Assessment using CNN for NPK Deficiency Detection and IoT for Soil Analysis
authors: Chester Jefferson I. Andaya, John Michael R. Reyes, Kyla Jane D. Jamito, Patrick R. Eva, Psalm Ashley B. Andal, Alice M. Lacorte, John Carlo G. Torres, Marvin L. Atanacio
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: This study presents a hybrid system for precision nutrient management in bitter gourd (Momordica charantia), integrating convolutional neural network (CNN)–based leafimage analysis with Internet of Things (IoT)–enabled soil sensing for nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), and potassium (K) deficiency detection. A dataset of 1,200 expert-validated leaf images was used to train and evaluate InceptionV3 and ResNet50 models, with ResNet50 achieving superior performance (F1-score up to 1.00) under a 70-20-10 training-validation-testing split. Parallel soil analysis employed NPK, pH, and moisture sensors calibrated against laboratory assays. Results demonstrate that CNN classification accurately identifies visual nutrient deficiencies, while IoT data provides real-time soil context. Integrating both modalities enhances diagnostic reliability, reduces misclassification, and enables data-driven fertilization recommendations. The combined approach supports precise, costeffective, and sustainable nutrient management, mitigating risks of under- or over-application. This study underscores the potential of AI–IoT hybrid systems in advancing site-specific agriculture and improving crop yield and environmental stewardship.

title: An EfficientNetV2B3-Based Deep Learning Model for Accurate Diagnosis and Classification of Liver Tumors
authors: Anbalagan S
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Diagnosis of liver tumor is critical in timely intervention and better patient outcomes. Here, a deep learning model named EfficientNetV2B3 was created to help conduct automated diagnosis and classification of liver tumors based on medical imaging data. The model was trained and fine-tuned on a labelled liver image dataset and its performance was examined based on important metrics, which included accuracy, loss, ROC curve, and confusion matrix analysis. The ROC-AUC was 0.53, which implied low discriminative capacity, and the training and validation accuracy was close to 50, which hinted at possible underfitting. The loss analysis indicated that there were slight overfitting tendencies and the confusion matrix indicated a bias in prediction towards a single class, which could be attributed to the imbalance in the data. Although the results were modest, the research demonstrates the potential of the EfficientNetV2B3 architecture on the medical image classification task and the significance of the data augmentation and hyperparameter optimization to improve the accuracy of the diagnosis in the further studies.

title: Transformer-Enhanced ShuffleLinkNet with Adaptive Feature Fusion for Cyber-Attack Detection in Big Data Environments
authors: Mantri Pavan Kalyan, Sadhu Radha Krishna
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The intensive development of cloud computing, Internet of Things (IoT), and software-defined networking has resulted in highly dynamic and large-scale network conditions, and a challenging task of cyber-attack detection is to be observed. Conventional intrusion detection systems do not scale well, feature heterogeneity and the capability to identify long-range dependencies of big data traffic. This paper offers a solution to these shortcomings by introducing Transformer-Enhanced ShuffleLinkNet with Adaptive Feature Fusion to overcome challenges in detecting cyber-attacks in big data conditions. The suggested scheme combines a transformer encoder to acquire the global contextual relationships a lightweight hybrid architecture of ShuffleNet and LinkNet to acquire efficient local discriminative features. The combination of PN-Pooling and adaptive feature fusion mechanism is used to maintain the salient feature information without using a large number of dimensions. A large number of experiments on the WUSTL-IIoT-2018 dataset prove that the proposed model performs much higher than the existing machine learning and deep learning algorithms in accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, specificity, and errors. The findings affirm that the suggested framework is scalable, precise, and sound solution to detecting cyber-attacks in contemporary big data network settings.

title: An Intelligent Home Interior Design Suggestion System Using Deep Learning
authors: Sunitha M R, Shreeya D, Druthi Jain S C, Shimsha K P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This electronic document is a "live" template and already defines the components of your paper [title, text, heads, etc.] in its style sheet. *CRITICAL: Do Not Use Symbols, Special Characters, Footnotes, or Math in Paper Title or Abstract. (Abstract) The increasing demand for personalized home interiors has exposed the limitations of traditional design recommendation approaches. This paper presents An Intelligent home interior design suggestion system that employs deep learning to generate context-aware and personalized design recommendations. The proposed system analyses room images and user preferences using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for style recognition and feature extraction, along with a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for realistic design visualization. The system performs room segmentation, furniture identification, and spatial analysis to recommend suitable colour palettes, furniture arrangements, and layout optimizations. A modular web architecture enables seamless interaction and real-time visualization. The system enhances design accuracy, accessibility, and decision-making while reducing dependence on professional interior designers.

title: Total Ionizing Dose Effects on Clock Systems and Peripheral Modules of the MSP430FR6989
authors: I. Hudson, J. Carpenter, T. Peyton, J. Kim, J. Ermi, R. Baltazar Felipe, T. D. Loveless
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Total ionizing dose (TID) testing was performed on the Texas Instruments (TI) MSP430FR6989 microcontroller to evaluate the effects of bias dependence and peripheral module degradation. The clock system, analog-to-digital converter (ADC), and Universal Asynchronous Receiver/Transmitter (UART) peripherals were tested across two low-power modes (LPMs) and more than 30 configurations. The results reveal bias- and peripheral-dependent variability in TID response, both functional and catastrophic failures, and recovery of device functionality through room-temperature annealing.

title: SEE Evaluation of a Serverless Computing Architecture Under 14-MeV Neutrons
authors: David Pacios, Mohammadreza Rezaei, José Luis Vázquez-Poletti, Sara Ignacio-Cerrato, Juan A. Clemente
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: This paper presents the effects of 14-MeV neutrons on a NVIDIA Jetson Nano running a serverless computing architecture. SDCs and DUEs were observed in its CPU and GPU when executing a serverless-based FFT function.

title: EnViD: A Predictive Vision Framework with Temporal Contrast for Efficient and Robust Video Analysis in Dynamic Visual Fields
authors: Sritej Padmanabhan
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Video-analysis applications and autonomous unmanned vehicles (drones, etc.) require fast and resource-efficient perception and control to perform in dynamic, unpredictable real-world environments. Comprehending visual reality requires the efficient acquisition of common-sense knowledge over several regularities in the visual world, e.g., object spatial relationships, temporal dynamics such as motion & rotation, and illuminations on objects in view. In the computer vision domain, a paramount challenge has resided in the performance of vision systems within dynamic environments such as glare and shadows, particularly in Single Object Tracking (SOT). This research specifically takes interest in improving telehealth applications such as disease diagnosis and monitoring. Prevailing methods, while sophisticated, are hindered by large datasets and high computational needs, with their performance being compromised under varying ambient conditions, which inevitably appear in real-world conditions. Considering these challenges, a novel event-based predictive vision framework (EnViD) was developed to better capture such real-world dynamics more efficiently. EnViD builds sophisticated and meaningful high-to-high associations, in comparison to high-to-low mappings. This generates semantically rich features, which can be associated with more abstract, low-dimensional concepts through a predictive vision framework assembled in a hierarchy. EnViD was tested on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano with a custom dataset simulating real-world visual conditions like glare, occlusion, and motion blur. EnViD demonstrated consistent improvements in tracking precision and robustness compared to classical frame-based methods. However, the predictive vision framework alone still suffers from high energy use, which is unrealistic for embedded applications. Therefore, a temporal contrast signal processing method similar to the eye's retina was utilized to reduce the computational burden of typical frame-based vision. By processing only per-pixel polarity changes, an attention-like mechanism can bring focus to the critical components of the frame. This resulted in a reduction of data usage by ∼92%. However, utilizing event-based vision in combination with a predictive hierarchy result in swift signal loss. Therefore, a novel microsaccade signal generation technique was developed to reintroduce signal from signal feedback, similar to the human eye. This technique provides a possible explanation for why microsaccades are utilized in the human visual system, validated in silico. EnViD's increased efficiency and performance in dynamic conditions make it suitable for many vision tasks. To assess its performance for telehealth video-analysis tasks, it was applied to an algorithm for monitoring a Parkinsonian hand tremor. The EnViD module was adapted as a predictive tracking backbone that localized and stabilized the hand region in each frame under dynamic visual conditions, created in a custom dataset. This region was then passed into a MediaPipe-based hand landmark detector to extract fine-grained tremor motion signals. By narrowing inference to EnViD-predicted regions, the system reduced landmark dropout and tracking jitter by approximately 5%, yielding more reliable tremor quantification in the presence of the mentioned ambient conditions. This research introduces EnViD as a promising and novel neuromorphic architecture for improved performance of vision systems (i.e. telehealth camera input, autonomous vehicles, etc.) in dynamic conditions. Future applications include expanding this tool to multi-object tracking and additional contrasts beyond temporal, for increased efficiency.

title: Study on Classroom Temperature Distribution in Hybrid Cooling System at Different Air Conditioner Setpoints
authors: Siti Zubaidah Bahrin, Nor Asiah Muhamad, Law Kah Haw
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
abstract: Air-conditioning (AC) is essential to maintain comfortable indoor environments, particularly in classrooms, resulting in higher energy consumption in educational buildings compared to neighboring countries. This study investigates the effectiveness of fan-assisted cooling as a hybrid strategy to reduce AC energy use. Experiments were conducted in two classrooms: Room 1 with a single fan and Room 2 with double fans, across AC setpoints from 26 deg C to 22 deg C. Hourly temperature and energy consumption data were collected and analyzed. Results show that fan-assisted cooling can significantly lower peak indoor temperatures and total energy use, with the magnitude of temperature reduction dependent on sensor location and airflow distribution. Notably, the double-fan configuration achieved the largest reductions, highlighting the importance of fan placement and room-specific airflow patterns. Daily energy analysis revealed that Room 1 achieved savings with AC and 1 fan at higher setpoints (26–24 deg C), while at lower setpoints (23–22 deg C), hybrid cooling sometimes consumed more energy than AC alone. In Room 2, the double-fan configuration resulted in consistently higher energy use across all setpoints, highlighting the influence of room-specific conditions and fan placement. These findings demonstrate that hybrid cooling strategies can enhance indoor comfort while reducing energy demand in educational buildings.

title: Vehicle Speed Estimation and Classification Using a Low-Cost Continuous-Wave Doppler Radar
authors: Dylan Kuming, Mohammed Yunus Abdul Gaffar, Lawrence Stanton
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This paper presents a low-cost continuous-wave (CW) Doppler radar system for vehicle speed estimation and classification. A 24GHz radar module was integrated with an AD620 amplifier and an ASUS Xonar sound card, with data processed offline in MATLAB. The processing pipeline comprised Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) spectrogram generation, Order Statistic Constant False Alarm Rate (OS-CFAR) detection, Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise (DBSCAN), and Joint Probabilistic Data Association (JPDA)-based multi-target tracking (MTT) using a constant-acceleration (CA) Kalman filter. Classification used a ramp-duration feature extracted near the zero-Doppler point and normalised by the estimated vehicle speed. Experimental validation showed mean speed errors below 1km/h against GPS ground truth and 99% classification accuracy in preliminary controlled and live traffic data, validating the system’s promising performance and affordability for traffic monitoring.

title: Efficient Partially-Connected Hybrid Beamforming for Communication Capacity Maximization in FD-DFRC Systems
authors: Mingjie Chen, Yanzhao Hou, Siyuan Li, Songning Gao, Gaoze Mu, Yongan Zheng, Zhiqing Wei, Qimei Cui, Xiaofeng Tao
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Dual-Function Radar and Communication (DFRC) system with full-duplex (FD) sensing and half-duplex (HD) communication is regarded as a promising way to improve safety and efficiency in vehicular networks, which have been widely used in vehicular networks. To achieve higher communication capacity, this paper extends the beamforming design of full-duplex sensing and communication DFRC systems to general scenarios within vehicular networks. Compared to the fully-connected hybrid beamforming architecture, the partially-connected hybrid beamforming architecture holds great potential of reducing hardware costs, thereby facilitating practical implementation in real-world wireless systems. However, the unique block-diagonal structure of its analog beamforming matrix introduces additional design challenges. This paper investigates an efficient partially-connected hybrid beamforming algorithm for FD-DFRC multi-input multi-output (MIMO) systems. Specifically, we formulate a multi-objective optimization problem to jointly optimize the dual-functional transmit beamformers for information receiving vehicles (IRVs) and the base station (BS), while also optimizing the received beamformers for information transmission vehicles (ITVs) at the full-duplex vehicle terminal (FD-VT). In particular, we decompose the original problem of maximizing the sum rate into several subproblems. We design an efficient algorithm based on lower bound maximization, Riemannian manifold optimization methods, and the alternating direction multiplier method (ADMM) algorithm. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed scheme effectively optimizes the sum rate while maintaining radar performance thresholds, requiring fewer radio frequency chains, and adhering to energy offloading constraints.

title: ICAD: Rethinking the Role of Inference and Cues for the Anomaly Detection of Time Series in IIoT
authors: Zhichao Wu, Li Zhu, Zitao Yin, Shunqi Zhang, Xirong Xu, Xiaopeng Wei, Xin Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Time series anomaly detection in real-world Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) systems is pivotal for identifying unsafe conditions and implementing timely preventive measures. While diffusion models are popular for capturing complex patterns, they often struggle to balance diversity and fidelity across scenarios due to limited exploration of context-window logical inference relationships and trend-pattern cues. To address these challenges, we propose ICAD, a novel method that rethinks the role of inference and cues in IIoT time series anomaly detection. ICAD defines trend patterns and uncertainties in textual form and utilizes a fine-tuned large language model to encode these descriptions as conditions for the diffusion model, thereby enhancing its generalization across diverse data distributions. Additionally, a “reasoning network for contextual window” mechanism is designed to capture temporal dependencies between adjacent windows, complemented by multi-scale and spatial feature adaptive fusion modules to further enhance the predictive performance. Empirical evaluations across four benchmark datasets and a large-scale ethylene oxide production process demonstrate that ICAD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, confirming its effectiveness and practicality in overcoming current anomaly detection model limitations.

title: Do Not Forget the Role of Address Mapping Policy in Achieving DRAM-Less SSD Performance
authors: Yang Zhou, Fang Wang, Zhan Shi, Dan Feng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: For a long time, fully exploiting the multi-level parallelism of SSDs has been an important technique for optimizing SSD performance. By distributing data to different flash parallel units through address mapping policies, SSDs can achieve multi-task parallel processing, thereby significantly improving data read and write performance. However, our experiments show that when SSD blocking causes task queues to stack up, excessive pursuit of flash parallelism can cause performance interference between multiple users, thereby reducing SSD performance. Additionally, in DRAM-less SSDs, differences in data access patterns directly impact the address mapping module, thereby exacerbating the degree of performance degradation. Based on the above analysis, this paper conducts a systematic study of the impact mechanism of different data flows on address mapping policies and proposes a hybrid address mapping allocation policy with dynamic switching (HMpp) for DRAM-less SSDs. This policy identifies and classifies different data access patterns to dynamically select the appropriate address mapping policy, thereby reducing multi-user interference and improving SSD performance. Experimental results based on real cloud block storage workloads show that this policy can achieve 87.2% performance improvement and 77.3% fairness optimization, while reducing the write amplification factor by 32.9% to 40.2%.

title: Variable Step-Size General Bias-Compensated M-estimate Adaptive Filtering for Robust DOA Estimation
authors: Fuyi Huang, Haiping Li, Haiquan Zhao, Haiqiang Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: Adaptive filters have been successfully applied in uniform linear array system to estimate the direction-of-arrival. However, traditional adaptive filtering algorithms suffer from degraded estimation accuracy against impulsive noise interferences or require a trade-off between the convergence speed and estimation accuracy. This article proposes a variable step-size general bias-compensated M-estimate (VSS-GBCM) adaptive filtering algorithm to address the above issues. This article first derives a bias-compensated M-estimate (BCM) adaptive filtering algorithm from a continuous bias-compensated M-estimate cost function, and then analyze its transient and steady-state mean square performance under common statistical assumptions. Sub-sequently, a variable step-size (VSS) mechanism is obtained by minimizing the mean square deviation at every snapshot, result ing in a VSS-BCM adaptive filtering algorithm. Moreover, the VSS-GBCM is developed by solving a general bias-compensated M-estimate cost function and using the above VSS strategy, where the VSS-BCM is its special case. Finally, simulation results validate that our solutions are superior to competing methods.

title: Virtually Supervised Depth-Aware Registration for Monocular Endoscopic Navigation
authors: Hao Fang, Guangcheng Luo, Ming Wu, Wenkang Fan, Xiangxing Chen, Xiongbiao Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Robotic-assisted endoscopy commonly uses various endoscopes for early detection and treatment of tumors or cancers. Tracking the endoscope 3D location from monocular endoscopic video sequences is the key to develop surgical navigation. This work proposes a new virtually supervised depth-aware registration method to track the monocular endoscope 3D location in the preoperative image space. Specifically, a virtually supervised monocular endoscopic structural depth estimation model is proposed and trained on virtual or synthesis endoscopic data, without using any manually annotated real endoscopic video images. This model can accomplish zero-shot generalization to precisely estimate dense depth maps of real endoscopic images with artifacts and illumination variations. Moreover, a new structure-invariant and depth-aware similarity function and spatial constraint are introduced for 2D-3D registration. We validated our method on clinical data from different medical centers, with the experimental results showing that the average tracked position and direction errors were reduced to (3.10±2.99mm, 7.22±7.13°), which significantly outperforms current vision-based surgical navigation methods.

title: FPGA Realization of an Operand-Aware Dual-Mode Hybrid Arithmetic Unit for 64-Bit Embedded Systems
authors: K Uma Maheswari, Kamatham Susmitha, Chinthakayala Manjeera, B Eramma, Peddinti Deven Kumar, Palla Chandana
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This research analyzes the realization of an operand-aware dual-mode hybrid arithmetic component on the FPGA technology with applications in 64-bit embedded processors. The arithmetic component is designed to support its operation, depending on the values of the operands and the corresponding opcode options, to accomplish arithmetic, logical, and multiplication operations. This is achieved with the support of the operand analysis module and the control logic system, enabling the selective operation of the calculation paths and thereby preventing any wastage because of switching processes and enhancing overall resource usage. The system also integrates the selective tile hybrid multiplier logic that can support high-precision 64-bit multipliers with an output that goes up to 128 bits, while maintaining a normalized footprint and power consumption level. The arithmetic component is modeled with the use of the Verilog HDL and implemented with the Xilinx Vivado 2023.2 platform and validated with simulation and reported to utilize minimal resources and control the power within the integrated chip.

title: Comparison of Multi-Modal Sentiment and Behavior Analysis of Chat Applications Using Text and Emoji-Based Features
authors: M Vighnesh Mallya, Roshan D Suvaris
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Because chat apps are so popular, there are a lot of brief, casual, and emoji-rich conversations that provide important insights about user behavior and emotion. However, typical text-only sentiment models are less successful due to multilingual material, code-mixing, spelling mistakes, and overuse of emojis. This work presents a multi-modal strategy that integrates text-, emoji-, and image-based feature extraction, supervised machine learning classifiers, and robust preprocessing for sentiment and behavioral analysis of chat data. Time-based usage profiling, message-level sentiment prediction, and improper message detection are all supported by the system. Combining emoji and (optional) image characteristics with textual representations enhances sentiment accuracy over text-only baselines and allows for more thorough behavioral insights for safety, analytics, and monitoring scenarios, according to experimental results on chat-like datasets [1]–[3].

title: AI-Enabled Voice Replication for Scalable and Personalized Education
authors: Nandini S, Manjunath K M, Darshan S Bhargav, Chethan A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid growth of digital learning environments has increased the need for accessible, personalized, and inclusive educational solutions that cater to learners across diverse linguistic and geographical contexts. Conventional educational content delivery relies largely on manual voice recordings and text-centric materials, which limit scalability, reduce learner engagement, and pose significant challenges for visually impaired students and multilingual classrooms. This paper introduces EduClone, an AI-powered voice intelligence platform that integrates Text-to-Speech (TTS), Speech-to-Text (STT), real-time translation, voice cloning, and voice isolation within a unified architecture tailored for educational applications. The proposed system employs transformer-based speech encoders, multilingual translation models, and deep neural speech synthesis techniques to automatically generate high-quality voice content in 11 Indian languages and over 50 additional global languages, while supporting low-latency streaming.Experimental evaluations demonstrate that EduClone achieves a 32% reduction in content creation time, a 28% improvement in comprehension levels among multilingual learners, and a 40% decrease in operational production costs compared to traditional recording-based workflows. The system’s scalability, robustness, and real-time performance were validated through user evaluation studies and classroom-level deployments. Overall, the findings provide empirical evidence that multimodal speech technologies significantly enhance accessibility, learner engagement, and academic outcomes in modern educational settings, while positioning EduClone as a practical and scalable framework for AI-driven voice-based learning.

title: Mitigating Context Loss in Legal RAG: A Metadata-Aware Chunking Strategy for Hierarchical Indian Statutes
authors: Venkatesh S Bati, Abhigna C B, Samarth H Sindhya, Kalpa R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems hold much promise for legal question answering; however, they often fail on statutory corpora as naive document segmentation removes crucial context. Meaning in hierarchically structured texts like the Indian Income Tax Act is encoded across acts, chapters, sections, provisos, and explanations, whereas token- or window-based chunking conventionally used ignores this structure and detaches qualifiers from their governing sections, exacerbating the "Lost in the Middle" problem. This paper proposes Smart Indexing, a deterministic, metadata-aware chunking and context-injection strategy that preserves statutory structure by embedding hierarchical metadata, namely Act, Chapter, Section ID, and section title, directly into each fine-grained chunk. Each chunk is thereby rendered semantically self-contained, allowing vector embeddings to carry legal identity besides semantic content. A novel parent-child retrieval architecture first selects small metadata-rich clauses for precision and then expands them to their parent sections in order to reconstruct full statutory context for generation.
