The approach has been implemented and evaluated within LegalLens, a privacy-preserving legal RAG framework powered by a local, quantized Llama 3.1 model. Experimental results demonstrate that metadata-driven context injection improves retrieval precision and recall for proviso and condition-sensitive statutory queries while reducing hallucinations without increasing context window size or model complexity, placing hierarchy-aware metadata injection as a practical, scalable basis for robust legal RAG systems.

title: Structure-Aware Mask and Detail-Augmented Transformer Network for Remote Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Zhaorui Wang, Bin Huang, Tong Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Transformer networks exhibit strong capabilities in modeling global spatial features and have achieved remarkable success in hyperspectral image (HSI) classification. However, Transformer-based methods also face several challenges, including a substantial increase in model parameters, strong dependence on abundant labeled samples, and difficulty effectively fusing spatial and spectral information. To address these issues, we propose a Structure-Aware Mask and Detail-Augmented Transformer (SAM-DAT) network. Specifically, a Spatial-Spectral Hybrid Perceptual Masking (SSHPM) strategy is proposed to highlight boundary regions based on pixel-level similarity analysis, thereby mitigating the loss of fine-grained information and noise introduction caused by conventional masking methods. A Local Spatial-Spectral Feature Extraction (LSSFE) module is designed by integrating two-dimension (2-D) and three-dimension (3-D) convolutions to jointly model spatial and spectral features at multiple scales. A Global Fine-Grained Augmentation (GFGA) Transformer is introduced to fuse local details and global dependencies through comprehensive feature encoding and a novel attention mechanism. Finally, a Decoupled Output Head (DOH) module is designed to simultaneously perform image reconstruction and classification, promoting consistency between discriminative and reconstructive feature learning. Extensive experiments on four benchmark HSI classification datasets demonstrate that the proposed SAM-DAT significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods. The code is available at https://github.com/wzr678/SAM-DAT.

title: FlashSAM: Lightweight Vision Model for Multi-UAV Token Communication in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks
authors: Feibo Jiang, Siwei Tu, Li Dong, Kezhi Wang, Kun Yang, Ruiqi Liu, Cunhua Pan, Jiangzhou Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: Token Communication (TokenCom) is a promising paradigm for low-altitude wireless networks, as it focuses on transmitting task-relevant core information, particularly in environments with uncertainty, noise, and stringent bandwidth constraints. However, existing TokenCom systems still face several challenges, including inefficient knowledge base construction, ineffective token encoding, and limited support for multi-user token sharing. To address these issues, we propose a Lightweight Vision Model-based Multi-Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) To ken Communication (LVM-MTC) system. First, we develop a lightweight Segment Anything Model (SAM), termed FlashSAM, which incorporates a set of lightweight convolutional modules to significantly reduce the number of model parameters. Building on FlashSAM, we construct a Lightweight Knowledge Base (LKB) to enable efficient object-level perception. Next, we design an Efficient Token Codec (ETC) based on the Masked Autoencoder (MAE) architecture. ETC improves compression efficiency at both the pixel and token levels, and provides lightweight token decoding tailored for resource-constrained UAVs. Furthermore, we propose a Multi-UAV Token Sharing (MTS) scheme for multi UAV TokenCom. By measuring token similarity across UAVs, MTS consolidates similar tokens and transmits them through broadcast transmission, thereby further improving transmission efficiency. Finally, simulation results validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed LVM-MTC system.

title: Koopman Operator based Integral Reinforcement Learning for H ∞ Tracking Control of Nonlinear Systems
authors: Muthu Logesh Shanmugam, Prakash Mani
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a novel data-driven control framework that integrates integral reinforcement learning (IRL) with H∞ robust control theory for tracking control of nonlinear dynamical systems. The proposed approach implements Koopman operator theory to transform the nonlinear system dynamics into a linear representation in a higher-dimensional lifted space. The IRL algorithm learns an optimal policy through the integral Bellman equation while maintaining H∞ performance guarantees for disturbance rejection. The control policy update of the IRL Bellamn equation associated withH∞ cost function is updated using the actor-critic RLS architecture. The numerical simulations are performed for three dynamical systems such as the duffing oscillator, the single-link robot manipulator model and quadrotor UAV model using the proposed control approach, and the results are compared with the two control schemes such as PID and MPC. The proposed control approach is implemented for the duffing oscillator in a physics-based MuJoCo environment, where the efficacy and efficiency of the proposed control approach are shown and proved.

title: Personal Healthcare Assistant Using AI
authors: Shruthi G, Anand Tilagul, Deepak B L, Chandana M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Natural Language Processing (NLP) has enabled the development of intelligent healthcare systems that assist individuals in managing their health more effectively. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Personal Healthcare Assistant using AI, an interactive system capable of analyzing user symptoms, providing preliminary health advice, and recommending lifestyle improvements. The assistant employs NLP techniques to understand user queries, a symptom-checking algorithm to predict possible conditions, and a machine learning model for personalized health recommendations. By integrating with wearable devices and cloud-based medical databases, the system ensures continuous health monitoring and timely alerts. The proposed model aims to bridge the gap between patients and healthcare providers, offering accessible, affordable, and real-time health support. Experimental results demonstrate that the assistant can provide accurate and context-aware responses, improving user engagement and promoting proactive healthcare management.

title: End-to-End Warehouse Automation: A System-Level Review of Robotics, Order Fulfillment, and Intelligent Software System
authors: Sai Preetham R V, Vishwanath Koti, Impana R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid growth of e-commerce and on-demand fulfillment has intensified the need for efficient, scalable, and intelligent warehouse operations. In response, warehouse automation has evolved from isolated mechanized solutions to complex cyber-physical systems integrating robotics, software intelligence, and real-time control. While significant research has been conducted on individual components such as automated storage and retrieval systems, autonomous mobile robots, order picking technologies, and optimization algorithms, existing studies largely remain fragmented and lack an end-to-end perspective. This review presents a comprehensive analysis of end-to-end warehouse automation systems, covering physical automation technologies, order fulfillment mechanisms, software architectures, and intelligent planning and control frameworks. Thirty peer-reviewed research works are systematically analyzed and classified based on their role within the warehouse automation pipeline, spanning task allocation, scheduling, path planning, system integration, and performance evaluation. The review highlights key trends, comparative insights, and limitations in current approaches, with particular emphasis on software coordination and system-level integration. Finally, open research challenges and future directions are identified, including unified planning frameworks, scalable decentralized control, and the need for standardized evaluation benchmarks, providing guidance for future research and industrial deployment

title: Emotion Detection from Text Using TF-IDF and Logistic Regression: A Hybrid NLP Framework with Real-Time Deployment
authors: Raghavendra Patil G E, Baba Fakruddin Ali B H, Mehar Afroz, Jeevitha S R, Swathi N, Vijaykumar G R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Sentiment analysis is critical to text mining due to rapid text data growth. Sentiment analysis is utilized to track social media, rate customer opinions, diagnose mental diseases, and understand how humans talk to computers. Most emotion-detecting methods today center around non-text inputs, but come on, much of what we learn about humans online is text-based. Therefore, the goal of this project is to develop a system with AI that detects emotions from text. The proposed system uses natural language processing (NLP) techniques and machine learning models to classify emotions such as happiness, sadness, anger, fear, and surprise, among others, from textual input.. It incorporates rule-based methods, sentiment, and context-sensitive tools aiding emotion detection. It employs some incredible advanced natural language processing technology such as sentence transformers, and BERT-like models to accurately pin down neutral sentiments Evaluation metrics, including accuracy, F1 score, and recall, demonstrate the effectiveness of the model, highlighting its potential for real-world deployment. The findings suggest that AI-based emotion analysis from text holds promise for improving human-computer interaction and enabling more emotionally responsive applications They test it on public sentiment analysis corpora, and the experiments prove that our model is better than current approaches in terms of accuracy, precision, and recall. This just shows how great AI-based emotion analysis is inimproving the way we communicate with computers and creating apps that can understand emotions.

title: A Geo-enabled Employment Platform for Connecting Rural Workforce to Rural Employers
authors: B Janardhana Rao, K Revathi, Koppu Rajeshwari, Tadoor Shradda, B Rakesh, Sreeja Reddy Nandhikonda
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: There are a lot of skilled and unskilled individuals in rural areas that could work in carpentry, farming, construction, and other manual jobs, but they aren't being exploited since there aren't adequate ways to talk to each other and network. Even when workers want to work, the lack of a central framework often means that chances are missed and work isn't done as well as it may be. The purpose of this article is to make it easy for rural workers to find jobs and for companies to locate workers by creating a digital platform. Employers can post job opportunities on the site and discover local individuals who have the right skills, live nearby, and are available. People who work in remote places, on the other hand, can build modest internet profiles to show off their skills, availability, and past work experience. The platform will be available to everyone and easy to use by introducing features like support for several languages, SMS and voice registration for people who can't read or write well, and real-time job matching. By making it easier for employers and workers to relate to each other plainly and directly, this method intends to make the labour market more efficient, reduce underemployment, and boost economic empowerment in rural areas.

title: A Toxicological Assessment of Solar Panels Via a Soil Microbiome Analysis Across the Northeast US
authors: Gary Leschinsky, Sophia Jarrar, Jeffrey Field
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The rapid increases in solar energy generation from solar panel installations have garnered concern for the impact of installations on soil, particularly due to shading. 16 S rRNA sequencing was used to analyze the bacterial microbiome in the soil under solar panel installations (test samples) compared to adjacent non-shaded soil samples (control samples) at 3 sites in the Northeast United States. The abundance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 8 6}$</tex> genera was significantly different between test and control samples and was nearly always significantly lower in tests than in controls (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P}<0.05$</tex>). Two genera with high richness that were significantly different in abundance between test and control samples were Brevitalea <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{P}=0.03)$</tex>, and Arenimicrobium <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{P}=0.03)$</tex>, both genera of the phylum Acidobacteriota. Alpha diversity indices emphasizing richness (Taxa, Chao, and Margalef) were greater for controls than tests, but evenness indices (Shannon, Pielou's J, and Simpson) were greater for tests than controls, although there were no significant differences between test and control values for any of the indices. Beta diversity was significantly different between test and control soil <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{P}=0.02)$</tex>. These findings highlight that the bacterial composition of soil is altered by solar panels, indicating a need for further study of the effects of solar panels on soil and solar farm ecosystems.

title: Data-Driven Predictive Maintenance Using Fuzzy Clustering Approaches for Oil Temperature Forecasting in 8E Electrical Locomotives
authors: Rofhiwa C. Mufamadi, Stephen O. Oladipo, Udochukwu B. Akuru
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: The South African rail sector is vital to the national economy but often faces reliability challenges due to recurring malfunctions in electric locomotives such as the Class 8E. This study presents a predictive maintenance framework for forecasting oil temperature using an adaptive neuro fuzzy inference system (ANFIS) supported by fuzzy clustering methods. Real time operational data, including line current, voltage, ambient temperature, and oil temperature, were collected from 8E locomotives at Impala Platinum Mine between August and October 2024. Models were built using subtractive clustering (SC), grid partitioning (GP), and fuzzy c-means (FCM) techniques. The model using SC with a radius of 0.3 achieved the best performance with RMSE of 0.1637, MAPE of 0.3545, MAE of 0.1168, and NSE of 0.9937. The proposed model offers a reliable and cost-effective solution for early fault detection, enabling timely maintenance and improved operational uptime in the electric rail sector.

title: A Comprehensive Review on Applications for Emotion AI and Affective Computing Across Various Fields
authors: Nidhishree M S, Amutha S, Rameshbabu D R, Swathi V, Archana S Patil
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Affective Computing is all about helping computers understand how we feel so that interacting with them feels more natural. Emotion AI picks up on things like facial expressions, how we talk, our behavior, and even physiological signals, then figures out our emotional state from there. With machine learning and deep learning making big leaps lately, emotion recognition systems have gotten a whole lot smarter. Researchers now use tools like Deep Neural Networks, Recurrent Neural Networks, and Support Vector Machines to dig into emotional data. Still, there are tough problems left. Integrating information from different sources isn’t easy, datasets are often imbalanced, and actually getting these systems to work out in the real world can be tricky. This paper pulls together a bunch of emotion recognition methods, sensing technologies, and datasets people rely on. The study also looks at what works, what doesn’t, and how Emotion AI is actually being used — whether that’s in healthcare, education, or autonomous systems.

title: IoT enabled Smart Classroom Management System using Raspberry Pi
authors: Sneha Murudi, Soujanya U, Manoj Kumar M, Sushma M, Shraddha C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traditional classroom management practices such as manual attendance recording and manual control of classroom resources are time-consuming and inefficient. This paper presents a software-based Smart Classroom Management System designed to automate attendance tracking and improve classroom efficiency. The proposed system uses a standard webcam to capture live video, from which student faces are detected using computer vision techniques implemented with the OpenCV library and Haar Cascade classifier. Attendance records are automatically generated and stored using a web-based application developed with Flask. The system was evaluated under controlled classroom conditions involving 30 students and achieved an attendance accuracy of approximately 98%. Although the system is lightweight and cost-effective, its performance may be affected by lighting variations and facial occlusions such as masks. The results demonstrate that the proposed software solution can effectively reduce administrative workload and support an enhanced learning environment.

title: An Intelligent ML-Based Decision Support System for Waste Trade and Recycling
authors: Priyadarshini S, Bhavana H N, Hema Chandra D M, K B Siddesh, Samruddhi R, Manjushree S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid increase in urban waste generation has exposed significant limitations in traditional waste management systems, particularly in waste segregation, recycling awareness, and structured trading of recyclable materials. Existing systems largely rely on manual classification and informal recycling practices, resulting in resource loss and environmental degradation. This paper proposes GreenMart, an intelligent web-based waste trade and recycling portal that integrates machine learning with a digital marketplace. The system employs transfer learning-based image classification to automatically categorize waste materials and provide real-time recycling guidance. Additionally, a structured marketplace connects waste generators with recycling vendors, promoting sustainable resource utilization. Experimental evaluation demonstrates a classification accuracy of 92.5%, validating the effectiveness of the proposed approach. The system contributes toward smart waste management and circular economy initiatives.

title: VisionLearn: An Intelligent Educational Platform for Touchless Interaction
authors: Nithya S, Pramila Diwate, Rakshita Patil, Sreeja A K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rapid growth of video-based e-learning platforms has introduced challenges related to cognitive overload, limited interactivity, and inefficient navigation of long instructional content. This paper presents VisionLearn, a multimodal artificial intelligence-driven educational framework that integrates hybrid deep learning-based video summarization with real-time touchless gesture interaction. In order to capture spatial, temporal, and long-range semantic dependencies for context-aware video compression, the suggested system uses a CNN–BiLSTM–Transformer architecture. Furthermore, WaveGestureNet, a Temporal Graph Neural Network (TGNN) model, uses structured hand landmark modeling to recognize gestures accurately and with minimal latency. A scalable Django-based backend that supports content management and adaptive analytics is used to deploy the platform. An F1-score of 82.4% and a ROUGE-L score of 0.72 are obtained through experimental evaluation on 50 lecture videos, outperforming baseline LSTM and Transformer-only models. Real-time interaction is made possible by gesture recognition's 94.1% accuracy and 38 ms average latency. According to a controlled user study (n = 30), retention scores improved by 61% and content consumption time decreased by 42% (p < 0.05).

title: Mediating Digital Security: Participatory Learning with Older Adults in Smartphone Café Sessions
authors: Parvin Ghadamighalandari, Roodabeh Seif, Margarita Grinko, Konstantin Aal, Mahya Mirzaeekharghani, Bilal Mohamad, Claudia Mueller, Volker Wulf

title: High Voltage Power Lines Conductor Anomaly Detection using Magnetic Field
authors: Francesco Messina, Emad Samuel Malki Ebeid, Marcello Chiaberge
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This paper introduces a contactless, fully passive approach for locating faults in transmission line conductors. The method relies on the magnetic fields generated by the line current and captures them using low-cost, three-axis magnetic sensor integrated into a custom-designed drone payload. Unlike traditional inspection techniques, the proposed system performs rapid, multi-position magnetic scanning around the conductors within a short time window. By analyzing the spatial variations and temporal signatures of the measured magnetic field, the method can detect and characterize both external and internal anomalies, including potential structural degradations or irregular current distributions. Identified anomalies can be georeferenced accurately using the RTK-enabled GPS of the drone, allowing operators to determine their precise position along the transmission line. This capability supports targeted, close-range inspections and greatly enhances both the efficiency and safety of transmission line maintenance activities. This paper presents the experimental validation of the proposed method using a laboratory prototype. The prototype was tested on a 30mm diameter All-Aluminum Alloy Conductor energized through a current transformer operating at 50 Hz and 350 A. These tests were designed to evaluate the system’s ability to capture magnetic field signatures under realistic operating conditions, assess sensor performance, and verify the method’s capability to detect and localize conductor anomalies in a repeatable, safe and controlled environment.

title: Performance Analysis of Multiple User Average BLER in Downlink NOMA Short-Packet Communications over $\alpha - \kappa - \mu$ Shadowed Fading
authors: Phu Tran Tin, Minh-Sang Van Nguyen, Tran Trung Duy, Symeon Chatzinotas, Byung-Seo Kim, Miroslav Voznak
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The spectral efficiency of sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks is anticipated to experience large improvements through the implementation of non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) technology. The integration of short-packet communications (SPC) into NOMA networks enables low-latency operation and high spectral efficiency. The present study investigates the performance of multiple users in a NOMA downlink SPC system operating over an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha - \kappa - \mu$</tex-math></inline-formula> shadowed fading channel. Precise and asymptotic closed-form approximations for the average block error rate (BLER), reliability, throughput, goodput, and overall BLER were derived using approximate Gaussian-Chebyshev quadrature. The analytical results were validated through numerical simulations, providing insights into the impact of fading parameters on system performance. The study's findings indicate that the proposed downlink NOMA SPC system is highly suitable for ultra-reliable and low-latency communications (URLLC), achieving reliability levels of 99.99% for multiple users. The study also determined the optimal transmission bit rate required to maximize throughput and goodput while minimizing the BLER. The proposed downlink NOMA SPC system operating with an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\alpha - \kappa - \mu$</tex-math></inline-formula> shadowed fading channel demonstrates high potential for improving Internet of Things (IoT) network performance over conventional downlink orthogonal multiple access (OMA) approaches. Most of the analysis adopts perfect successive interference cancellation (pSIC) and perfect channel state information (pCSI) as theoretical benchmarks, while additional results explicitly quantify the performance degradation caused by residual interference and channel estimation errors (CEE). The results reveal that imperfect SIC (ipSIC) dominates the BLER floor at high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), whereas imperfect CSI (ipCSI) primarily affects the moderate-SNR regime, highlighting distinct impairment-driven performance bottlenecks. Therefore, we have extended the study by including two additional scenarios: pCSI combined with ipSIC, and ipCSI combined with ipSIC. This extension allows us to compare the performance of these systems and clearly shows that the system with pSIC and pCSI achieves the best performance. Finally, the results were validated with Monte Carlo simulations.

title: Foundations of Efficient Model Reconstruction Using Counterfactual Explanations_supp1-3695917.pdf
authors: Pasan Dissanayake
publish: N/A
abstract: With the ever-growing computational demands of AI, extracting richer information from fewer data points is of utmost importance. This work proposes data-efficient model reconstruction and compression by leveraging an explainability technique called counterfactual explanations. Counterfactual explanations are traditionally used to explain black-box machine learning models by providing ways of achieving a favorable model outcome with minimum input perturbation (essentially, the closest point on the other side of the decision boundary). Interestingly, such counterfactuals can also be used to reconstruct a model by training a surrogate model on them to give similar predictions as the original model. In this work, we analyze how counterfactual explanations can improve model reconstruction by specifically utilizing the fact that the counterfactuals lie quite close to the decision boundary. Our main contribution is to derive novel theoretical relationships between the error in model reconstruction and the number of counterfactual queries required by leveraging polytope theory. We also derive additional bounds considering multiple model families such as ReLU networks and Lipschitz-continuous models. Our results establish that counterfactual explanations can act as information-rich probes for model reconstruction, which has significant implications for energy-efficiency, sustainability, and also privacy.

title: Estimating the Temperature Distribution of Pouch-Type Li-Ion Batteries Based on a Distributed-Parameter Electrothermal Coupling Model
authors: Jiale Xie, Mingyu Song, Zongyang Song, Zhongbao Wei, Zhekang Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Pouch-type lithium-ion batteries (PLiBs) often suffer from nonuniform temperature distributions, which accelerate degradation and increase thermal safety risks. To address this, a 2-D electrothermal coupled model is developed by discretizing the cell into grid elements, each represented by a second-order equivalent circuit. Electrothermal interactions are captured through electrical and thermal resistances. Model parameters are identified using a hybrid method that combines parameter mapping with particle swarm optimization, and are validated under dynamic stress test and multirate discharge conditions. Experimental results demonstrate high predictive accuracy, with mean voltage errors below 7.5 mV and temperature deviations within 1 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^\boldsymbol{\circ}$</tex-math></inline-formula>C. The model also successfully reproduces localized hot spots near the current tabs during high-rate discharge. Analysis reveals two coupled causes of thermal nonuniformity: uneven current distribution due to ohmic drops in the current collectors, and heterogeneous heat dissipation across the cell surface. The superposition of these effects exacerbates temperature nonuniformity. This modeling framework offers a robust tool for the design and optimization of battery thermal management systems, providing practical insights into improving safety and extending the service life of large-format PLiBs.

title: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion

title: Impedance-Spectrum Driven Frequency Weighted Functional Fault Location in Undersea Observation Network Power Systems
authors: Erjia Zhang, Mouduo Yu, Wentao Huang, Nengling Tai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Fault location in the power supply systems of undersea observation networks is constrained by limited observability and the frequency-dependent characteristics of the electrode–seawater–cable loop. This paper proposes a frequency-domain fault location method based on impedance spectrum analysis, in which the identification of fault parameters is formulated as a minimization problem of a frequency-weighted residual functional. A physical model of the electrode–cable–fault path is constructed to compute theoretical input impedance spectra under different fault distances and fault impedances. These spectra are then compared to the measured impedance spectrum obtained at the shore station under swept-frequency excitation. The residual functional is constructed by separating the real and imaginary components of the spectral difference, reflecting the deviation of complex input impedance at each frequency point. Considering the influence of impedance sensitivity across frequencies, independent weighting functions are introduced for the real and imaginary components to enhance contributions from informative bands and suppress unreliable regions. This yields a frequency-weighted residual functional with physical significance, characterizing the correspondence between fault parameters and spectral discrepancy. The functional is minimized via a gradient-based optimization strategy, enabling simultaneous determination of fault distance and fault impedance based solely on shore-side measurements. Simulation studies across various fault conditions validate the method's performance in terms of location accuracy, robustness under high impedance scenarios, and numerical stability over long transmission distances. The proposed framework fuses spectral sensitivity with physical transmission modeling to infer spatial fault characteristics solely from frequency-domain observations, without relying on internal measurements or reflectometry.

title: Urban Environmental Challenges and Health: Environmental Determinants of Respiratory Illnesses in Samarkand
authors: Bahodirhon Safarov, Ana Cornelia Pereş, Nargiza Mansurova, Monica Costea, Bekzot Janzakov, Nandor Koteles, Habibullo Hasanov, Alexandru Bogdan Ilies
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Rapid urban development and changing environmental conditions increasingly challenge public health in historic cities such as Samarkand, Uzbekistan. In recent years, respiratory illnesses have risen in the region, raising concerns about the role of environmental factors, particularly air quality. This study examines the influence of atmospheric dust and ambient temperature on asthma and other respiratory illnesses in Samarkand using monthly data from 2022–2023. The analysis applies regression-based time series technique, namely distributed lag model to capture both immediate and delayed environmental effects. The results show that short-term fluctuations in dust and temperature do not significantly affect asthma incidence. However, the distributed lag analysis suggests that atmospheric dust contributes to broader respiratory morbidity when delayed effects are considered, indicating that pollution-related health impacts may develop gradually over time. Temperature variations did not show a meaningful association with respiratory diseases. These findings emphasize the importance of strengthening air-quality monitoring, pollution mitigation strategies, and environmental health management in Samarkand.

title: Multi-Objective UAV–HAP Collaborative Offloading for Hotspot Region Computing in Urban Non-Terrestrial Networks
authors: Hai Huang, Zhiyong Wu, Cong Liu, Yulong Han, Runrulin He, Yuancan Li
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: With the proliferation of urban vehicles, traditional static edge computing faces significant challenges in ensuring service quality under high mobility. To address this issue, this paper investigates Non-Terrestrial Networks (NTNs) enabled by high-altitude platforms (HAPs) and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), and proposes a Hotspot-Guided Collaborative Matching Offloading (HGCMO) framework for task hotspot regions (THRs). The objective is to jointly optimize energy consumption and computational latency. Specifically, we develop a stable matching-based UAV-Hotspot algorithm (UHRMA) and a density-delay-driven positioning scheme (DDUPO) to optimize resource allocation and deployment. Furthermore, a Dynamic Weighted Load-Aware Offloading Algorithm (DWLAO) is introduced to balance task delay and energy consumption under resource constraints. DWLAO dynamically balances latency and energy according to system load. The framework is evaluated under varying traffic intensity, user density, and UAV computing resources. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed HGCMO-based scheme achieves significant performance gains compared with baseline methods, validating its effectiveness in hotspot-driven task offloading scenarios.

title: Research on Data Integrity Verification and Tracing in the Power Internet of Things
authors: Shanyi Xie, Kai Zhang, Siyao Xu, Yuze Chen, Ying Li, Zhiwei Xu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Aiming at the security threats of eavesdropping and tampering that may arise during terminal data collection in the Power Internet of Things (PIoT), this paper proposes a data integrity verification and tracing scheme suitable for data collection networks by combining asymmetric encryption and digital signature technologies. Considering the tree topology of data collection networks, the scheme integrates network topology with hash calculation and adopts a multi-level hash approach, enabling the data server to verify the integrity of all terminal data under a gateway in a single operation. Furthermore, if the integrity verification fails, the abnormal nodes in the data transmission process can be accurately located through a step-by-step hash comparison method, thus achieving effective anomaly tracing of data.

title: A Concise Review of Resilience Assessment for Power Systems Against Typhoons
authors: Wenzhe Zheng, Zhenguo Wang, Wenqi Fan, Fei Yi, Fu Gao, Hui Hou
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The increasing frequency and intensity of typhoon disasters pose significant threats to the security and reliability of modern power systems. This paper provides a comprehensive review of the resilience assessment framework by synthesizing current methodologies in component vulnerability modeling, multi-dimensional resilience performance metrics, and uncertainty handling mechanisms. The review systematically examines the integration of typhoon wind field simulations, fragility curve development, and fault correlation analysis to quantify system degradation under extreme wind loads. Furthermore, it categorizes multi-dimensional metrics, specifically the Failure Loss, Extent, and Persistence indicator system and comprehensive resilience indices, which evaluate the evolution of risk throughout the disaster lifecycle. By characterizing the transition from short-term structural damage prediction to mid-to-long-term risk evolution, this review provides a theoretical foundation for identifying critical grid vulnerabilities and enhancing resilience in typhoon-prone regions.

title: Short-Term Load Forecasting Based on Graph Convolutional and Kolmogorov-Arnold Network
authors: Zeming Li, Peiying Li
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Short-term electric load is influenced by factors such as meteorological conditions and consumption behavior, exhibiting nonlinearity and multi-scale temporal dependencies. We propose GCKAN, a short-term load forecasting model integrating a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN), a Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), and Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KAN). First, historical load segments are encoded as causally constrained, multi-granularity temporal graphs, and the GCN aggregates temporal-neighborhood features while enhancing local trends. Next, with state-decay and harmonic time modulation, the GRU captures long-term and periodic dynamics. Finally, the KAN head maps high-dimensional features to symbolically expressible loadresponse functions, improving interpretability without sacrificing accuracy. Experiments on a public hourly load dataset from Panama and a private single-node dataset from Guizhou show that GCKAN achieves the best performance: on the Panama dataset, MAE and RMSE drop by 6.11% and 12.05%; on the Guizhou dataset, by 7.90% and 14.51%. The model maintains high predictive accuracy and adapts to abrupt load changes and peakvalley fluctuations. Ablation studies confirm that the proposed multi-granularity temporal graph encoding, the GRU with statedecay and temporal-modulation mechanisms, and the KAN are key contributors to the gains.

title: High-Sensitivity MEMS Optical Switch with Wide Spectral Response
authors: Chenyang Liu, Lei Zhang, Yuehao Zhang, Liming Zheng
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To address the endurance bottleneck caused by standby power consumption in embodied intelligent agents, this paper proposes a near-zero-power wake-up scheme based on a broadband infrared absorber and a MEMS optical switch. Systematical modeling was conducted using FDTD and Finite Element Methods. Results show that the designed absorber achieves an average absorptivity over 80% in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8-14 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex> band (peaking at 98% at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.8 \mu ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>) with a fabrication-friendly linewidth <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(>1 \mu ~\mathrm{m})$</tex>. In the mechanical domain, the switch utilizes a bimetallic beam mechanism to achieve a high sensitivity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.284 \mu ~\mathrm{m} / \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> and a low trigger threshold of 216 nW. Combined with a symmetric temperature compensation design, this device enables high-sensitivity, interference-resistant physical wake-up, supporting the long-term autonomous operation of embodied agents.

title: Hierarchical Long-Term Operation Optimization of Flexible Resources Considering Multi-Scale Temporal Characteristics
authors: Yonggang Jiao, Yangyang Qi, Xuan Wen
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The efficient long-term operation of flexible resources in renewable-rich power systems necessitates coordinated decision-making across multiple time scales. This paper proposes a tri-layer hierarchical optimization framework to address the coupling challenges between long-term investment and short-term dispatch. The model integrates annual capacity planning, quarterly/monthly balancing, and weekly/intraday realtime dispatch into a cohesive system. A bidirectional coordination mechanism is designed to ensure consistency between layers, effectively incorporating the time-varying operational characteristics of diverse resources. Case studies demonstrate that the proposed model significantly outperforms single-scale approaches: it increases annual revenue by 12.3%, reduces carbon emissions by 19.0%, and drastically curtails renewable energy waste while enhancing system stability. The proposed framework offers a practical and scalable solution for optimizing flexible resource operations in high-renewable penetration scenarios

title: Optimization Design of Uplift Safety Reserve Factor for Transmission Line Foundations in Collapsible Loess Sites
authors: Shaokang Han, Liping Huang, Shaohui Wang, Fan Wen, Lei Yang
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The design of transmission line foundations in collapsible loess regions is critically constrained by the coupled effects of soil collapse-induced softening, differential settlement, and dynamic loading. Conventional design approaches—predicated on the assumption of homogeneous soil behavior—fail to capture the transient and path-dependent hydro-mechanical response of collapsible loess under wetting and loading, leading to arbitrary or empirically calibrated safety reserve factors that compromise either structural reliability or economic efficiency. To address this gap, this study develops a moisture-content-dependent hydro-mechanical coupling constitutive model; uncovers the progressive degradation mechanism at the foundation-soil interface through high-fidelity finite element simulations; proposes a reliability-based multi-objective optimization framework for determining rational safety reserve factors; and validates the integrated methodology via comprehensive in-situ tests conducted across representative collapsible loess sites. The resulting design paradigm achieves an explicit trade-off between safety and economy, resolves the fundamental inconsistency between static safety-factor conventions and dynamic soil behavior, and provides both theoretical grounding and implementable guidance for uplift-resistant foundation design in collapsible loess environments.

title: Research on Optimization Method of Medium Voltage Target Network Based on Reliability and Economy
authors: Yuze Yang, Jian Guan, Jiepeng Huang, Luying Cai, Hong Hu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the continuous development of the economy and the iteration of science and technology, users' demand for electricity is no longer limited to meeting daily needs but has higher requirements for power supply reliability. However, higher reliability also implies higher construction costs. How to balance the relationship between the two and realize the reasonable optimization of the medium voltage target network has become a core issue in current distribution network planning. To address this problem, this paper conducts research on the optimization method of the medium voltage target network based on reliability and economy. It quantifies power supply reliability into an economic indicator through power outage losses. Under certain boundary conditions, the construction cost and this economic indicator are integrated as the objective for optimizing the medium voltage target network, and an optimization model for the medium voltage target network is built combined with the minimum spanning tree algorithm. Case simulations verify that the network optimized by this method has better comprehensive economic benefits and can effectively improve the optimization efficiency of the medium voltage target network.

title: Research and Application of Artificial Intelligence in Pattern Recognition of Power Grid Connection
authors: Chen Ye, Shangxing Ye, Hang Zheng, Bingwu Lou, Gui Cheng, Qian Wu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: The rationality of distribution network wiring is crucial to the safety and economy of the power grid. This paper investigates the application of artificial intelligence (AI) to distribution network wiring pattern recognition, and proposes a comprehensive recognition method based on the combination of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL). This method constructs a distribution network topology diagram, extracts node and edge features, and combines graph neural networks and traditional machine learning for pattern learning to classify typical connection modes such as single ring network, double ring network, double chain, double radiation, and network type. Through verification, the method proposed in this paper outperforms both the single GNN method and traditional graph theory methods in terms of accuracy and recall. The method shows varying degrees of improvement in accuracy and recall, particularly in the recognition of complex lattice structures. Finally, this paper explores potential research directions for combining deep reinforcement learning and intelligent optimization algorithms in the future.

title: Reinforcement Learning-based Sequential Route Recommendation for System-Optimal Traffic Assignment
authors: Leizhen Wang, Peibo Duan, Cheng Lyu, Zhenliang Ma
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Modern navigation systems and shared mobility platforms increasingly rely on personalized route recommendations to improve individual travel experience and operational efficiency. However, a key question remains: can sequential route recommendations issued by a centralized platform collectively drive the system toward the theoretical system optimum (SO)? This paper addresses this question by proposing a learning-based framework that reformulates the static SO traffic assignment problem as a single-agent deep reinforcement learning (RL) task. A central agent sequentially recommends routes to travelers as origin-destination (OD) demands arrive, to minimize total system travel time. To enhance learning efficiency and solution quality, we develop an MSA-guided deep Q-learning algorithm that integrates the iterative structure of traditional traffic assignment methods into the RL training process. The proposed approach is evaluated on both the Braess and Ortúzar–Willumsen (OW) networks. Results show that the RL agent converges to the theoretical SO solution in the Braess network and achieves only a 0.35% deviation in the OW network. Further ablation studies demonstrate that the route action set’s design significantly impacts convergence speed and final performance, with SO-informed route sets leading to faster learning and better outcomes. This study establishes an theoretically grounded framework that demonstrates the feasibility of aligning centralized sequential recommendations with SO traffic assignment, thereby connecting reinforcement learning methods with classical transportation optimization.

title: Adaptive Inverse Reinforcement Learning Optimal for Nonlinear System via Takagi-Sugeno Fuzzy Model
authors: Wenting Song, Shaocheng Tong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Inverse reinforcement learning (RL) optimal control is under the framework of expert-learner, where the learner system can imitate expert system's trajectory and optimal solutions via an inverse RL algorithm and minimize the cost function, thus it can deal with optimal control problem effectively. This paper presents a fuzzy inverse RL optimal control approach for nonlinear system with partially unknown dynamics. Firstly, the controlled nonlinear system is modeled by Takagi-Sugeno (T-S) fuzzy system. Secondly, the fuzzy optimal controller and the worst-case disturbance input are given via a zero-sum game. To imitate expert system's demonstrated behavior and achieve optimization, an online adaptive inverse RL algorithm is developed. The presented algorithm consists of four synchronous neural networks (NNs): a critic NN, an actor NN, a rival NN and a state-penalty NN, which are used to reconstruct expert system's cost function, optimal controller, disturbance input and state-penalty weight. Moreover, the tuning law of critic NN is implemented by using experience replay technique, instead of persistent excitation (PE) condition. It is proved that the proposed online adaptive inverse RL algorithm can learn expert system's optimal solutions and imitate its behavior. And also the presented fuzzy inverse RL optimal control scheme can make the controlled fuzzy learner system be asymptotically stable and achieve Nash equilibrium solution. Finally, a continuous stirring tank reactor (CSTR) system is provided to illustrate the feasibility and superiority of the developed algorithm.

title: Integrating Robust CRT with Signal Unwrapping for Modulo Sampling
authors: Chutong Shen, Wenyi Yan, Yimin D. Zhang, Lu Gan
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Two-channel modulo sampling based on the robust Chinese remainder theorem (RCRT) enables closed-form, point wise reconstruction, yet its recovery remains fundamentally limited by the effective RCRT threshold. This letter addresses this limitation through a hybrid architecture that integrates RCRT based recovery with difference-based unwrapping. The RCRT stage is reinterpreted as a coarse modulo operator with an expanded threshold, while the difference stage resolves the residual ambiguity by exploiting temporal structure. The proposed scheme extends recovery beyond the intrinsic RCRT range and simultaneously relaxes the oversampling requirement for a fixed difference order. Theoretical analysis establishes the resulting sampling condition, and the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through both simulations and hardware experiments.

title: From the Society: President's Message
authors: Shervin Shirmohammadi
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine
abstract: Dear members, colleagues, and friends of the IEEE Instrumentation and Measurement Society (IMS)—Welcome to the June 2026 issue of the Magazine. I would like to update you on three specific activities.

title: VN-YOLO: Detail-Preserving and Recalibrated Multi-Scale YOLO for Small Objects in UAV Imagery
authors: Ngoc-Tuan Hoang, Cao-Phuc Ha, Phu-Nguyen Le
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) have fundamentally altered many fields due to their flexibility and ability to reach inaccessible areas. They are used in military reconnaissance, agricultural operations, disaster response, environmental monitoring, and traffic surveillance. Nevertheless, identifying diminutive objects in UAV imagery remains a persistent challenge due to low resolution, complex backgrounds, and hardware limitations. Conventional object detection algorithms, including two-stage approaches such as Faster R-CNN and one-stage detectors such as YOLO, often face challenges in these scenarios, particularly when real-time performance is essential. Consequently, this study presents VN-YOLO, a modified version of YOLOv11n, specifically designed to detect small objects in drone imagery. The contributions include: (1) a detail-preserving backbone that incorporates SPDConv (Space-to-Depth Convolution), Learnable Adaptive Downsampling (LAD), RepVGG, SwinTransformer, and SPPF_LSKA for enhanced feature extraction; (2) a robust neck with C2fFast, CBAM/RFA attention, ReCalibrationFPN, AMFE, and DCNv3 for multi-scale fusion and deformation handling; and (3) a stable detection head with channel alignment and MPDIoU loss for improved localization. Empirical findings derived from theVisDrone dataset demonstrate that VN-YOLO attains a mAP50 of 0.52 and a mAP50:95 of 0.356, surpassing the baseline YOLOv11n (mAP50 of 0.3314) by 57%, all the while preserving computational efficacy. Thus, this study advances real-time small-object detection in UAV applications by introducing a model that balances accuracy and efficiency.

title: Harmonic Mitigation and Switching Frequency Reduction in LCL-Filtered Grid-Tied Converters
authors: Ali Rafiee, Yazdan Batmani, Naser Souri, Ali Mehrizi-Sani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: LCL-type grid-tied power converters play a critical role in integrating renewable energy sources while meeting stringent power quality requirements. This paper presents a novel multivariable control strategy that combines singular perturbation theory with high-gain proportional-integral (PI) control to simultaneously address three key challenges: resonance damping, harmonic distortion reduction, and switching frequency limitations. The proposed approach decomposes the system dynamics into distinct slow and fast subsystems, enabling simplified controller design and overdamped operation without passive damping elements. A systematic design methodology ensures robust stability against parameter variations and grid disturbances while maintaining decoupled control of active and reactive power. The controller achieves superior performance with a switching frequency of only 5 kHz, reducing switching losses by approximately 50% compared with conventional active damping techniques operating at 10 kHz. Comprehensive simulation studies demonstrate an 80% reduction in total harmonic distortion (THD) of grid-injected current, with experimental validation confirming current THD levels below 3% under stiff grid conditions. The results show significant improvements in power quality and system efficiency, making the proposed method particularly suitable for medium- and high-power renewable energy applications where switching loss reduction is critical

title: Flexible Physical Camouflage Generation Based on a Differential Approach
authors: Yang Li, Haonan Li, Wenyi Tan, Tingrui Wang, Quan Pan
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Achieving physical-world attacks on object detectors often relies on adversarial camouflage, which applies textures to target surfaces. However, existing methods typically simulate environmental variations through post-rendering image transformations, which do not fully account for the 3D object’s geometry and its interaction with lighting. To address this, we propose the Flexible Physical-camouflage Attack (FPA), a framework that integrates a differentiable 3D renderer with comprehensive, multi-parameter environmental randomization such as lighting, material, and viewpoint directly into the optimization loop. This ensures that the generated textures are robust to real-world variations. For texture generation, FPA leverages a denoising diffusion probabilistic model whose generative prior helps produce structurally coherent and visually realistic patterns. These are jointly optimized with a set of task-oriented loss functions including adversarial, smoothness, non-printability, and concealment constraints within the unified framework. Through systematic ablation and extensive physical experiments on a 1:24 scale model, we demonstrate that FPA achieves a high attack success rate (ASR) and strong transferability to black-box detectors. Crucially, our analysis reveals a controllable trade-off between adversarial effectiveness and visual stealth, validated by perceptual metrics and human evaluation. Our findings highlight the importance of integrated physical modeling and systematic evaluation for advancing physically realizable adversarial camouflage.

title: CCSFusion: A Hierarchical Semantic Chain-of-Thought Reasoning Architecture for Infrared-Visible Image Fusion and Captioning
authors: Miaoshan Lin, Guoheng Huang, Jietao Yang, Jiehao Zheng, Xiaochen Yuan, Yan Li, Xiaofeng Zhang, Kim-Fung Tsang, Chi-Man Pun
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Infrared-Visible Image Fusion (IVIF) aims to generate a single, information-rich image for downstream tasks. However, prevailing methods exhibit two key limitations. First, many approaches lack explicit hierarchical semantic decoupling, failing to effectively integrate semantic features across different levels, which restricts their ability to capture complex scene structures. Second, task-driven fusion frameworks typically adopt a cascaded design, with unidirectional supervision provided by geometry-centric downstream tasks like detection. This architecture not only limits mutual reinforcement between the fusion and task networks, but also creates a ”supervision bottleneck” by lacking interaction with the linguistic modality that captures richer scene relationships. To tackle these challenges, we propose CCSFusion, the first framework that leverages Chain-of-Thought captioning as supervision, redirecting IVIF optimization from narrow geometric accuracy to multimodal scene comprehension. It establishes a mutually reinforcing coupling between the fusion network and the captioning task. Specifically, we introduce a Segmentation Mask Calibration Unit (SMCU) to refine coarse semantic priors, providing precise pixel-level guidance. Subsequently, the calibrated features are fed into Chained Semantic Fusion Module (CSFM) which explicitly decomposes the semantic priors into three hierarchical levels, and then feeds them into the Hierarchical Semantic Attention module. Finally, a bidirectional knowledge distillation mechanism transfers the reasoning ability of the teacher network to the student. Experiments show that CCSFusion achieves superior fusion performance and generates more semantically coherent images for high-level cognitive tasks. The code is available at: https://github.com/Snaillms/CCSFusion.

title: Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings Based on 1D-CNN
authors: Man Qiu, Kulevome Delanyo, Qian Wang, Wanzhen Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Bearings are one of the common key components in rotating machinery, and their faults can lead to equipment shutdown and production losses. Traditional methods, which combine feature extraction and classifiers, have problems such as the subjectivity of feature selection and the complexity of feature extraction. To address this, a rolling bearing fault diagnosis strategy based on a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture is proposed. Using the open-source dataset from Southeast University, normalization technology is adopted for feature extraction, and the CNN is used to train the processed data. By adjusting the model parameters, automatic fault identification and diagnosis are realized. Tests show that this method has an accuracy rate of 99.75%, exhibiting better performance and robustness.
