title: Resource Allocation for Multi-Domain Network Slicing: A Transfer Learning Approach
authors: Jihong Zhao, Yingtong Wang, Lin Wen
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of 5 G technology, network slicing has emerged as a critical solution to accommodate diverse service demands. In multi-domain environments, mobile network operators must manage multiple radio access networks (RANs) domains independently, increasing operational complexity and cost. This paper proposes a resource allocation method based on transfer learning (TL) to enhance the adaptability of scheduling policies across RAN domains. A resource allocation policy is initially pre-trained using the proximal policy optimization (PPO) algorithm in a source domain. The learned policy is then transferred to the target domains via policy fine-tuning and distillation. To ensure stable convergence, a kullback-leibler (KL) divergence regularization term and a transfer decay mechanism are introduced. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method improves the average training efficiency by $44.81 \backslash\%$ and reduces the training steps by ${4 0} \backslash \%-{5 3} \backslash \%$ compared to training from scratch, effectively improving resource allocation and reducing operational costs in multi-domain scenarios.

title: Research on Gear Deviation Patterns Based on Rolling Inspection Technology
authors: Shitao Wang, Zhaoyao Shi, Bo Yu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: With the increasing performance demands on gears for new energy vehicles, it has become essential to extract and decouple the gear error information contained in transmission error (TE) and radial composite deviation (RCD). However, a quantitative relationship between TE and RCD has not yet been clearly established. To address this issue, a numerical model of double-flank meshing is developed to reveal their intrinsic relationship. Based on this model, the effects of crowning and pressure-angle deviation on the relationship between TE and RCD are further analyzed.

title: Fake Review Detection Based on LOFD-EGCNN Model
authors: Weixue Shen, Ming Li, Jing Wang, Linlin Fan
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: To address the shortcomings of poor data adaptability and insufficient feature utilization in fake review detection for Chinese e-commerce scenarios, this study proposes the LOFD-EGCNN model. The model integrates the LOFD feature weighting algorithm with a gated convolutional network, enhances domain-specific features through emoji semantic mapping and special punctuation retention, and realizes adaptive fusion of textual semantic features and statistical features via a gating mechanism. Experimental results show that the model outperforms mainstream deep learning methods in recognition performance on specific product review datasets and maintains favorable generalization in cross-domain transfer scenarios. Adapted to the typical characteristics of Chinese fake reviews including emotional exaggeration and symbol intensity, the model can provide technical support for optimizing the review audit systems of e-commerce platforms and facilitate the construction of a credible e-commerce information ecosystem.

title: A Unified Perceptual-Physical Framework for Real-Time Underwater Motion Simulation of Immersive Agents and Large-Scale Crowds
authors: Jong-Hyun Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a unified perceptual–physical underwater motion simulation framework that simultaneously supports immersive user interaction and large-scale crowd behaviors. In underwater environments, various fluid forces, including drag, buoyancy, gravity, and Magnus effects induced by rotation, act on the human body in a coupled manner. However, conventional virtual reality systems have limitations in realistically conveying such forces due to restricted hardware input and the absence of force-feedback devices. To address this problem, we model underwater forces not as direct force feedback but as the temporal evolution of velocity states, and present a unified motion generation framework that can be applied consistently to both user-controlled avatars and non-player characters (NPCs), as well as large-scale crowd simulations. The proposed method explicitly computes buoyancy, drag, and Magnus effects at the level of joints and body segments to generate physically plausible full-body motion, while biomechanically valid joint range constraints prevent unrealistic postures caused by external forces. For user interaction, a controllable perceptual coupling between the simulated physical state and the actual user input intentionally introduces visual discrepancies, allowing users to intuitively perceive underwater resistance without additional haptic devices. The same physical model is further used to naturally produce diverse underwater behaviors in large-scale crowds through variations in initial conditions and body parameters. A real-time implementation in Unity validates the efficiency and scalability of the proposed framework, and experimental results demonstrate stable and consistent underwater motion representation in both immersive interaction scenarios and large-scale crowd simulations. The proposed approach provides a practical solution for integrating physical realism and perceptual experience in underwater environments without requiring additional hardware, and shows potential for applications in virtual reality training, simulation, games, and digital twin environments.

title: False Alarm Rate-Controllable Maritime Target Detection Via Cross-Attention Transformer Network
authors: Qingsong Jiang, Wenjing Zhao, Weiqing Zhao, Guolong Cui
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: To address degraded maritime target detection and false alarm rate (FAR) control under strong sea clutter, this paper proposes FARC-CAT, a FAR-controllable detector based on a dual-encoder cross-attention Transformer. An energy-frequency dual-branch encoder is designed for feature extraction, followed by bidirectional cross-attention and gated fusion to enhance feature interaction. In addition, a hybrid loss with a learnable threshold is introduced to constrain FAR during training. Experiments on publicly available measured datasets show that FARC-CAT outperforms representative methods in low-SCR scenarios while maintaining a low FAR. In particular, under a preset FAR of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10^{-4}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, it achieves an average detection probability of 91.97%, outperforming the existing representative methods.

title: PreSFC: Predictive SFC Migration via Multi-slot Mobility Forecasting in MEC Networks
authors: Ji Li, Songtao Guo, Quanjun Zhao, Guiyan Liu, Liang Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Network Function Virtualization (NFV) is a foundational technology for Mobile Edge Computing (MEC). It delivers network services by chaining Virtual Network Functions (VNFs) into sequential Service Function Chains (SFCs). One of the most critical challenges in MEC is how to provide continuous and stable services to high-mobility user, such as intelligent vehicles and drones. However, current mobility-aware SFC migration methods remain constrained by either post-hoc reaction or myopic prediction horizons, failing to reconcile the divergent timescales of network services and user mobility, thus resulting in suboptimal resource allocation and service delivery. In this paper, we first formulate the predictive mobility-aware SFC migration problem as an NP-hard Integer Linear Programming (ILP) problem. Aiming to mitigate service disruption for mobile users in MEC networks, we propose PreSFC, a predictive SFC migration framework that integrates multi-slot mobility forecasting with fine-grained network state tracking. We first design Gformer, a deep learning-based long sequence time-series forecasting model, which operates on short time slots (less than 200 ms) to sensitively capture network dynamics while predicting over multiple slots (e.g., 50 slots) to effectively track user mobility. This dual-scale design explicitly addresses the temporal disparity between mobility patterns and service requirements. Based on the predictions, we further propose an Optimal Sub-period Partitioning Migration (OSPM) algorithm to determine migration timing and locations. Extensive simulations show that our approach reduces the maximum and average downtime by approximately 55% and 40%, respectively, compared to benchmark methods.

title: Probabilistic Sparse Attention and Adaptive Domain Modeling for External Defect Detection in Transformers
authors: Ning Sun, Jiabao Zhang, Sicheng Zhu, Zihao Wang, Hucheng Xu, Penghui Liu, Guangan Ding, Ying Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The images of the external equipment of the transformer obtained by the cameras and other devices have complex backgrounds, and the small targets with minor defects or small images have low resolution, resulting in a decrease in the recognition rate. To address the environment with complex feature backgrounds, this paper proposes an adaptive detection network for external transformer defects. The network architecture incorporates a specialized stage structure, an enhanced transformer layer, an efficient layer aggregation mechanism, and a real-time detection transformer decoder. Initially, raw images of transformer equipment are utilized as input. The primary module, designated as the probabilistic sparse transformer layer, integrates a novel encoder module as the core channel mechanism. This module includes a patch embedding layer, a sparse multi-head probabilistic attention layer, a residual connection structure, a layer normalization operation, and a feedforward layer. This design efficiently extracts irregularly varying feature information under limited fault samples while globally capturing data anomalies induced by external defects. Additionally, the incorporation of an adaptive activation function augments the expressive capacity of nonlinear mappings. The piecewise linear properties of the function prevent extreme fluctuations in output values, thereby mitigating gradient vanishing issues while enhancing expressive capability. Furthermore, the integration of domain-adaptive batch normalization technology reduces feature distribution discrepancies between source and target domains. This study uses real engineering data sets, and the results show that DEIM-PPSformer, based on DEIM, increased mAP@0.5 by 3.1%, reaching 92.1%. mAP@0.5-0.95 increased by 5.8%, reaching 71.6%. This indicates that the proposed method has a significant advantage in detecting defects in external transformer equipment.

title: Smart Farming: Eggplant Leaf Disease Detection through Fine-Tuned DenseNet201 Deep Learning Model
authors: Khushdeep Kaur, Husanpreet Kaur, Avneet Kaur, Varun Gupta, Shobhit Tomar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In the majority of developing countries, farmers use eggplant that is readily infected by different diseases that affect leaves. The early and correct detection of these diseases will help in provision of food, sustainable agriculture and cost reduction. A precise fine-tuning of the DenseNet201 and a deep learning methodology allowed scholars to differentiate the eggplant leaves state of a healthy plant, insect pest disease, leaf spot disease, mosaic virus disease, small leaf disease, white mold disease and wilt disease. Overall, 3552 images of open-source Kaggle platform were selected and divided into the following subsets: training, validation and testing: 70, 15 and 15 percent. The given system demonstrated a 86% hit rate with test data and a high score in precision, recall and F1scores on most of the classes. The analysis of the confusion matrices and the training curves show clearly that there is no error rate or the model is firm. Artificial intelligence in plant pathology can help find a faster solution to plant diseases, responsible eating patterns, and fight climate change and hunger. The study satisfies the objectives of sustainable development by contributing to the effectiveness of the farming process, by facilitating the new ideas in the process of finding the solutions and effective use of the resources. The recommended method can enable farmers and other agriculturalists to identify diseases at an earlier age, shift toward low-pesticide practices and achieve greater yields.

title: A Non-Contact Volume Measurement System using a RGB-D Camera
authors: Jin Seong Son, Jae Hong Shim
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: In recent years, the demand for three-dimensional (3D) scanners for shape reconstruction and volume measurement has grown significantly in the fields of manufacturing and quality inspection. However, most commercial 3D scanners rely on expensive equipment or complex robotic systems, making them cost-prohibitive and difficult to access for general users. To address these limitations, this study proposes the design and implementation of a low-cost 3D scanning system that integrates an RGB-D camera with a two degree of freedom (DOF) rotational structure. The proposed system consists of an RGB-D camera mounted on a servo motor enabling horizontal rotation and a turntable that rotates the target object. These two components operate independently, allowing the system to capture point cloud data from multiple perspectives and minimize occluded areas. The acquired point clouds from multiple viewpoints are aligned and post-processed to reconstruct the complete 3D geometry of the object. A volume estimation algorithm is then applied to automatically calculate the object's volume. Experimental results show that despite its simple mechanical configuration, the proposed system achieves accurate shape reconstruction and consistent volume measurement.

title: Heavy Ion-Induced Single-Event Effects in eGaN HEMTS
authors: Arefa Billa, Patrick Maloney, Grant Mayberry, Tianfang Liu, Benjamin Bolton, Hector Parra, Fahim Ahmed, Joyprokash Debnath, Daniel M. Fleetwood, En Xia Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: This study investigates the Single Event Effects (SEE) enhancement-mode GaN High Electron Mobility Transistors (eGaN HEMTs) under off-bias conditions induced by heavy ion irradiation with varying incident angles. Two commercially available eGaN devices with different voltage and current ratings were subjected to 16 MeV and 20 MeV ion beams to assess their susceptibility to SEE-induced degradation. Significant sensitivity to single-event leakage current (SELC) and single-event burnout (SEB) was observed under xenon (Xe) irradiation at high drain bias. Additionally, low-level SELC events were recorded during argon (Ar) irradiation at elevated drain voltages and high incident angles. The results highlight the importance of ion species, angle of incidence, and bias conditions in determining SEE vulnerability in commercial eGaN power transistors.

title: A Multimodal Deep Learning Approach for Emotion-Aware Speech Recognition
authors: Deepak. K. S, Elwin Gonsalves, Deepak.R, H.S.Veeresh, Ashwini Kodipalli, K.C.Narendra
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The following research proposes an integrated Deep Learning pipeline that unifies speech recognition, natural language understanding, and emotion-aware analysis to achieve human-like comprehension of spoken interactions. The system initiates with the fine-tuning of OpenAI's Whisper model by LoRA-based parameter-efficient training on the LibriSpeech ASR dataset for high-quality, lightweight speech-to-text transcription. The model performance was evaluated against metrics such as WER, CER, and BLEU score for linguistic fidelity. Subsequently, the resultant text is subjected to several steps involved in a multi-stage Natural Language Processing framework about Glove embedding, an Autoencoder, and then a Bidirectional GRU-based RNN for extractive summarisations, while a Keyword CNN highlights semantically important terms. The most relevant sentences, identified by the RNN, are passed to advanced transformer models such as Google Gemini and T5 for abstractive summarization, yielding coherent, context-rich summaries of conversations. In parallel, the system includes an emotion recognition module trained on the RAVDESS dataset using MFCC features extracted with torch audio and modelled through both MLP and GRU architectures. By analyzing pitch variation and RMS energy, the system maps detected emotions into expressive "vibe" categories such as enthusiastic, frustrated, hesitant, or calm, providing an additional emotional layer to dialogue understanding. Together, these components create a comprehensive AI framework capable of transcribing speech, understanding its semantic essence, summarizing key information, and interpreting emotional tone, forming a foundation for the next generation of emotionally intelligent and context-aware conversational systems.

title: Deep Learning for Soil Type Detection: A CNN Approach to Agricultural Insights
authors: J. Brahmaiahnaik, KastalaKarthik, ThummalaPavankumar Reddy, VallemAnkammarao, Karibandi Venkata Sai Lakshmi Harika, Maddala Vijayalakshmi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In land management, agriculture, and environmental monitoring, soil variety is essential. Conventional techniques for classifying soils depend on expert interpretation and laboratory examination, both of which are labor-intensive and subject to human error. In this work, we suggest a Convolution Neural Network (CNN)-based deep learning approach for soil type identification. Images from four distinct soil types make up the data set, which has been pre-processed and enhanced to enhance generalization. To efficiently extract spatial data, a custom CNN architecture with separable convolution layers and residual connections was set up. To address dataset imbalances, an adaptive learning approach with class weight balancing was used to train the model. The suggested method demonstrated its effectiveness in correctly categorizing soil types with a validation accuracy of 95.00%. By automating soil classification, this effort will lessen the need for manual inspection and provide a scalable and effective solution.

title: AI-Powered Cyber Defense Desktop Application with Real-Time Behavioral Analysis, Threat Intelligence
authors: Shrihari M R, Ajay N, Chethan M, Charvick D raj, Dhanush p s
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The rise of sophisticated cyberattacks has outpaced the capabilities of conventional security systems, prompting the need for intelligent, autonomous, and real-time defensive solutions. This paper presents the design and development of an AI-powered Cyber Defense Desktop Application equipped with real-time behavioral analytics, multi-model machine learning–based anomaly detection, network monitoring, user authentication via email-based OTP, and automated threat alerting. The proposed architecture integrates simulated ensemble machine learning algorithms including Isolation Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) anomaly prediction, and behavioral deviation analysis to compute a composite threat score. Real-time telemetry is collected from system resources, network connections, and operational patterns to derive risk levels ranging from NORMAL to CRITICAL. Additional capabilities include threat intelligence lookup, automated HTML email reporting, weekly summary generation, and an interactive security dashboard. Results indicate significant improvements in early threat identification and risk estimation, demonstrating the practicality of AI-driven endpoint defense. This paper provides a complete analysis of system architecture, algorithm design, security mechanisms, implementation details, performance evaluation, and recommendations for future enhancements.

title: How State Space Machines can help African Speech Language Identification
authors: Howard Prioleau, Saurav K. Aryal, Jeremy Blackstone
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This paper investigates advanced modeling techniques tailored for the identification of African language from audio data. We leverage state-of-the-art deep learning architectures, specifically the Audio Spectrogram Transformer (AST) and Mamba, a state space model optimized for efficient sequential data processing. Using a meticulously curated dataset comprising six African languages and performing a comparative analysis with traditional machine learning models, our aim is to evaluate the effectiveness and computational efficiency of these advanced approaches. Although AST achieves perfect accuracy in all languages tested, AfriMamba offers an impressive accuracy of 97. 9% along with nearly double the training speed and six times faster inference, demonstrating its transformative potential.

title: Advancements in Brain Tumor Analysis and Early Detection through MRI Imaging and Explainable AI Techniques
authors: Raghavendra Patil G E, Baba Fakruddin Ali B H, Swathi N, Jeevitha S R, Mehar Afroz, Umamaheswaran A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This research uses deep learning and Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methodologies in conjunction with Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) to classify and diagnose brain tumor early. The technique begins with the preprocessing of MRI images, which includes scaling, normalization, and enhancement of contrast, in order to prepare the data for modeling training. This work classifies brain tumor using architectures in InceptionV3 and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) in combination with transfer learning. The project uses XAI strategies like Grad-CAM and saliency maps, which visually highlight and show which areas are influencing model predictive accuracy, to promote transparency and clinical confidence. The project made use of cloud-based solutions to host the MRI image viewer and implementation of the system to manage large datasets efficiently. This multidisciplinary methodology enables health care providers to make accurate decisions while providing interpretable data through deep learning and XAI approaches to improve diagnostic precision. The case study demonstrates how deep learning, explainability, and complex image processing can be effectively combined to properly detect and classify brain tumors.

title: Comprehensive Voice-Based Pain Level Classification Utilizing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)
authors: Andrew Y. Lu, Wei Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Accurate classification of pain level is a persistent challenge in both clinical and non-clinical settings. Traditional approaches such as self-reporting, physiological signal monitoring, and facial expression analysis often suffer from limitations related to accessibility, equipment cost, and professional support. In this work, we propose a lightweight framework for real-time pain level classification using vocal signals. The proposed framework exploits the unique spectrogram features of voice signals to train Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) models. Our framework has been validated through simulations in Jupiter Notebook and a Raspberry Pi based prototype. Experimental results show that our approach outperforms existing methods by more than 20 % in accuracy. Our case studies also examined the impact of sample size and label imbalance on classification accuracy, showing that biased training leads to overfitting and a significant decrease in classification accuracy. Our hardware prototype, built from commercial off-the-shelf components for under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> USD, achieves real-time processing speeds ranging from approximately 6 to 22 seconds. In addition to CNN models, our experiments demonstrate that other machine learning algorithms such as Artificial Neural Networks, XGBoost, Random Forest, and Decision Tree have also proven applicable within our pain level classification framework.

title: Moodify AI: An Intelligent Air-Drumming System Using Computer Vision and Artificial Intelligence for Real-Time Musical Interaction
authors: Subrahmanya H M, Medha M, Nithushree, Vidhya Shree M, Deepika B O
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traditional drum kits are expensive, bulky, and space-consuming, limiting accessibility for beginners and casual learners. This paper presents Moodify AI, a marker less, vision-based air-drumming system that uses OpenCV and MediaPipe for real-time hand-landmark extraction and a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) classifier for gesture recognition. Unlike sensor-based or wearable systems, the proposed approach requires only a standard webcam and performs velocity-based gesture detection to simulate dynamic drum intensity. Experimental evaluation shows an average gesture recognition accuracy of 92% in well-lit conditions and 87% under low-light environments, with an average latency of 150–250ms, making it suitable for real-time musical interaction. The system demonstrates a low-cost, portable, and scalable alternative to hardware-dependent virtual percussion systems while enabling expressive gesture-controlled music performance.

title: Revolutionizing Cloud Security through Federated Learning for Next-Gen Healthcare Systems in South Africa
authors: Ramahlapane Lerato Moila, Mthulisi Velempini
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Our study presents an innovative federated learning (FL) approach secured by a hybrid zero-trust cloud architecture, specifically designed for the healthcare system in South Africa. Amid increasing cybersecurity threats and growing privacy concerns in hospitals, our approach enables collaborative model training across decentralized medical institutions without the sharing of raw patient data. This study used the CSE-CIC-IDS2018 dataset to simulate distributed environments and evaluate the performance of various algorithms, including logistic regression, random forests (RF), and deep learning, in both local and federated environments. Simulation results show that while the deep learning model performs well during local training, it degrades under global aggregation due to data heterogeneity. RF and Logistic Regression (LR) demonstrate stronger, more stable performance during FL. Compared with existing studies, our proposed scheme achieves higher accuracy—96% in LR and 97% in RF, improved scalability, and optimized computational efficiency, positioning it as a viable and secure foundation for next-generation healthcare systems in South Africa.

title: VTMedSeg: Geometry-guided Vision-Text Model with Concept-aware Fusion for Medical Ultrasound Image Segmentation
authors: Huan Wan, Wujian Xu, Yiwen Zou, Jinshan Zeng, Xin Wei
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Medical ultrasound image segmentation is a vital diagnostic technique for identifying abnormalities, including tumors, cysts, and inflammation. To obtain accurate segmentation efficiently, many methods have been proposed. Amongst, vision–text models have shown promising progress; however, their performance is limited by the lack of high-quality paired text descriptions in ultrasound datasets. To address this issue, we propose VTMedSeg. In VTMedSeg, a geometry-guided text generator is developed to automatically synthesize medical descriptions by using connected component analysis and multi-dimensional geometric analysis. Then, the text features of these descriptions and the visual features of the corresponding ultrasound images are effectively integrated into a concept-aware cross-modal fusion module through multi-scale cross-modal alignment and medical knowledge graph modeling. Extensive experiments on four public medical ultrasound datasets demonstrate that VTMedSeg outperforms state-of-the-art vision–text models across multiple metrics.

title: Hearing Materials, Feeling Participation, Caring for Personhood: Situated Lessons from Participatory Design with Nonverbal Participants
authors: Amber De Coen, Ralf Vetter, Sanne Nieuwenhuis
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers
abstract: None

title: Enacting Utopias: A Participatory Design Approach to AI
authors: Christian Dindler, Ole Sejer Iversen, Claus Bossen, Magnus Høholt Kaspersen, Peter Dalsgaard
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers
abstract: None

title: Participatory Design through the Lenses of Women Architects and Designers
authors: Alana Yasmim Lampert, Leandro Miletto Tonetto, Claudia de Souza Libânio
publish: Proceedings of the 19th Participatory Design Conference 2026, Vol. 1: Full Papers

title: FedGDD: Defending Federated Learning Against Targeted Model Poisoning Sybil Attacks Based on Gradient Drift Detection
authors: Qi Xiong, Hai Dong, Nasrin Sohrabi, Zahir Tari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Sybil attacks pose a significant threat to federated learning, as malicious nodes can collaborate to form a majority and overwhelm the system. Therefore, developing effective countermeasures is essential to ensure the security of federated learning systems. To address this challenge, we introduce a novel targeted model poisoning defence method for federated learning, named Gradient Drift Detection (FedGDD). Unlike existing approaches, such as clustering, statistical analysis, and re-training, which struggle in scenarios where malicious nodes constitute the majority, FedGDD reframes malicious detection as a gradient drift detection problem. This approach identifies potential attacks by detecting deviations in gradients, operating on the premise that the loss functions for benign and malicious nodes are inherently different. Extensive experimental evaluations demonstrate the efficacy of FedGDD compared to six well-established methods: Flame, FLTrust, FedCPA, Median, Krum, and FL-WBC. Using tasks from both image classification and natural language processing, the experiments confirm that FedGDD is robust and independent of specific application settings. Results show that FedGDD effectively safeguards federated learning systems across a wide range of malicious node ratios. Specifically, FedGDD maintains a low attack success rate for malicious nodes when their ratio ranges from 0.2 to 0.8. Additionally, it preserves high model accuracy when the malicious node ratio is between 0.2 and 0.5. These findings highlight FedGDD's potential to enhance the reliability and performance of Federated Learning systems.

title: Implicit Continuous Full-spectrum Response Network for Visible-to-Infrared Image Translation
authors: Qiaoying Qu, Bin Pan, Ruiying Li, Zhenwei Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The acquisition of infrared remote sensing imagery is often constrained by high sensor costs and complex atmospheric conditions, leading to a scarcity of infrared data. While data-driven visible-to-infrared image translation has demonstrated potential, existing methods typically neglect to merge sensor imaging mechanisms. To bridge this gap, a joint data-mechanism-driven visible-to-infrared translation framework, named ImconfuseNet, is proposed, which consists of an implicit full-spectrum reconstruction module and two spectral response imaging modules. The sensor imaging process intended for modelling comprises two fundamental steps: the continuous radiation enegry incident on the sensor, and the sensor responds to the enegry via a specific spectral response function. In order to model the radiation enegry, the implicit full-spectrum reconstruction module learns a spatial-spectral-wise continuous representation, which projects a visible image into a continuous full-spectrum image spanning visible and infrared wavelengths. Afterward, two spectral response imaging modules simulate the sensor-specific response process to generate the infrared (and visible) images. Moreover, we construct a similar imaging mechanism-driven infrared-to-visible reverse generator and adopt a cycle-consistent training scheme to enforce domain consistency. The major contribution of ImconfuseNet is that we realize visible-to-infrared image translation by incorporating the spectral response imaging process and integrating it into a data-driven neural network. Experimental results indicate that the proposed network outperforms several state-of-the-art methods. Our code will be available at https://github.com/qffang/ImconfuseNet.

title: Integrated UAV and Satellite Observations for Antarctic Melt Pond Monitoring: Assessing the Melt-induced Risk for Transportation on Landfast Ice_supp1-3695914.docx
authors: Lei Zheng
publish: N/A
abstract: In recent years, the extent of Antarctic sea ice has frequently reached historically low levels. Melt ponds significantly affect the sea ice heat balance and mass balance while posing challenges to ice surface transportation. Melt ponds in the Arctic have been extensively monitored using satellite remote sensing. However, the Antarctic has received very little attention, and small-scale melt pond distribution patterns remain virtually unexplored. This study combines high-resolution Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) imagery with satellite remote sensing to investigate the characteristics of melt ponds on landfast ice near Zhongshan Station, East Antarctica. UAV-derived data reveal that the melt ponds in this region are small (median area = 0.34 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and irregular in shape (mean roundness = 4.97). Validation against the UAV data suggests poor performance of the traditional melt pond fraction (MPF) retrieval model in the Antarctic region. Therefore, we develop an MPF retrieval algorithm by integrating UAV and Landsat 8 images. The correlation coefficient is as high as 0.82, substantially outperforming the traditional method. Dynamic MPF monitoring indicates that melt ponds concentrate near the coastline, especially to the west of bare rock areas, as the persistent summer easterly winds transport low-albedo dust westward. The integrated melt-induced risk assessment, combining MPF and Normalized Difference Water Index for ice, shows that high-risk areas are concentrated in Nella Fjord, west of the Mirror Peninsula. Ice surface transport should avoid this area and be completed before mid-November.

title: An Accelerated Vectorized Discrete Ordinate Method With Forward and Jacobian Modules Developed Toward Advanced Satellite Data Assimilation
authors: Yi-Ning Shi, Jun Yang, Fuzhong Weng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The application of vectorized radiative transfer (RT) models and their associated Jacobian computations is essential for satellite variational data assimilation, yet remains substantially more expensive than scalar RT. To address this limitation, this study extends the Accelerated Discrete Ordinate Method to a fully vectorized formulation, ADOM_V, together with its tangent-linear and adjoint modules, for efficient and accurate simulations of polarized RT and its corresponding Jacobians. In ADOM_V, the full vectorized discrete ordinate solver is applied only to scattering-dominant layers, while adjacent effectively non-scattering layers are analytically merged using absorption–emission theory. This hybrid treatment preserves the key vertical RT structure while reducing computational cost, and is applicable from visible to microwave wavelengths. Idealized experiments show that the Stokes vectors simulated by ADOM_V agree closely with reference solutions from the conventional vectorized discrete ordinate method, while tangent-linear and adjoint tests confirm the numerical consistency of the Jacobian module. ADOM_V achieves speedup factors of about 4–9 relative to the conventional vectorized solver, depending on the numbers of layers, streams, and Stokes components. Incorporated into ARMS version 1.2, ADOM_V is further applied to all-sky visible radiance simulations for AGRI/FY-4B. Compared with scalar ADOM simulations, the inclusion of polarization yields physically meaningful improvements, especially for water-cloud-sensitive channels, and reveals a pronounced dependence on scattering geometry. These results demonstrate that ADOM_V is a physically consistent, numerically accurate, and computationally efficient polarized RT solver, providing an important step toward the use of vectorized RT solvers in all-sky variational data assimilation, although further acceleration is still needed for operational implementation.

title: Disk-Integrated Lunar Phase Curves from FY-4B/AGRI: Hapke Photometric Modeling from 470 nm to 2230 nm
authors: Wei Cai, Tianyi Xu, Yunzhao Wu, Jun Wang, Min Shu, Yong Zhang, Ling Sun, Zhiwei Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The photometric behavior of the lunar surface provides important constraints on regolith scattering processes and serves as a key tool for interpreting remote sensing observations and understanding the physical mechanisms responsible for the opposition effect. In this study, lunar observations acquired by the Advanced Geostationary Radiation Imager (AGRI) onboard the Fengyun-4B (FY-4B) geostationary meteorological satellite are processed to construct a photometric dataset spanning wavelengths from 470 nm to 2230 nm and phase angles from −133.7° to 122.7°. From this dataset, disk-integrated reflectance is derived, and phase curves in five spectral bands are constructed and modeled using the Hapke photometric formulation. The opposition surge amplitude exhibits weak wavelength dependence within the sampled range, suggesting that shadow-hiding effects dominate over coherent backscatter at disk-integrated scale. While most band ratios increase monotonically with phase angle, ratios involving the 2230 nm band exhibit a distinct arch-shaped dependence whose peak phase angle increases systematically with wavelength separation, indicating a transition in scattering regimes toward the shortwave infrared. Photometric correction tests demonstrate that the retrieved Hapke parameters provide effective reflectance normalization, with cross-phase reflectance stability better than ∼8%. These results demonstrate that geostationary disk-integrated observations provide robust constraints on lunar photometric properties and contribute to improved characterization of regolith scattering at global scales.

title: Point-RMAE: Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D Representation Learning_supp1-3694193.pdf
authors: Jihua Zhu
publish: N/A
abstract: The Mainstream 3D masked point modeling representation learning community typically employs predefined, fixed-ratio random or block masking strategies, aiming to obtain optimal representations and achieve high downstream performance. However, these empirical designs overlook the significant geometric information and structural importance differences that are inherent among different 3D points, leading to a suboptimal trade-off between the representation capture capabilities and reconstruction difficulty of such masking strategies. To address this issue, we are the first to present this decision-making problem to a reinforcement learning agent and propose a Reinforcement Masked Autoencoder for 3D representation learning, named Point-RMAE. Guided by geometric features as state factor, this method leverages the Masking Strategy Analyzer and the Dynamic Masking Generator to adaptively decide and apply the masking strategy during pretraining. The Masking Ratio Scheduling module dynamically adjusts the masking ratio based on the optimal strategy. Subsequently, the analyzer is updated by multiscale rewards derived from reconstruction quality level, distribution-aware feedback, and policy exploration. Notably, to enrich the Reward Function with distribution-aware signals and avoid decision collapse issue, we propose a Flow Matching Point Cloud Fast Generator that guides the selected masking decisions. Our method achieves outstanding performance across downstream tasks such as shape classification, medical diagnosis, object detection, action recognition, denoising and multiscale scene segmentation on ten popular 3D and 4D datasets. More importantly, Point-RMAE pioneers the application of reinforcement learning in 3D self-supervised representation learning.

title: High-Speed CMOS ADCs for 5G New Radio and AI-Edge Systems: Design Trends and Trade-Offs
authors: Norhamizah Idros, Jagadheswaran Rajendran, Selvakumar Mariappan, Asrulnizam Abd Manaf, Narendra Kumar, Arokia Nathan, Binboga Siddik Yarman
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The rapid growth of wideband wireless communication and edge intelligence applications has driven stringent requirements for analog to digital converters (ADCs) that simultaneously achieve high sampling rates, robust dynamic performance, energy efficiency, and scalability across advanced CMOS technology nodes. This paper presents a comprehensive review of high-speed CMOS ADC architectures, including flash, advanced flash, successive approximation-register (SAR), pipelined, pipelined-SAR, and time-interleaved (TI) designs, with emphasis on their suitability for emerging 5G New Radio (NR) and artificial intelligence (AI)-edge systems. Recent state of art CMOS implementations are critically examined and benchmarked, and the fundamental trade-offs among sampling speed, resolution, power consumption, and silicon area are systematically analyzed across different architectures and technology nodes. Key architectural techniques and scaling trends enabling performance improvement are discussed, together with a comparative evaluation using Schreier Figure of Merit (FoMS). The analysis indicates that continued CMOS scaling and architectural innovation have enabled FoMS values exceeding 160 dB, meeting the demands of low-power, high speed, and high resolution data conversion in next-generation wireless and artificial intelligence (AI)-edge platforms.

title: S-Edge: A Multi-Region Edge Computing Framework with Adaptive Data Compression and Dynamic Load Balancing
authors: Emir Husain, Dev Patel, Vedang Kore, Manik Gupta, S Sharmiladevi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has posed serious problems in terms of bandwidth usage, latency, and data integrity in centralised cloud systems. Edge computing can help resolve these problems by processing data closer to the source, but existing frameworks frequently lack built-in mechanisms for dynamic load distribution and adaptive data optimisation. This paper presents S-Edge, a multi-region edge computing framework that combines real-time anomaly detection, smart load balancing, and dynamic compression to maximise resource utilisation. A smart-switching compression layer is introduced that selects dynamically among ZLIB, BZ2, and LZMA based on data priority and compression efficiency threshold τ = 0.5, achieving significant storage reduction. The system employs a capacity-aware load balancing algorithm that redistributes traffic across regions when utilisation exceeds Lthresh = 1000 bytes (80% capacity), maintaining system stability. Anomaly detection using Isolation Forest achieves a precision, recall, and F1-score of 0.99 under controlled simulation conditions, representing an upper bound specific to the deliberately separated synthetic distributions used; robustness analysis under overlapping distributions yields F1 of 0.39–0.63 depending on overlap severity. Security is provided through AES-256-GCM encryption and SHA-256 integrity checks. The system achieves a primary storage recovery latency of under 1 ms, with asynchronous multi-region replication completing within approximately 153 ms. Experimental results confirm the applicability of the framework to scalable, resource-constrained IoT deployments.

title: Quantum Key Distribution For Optical Satellite Communications: An Engineering-Oriented Review
authors: A. Popescu, A.-M. Badescu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Satellite quantum key distribution (SatQKD) is widely regarded as a promising approach for extending quantum-secure key establishment beyond the distance limitations of terrestrial optical fiber links. However, practical assessment of SatQKD remains difficult because published results rely on heterogeneous assumptions regarding channel loss, orbital visibility, atmospheric conditions, detector behavior, finite-key analysis, and classical post-processing efficiency. As a result, comparisons across protocols, architectures, and mission concepts are often difficult to interpret from an operational engineering perspective. This paper presents an engineering-oriented review of SatQKD in the context of hybrid optical satellite communication systems. The review synthesizes prior results reported across protocol-level, link-budget, orbital, finite-key, and network-level studies. Within this scope, service-level notions such as pass-integrated and availability-limited key delivery are used as an interpretive lens for discussing practical relevance under orbital intermittency, atmospheric attenuation, receiver non-idealities, finite-key constraints, and classical post-processing limitations. The contribution of this review is a cross-layer engineering synthesis of prior SatQKD results reported under heterogeneous assumptions, aimed at supporting more consistent operational interpretation across protocols, architectures, and mission concepts.

title: Enhanced Sensing in Large-Scale Array PMCW Systems by Range-Angle Decoupling
authors: Hwanhee Park, Shinyoung Park, Jingon Joung, Seongwook Lee, Jeong-Hoon Park
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Recently, phase-modulated continuous-wave (PMCW) systems have been gaining attention as a next-generation waveform for autonomous driving systems due to their capability of integrated sensing and communication with high-resolution sensing ability. Because high-resolution PMCW radar systems require a wide array aperture and wide bandwidth, the severe range-angle coupling effect can degrade the sensing performance. To address this issue, we first formulate a signal model for PMCW radar system that accounts for range-angle coupling, also referred to as the spatial-wideband effect, and classify this effect into range migration and beam squint. Based on the formulation, our analysis shows that PMCW radar systems are more vulnerable to range-angle coupling than frequency-modulated continuous-wave radar systems, which highlights the importance of the proposed mitigation technique. To mitigate the issue, we propose a novel range-angle decoupling method for PMCW radar systems that uses a modified correlation process. The simulation results show that the proposed method improves both the peak power and the half-power beam width of the target angle response, even in scenarios with multiple targets. Furthermore, the computational complexity of the proposed method is relaxed by Fourier transform-based interpolation, which enables significantly faster computation with negligible performance degradation.

title: AC Power Flow-Based Demand Response for Multi-Energy Three-Phase Distribution Systems
authors: Shahin Shaikh, Md Asif Shaharear
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: This paper presents an unbalance-aware demand response (DR) framework for three-phase unbalanced distribution systems with integrated multi-energy flexibility, including electricity, heat, and gas loads. Voltage unbalance is explicitly incorporated into the optimization objective to achieve a trade-off between economic performance and network operation. A nonlinear optimization problem is formulated over a 24-hour horizon and solved using Sequential Quadratic Programming (SQP), with embedded three-phase AC power flow ensuring accurate network validation and constraint satisfaction. Simulation results on a modified IEEE 37-bus system demonstrate that cost-only DR achieves lower cost but results in higher voltage imbalance, whereas the proposed method reduces average voltage unbalance and improves voltage profiles with a moderate increase in cost. Additional analyses, including voltage constraint verification, power loss comparison, and sensitivity studies, further confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the approach.

title: Optimizing Dropout in LLM Training: Performance Comparison of Fusion and Overlap
authors: Haiyue Ma, Jian Liu, Ronny Krashinsky
publish: Abstracts of the 2026 ACM SIGMETRICS International Conference on Measurement and Modeling of Computer Systems
abstract: None

title: Offline Local Search for Online Stochastic Bandits
authors: Gerdus Benade, Rathish Das, Thomas Lavastida
publish: Abstracts of the 2026 ACM SIGMETRICS International Conference on Measurement and Modeling of Computer Systems
abstract: None

title: Multimodal Knowledge Distillation on MRI-based Alzheimer’s Disease Detection with Clinical-Guided Teacher Learning
authors: Vaishnavi A Jadhav, Shrusti M Jamakhandi, Shrinivas A Naganur, Kaushik Malhibhat
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurode-generative disease, Existing clinical manifestations of AD are heterogeneous and incomplete making it difficult to accurately detect the condition because of lack of access to multi-modal data. The paper presents a multi-modal knowledge distillation model to be applied in the detection of AD, based on the OASIS dataset. Multi-modal teacher network is trained to learn together with MRI images and structured clinical features in order to capture disease-specific representations that are not complementary. The acquired knowledge is condensed to a student model of light weight, which is limited to the MRI data. The accuracy, AUC, precision, recall, F1-score, confusion matrix, ROC analysis, inference time and model complexity are used to test the proposed framework. The experimental results indicate that the teacher model has high performance and the distilled student model has better generalization and efficiency than the traditional single-modal training. The findings indicate that knowledge distillation is an effective technique with respect to balancing diagnostic performance and computational efficiency in clinical practice.

title: Linguistic Biomarkers for Dementia Detection: A Comparative Study of Machine Learning Models
authors: Nandu Dammalapati
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Dementia is one of the most common types of progressive neurodegenerative disorders, with Alzheimer being one of the most prevalent. Currently, research and diagnostic methods often explore only clinical interviews, cognitive testing, and neuroimaging approaches that can be difficult to receive due to their cost and inaccessibility. This study aims to evaluate the effectiveness of machine learning-based pipeline for early dementia detection through speech transcript analysis. Utilizing the Pitt Corpus from the DementiaBank database, patient interactions with clinicians are transcribed for training and testing. The dataset is composed of 1541 samples, with 955 healthy and 586 samples with dementia. These trials are preprocessed and transformed through multiple feature extraction strategies including linguistic feature engineering, TF-IDF vectorization, Word2Vec, GloVe, and sentence embeddings. The dataset was split into 75-25 train-test split; specifically, 75% of the data used for training and 25% used for testing. The models utilized were Support Vector Machines (SVM), KNearest Neighbors (KNN), and Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) were evaluated using cross-validation across different embedding configurations. Model performance was evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. The ANN consistently achieves top-tier performance, with accuracies surpassing 97% across embedding types. Suspecting data leakage through these highly-accurate results, the trials were isolated in preprocessing and then deemed the same results. This research demonstrates the potential of computational linguistics for scalable, early-stage dementia screening, and possible real-world clinical deployment.

title: ChaosRetina: Chaos-theory Based Feature Extraction for Multi-Label Retinal Disease Classification
authors: Sindhu P Menon, Subash S, S A Shanjith, Harshith K N, Yashas P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The early-stage retinal diseases usually manifest in subtle and hard-to-recognize visual patterns, which are challenging for even the experienced clinicians to identify successfully. Despite the success of CNNs in automatic retinal image analysis, they struggle to recognize faint and irregular pathological cues, especially in a multi-label and heavily imbalanced context. Contribution: This paper introduces ChaosRetina, a two-stage deep learning architecture which incorporates chaos theory–inspired feature transformation speaker identification module into traditional CNN architectures. The proposed ChaosFEX constructs deep features by using iterative logistic map dynamics allowing complex minuscule details to be acknowledged after being suppressed by traditional activations. Experiments on the RFMiD multi-disease fundus dataset show that the proposed method achieves AUROC of 0.957 in binary disease detection and macro-AUROC of 0.869 in multi-label classification, outperforming multiple CNN based baseline methods. Extensive ablation studies show consistent improvement from ChaosFEX, especially for rare diseases. The findings indicate that chaos-based nonlinear transforms can be effective combined with deep learning representations for medical image analysis.

title: AI in the Antenna Design Life Cycle: An AI-Assisted Systematic Review
authors: Ike Mafa, Simon Winberg
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: The increasing integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in antenna design presents opportunities for optimizing the design process. This paper presents a comprehensive AI-assisted systematic review of the use of AI in the antenna design life cycle. A Systematic Mapping Study (SMS) is first employed to map the landscape of AI applications in antenna design, identifying key research areas, trends, and gaps. The SMS categorizes studies into three primary domains: AI for Optimization, AI for Synthesis, and AI for Performance Prediction. In the second phase, a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) is conducted to critically evaluate and synthesize empirical findings from the selected studies. The SLR examines the various AI techniques used in each step of the lifecycle. The results from both methods provide a comprehensive overview of AI’s current applications in the field, highlighting key challenges and opportunities for future research. This hybrid approach, combining AI-assisted SMS and SLR, offers both a broad mapping of AI applications and a deep, critical analysis of their real-world effectiveness, making it a valuable contribution to the field of AI-enhanced antenna design and AI-assisted systematic reviews.

title: Design of Radix-4 Booth (Radix 256 booth encoding) Multiplication Algorithm
authors: Patnala Uma Mehar Sai Nagesh, Vella Satyanarayana
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Almost allelectronic systems, including notably portable ones like smartphones, tablets, and the other devices, have energy reduction as one of their primary design considerations. These handheld gadgets rely on digital signal processing (DSP) blocks to power their multimedia capabilities. Mathematical logic units, which are at the heart of these blocks’ processing, are responsible for the vast majority of DSP systems’ arithmetic operations, the most common of which is multiplication. For low power, low complexity applications, an effective approximate 8 bit multiplier is presented using the Radix-4 Booth (Radix 256 booth encoding) multiplication approach. Some have suggested using approximate Booth encoders to lower the PDP and simplify MBE by inserting false entries into the truth table. The partial product accumulation array makes use of an approximation of the Wallace tree structure. Radix 16 booth multiplication, which improves hardware efficiency with low latency, is also used to enhance this project.

title: Traffic Sign Detection Using CNN in Adverse Imaging Environments
authors: Prathibha R, Yogaraja G S R, Amrutha N, Bhavya Shree D, Charitha N R, Harika K S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Traffic sign detection plays a very important role in modern transportation technologies, especially in systems designed for autonomous driving and driver assistance. These signs provide timely guidance that helps vehicles make safe and informed decisions on the road. Earlier recognition methods relied heavily on manually designed features, which often struggled when signs appeared blurred, partially hidden, or affected by changes in lighting or weather. In this work, a CNN-based architecture is proposed for the detection and classification of traffic signs using the Indian Traffic Sign Computer Vision dataset. The results indicate that CNN-based detection systems are well-suited for real-world deployment in driver assistance systems and autonomous driving, contributing to enhanced road safety and efficient traffic management.

title: Descriptor: University of Rhode Island Photovoltaic Battery Energy Storage SOC Tracking Dataset (URI-PBEST)
authors: Showrov Rahman, Kaushallya Adhikari, Kaan Icer
publish: IEEE Data Descriptions
abstract: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) have emerged as a premier energy storage solution due to their modular architecture, decreasing costs, and increasing energy densities. These systems are increasingly integrated with photovoltaic (PV) arrays to mitigate energy wastage and enable sophisticated energy management. However, the operational environment and charging/discharging profiles of a PV-BESS differ significantly from other applications that also deploy BESSs, such as electric vehicles (EVs). While EV batteries typically undergo controlled, high-power charging cycles, a PV-BESS must manage the stochastic and intermittent nature of solar irradiance, leading to irregular cycling patterns and complex power dynamics. In this article, we introduce the URI-PBEST dataset, specifically developed for the State of Charge (SOC) tracking of PV-integrated battery systems. The data were collected at a high temporal resolution (1-minute intervals) during the 2024–2025 period at the University of Rhode Island (URI) Kingston Campus and are provided in a standard spreadsheet (.xlsx) format. The dataset captures distinct charging and discharging phases; specifically, the system records energy storage under highly variable weather conditions and discharge cycles under constant current conditions. The primary contribution of this dataset is its unique ability to facilitate investigations into the impact of environmental variables, such as solar irradiance, ambient temperature, and humidity, on SOC estimation of PV batteries. By capturing operational data across diverse weather profiles, including sunny, partly cloudy, and overcast days, URI-PBEST offers a realistic benchmark for analyzing how weather-related fluctuations influence the charging dynamics of PV-BESS.

title: Hybrid CNN Architectures for Bengali Pitha Recognition: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Dewan Rita Faria Prachi, Pabon Shaha, Muhammad Minoar Hossain, Yadab Sutradhar, Md. Moradul Siddique, Md. Sadiq Iqbal
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The Bangla terms pitha, pithe, and peetha refer to traditional native foods of Bangladesh and certain regions of India, particularly in areas where Bangla is the predominant language. Pithas hold a significant place in Bengali and Bangladeshi culture, encompassing a diverse range of traditional delicacies. Accurately classifying different types of pitha is essential for advancing the food industry and preserving cultural heritage. The goal of this research is to create an effective model for image-based classification of traditional foods, specifically focusing on various types of pitha. With the rapid progress of deep learning, remarkable advancements have been achieved in computer vision and related fields. Building on this, the present work provides a comprehensive evaluation of CNN-based techniques for recognizing and classifying pitha images.In this study, several deep learning architectures—ResNet50, DenseNet121, InceptionV3, and MobileNetV2—were implemented alongside two hybrid combinations: MobileNetV2 + DenseNet121 and InceptionV3 + DenseNet121. The hybrid model achieved a robust performance with an accuracy of 97.00% across diverse testing scenarios, underscoring its potential for real-world applications. These findings demonstrate the effectiveness of CNN-based methods in food image classification, contributing to both technological innovation and the preservation of cultural heritage. Furthermore, this research supports future developments in educational tools, automated food labeling systems, and digital food preservation initiatives.

title: Hybrid Transformer Models for Multimodal Brain Tumor Detection and Grading
authors: Prathiba R, Sunitha B S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Because of the complexity of brain tumor structures, variations in imaging data, and common modality absence, it is a challenging task to detect and grade brain tumors using MRI scans. Although conventional CNN models have shown great ability to learn local structures, such as edges and textures, the attention mechanism in transformer models has proven successful for learning broader contextual relationships but not without high computational resources. In an attempt to overcome these limitations, the current study proposes a hybrid framework that mixes CNNs and transformers for better performance. The model implements CNNs for the detailed extraction of spatial features and uses transformer encoders to learn global contextual information. Then, an attention based fusion mechanism is proposed to handle the multi-modal MRI images such as T1, T2 and FLAIR modalities when some of them are missing. As the experimental results show, our method achieves higher accuracy in detecting and grading tumors even with lower false positive rate and clearer boundary delineation when compared to previous methods. In summary, provides a robust and scalable framework to assist decision-making in the diagnosis of brain tumors.

title: AI-Vision: Deep Learning Based Facial Recognition System
authors: Ajay N, Monisha M, Shrihari M R, Namratha S M, Navya K, Shwetha B V
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This paper focuses on real-time face recognition in videos using a user-uploaded target face image. It operates entirely on a CPU, eliminating the need for GPU hardware. The system leverages advanced face recognition models like ArcFace and robust detectors such as MTCNN and RetinaFace to ensure accuracy. Key features include adjustable thresholds, frame skipping, and resizing for performance optimization. The application is built on a modern Flask-based web interface, allowing users to upload videos and view matched frames with bounding boxes and similarity scores. The backend processes video frames using OpenCV and DeepFace, extracting embeddings and comparing them to the target face.

title: A Bandwidth-Enhanced Metasurface-Based RF Energy Harvester With Wide-Angle Reception
authors: Xiangyan Liu, Ning Liu, Xianjun Sheng, Tianxi Feng, Hui Li, Lei Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents an efficiency enhancement strategy for the bandwidth-enhanced metasurface-based harvester operating at wide incidence angles. A current-constrained strategy, combined with a bandwidth-enhancement scheme, is first employed to achieve a high harvesting efficiency over a wide frequency range. Subsequently, by adjusting the positions of through-vias in adjacent metasurface units, the effective coupling at large incidence angles is enhanced, and the collected energy is driven toward the output ports. As a result, the harvesting efficiency at large angles is significantly improved, thus enabling wide incidence angle reception. To validate the proposed strategy, a bandwidth-enhanced metasurface with different through-via positions and a DC combining network consisting of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 2$</tex-math> </inline-formula> rectifiers has been designed, fabricated, and measured. At normal incidence and an incident power density of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16.4~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, the measured total harvesting efficiency (RF–DC) remains above 27.2% (TE) and 26.6% (TM) at 3.425–3.7 GHz, corresponding to a relative bandwidth (RBW) of 7.72%. At the maximum incidence angle of 75°, the peak total efficiency reaches 19.9% under TE polarization and 24.5% under TM polarization. The proposed harvester has high efficiency, bandwidth enhancement, and wide incidence angle reception characteristics, which are expected to provide supplementary power to wireless sensing nodes in the complex electromagnetic (EM) environment.

title: Oil Temperature Estimation for Preheating Optimization in Air Conditioner Compressor Drives
authors: Anton Dianov, Jiawei Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Air conditioners are essential appliances, used for both cooling and heating, in a highly competitive market where manufacturers focus on cost reduction, functionality, and efficiency. While these areas are well explored, substantial improvements remain challenging. This paper identifies an opportunity to enhance compressor efficiency and user comfort by optimizing the preheating mode, which warms the oil before compressor startup. Air conditioning compressors typically lack lubricant temperature sensors, so conventional methods assume oil and ambient temperatures are equal. However, after a restart, the oil is often warmer, allowing reduced preheating time and energy savings. This paper proposes using existing temperature sensors, along with specialized algorithms, to estimate oil temperature and shorten preheating. The compressor’s cooling behavior is analyzed, and a model describing the relationship between lubricant, shell, and ambient temperatures is developed for various preheaters. This model enables accurate oil temperature estimation, optimizes preheating, improves system efficiency, saves energy, and enhances comfort.

title: Heart Disease Prediction Using Hybrid Quantum-Classical Machine Learning Models
authors: Pavan Reddy T, Vishwachetan D, Rajarajeswari S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases remain the leading cause of mortality globally, necessitating accurate diagnostic tools for early detection and intervention. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative and hybrid analysis of quantum and classical machine learning models for heart disease prediction using the UCI Cleveland dataset. We implement classical baseline models including Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and XGBoost, alongside quantum models: Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) with statevector fidelity kernels, Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC) with data-reuploading ansätze, and Quantum Convolutional Neural Networks (QCNN) with parametric pooling. The primary contribution is the development of a hybrid quantum classical ensemble meta-classifier that fuses predictions from all classical and quantum learners using XGBoost as a meta learner. Experiments conducted in Google Colab using Qiskit 1.2+ and Penny Lane 0.36+ demonstrate that the proposed hybrid ensemble achieves superior performance with 98.33accuracy, 100perfect AUC-ROC of 1.0, substantially outperforming standalone classical models (best: 88.33(best: 80hybrid model’s superiority. These results underscore the potential of quantum feature spaces combined with classical decision boundaries in healthcare prediction systems, and demonstrate the immediate utility of hybrid quantum-classical integration in medical diagnostics on near-term quantum hardware.

title: Service Orchestration in the Computing Continuum: Structural Challenges and Vision
authors: Boris Sedlak, Víctor Casamayor Pujol, Ildefons Magrans de Abril, Praveen Kumar Donta, Adel N. Toosi, Schahram Dustdar
publish: IEEE Internet Computing
abstract: The computing continuum (CC) integrates different layers of processing infrastructure, from edge to cloud, to optimize service quality through ubiquitous and reliable computation. Compared to central architectures, however, heterogeneous and dynamic infrastructure increases the complexity for service orchestration. To guide research, this article first summarizes structural problems of the CC, and then, envisions an ideal solution for autonomous service orchestration across the CC. As one instantiation, we show how active inference—a concept from neuroscience—can support self-organizing services in continuously interpreting their environment to optimize service quality. Still, we conclude that no existing solution achieves our vision, but that research on service orchestration faces several structural challenges. Most notably: to provide standardized simulation and evaluation environments for comparing the performance of orchestration mechanisms. Together, the challenges outline a research roadmap toward resilient and scalable service orchestration in the CC.

title: Scene-adaptive Camera Network Construction for Efficient Nap-of-the-Object Photogrammetry
authors: Ke Xi, Pengjie Tao, Zhuangqun Niu, Qi Chen, Jianan He, Zheng Ji, Yansong Duan, Tao Ke, Zuxun Zhang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Advances in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) technology have significantly lowered the barriers to achieving close-range photogrammetry through aerial imaging, enabling the widespread application of object-level real-scene 3D modeling in large-scale scenarios. However, existing photogrammetric camera network construction methods for object-level 3D modeling lack explicit constraints on the regularity of UAV shooting points, resulting in frequent start-stop cycles and a reliance on manual operations. This significantly reduces the efficiency of UAV data collection and demonstrates suboptimal performance in balancing modeling accuracy and flight efficiency. To address this, building on the framework of our previously proposed concept of the nap-of-the-object photogrammetry, we propose a scene-adaptive camera network construction method aimed at improving the time and energy efficiency of UAV photography in ultra-fine (i.e., millimeter-level) 3D modeling. Using a pre-acquired coarse point cloud of the target scene, we divide the scene into supervoxels and perform an initial clustering into several regions. Then, we apply a Markov random field (MRF) optimization to refine the point cloud segmentation, approximating each segmented region as a plane while minimizing their number. Finally, strip-wise camera network construction is applied to each segmented region to generate the final camera network. Experimental results on two buildings at Wuhan University demonstrate that, compared to existing methods, our approach achieves high-precision detailed modeling while improving the time and energy efficiency, and also outperforms other relative methods in balancing modeling precision and flight efficiency.

title: Stochastic Computing Circuits for Nonlinear Functions Based on Multiplexers
authors: Yongqiang Zhang, Defang Meng, Jie Han, Shaowei Wang, Guangjun Xie, Xin Cheng
publish: IEEE Embedded Systems Letters
abstract: Stochastic computing (SC) is a renewing computing paradigm that differs from weighted binary ones. It operates on binary stochastic bitstreams, or called stochastic numbers (SNs), instead of binary numbers (BNs). This work proposes circuits based on multiplexers (MUXs) to address the issue of SC in computing nonlinear functions. These circuits significantly reduce the number of required stochastic number generators (SNGs) and shorten critical path delay (CPD), thereby lowering hardware costs. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed designs outperform existing designs in both computing accuracy and hardware overhead. For example, the proposed design for sin(x) using a 3rd-order Maclaurin series achieves averaged reductions of 66.84% in mean square error (MSE) and 24.62% in power-delay product (PDP), respectively, compared with previous designs.

title: A High-Power Ku -Band PA Using Multi-Combiner Architecture
authors: Yiting Zhang, Nengxu Zhu, Fanyi Meng
publish: IEEE Nanotechnology Magazine
abstract: This paper presents a Ku-band multi-combined power amplifier (PA) implemented in 130-nm CMOS SOI technology for LEO satellite communication phased arrays. To overcome CMOS integration challenges while attaining watt-level output, the design incorporates two key techniques: a triple-cascode topology and a low-loss, multi-stage power combiner. The triple-cascode structure utilizes RF-grounded symmetrical stacking to eliminate external gate capacitors, thereby enhancing voltage gain, output power, and robustness against input imbalance. The multi-stage combiner efficiently aggregates power from 32 unit cells by integrating four-way transformers with strip-line and transmission-line networks, achieving low insertion loss within a compact footprint. Experimental results show a 3-dB bandwidth of 10.5–14.5 GHz, a saturated output power (Psat) of 30.8 dBm (1.2 W), and a peak power-added efficiency (PAE) of 19.9%. The proposed PA achieves superior output power among state-of-the-art 8–18 GHz CMOS PAs, demonstrating its strong potential for next-generation space-borne transmitters.

title: A Bad Data Identification Method Based on Optimal Weight-Tuning for Power System State Estimation
authors: Bowen Hu, Yadong Zhou, Sizhe He, Yujie Yang, Yang Liu, Ting Liu, Xiaohong Guan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: The weighted least squares (WLS) method has been widely used in power systems for state estimation (SE), but in the presence of gross errors, the results of WLS SE can be significantly biased. While various bad data identification (BDI) methods exist, identifying multiple bad data for SE is still challenging, especially when bad data located at leverage points can disproportionately corrupt SE accuracy. This paper proposes an Optimal-Weighted Least Squares State Estimation (OWLS-SE) method with an optimal weight-tuning strategy to address these challenges. The impact of bad data at leverage points on estimation errors is first mathematically demonstrated through a theoretical upper bound of the SE relative error. Then, the optimal dynamic weights are developed to minimize this error bound by quantifying leverage point impacts and integrating them into a multi-objective optimization framework. The proposed OWLS-SE achieves simultaneous SE and BDI within a single optimization model, eliminating conventional two-stage processing. Numerical experiments on the 6- bus system, IEEE 30-, 118- bus systems, and the 2869- bus PEGASE system demonstrate that OWLS-SE achieves superior BDI and SE accuracy with robust performance even when multiple gross errors occur at leverage points, while maintaining computational efficiency comparable to the simplest WLS method.

title: Research on the application of prior state vector and covariance matrix in GPS PPP time comparison
authors: Jihai Zhang, Zhiling Ren, Yu Zhang, Hong Zhang, Shaowu Dong, Haibo Yuan, Yiheng Wang
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine
abstract: In precise point positioning (PPP) high-precision time comparison, the estimated receiver clock bias exhibits discontinuity at day boundary. Usually, the time comparison between two time keeping laboratories requires long-term and continuous in practical applications. Therefore, the discontinuous receiver clock bias cannot truly reflect the time difference between two time keeping laboratories, and the performance evaluation and synchronization control of the time comparison were impacted. In this paper, the correlation of state vector and covariance matrix between epochs in PPP data processing was used, the state vector and covariance matrix from the final epoch of the preceding day were used as the prior values for the first epoch on the subsequent day, the convergence time of the initial estimate for the next day was reduced, and the accuracy of the time comparison of clock bias was improved. Relying on the PPP time comparison links among major international time keeping laboratories, the prior information of state vector and covariance matrix were used in PPP time comparison estimation algorithm, and the clock bias discontinuity at the day boundary has been improved effectively. The results show that the PPP time comparison technology is used between different continents, and the state vector and covariance matrix from the final epoch of the preceding day were used as the prior values for the first epoch on the subsequent day, the clock bias of multi-day continuous estimation discontinuity at the day boundary is improved compared with the single-day estimation, the variation of clock bias between epochs at day boundary has decreased significantly, the Allan deviation (ADEV) and Time deviation (TDEV) values of clock bias have decreased, and the performance of ADEV and TDEV have increased by more than 30%.

title: Physics-Informed Prostate MR–US Registration by Biomechanical Fields Prediction Network
authors: Shixing Ma, Zhaoxi Lin, Xinzhe Du, Yipeng Hu, Zhe Min
