title: Secure Optimization with Asynchronous Structured Skyline Predicates under Vertical Data Federation
authors: Yu Chen, Rongmao Chen, Shaojing Fu, Mingwu Zhang, Yuexiang Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Skyline optimization is a powerful tool for filtering prominent data to support analysis and decision-making. However, traditional centralized skyline predicates are inadequate for contemporary data islands, and shallow data federation poses a threat to privacy with sensitive data. In existing distributed environments, achieving both efficiency and security in skyline computation remains a critical challenge. This paper addresses the challenge of performing secure skyline predicates on encrypted data federation while safeguarding both the dataset and skyline from unauthorized access. We propose a novel asynchronous structured skyline predicate based on vertical dominance and truth-value conversion, taking full advantage of distributed computing. Furthermore, we introduce a secure optimization that balances security and efficiency, thereby facilitating a distributed skyline predicate. We evaluate the efficiency and scalability across various parameters, demonstrating improvements in traversal overhead and expensive ciphertext operations.

title: An Ultrawideband and Low-Profile Phased Array Design for Satellite-assisted Internet of Things Vehicular Communication
authors: Liangliang Zhao, Hailiang Zhu, Aidong Li, Lehu Wen, Shi-Tong Wang, Kai-Cheng Wang, Huiling Zhao, Chufeng Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This article presents a low-profile dual-polarized phased array antenna featuring an improved feed structure and a multiresonant wide-angle impedance matching (WAIM) layer for satellite-assisted Internet-of-Things (SATA-IoT) vehicular communications across the L-, S-, C-, and X-bands. First, an improved feed structure based on a double-sided multi-notch balun (DMB) is developed to provide stable impedance transformation and tailored field distribution, thereby enhancing impedance matching in the low- and mid-frequency bands. Second, a planar nonuniform metasurface (PNM)-based multiresonant WAIM layer is introduced. By properly engineering its effective permittivity and permeability, two desired resonances together with inductive coupling are achieved, enabling wide-angle scanning performance in the high-frequency band. These two key techniques jointly allow the proposed array to realize ultrawide bandwidth, wide-angle scanning capability, and a low-profile configuration. To validate the design, a 5×5 prototype was fabricated and measured. The measured results show a 9:1 impedance bandwidth from 1.4 to 12.6 GHz. In particular, both array configurations exhibit an active VSWR below 3 at broadside, and maintain active VSWR below 3.3 for scan angles up to ±70° across the entire operating band. The overall profile is only 0.075 λlow. These results demonstrate that the proposed array is a promising candidate for SATA-IoT and related vehicular IoT communication systems.

title: Decoupled Seg Tokens Make Stronger Reasoning Video Segmenter and Grounder
authors: Jisheng Dang, Xudong Wu, Haowen Yan, Huicheng Zheng, Wei-Shi Zheng, Jianhuang Lai, Bin Hu, Tat-Seng Chua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Existing video segmenter and grounder approaches, exemplified by Sa2VA, directly fuse features within segmentation models. This often results in an undesirable entanglement of dynamic visual information and static semantics, thereby degrading segmentation accuracy. To systematically mitigate these issues, we propose DeSa2VA, a decoupling-enhanced prompting scheme integrating text pre-training and a linear decoupling module to address the information processing limitations inherent in SAM-2. Specifically, first, we devise a pre-training paradigm that converts textual ground-truth labels into point-level prompts while generating corresponding text masks. These masks are refined through a hybrid loss function to strengthen the model's semantic grounding capabilities. Next, we employ linear projection to disentangle hidden states generated by a large language model into distinct textual and visual feature subspaces. Finally, a dynamic mask fusion strategy synergistically combines these decoupled features through triple supervision from predicted text/visual masks and ground-truth annotations. Extensive experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art performance across diverse tasks, including image segmentation, image question answering, video segmentation, and video question answering. Our codes are available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/longmalongma/DeSa2VA</uri>.

title: A 210-GHz Planar Balanced Third-harmonic Mixer Based on Dual-band Rat-race Coupler
authors: Zi'ang Xu, Junjie Zhang, Zihan Zhang, Feng Xu, Jian Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Terahertz Science and Technology
abstract: This article presents a 210-GHz planar balanced third-harmonic mixer with a dual-band rat-race coupler as the core of balanced configuration. Theoretical analyses and simulations validate the dual-band characteristics of the rat-race coupler and its feasibility as a 180° hybrid in third-harmonic mixing. It mitigates fabrication tolerances, enabling implementation on a quartz substrate and hybrid integration with flip-chip quasi-vertical Schottky barrier diodes (SBDs) for improved conversion loss and reduced local oscillator (LO) power requirement. An analytical validation shows that third-harmonic mixing achieves effective frequency conversion performance with inherent suppression of most spurious components. Integrated filters further mitigate spurious leakage, recycling spurious power and decreasing susceptibility to out-of-band impedance variations. Measurements demonstrate a down-conversion loss of 13∼16 dB across 196∼223 GHz and an up-conversion loss of 10∼16 dB across 198∼227 GHz with a LO drive of 8 dBm at 67.3 GHz. In-band spurious suppression exceeding 25 dBc with radio frequency (RF) input power of -10 dBm ensures a relatively pure intermediate frequency (IF) spectrum in down-conversion mode. The planar topology, which is scalable to higher frequencies, obviates the need for waveguide-based packaging, thus facilitating simplified system integration and cost reduction. By achieving superior performance comparable to, or even better than waveguide-based mixers and the integrability advantage identical to planar MMIC technologies, the proposed mixer is well-suited for compact systems in THz applications.

title: Reinforcement Learning Neural Network Observer-Based Adaptive Optimal Time-Varying Formation Control for Uncertain Multi-agent Systems With External Disturbance
authors: Yanzhou Li, Shenghuang He, Bangquan Xie, Wenjian Zhong, Yongkang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The traditional formation control methods of multi-agent systems (MASs) often rely on restrictive linear matrix inequalities and strong assumptions. To overcome this limitation, this work proposes an adaptive optimal time-varying formation control protocol for uncertain MASs, integrating reinforcement learning (RL)-based neural network observer. First, a radial basis function neural network (RBFNN) with adaptive law is designed to approximate the unknown nonlinear dynamics. An RBFNN-based state estimator and a novel disturbance estimator are developed to reconstruct the unmeasurable system state and the disturbance generated by an exosystem, respectively. Second, using the state estimate, a distributed formation control error system is established, and a performance index based on a Hamilton–Jacobi–Bellman (HJB) equation is introduced to achieve the objective of optimal formation control. Third, leveraging the state and disturbance estimates, an actor-critic RL-based optimal time-varying formation control protocol with adaptive update laws is proposed. Actor and critic networks are constructed to approximate unknown nonlinear terms in HJB equation. It is proved that estimator error systems and RBFNN approximation errors are semi-globally uniformly ultimately bounded (SGUUB). Simulation examples are provided to verify the effectiveness of the proposed estimators and optimal formation control protocol. Moreover, the method is successfully applied to unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) formation control in the Isaac Sim simulation environment, demonstrating the method’s practical feasibility.

title: Style Backdoor: A Robust Style-Decoupled Clean-Label Backdoor Attack
authors: Yanchun Li, Jianghui Bi, Shujuan Tian, Xingxia Dai, Saiqin Long
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Backdoor attacks on deep models have received increasing attention from researchers due to their stealth and destructiveness, leading to the proposal of numerous attack methods. Among these attack approaches, clean-label attacks exhibit superior concealment and practicality because they can bypass manual checks. However, current clean-label triggers are usually independent of the original image and rely on simple patterns, making them easy to detect and recognize. To address these issues, we propose a novel clean-label backdoor attack framework that systematically decouples style and content via a style transfer network, leveraging style features as stealthy triggers to achieve semantic consistency and evade advanced defense mechanisms. Specifically, utilizing a style predictor network to achieve precise extraction and effective separation of image styles, we have carefully trained a style trigger generator that combines them with predefined style features and image content, thereby constructing style triggers highly correlated with the image. To further enhance the attack's aggressiveness and stealthiness, we introduce a consistency constraint composed of style loss and content loss, which encourages poisoned images to have a similar style to the style trigger and the same content as the clean images. Comprehensive experiments on standard benchmark datasets have demonstrated that our style backdoor attack achieves nearly perfect success rates on poisoned samples, with a minimal impact on the accuracy of benign samples, and is capable of bypassing almost all state-of-the-art backdoor defenses.

title: Field Emission From Polymers Relevant to Anode-Initiated Flashover Development
authors: Raimi Clark, Colin Lynch, Luke Boswell, Jacob Stephens, Adam Steiner, Andreas Neuber
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Pulsed electron field emission is measured from an assortment of plastic insulators with various surface preparations. The emission is markedly stochastic in nature. Generally, there is a spatially diffuse, baseline level of emission which is observed after a few repeated pulses on a sample. Additionally, localized, highly emissive sites contribute sporadically, with the estimated current for those excitations sometimes exceeding the baseline emission by orders of magnitude. Under 40 ns pulse excitation, Rexolite® produces measurable currents spanning from tens of nA up to hundreds of μA. These measurements mark a first step towards understanding initial plasma formation in anode-initiated vacuum flashover.

title: High Efficient and Near-Optimal Binary Computation Offloading Strategy Based on Game Theory and Greedy Optimization
authors: Lipei Liu, Rugui Yao, Xiaoya Zuo, Aris Karampelas Timotijevic, Ye Fan, Lin Yu, Theodoros A. Tsiftsis
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) is considered as a promising paradigm to overcome the computational constraints of mobile devices by offloading intensive tasks to nearby edge servers. As the number of users in the MEC network increases, users inevitably compete for limited wireless and computing resources, leading to a substantial escalation in the complexity of network resource allocation. Motivated by this challenge, this paper focuses on a multi-user binary computation offloading system in an MEC environment over quasi-static competitive wireless channels. We propose a non-cooperative game model, where user devices strategically optimize their offloading decisions to minimize total costs in terms of latency and energy consumption. Building upon this, the existence and feasibility of a Nash equilibrium is rigorously proved, thereby ensuring stability within the system. Furthermore, a distributed computation offloading algorithm is proposed based on game optimization, which enables user devices to adaptively attain balanced offloading strategies with minimal computational overhead. Extensive simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm, demonstrating that it achieves near-optimal performance compared with the centralized optimization methods while avoiding additional server load or the need for user-specific configuration.

title: Edge-Based Vision-Language Assistive System for the Visually Impaired: A Quantized VLM Approach
authors: Batyr Arystanbekov, Huseyin Atakan Varol, Adnan Yazici
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: This paper presents a novel edge-deployable assistive system for visually impaired individuals, powered by vision-language models (VLMs). Existing cloud-based solutions for image captioning suffer from latency, dependency on internet connectivity, and overly simplistic scene descriptions that fail to convey the rich contextual information needed for meaningful real-world navigation. To address these challenges, we propose a self-contained, wearable system that performs real-time scene interpretation on-device without cloud reliance. The system integrates a quantized version of the LLaVA-NeXT-13B VLM with speech recognition (Whisper) and speech synthesis (PIPER), running on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin NX and Raspberry Pi-based input module. Our framework emphasizes intuitive user interaction through a button-based interface and Bluetooth audio output, minimizing cognitive load. We validate the quantization approach through large-scale benchmarks, such as VizWiz-VQA and VQAv2, demonstrating minimal accuracy degradation (2.6% and 0.9%, respectively) compared to the original model. User evaluations involving 28 participants compared the system to a baseline image captioning model. Objective results demonstrated a 25% increase in image identification accuracy. The system achieved high usability scores and maintained practical latency (4–5 seconds per query), supporting real-world feasibility. This work advances the development of scalable, interpretable, and accessible AI-driven assistive technologies, enabling greater independence and interaction for the visually impaired.

title: M2RU: Memristive Minion Recurrent Unit for On-Chip Continual Learning at the Edge
authors: Abdullah M. Zyarah, Dhireesha Kudithipudi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: Continual learning on edge platforms remains challenging because recurrent networks depend on energy-intensive training procedures and frequent data movement that are impractical for embedded deployments. This work introduces M2RU, a mixed-signal architecture that implements the minion recurrent unit for efficient temporal processing with on-chip continual learning. The architecture integrates weighted-bit streaming, which enables multi-bit digital inputs to be processed in crossbars without high-resolution conversion, and an experience replay mechanism that stabilizes learning under domain shifts. M2RU achieves ~13 GOPS at 16.76 mW, corresponding to 776 GOPS per watt, and maintains accuracy within 5 percent of software baselines on sequential MNIST, CIFAR-10, and Google Speech Commands tasks. Compared with a CMOS digital design, the accelerator provides 25× improvement in energy efficiency. Device-aware analysis shows an expected operational lifetime of 12.2 years under continual learning workloads. These results establish M2RU as a scalable and energy-efficient platform for real-time adaptation in edge-level temporal intelligence.

title: NEMESI: 5G Non-invasive EMF Multi-user Exposure Surveillance and Inspection
authors: Daniele Franci, Ivan Palama, Stefano Mangione, Sara Adda, Mattia Vaccarono, Giuseppe Bianchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Surveillance of electromagnetic field exposure in 5G networks is crucial for regulatory compliance but currently relies on intrusive and expensive active traffic injection. We propose a hardware-agnostic, automated, and non-invasive framework for exposure assessment in real-world deployments. By leveraging a blind Downlink Control Information (DCI) decoding mechanism, our method extracts scheduling information to isolate user-specific Physical Downlink Shared Channel (PDSCH) resources. Experimental validation in frequency range (FR)1 and FR2 demonstrates that this approach enables a procedural mapping of radiated power while obviating the need for full carrier bandwidth saturation. By abstracting the acquisition hardware as an IQ sample provider, the pipeline is designed to operate across heterogeneous receivers, including low-cost Software-Defined Radios (SDRs). While absolute metrological accuracy remains dependent on specific device calibration, this methodology supports a streamlined, user-specific Maximum-Power Extrapolation (MPE) analysis workflow at the signal-processing level that is independent of network traffic conditions.

title: Current Stress Optimization Control of Dual Active Bridge DC-DC Converter Based on Dual Integral Sliding Mode Control
authors: Xinrui Pei, Fei Yu, Jiaming Qi, Xiaoqian Yue, Mingren Wu, Jie Li, Jinjin Zan
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: As clean energy gradually replaces traditional energy, the dual active bridge DC-DC converter, as an important component of DC microgrids, is prone to issues such as slow dynamic response and reduced efficiency when subjected to disturbances. In this paper, by optimizing the combination relationship of current stress under extended phase shift modulation, a reduced-order model of the DAB converter is established, and an optimal control strategy integrating dualintegral sliding mode control and minimum current stress is proposed. Simulation results show that, compared with traditional PI control, the proposed control strategy can effectively reduce current stress and effectively improve the system dynamic performance.

title: A Multi-Objective Operational Management Approach for Photovoltaic Charging Stations Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xingyu Shen, Kai Liao, Jianwei Yang, Zhengyou He
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Coupling photovoltaic (PV) generation with energy storage significantly enhances the economic and environmental performance of service area charging stations. Nevertheless, the volatility of PV output and the uncertainty of electric vehicles (EV) charging demands lead to conflicts among multiple objectives, including operating costs, user satisfaction, and PV consumption. Consequently, this paper presents a Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) based multi-objective energy management method for PV-storage charging stations. First, a charging event generation model based on adversarial networks is established. Second, a deep reinforcement learning approach utilizing TD3 is developed, enabling real-time optimal energy dispatching. Finally, the comparison results show that the proposed method outperforms the benchmark algorithms, successfully achieving a real-time optimal balance among multiple objectives.

title: Optimization Model of Investment Benefit for Power Grid Enterprises Based on Improved Genetic Algorithm
authors: Weiyang You, Xiuna Wang, Gang Yu, Ling Wei, Lei Zhu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of single objective setting, low optimization accuracy and premature convergence of standard genetic algorithm (GA) in the investment decisionmaking of power grid enterprises, this paper proposes an investment benefit optimization model based on improved genetic algorithm. Firstly, combined with the operational characteristics and regulatory requirements of power grid enterprises, a multiobjective optimization framework is constructed, which takes economic benefit maximization, power grid reliability improvement and social service benefit enhancement as core objectives, and integrates investment quota limit, grid structure constraints and policy compliance as constraint conditions. Secondly, aiming at the defects of standard GA such as poor local search ability and easy to fall into local optimal solution, an improved genetic algorithm is designed by introducing adaptive crossover-mutation operator, elite retention strategy and adaptive weight adjustment mechanism of fitness function. Finally, the simulation verification is carried out based on the actual investment data of a regional power grid enterprise.

title: Prototype Development of an Integrated Distributed Energy-Dissipating Modular Multi-Level Converter Based on 6.5kV IGBT
authors: Yiliang Xu, Yuebin Zhou, Wanyu Cao, Liu Yang, Yue Wu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To address the AC fault ride-through of voltage source converter-based high voltage direct current (VSC-HVDC) systems, a submodule was developed using <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6.5 \text{kV} / 3 \text{kA}$</tex> IGBT power devices. The topology and control methods of the submodule are introduced, and its control principles are verified through system simulation. The insulation, thermal field, magnetic field strength, and busbar electrodynamic force of the submodule are confirmed through multi-physics simulation, ensuring the design's reasonableness. Finally, experimental tests verify the submodule's current-carrying capacity and power dissipation capacity. The results show that the submodule with the power dissipation function can regulate the submodule capacitor voltage within set parameters, ensuring the converter's normal operation during fault ride-through.

title: Precision-Aware Lightweight Deep Learning Framework for Automated Skin Cancer Classification from Dermoscopic Images
authors: Goldy Verma, Komuravelly Sudheer Kumar, Pankaj Negi, Pawan Kumar Mishra, Neha Sharma, Rupesh Gupta
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Skin cancer is among the most frequent and deadlier conditions in the field of dermatology, and early and correct diagnosis is of much significance in enhancing the rates of survival among the patients. In this work, the systematic review of the literature shows that automated classification of skin cancer based on dermoscopic images with the help of a deep learning-based system has been developed effectively. A narrowed down version of MobileNetV2 is used to classify both the benign and malignant skin lesions with low computational complexity. The methodology suggested allows combining efficient data preprocessing, augmentation, transfer learning, and optimal fine-tuning to improve the feature representation and generalization ability. A publicly available dataset of dermoscopic images was experimentally evaluated and the model performance evaluated in terms of standard evaluation measures. The average classification accuracy of the proposed approach is 96% which is a better result than some commonly used deep learning models. Strong learning and low overfitting are observed by the close correspondence between training and validation results. The proposed model is also easily scalable to work with clinical decision support systems and resource-constrained healthcare settings and is lightweight, which makes it suitable to the work of a real-time system because of high accuracy and low resource requirements.

title: Research on the Oscillation Mechanism of Induction Motor V/f System
authors: Min Wu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper investigates the three primary causes of instability in induction motor V/f control systems: the inherent unstable regions at medium-to-low frequencies, resonance within the DC-link filter, and the dead-time effect of the inverter. By establishing a mathematical model of the inverter-fed induction motor system, the study analyzes how various parameters-such as motor constants and DC-side inductance/capacitance-affect the system's inherent stability. Through impedance analysis, the paper clarifies the mechanism of oscillations triggered by energy exchange between the motor and the DC-link components. Additionally, it examines how error voltages and zero-current clamping caused by dead-time effects lead to discontinuous power supply and subsequent system oscillations. This research provides a theoretical foundation for improving the stability and performance of V/f speed control systems.

title: Emotion Detection From Facial Expressions using AI and ML
authors: J. Karthika, Kuncham Lalitesh Sampath Vinay, Kuruva Siva Kumar, M. Vanathi, B. Sandhiya, R.S. Amshavalli
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Real-time emotion recognition enhances user interaction by allowing systems to interpret and respond to facial cues instantly. The suggested system approach combines deep learning with traditional machine-learning methods to achieve reliable emotion detection in changing environments. The central part of the system is MobileNet convolutional neural network that was chosen because it balances speed and accuracy well hence the suitability of real time use. Thereafter, one more model, a Random Forest (RF) classifier, is included and trained on manually identified features, including facial landmarks and gradient-based features. This is a hybrid approach that makes the model more robust and allows flexibility in how it is deployed in different computing environments. The application is implemented in Flask web framework and takes live video streams recorded with the help of a webcam. It extracts significant facial features and classifies expressions into categories such as happiness, sadness, anger, surprise, and neutrality. Flask responds to and delivers model predictions to the front end without complications, and the response is fast. The system can be used in the mood-adaptive system, educational tools, and personalized marketing platforms because it provides highperformance and real-time emotional feedback through a hybrid model that merges deep-learning strategies with conventional classification algorithms.

title: Explainable AI Framework for Transparent and Fair Recruitment Decision-Making in Automated HR Systems
authors: Revati Ramrao Rautrao, M. Shailaja, N. Roopalatha, K. Kalaivani, Feras Almarashdi, M. Karthik
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Human Resource hiring using Artificial Intelligence has revolutionized the traditional hiring practices, one that has not only increased the effectiveness of candidate evaluation and the efficiency of candidate selection. Current literature where graph-based and deep learning models are used is characterized by fairness, explainability, and biases mitigation challenges, which often result in opaque decisions that are difficult to trust. The suggested HR-GNN (Human Resource Graph Neural Network), which takes advantage of graph neural networks, represents the connection among candidates, job descriptions, and skills with fairness restrictions and SHAP-based clarifying modules. The methodology is implemented in Python and builds on heterogeneous graphs, which are created and the results produced are fed to relational learning to enhance both prediction accuracy and transparency. HR-GNN has an accuracy of 92.5% and F1-score, precision, fairness, and explainability of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91.9,0.92,0.72$</tex>, and 0.74 respectively, compared to traditional machine learning (78.5% accuracy) and deep learning (85.2% accuracy). The presented framework will allow HR professionals to make ethical, transparent, and biasconscience hiring choices to help them comply with the regulations, enhance trust in an organization, and enable the responsible use of AI-based recruitment systems in the actual HR setting.

title: An Ideal Approach to 3D NAND Flash Design for Ultra-High IOPS SSDs (>100M) with Low-Latency (<=us), Fine-Grained (Page Size=512B), and a Novel MB-level Embedded DRAM to support On-Chip NAND Managements
authors: Hang-Ting Lue, Teng-Hao Yeh, Chin-Shan Yuan, Keh-Chung Wang, Chih-Yuan Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: Recently, there has been a growing demand for ultra-high IOPS (Input/Output Operations Per Second) SSDs (Solid-State Drives) with fine granularity (small page size <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=512 ~\mathrm{B}$</tex>) to meet the requirements of various AI computing applications. Beyond the commonly referenced “low-latency” SLC NAND design, this paper proposes several solutions to further enhance the performance of nextgeneration SSDs: (1) An ultra-small plane design utilizing “CuA+CbA” (CMOS under Array and CMOS bonded Array) co-integration to optimize layout efficiency of minimized word line and bit line lengths; (2) Small block size <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(<1 \text{MB})$</tex> to reduce WL capacitance loading, enabling faster WL setup times; (3) Double-pitch M1 bit lines (BL) combined with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1} \boldsymbol{/} \mathbf{4}$</tex> period page buffer (PB) sensing circuits to support true Page_Size=512B small granularity for each plane; (4) We develop a MB-level embedded DRAM inside NAND Flash chip using 3T1C cell without process change, where the transistors are realized from existing peripheral low-voltage NMOS devices, and the capacitors (a few fF) are constructed using existing through-array vias (TAV). This internal eDRAM serves as a versatile cache, supporting on-chip managements such as write buffering and block address mapping tables. We anticipate that these innovative design techniques can improve the 3D NAND technology for Memory-Class Storage (MCS).

title: Orientation–Frequency Fields and Periodic Prior Reranking for Robust Cross-Modal Tire Indentation Retrieval
authors: Xiaosong Hu, Ziqi Sun, Ying Liu, Zhijie Xu, Yushan Pan, Nan Xiang, Weidong Zhang, Yu Hao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Cross-modal tire indentation retrieval refers to the task of retrieving corresponding tire surface images from a gallery using indentation images as queries. This task is inherently challenging due to factors such as low contrast, directional misalignment, local deformation, and other environmental disturbances. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel framework that integrates representation learning with geometric priors to enhance matching robustness and accuracy. During training, Orientation-Frequency Fields (OFF) are employed to guide the backbone network in capturing discriminative features related to tread orientation and pitch. During inference, a Periodic Prior Reranking (PPR) module is introduced, which performs normalization, period estimation, cyclic crosscorrelation for phase alignment, and period-wise tokenization for structural consistency verification, with the results fused through similarity scoring. To improve practical applicability, a phase-correlation-based coarse filtering module is incorporated to reduce the candidate search space. In addition, feature and periodic caches are adopted to eliminate redundant computations during retrieval. Finally, mask fallback, de-duplication, and tail completion strategies are integrated to further enhance robustness under challenging real-world conditions. On the test dataset, the proposed method achieves a Hit@10 accuracy of 75.81, outperforming the baseline model by a margin of 5.59. These results demonstrate that the proposed framework provides an effective solution for cross-modal tire indentation retrieval.

title: Multi-Path Regulation of Regenerative Braking Energy for Electric Drive Tracked Vehicles
authors: Zifan Song, Jiangtao Gai, Yaoheng Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Under severe operating conditions such as highspeed emergency braking and sustained downhill braking, electric tracked vehicles are highly susceptible to thermal fade of the mechanical brakes caused by excessive frictional loading. Although regenerative braking can effectively alleviate the mechanical braking burden, the limited charging power capacity of the onboard battery pack prevents the regenerated electrical energy from being adequately absorbed and dissipated, readily leading to overvoltage on the high-voltage DC bus and consequently preventing the electric brake from fully exploiting its inherent braking capability. To address this issue, this paper proposes a multi-path braking energy dissipation strategy for electric tracked vehicles. The proposed approach overcomes the conventional limitation of relying solely on battery energy storage as the single absorption channel, and introduces a multidimensional energy dissipation architecture encompassing traction motor self-heating through maximum-loss drive control, active cooling fan power absorption, and engine compression braking. Simulation results demonstrate that, with the multi-path dissipation mechanism engaged, the system is capable of safely absorbing hundreds of kilowatts of excess regenerative power, substantially relieving the constraints on electric braking output and reducing the thermal loading on the mechanical brakes. This study effectively extends the energy absorption boundary of the vehicle electrical system, fully unlocks the braking potential of the electric drivetrain platform, and significantly enhances both the electromechanical compound braking safety and the electrical network stability under extreme operating conditions.

title: Communication System for the Deaf and Mute
authors: Divya Lingineni, Prasanna DRL, Revanth Kumar Addanki, SaiPraneetha Singarapu, Jaya Bharath Reddy
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The paper proposes a new and efficient full-duplex communication system that fills the gap between Deaf and Mute (D-M) and Non-Deaf and Mute (ND-M) people without the need for ND-M people to learn sign language. In contrast to other models that only support unidirectional translation or translation in a single language, this system allows for real-time bidirectional translation in multiple sign languages, namely Indian Sign Language (ISL) and American Sign Language (ASL). The system also supports Speech-to-ISL/ASL, Text-to-ISL/ASL, ASL- to-ISL, and ASL-to-Speech translation. The system is developed using Machine Learning (ML), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to provide high accuracy in gesture recognition and context-aware translation. The system is cost-effective, scalable, and can be applied in various settings such as education, healthcare, and public service sectors. The system is modular and can be easily extended to incorporate other sign languages. Experimental evaluations show over 96% accuracy with real-time responsiveness. The system and codebase are openly available to encourage replicability and wider adoption, thereby promoting digital inclusivity for the D-M community. Future enhancements include dataset expansion, full-word recognition, and collaborative deployment with accessibility-focused organizations.

title: Enhancing Offline Maritime Translation Safety: A Retrieval-Gated Generation Approach with Entity Constraints
authors: Linfeng Wu, Enjiao Zhao, Wei Zhou
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Maritime communication faces a critical dilemma: it demands a near-zero tolerance for errors to ensure safety, yet relies on offline edge deployment where computational resources are strictly limited. To resolve this conflict, this paper proposes a retrieval-gated generation framework for bidirectional maritime translation. The system prioritizes a domain knowledge base for standardized phrase recall, utilizing a neural model as a fallback only when retrieval confidence is insufficient. By integrating similarity-based gating with entity-consistency verification, our method minimizes high-risk errors in numbers, headings, bearings, and distress keywords. Experiments on an in-domain dataset demonstrate that the system improves BLEU scores from 14.84 to 16.24 for Chinese-to-English translation and from 21.56 to 23.88 for English-to-Chinese translation. Furthermore, analysis of knowledge-base hit rates confirms that the proposed approach effectively balances deterministic safety with the efficiency constraints of edge environments.

title: Multi-level In-Memory Computing with 3D Flash Memory using Activation-Time-Modulated Sequential Multi-Block Access and Tightly Integrated Current Control Cell (CC Cell)
authors: Kana Kudo, Yuta Aiba, Kazuma Hasegawa, Xu Li, Yuichi Sano, Tomoya Sanuki
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: We show that multi-level approximate search can be performed by in-memory computing using 3D flash memory. In our previous work, we proposed the in-memory computing method using the current control cell (CC cell), which is fully compatible with conventional 3D flash memory, and demonstrated 1-bit and 128-dimensional approximate search with one cell per bit. In this work, we propose a novel approximate search method of activation-time-modulated sequential multi-block access with tightly integrated CC cell. (1) To enhance accuracy, we optimized the CC cell Vth distribution tightly and maintained normal Single Level Cell (SLC) operation in the data region. (2) To perform computation with multi-level data, we applied new key data placement. Assigning key values with different bit depths to each cell, we modulate the activation time according to the bit depths. The linearity between activation time and the number of multi block accesses allows bit depth representation. Suppressing the increase in storage capacity enables a large amount of source data to be stored as key vectors. We successfully demonstrated approximate search with sufficient accuracy using 3-bit and 128-dimensional data. Additionally, energy consumption is significantly reduced when compared to the normal TLC read operation, and no additional WL control circuit is required. This technology is a promising candidate for energy harvesting in-memory computing of multi-level data using conventional 3D flash memory.

title: MEMS-Based Gas Sensing via Broadband Dielectric Relaxation Spectroscopy
authors: Satish K. Verma, Mahesh Menon, Aravind N. Warrier, Andreas Mrosk, Markus Gardill, Harald Schenk
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Reliable and selective gas sensing demands a low-cost, energy-efficient, single-unit multi-gas sensor robust to ambient condition variations. This paper presents a novel broadband dielectric spectroscopy-based gas sensing platform. The sensor leverages strong localized electric fields (> 106 V/m) across microscale gaps under a hybrid bias (0.5 V ac + 5 V dc), inducing polarization within the gas-domain at ambient temperature. It employs MEMS based parallel comb electrodes for the detection and characterization of gas-phase analytes, demonstrated here with acetone vapor. An acetone specific loss peak at ∼350 Hz, attributed to gas-phase dielectric relaxation, scales with acetone concentration with sensitivity of 33.7m/ppm. This mechanism is promising for a miniaturized, low power, label-free, frequency-resolved dielectric sensing framework, scalable for multi-analyte, CMOS-integrated, chip-scale sensor systems.

title: Lightweight Pause Detector-Based ECG Sensing Device for Intelligent Cardiac Health Monitoring
authors: Achinta Mondal, M. Sabarimalai Manikandan, Ram Bilas Pachori
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Sick sinus syndrome and heart blocks are types of heart rhythm disorders (arrhythmia) that are characterized by anirregular rhythm associated with pauses in the electrocardiogram (ECG) signal. The pauses in the ECG signal indicate a delay or complete interruption of electrical impulses from the atria to the ventricles, associated with life-threatening cardiac arrhythmias, with the severity and specific ECG patterns. Therefore, in this letter, we propose a lightweight automatic pause detector-based ECG sensing device by considering the resource constraints of affordable wearable cardiac health monitoring devices. The proposed pause detector consists of preprocessing, bipolar sequence addition, zero-crossing rate, and a decision rule that is integrated with a resource-constrained ECG computing platform. The proposed system achieved a sensitivity of 100% and a specificity of 99.75% across a wide variety of ECG morphologies, including local interval segments and beat-to-beat intervals. Further, evaluation results demonstrated that the pause detector can reduce the false R-peak detection rate by 86.06%-96.19%, leading to reliable R-peak detection without a search-back algorithm. On the Arduino Due (ARM Cortex-M3 CPU, 84 MHz clock, 96 kB SRAM, 512 kB flash) platform, interfacing with the ECG sensor, Bluetooth, and a Smartphone, it consumes energy of 58.85 mJ with a latency of 121 ms for processing a 9 s ECG signal. The proposed pause detector can be integrated with a resource-constrained ECG sensing device to detect the sinus node dysfunctions and heart blocks, and also improves the accuracy and reliability of R-peak detectors.

title: DT-MAEI: A Dual Timescale MARL-Embedded Embodied Intelligence Architecture to Enable AUV Cluster Network-Based Multi-Target Tracking
authors: Shengchao Zhu, Guangjie Han, Chuan Lin, Fan Yang, Qianwen Qi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: With the rapid development of underwater robotics and multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) technologies, autonomous underwater vehicle (AUV) cluster networks have emerged as a promising paradigm for smart multi-target tracking. However, mainstream intelligent algorithms are constrained by the training distribution and cannot effectively handle oceanic disturbances, particularly in MARL-based underwater target tracking. In this paper, we focus on constructing an MARL-embedded embodied intelligence architecture to enable smart and disturbance-robust underwater target tracking. Specifically, we introduce the concept of underwater multi-agent embodied intelligence and propose the Dual Timescale MARL-embedded Multi-Agent Embodied Intelligence (DT-MAEI) architecture, improving the intelligence of the AUV cluster network. For the dedicated MARL algorithm in the proposed DT-MAEI, we propose the dual timescale-based excellence influencing MARL algorithm, to enhance the convergence speed and adaptability of MARL under disturbed underwater environments. Moreover, to enhance the inter-AUV influence in the DT-MAEI, we propose an excellence-alignment reward function and provide a detailed theoretical derivation. Furthermore, we propose the DT-MAEI-based underwater target tracking scheme, to enable AUV cluster network-based smart, efficient, and disturbance-robust multi-target tracking. Extensive evaluation results showcase that our proposed approaches can perform accurate underwater target tracking and outperform some recent research products in terms of convergence speed, tracking performance.

title: Neighbor-Consistent Transformer for Domain Adaptation in Remote Sensing Scene Classification
authors: Wenchao Liu, Lingyun Chen, Jue Wang, Tianyu Wei, Guoqing Wang, Xiaodong Gong
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) has become a research hotspot in remote sensing scene classification (RSSC) to reduce dependence on labeled samples and mitigate performance degradation caused by domain shift. Most existing UDA approaches rely on convolutional neural networks (CNNs), which are constrained by limited receptive fields and struggle to capture global contextual dependencies in complex remote sensing scenes. Vision Transformers (ViTs) provide strong global modeling capability; however, their sequence-based representation lacks explicit constraints on spatial neighborhood structures, resulting in insufficient perception of fine-grained local structures, thereby causing attention misdirection under domain shift. To address this critical problem, a Neighbor Consistency Modulation (NCM) module is first designed to extract local structural consistency representations and fuse them with global tokens, explicitly enhancing fine-grained land object perception. Furthermore, to achieve reliable category-level feature alignment, an Adaptive Probability Pairwise Alignment (APPA) method is introduced. Specifically, an adaptive threshold is designed to retain reliable target pseudo-labels, and discriminator-free pairwise constraints are proposed to align these retained samples with source samples via probability matching, thereby stabilizing cross-domain representations. Ultimately, these two complementary components are seamlessly integrated to form a novel transformer-based method, termed Neighbor-Consistent Transformer (NCT). Extensive experiments on three public remote sensing datasets demonstrate the superiority of the proposed NCT, achieving state-of-the-art performance in cross-domain adaptation. The code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/CccLYChen/NCT-UDA.

title: ZAP: Zoned Architecture and Performant Compiler for Field Programmable Atom Array
authors: Chen Huang, Xi Zhao, Hongze Xu, Weifeng Zhuang, Meng-Jun Hu, Dong E. Liu, Jingbo Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: The scalability of neutral-atom quantum computing is increasingly limited by a compiler–architecture challenge: logical circuits must be mapped onto dynamically reconfigurable atom arrays while controlling crosstalk, transport overhead, and hardware constraints. To address this problem, we present ZAP, a co-designed zoned architecture and deterministic compiler for field-programmable atom arrays. ZAP partitions the array into storage and entanglement zones and combines hardware-aware ASAP-separate scheduling, look-ahead placement, and conflict-aware routing in a single-pass compilation flow, thereby avoiding the repeated global search used in prior approaches. Evaluated on structured quantum benchmarks and random 3-regular circuits, ZAP consistently delivers multi-order-of-magnitude compilation speedups while maintaining competitive or superior execution quality. Relative to ZAC and PowerMove, ZAP typically reduces compilation time from tens of seconds to below 0.1 s and achieves speedups exceeding 1,000×; relative to Enola, the speedup exceeds 10,000× on the evaluated suite. ZAP's fidelity gains are most pronounced on structured workloads with irregular connectivity and nonuniform qubit reuse, where its scheduling and placement decisions more effectively suppress crosstalk and limit transport-related loss, while on random circuits it remains competitive and preserves the same scalability advantage. These results show that hardware-structured, non-iterative compilation provides a practical path toward fast, scalable, and noise-aware neutral-atom quantum computing.

title: The Flashover characteristics and arc development patterns of train roof insulator under ultra - high altitude
authors: Yujun Guo, Ningning Su, Limei Hong, Ye Cao, Chuan Zhang, Xueqin Zhang, Guoqiang Gao, Guangning Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: With the continuous expansion of railway networks, trains are increasingly operating in extreme environments exceeding 4000 m in altitude, where roof insulators face the challenge of significantly reduced air pressure. Therefore, the flashover characteristics and arc propagation behavior of roof insulators are investigated under ultra - high altitude conditions based on tests in an artificial climate chamber. First, flashover tests were performed on four types of roof insulators with different shed structures under varying air pressure and contamination levels. Subsequently, the effects of different shed structures on flashover voltage and arc development under low - pressure conditions were analyzed. Last, the effective utilization rate of creepage distance, denoted as kE, is proposed to quantify the influence of the arc - floatation effect in ultra - high altitude environments. It is indicated that the flashover voltage of roof insulators decreases more significantly under ultra - high altitude conditions, as air pressure characteristics impact index n exhibits an initial increase followed by a decrease when the equivalent salt deposit density (ESDD) increases. The effective utilization rate of creepage distance kE decreases following a power-law trend with reducing pressure. If the creepage distance is sufficient, appropriately increasing the shed spacing and large shed diameter can mitigate the impact of the arc - floatation effect, improving insulation performance in ultra - high altitude environments. Conclusion above can provide valuable references for the design and performance evaluation of roof insulators intended for use in such demanding conditions.

title: TeleQNet: Defense-Grade Messaging with Quantum Teleportation
authors: Sandhia G K, M. Ranjani, Sandeep Raj Saravanan, Himadhri Hariharasudhan, Santhosh P
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The rapid advancement of quantum computing raises concerns about the long-term security of existing cryptographic systems. In response, this paper presents TeleQNet, a secure communication framework that combines classical encryption with quantum-inspired techniques. The system integrates Quantum Key Distribution based on the BB84 protocol and a simulated quantum teleportation mechanism implemented using Qiskit. To strengthen security, a layered encryption approach is adopted using the Hill Cipher followed by AES-256 encryption. The framework is implemented as a web-based system using React and FastAPI, with an AI-based monitoring module that tracks parameters such as teleportation fidelity, channel noise, and communication latency. Experimental results show that the system achieves high reliability, with teleportation fidelity exceeding 99%, encryption accuracy around 99.5%, and average latency below 120 ms. However, the current implementation is limited to simulation and does not involve real quantum hardware. The results indicate that combining quantum-inspired methods with classical techniques can be a practical approach for building future secure communication systems.

title: Trajectory Design of Angular Displacement for Centrifugal Actuator Based on Model Predictive Control and Research on Vehicle Lateral Vibration Reduction
authors: Haiyan Xu, Bing Xu, Yu Zhang, Yizhe Song, Yijue Sun
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Large vehicles are prone to significant vibration during driving, which impairs driving stability and the service life of components. To address this issue, this paper proposes an angular displacement trajectory optimization method based on Model Predictive Control (MPC) for effective suppression of vehicle lateral vibration. Meanwhile, combined with the structural characteristics of the body of large vehicles, a dynamic model of vehicle lateral vibration is established (ignoring the influence of wheel lateral movement), and a linear mapping relationship between the output force of the centrifugal actuator and angular displacement is clarified. The particle swarm optimization algorithm is adopted to solve the optimal angular displacement trajectory, ensuring that the actuator output accurately matches the demand for vehicle body vibration suppression. A simulation platform is built by MATLAB, with the mapping relationship between the corresponding disturbance force and angular displacement set based on actual engineering scenarios. Finally, the effect differences between passive vibration reduction and active control are compared to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy, which provides a feasible engineering technical solution for lateral vibration suppression of large vehicles.Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed MPCPSO combined control strategy effectively reduces vehicle body vibration levels, with the vibration index decreasing from 0.88 to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.57\left(~\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}\right)^{2}$</tex> and achieving a vibration suppression rate of 35.2 %-significantly outperforming traditional passive damping solutions. The optimized angular displacement trajectory exhibits continuous smoothness, fully complying with the physical constraints of centrifugal actuators. This strategy enables real-time tracking of lateral disturbance forces, effectively enhancing ride comfort and improving structural durability. The findings provide reliable theoretical foundations and technical support for engineering applications of lateral active damping technologies in large vehicles.

title: HLS Driven Obfuscation of DSP Hardware IP Designs using Non-invertible Arbitrary Function and Reconfigurable Intertwined Obfuscation Logic
authors: Anirban Sengupta, Nabendu Bhui, Vishal Chourasia, Ayush Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Digital Signal Processing (DSP) hardware intellectual property (IP) designs are pivotal to the proliferation of consumer electronics (CE) systems such as camera, camcorder, smart watch, smart TVs, IoT etc. Such DSP hardware IPs is typically designed using high level synthesis (HLS) framework. However, with the globalization of integrated circuit/system-on-chip (SoC) design supply chain, emergence of threats such as reverse engineering (RE) has become a matter of grave concern. This paper presents novel HLS-driven functional obfuscation of DSP hardware IP designs using non-invertible arbitrary function and reconfigurable intertwined obfuscation logic. The proposed approach thwarts RE attack by hindering various forms of threats such as key-sensitization attack, removal attack, different versions of SAT attack and ML attack. Results of the proposed approach revealed significantly greater obfuscation strength (causing reduced probability of attacks) than prior approaches.

title: Stepwise Dynamic Parameter Identification of Serial Robots with a Nonlinear Friction Model
authors: Haonan Ma, Kun Chen, Peng Xu, Bing Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Dynamic parameter identification is essential for achieving high-performance robot control. Conventional identification methods are mostly based on linear friction models and therefore cannot accurately capture joint friction characteristics. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes a stepwise dynamic parameter identification method. To enhance the accuracy of friction modeling, the sign function is replaced with an arctangent function, an exponential velocity term is introduced to describe viscous friction, and two additional coefficients are incorporated to characterize the Stribeck effect of Coulomb and static friction. To reduce identification errors, a reciprocating S-curve trajectory is employed for the preidentification of nonlinear friction parameters. An iterative weighted estimator that can accommodate the nonlinear friction model is then developed, and the pre-identified results are used as its initial inputs. Experiments on a UR16e robot demonstrate that the proposed method improves joint torque prediction accuracy by nearly 22.35% compared with conventional methods.

title: Analog In-Memory Computing for Large Language Model Inference: Opportunities and Challenges
authors: A. Vasilopoulos, H. Benmeziane, J. Büchel, W. Simon, A. Singh, I. Boybat, J. Luquin, P. Narayanan, H. Tsai, G. W. Burr, A. Rahimi, M. Le Gallo, V. Narayanan, A. Sebastian
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have shifted the primary bottleneck of AI hardware from compute to memory capacity and data movement. Analog in-memory computing (AIMC) offers a promising path to address this challenge by enabling matrix-vector multiplication directly within memory arrays, significantly reducing data transfers associated with model weights. In this paper, we discuss the role of AIMC in LLM inference workloads from a holistic systems perspective. We analyze the architecture of modern LLMs and identify which operations are well-suited for AIMC. We further discuss key challenges and opportunities in memory technologies, algorithms, system architecture, and heterogeneous system composition that must be addressed to enable AIMC as a practical accelerator for future AI inference infrastructure.

title: Identification of Position-Dependent Geometric Errors in Five-Axis Machine Tools Based on an Attention-Mechanism CNN-LSTM
authors: Biting Lei, Hanyue Bao, Simi Li, Huaijie Wang, Xiong Fu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: With the increasing requirements for processing accuracy of complex free-form surface parts in aerospace and automotive manufacturing, the position-dependent geometric error (PDGE) of five-axis machine tools has become an important factor affecting processing quality. Traditional PDGE identification methods use expensive measurement equipment, such as laser interferometers, R-test, and ball bar instruments. However, these cannot accurately reflect the real processing state. This paper proposes a five-axis machine tool PDGE reverse identification method that integrates a geometric error model with the attention mechanism CNN-LSTM. First, a geometric error model from the tool end to the workpiece end is constructed. Based on sensitivity analysis, 30 PDGE items are decomposed into 15 suberror models to reduce the complexity of error coupling identification. Next, a sequence input integrating position features, mean-zero features, and difference features is designed. The CNN-LSTM-Attention algorithm is used to achieve a nonlinear mapping from the simulation contour error to each axis PDGE. Additionally, a PDGE supervision term and a simulation contour error constraint term are introduced to construct a loss function for joint optimization of the prediction results. This improves identification accuracy. The results show that the deviation between the predicted contour error of each axis and the simulation contour error is less than 2.93% of the simulation contour error. The proposed method achieves high-precision and interpretable PDGE reverse identification without the need for professional measurement equipment. This provides a feasible technical path for PDGE identification, integrating geometric error models and deep learning.

title: Multi-Model Deep Learning Computer Vision System for Brain Disorder Classification and Tumor Segmentation with Treatment Recommendations
authors: Sasirekha R, Narendiaran N, Nirenjana JV
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: This research study presents the design and implementation of a high-performance, modular frontend platform built using modern web technologies. The application takes advantage of server-side rendering, clientside hydration and React Server Components to create an efficient rendering pipeline with less latency and a better user facing experience. Build-layer manifests (including routing, middleware, dynamic chunk mapping, and font configuration) expression of an optimized method of deployment that entails the least client overhead and standardized performance traits. The architecture of the system includes the use of modular TypeScript configmanage, a custom document layout, and incremental compilation for maintaining system and operation throughout the development and production processes. With light weighted runtime behaviour, lack of client middleware and clean assets bundling, the platform offers strong foundation that can be used as a basis for secure, responsive and extendable web interfaces. This research is an analysis about the structure of the system, its performance implications, and the opportunities for it to be incorporated into large-scale Web solutions.

title: Non-Invasive Blood Group Classification using Fingerprint Images and Deep Residual Learning
authors: K Shilpa, J Revanth Kumar, E Sravanthi, Gousiya Begum, V S Manoj Kumar, M Sireesha
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Knowing blood type is essential in case of a medical emergency, transfusion organizations, and the overall handling of the healthcare system. The classical techniques for blood typing require blood samples, are time-consuming, and imposes a laboratory-grade infrastructure. To mitigate these problems, a novel blood type classification system that combines fingerprint images and deep residual learning is proposed in this paper. A pretrained ResNet-based convolutional neural network is employed to sort fingerprint images into the eight blood type classes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{A}^{+}, \mathrm{A}^{-}, \text{AB}^{+}, \text{AB}^{-}, \mathrm{B}^{+}, \mathrm{B}^{-}, \mathrm{O}^{+}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}^{-}$</tex>). The model takes advantage of the residual connections to effectively learn even the most subtle ridge and texture patterns of the fingerprint images. Imaging preprocessing and data augmentation methods are applied to augment generalization. The experiments rest on nearly 6,000 fingerprint images, divided into training, validation, and test sets. The proposed method achieves a 77.5% accuracy on the test set, with an even distribution of class-wise precision and recall across the blood group categories. The results indicate that fingerprint biometrics possess undisclosed discriminative information that is relevant to blood group classification. The proposed method offers a low-cost, non-invasive, and fast alternative in the preliminary blood group identification test which is very advantageous in case of emergencies or hospitals with limited resources.

title: Infrared Thermal Bushing Fault Diagnosis Based on Adaptive Roi Extraction and Deep Learning Fusion Model
authors: Xianfeng Zhu, Shaobo Liu, Changdong Zhu, Yi Du, Songlin Guo, Zihan Teng, Zixin Jiao, Zhixian Zhang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Accurate fault diagnosis of transformer bushings via infrared thermal imaging is challenging due to complex background interference and the limited capability of singlearchitecture model. This paper proposes a bushing fault diagnosis framework integrating adaptive region-of-interest (ROI) extraction with a CNN-Vision Transformer (ViT) fusion model. An adaptive ROI extraction method combining infrared thermal distribution with geometric structural constraints is first designed to precisely isolate the bushing region. A Swin-CNN-ViT fusion architecture is then constructed, where the CNN branch extracts local hotspot features and the Swin Transformer branch models long-range spatial dependencies, with the two fused via weighted feature aggregation for classification into normal, overheating, insulation defect, and contamination states. Experiments on a 5,600-image infrared power equipment dataset show that the proposed method achieves 95.0 % validation accuracy, a 6.5 percentage-point improvement over the ResNet50 baseline, with faster convergence and fewer missed detections in defect categories.

title: A Control Method for Electronic Expansion Valves Based on Superheat Variations and its Application in Cold Storage Temperature Control
authors: Shuai Ye, Shan Jiang, Mingxue Sun, Yahong Li
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Cold storage refrigeration systems inherently suffer from strong non-linearity, time-varying parameters, and significant response lag. Conventional PID controllers often react too slowly to these dynamics, leading to temperature overshoot and insufficient safety margins. Conversely, advanced model-based predictive controls (such as DMC) demand highly precise models and complex computations, limiting their practical engineering application. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a novel, rule-optimized predictive control strategy for electronic expansion valves (EEV). By forecasting the dynamic trends of evaporator superheat online, the controller preemptively adjusts the valve aperture before the superheat deviates from its setpoint. This predictive action is integrated with a multi-rule coordination mechanism that evaluates the current superheat state, cold store temperature deviations, and mechanical valve constraints. Experimental validation confirms that this method effectively prevents the superheat from dropping below safe thresholds while significantly mitigating fluctuations caused by inherent system delays. Compared to traditional feedback techniques, the proposed strategy accelerates the cooling response, enhances temperature stability, and offers superior engineering adaptability through its simplified, model-independent implementation[1]

title: Semantic Preservation in LLM-Based Cultural Narrative Generation for Marketing Communication Contexts
authors: Pagon Gatchalee
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed to transform culturally embedded narratives into digital storytelling formats. While these systems demonstrate fluency and stylistic flexibility, less attention has been paid to how generative processes reshape narrative structure, symbolic specificity, and interpretive framing. This paper examines semantic preservation in LLM-based cultural narrative generation through a literaturebased thematic analysis of LLM-focused research spanning bias analysis, creativity modeling, narrative reconstruction, and knowledge-augmented heritage applications. The analysis identifies recurring patterns of structural compression, salience redistribution, pragmatic drift, symbolic attenuation, and circulation-driven stabilization. These patterns suggest that semantic preservation cannot be reduced to surface-level correctness or lexical similarity but instead reflects layered representational dynamics that operate across structural, interpretive, symbolic, and distributional contexts. Building on these findings, the study formalizes a semantic preservation evaluation framework designed to guide discourse-aware assessment, knowledge-augmented generation strategies, and culturally grounded benchmarking in LLM research. By repositioning semantic preservation as a structured evaluation problem, this work contributes a conceptual foundation for culturally robust generative modeling and discourse-aware assessment in marketing communication contexts involving culturally sensitive narrative transformation.

title: A 1781 GOPS Compute-In-Memory CIM Macro with a Novel Dual-Port 8T SRAM for Implementing Multiple Logical Operations
authors: Cheena Singhal, Abhishek Goel, Kartik Shahi, Palak, Sparsh Mittal, Sudeb Dasgupta
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We propose a novel compute-in-memory (CIM) enabled dual-port 8T (DP-8T) SRAM bitcell design, which can simultaneously perform two Boolean operations, such as NAND and OR, in a single cycle. By contrast, conventional single-port SRAM bitcells are limited to performing only one Boolean operation per cycle. We use additional logic gates to realize other Boolean operations such as XOR/XNOR and OR/AND, along with a half-adder. The proposed 64x128 design achieves a throughput of 1780.86 GOPS with an energy efficiency of 12 fJ/bit over an operating frequency range of 746.3 to 869.5 MHz. The robustness of the proposed architecture is validated by demonstrating correct functionality of all supported logic operations under worst-case process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) corners. The proposed work achieves an accuracy of 99.6% for a binarized neural network (BNN) on the MNIST dataset.

title: Research on the Pragmatic Functions and Phonetic Realization of Pause Signals in Natural Conversation in the Mongolian Khalkha Dialect
authors: Cheliger
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: To examine the pragmatic functions and phonetic realization of pause signals in natural conversations in the Khalkha dialect of Mongolian, this study analyzes 174 minutes of conversational data (22,744 prosodic words and 1,103 turns). Statistical analyses show that the 1,700 phonetic pauses have a mean duration of 0.480 s and a right-skewed distribution, with most pauses concentrated between 0.00 and 1.28 s, indicating clear quantitative linguistic regularities. Pauses are classified into three types: (1) pauses between prosodic words, (2) pauses between turn-constructional units (turn-holding), and (3) pauses between turns (turn-yielding). Inter-turn pauses have the longest mean duration (0.540 s), with a maximum of 4.032 s. Pause duration differs significantly between pauses between prosodic words and the other two types ($\mathbf{p}{\lt}0.05$), whereas the difference between the latter two types is not significant. Turn-yielding pauses are significantly longer than turn-holding pauses, which may reflect cognitive preparation during turn transition. This study provides empirical evidence for research on speech prosody and conversational interaction.

title: Development of Nitrogen-polar GaN HEMTs
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)

title: Novel Full Ferroelectric-based Continuous-Time Ising Machine with Ultra-Low Hardware Cost for Real-Time Combinatorial Optimization
authors: Weikai Xu, Qianqian Huang, Ru Huang
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this work, for the first time, a novel full ferroelectric (FE)-based continuous-time Ising machine is proposed with significantly reduced hardware cost. By utilizing the FE polarization to compensate process variation, a FE-based latch is proposed for spin implementation, which can randomly generate spin-up or spin-down states, eliminating the need for extra bulky calibration circuits. Moreover, by leveraging the multi-level storage and gate controllable structure of FeFET, the spin interactions with in-situ precision extension can be efficiently implemented, further reducing hardware cost. Based on the above design, the max-cut problem is solved with enhanced success probability and reduced hardware cost, showing its great potential for solving the real-time combinatorial optimization problems.

title:
