                    Heterojunction Based Optoelectronic Neuro-inspired Memristor
authors: Saransh Shrivastava, Yu-Hang Poh, Chen-Wei Chang, Ke-Yue Wen, Stephen Ekaputra Limantoro, Tseung-Yuen Tseng
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: In this work, we propose a ZnO/Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> heterojunction-based optoelectronic memristive synapse (OMS). It shows the analog-type resistance switching behavior with a large memory window of ~1498. The long-term potentiation/ depression behavior of biological synapse is emulated by OMS with small non-linearity, which is further utilized to simulate convolution neural network to classify the face images of Yale dataset. The same OMS also responds to violet light of 405 nm with an excitatory change in the device current, which further follows the persistent photo conductive phenomenon under dark and exhibits the emulation of synaptic functions such as short to long term memory transition and paired pulse facilitation. All these findings indicate the OMS’s potential applications in optoelectronic neuro-inspired applications.

title: Intelligent Fruit Detection Using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Manoj Kumar, Urmila Pilania, Roshi Saxena, Jai Gupta, Drish Bhalla
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Accurate fruit detection is a fundamental task in computer vision with significant applications in agriculture, retail automation, and assistive technologies. In the proposed work authors utilized Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model for fruit detection from the images. For training the CNN model Fruit-360 dataset from Kaggle has been utilized. The CNN technique is capable of retrieving deep feature and from these features it learns discriminative visual patterns. The model is capable of identifying and classifying fruit images into many categories. The dataset used contain verity of labeled images which are taken under controlled conditions. The proposed work is evaluated based on performance metrics including: accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score. With an accuracy of 74% on the test dataset, precision of 75.6%, and recall of 74%, experimental results confirm the effectiveness of the suggested model and are deemed adequate for real-world uses. The web app is also developed for the real-time detection and classification of fruit images. In future authors will integrate CNN model with neutral language processing for answering the questions of the users.

title: Human centric conversational system in Tamil using Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Yogita Bhavanishankar, Vishnu Siva B, J Cruz Antony
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Living in rural and under-resourced communities, people are frequently relying on the use of simple telephone as there is a low access to the internet and low smartphone rates. Traditional Interactive Voice Response systems are menu-based, rigid and not very conducive to natural speech in local languages. This paper proposes an artificial intelligence-based bilingual voice-first application that translates Tamil and English while talking using a regular GSM phone. The design that is proposed combines multi-language speech recognition, generative text that is retrieval augmented, natural language understanding, and neural text-to-speech synthesis as part of a flexible telephony platform that can be configured to operate with little or no network connectivity. Local audio routing based GSM call simulation layer is used to eliminate the expensive third-party telephony APIs and minimize the cost of testing and deployment. Experimental assessments reveal a high degree of cross-accent speech recognition, appropriate response generation, and use as a result of conversations, as well as a high level of user-satisfaction. It provides the telephonic services with the much-needed accessibility increase and creates a feasible platform on which voice automation can be easily scaled in such areas like education, healthcare, customer care, and delivery of public services.

title: Research of FastSLAM Based on Butterfly Optimization Algorithm with Hybrid Strategies
authors: Shuai Yuan, Jiarui Liu, Haoyan Li, Zhaoqing Zhang
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: To address the issue where the classical Fast SLAM algorithm utilizes a motion model as the proposal distribution, leading to a deviation of particle distribution from the actual posterior probability distribution, this paper proposes an improved FastSLAM algorithm combined with a hybrid-strategy based Butterfly Optimization Algorithm. First of all, the STC (Sine-Tent-Cosine) chaotic mapping is introduced into the initialization stage of the Butterfly Optimization Algorithm to enhance population diversity. In the next place, an adaptive inertia weight strategy is introduced in the global search phase to balance the global exploration and local exploitation capabilities of the algorithm. In order to prevent the algorithm from falling into local optima, an improved strategy based on Cauchy mutation and lens opposition-based learning(OBL) is introduced. Finally, the improved Butterfly Optimization Algorithm is utilized to handle the prediction step of FastSLAM, which optimizes the particle poses and generates the new proposal distribution. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm effectively enhances the accuracy of mobile robot localization and mapping, providing an efficient solution for FastSLAM research.

title: Smart Disease Prediction and Medicine Recommendation System
authors: Ushashree R, Aishwarya M, Deepthi, Hamsaveni D, Jayanth Kumar P N, Akash Kamath
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The healthcare sector increasingly depends on technological advancements to provide rapid and reliable assistance for individuals facing medical issues. Central to this transformation is the Intelligent Disease Prediction and Medicine Recommendation System a platform where users can enter their symptoms through an easy-to-use web interface to identify potential health conditions. Utilizing the Random Forest algorithm, which is particularly effective in analyzing intricate symptom patterns and producing accurate predictions, it is trained on curated datasets that encompass diseases, related symptoms, standard treatments, preventive measures, dietary advice, and lifestyle modifications. Users can input their symptoms, and within moments, the system generates a primary diagnosis along with customized recommendations regarding medications, precautions, dietary practices, and daily routines to enhance self-management and awareness. Developed using Python, Flask, and publicly available machine learning libraries, it operates seamlessly in any web browser, ensuring affordability and simplicity without the need for complicated installations. Acknowledging its limitations, the system consistently indicates that its outputs are to be considered as preliminary guidance only, emphasizing the importance of consulting healthcare professionals to guarantee accuracy and safety. Initiatives like this highlight the potential of machine learning in facilitating early health assessments and recommendations, particularly in underserved areas, while also incorporating safeguards against oversights such as individual patient characteristics or rare diseases.

title: A TD3 Approach Based on Two-Sided Evolutionary Fusion for Robotic Arm Path Planning
authors: Feng Zhang, Haiyan Liu, Chengjie Fan, Shuai Yuan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: A Two Side Evolutionary Fusion-Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TEA-TD3) method is proposed focused on low planning efficiency along with long and tortuous paths for robotic arm path planning. Firstly, TEA-TD3 utilizes the Improved Cross-Entropy Method (ICEM) to alternately optimize the actor network and the critic network of TD3, thereby obtaining a better critic policy and improving the overall convergence speed. Secondly, hybrid distribution evolutionary perturbation mechanism and elite individual recycling mechanism are proposed. These mechanisms balance the scope of initialization perturbations for the actor network population and reintroduce the best critic network individual from the previous generation into the current population, thereby enhancing the overall explorative capability and stability of the method. Finally, the robotic arm conducts path planning experiments in the Robot Operating System (ROS)-Gazebo simulator. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms the ICEM-TD3 and TD3 methods in terms of success rate, average path length, average distance to the target, and average steps to completion.

title: Smart Signal Booster for Cellular and Wi-Fi Communication
authors: S Bhargavi, Abhijith J V, Abhishek H K, Govardhan V K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: In today’s digitized world, dependable access to Both cellular networks and internet services are needs. However, many remote and rural communities continue to suffer from weak signals. and poor infrastructure, which prevents access to basic resources such as education, healthcare, and emergency services. This growing digital divide requires innovative and affordable solutions that guarantee dependable connectivity reaches even the most Isolated regions. The proposed paper directly addresses this. Challenge by improving network coverage and providing internet Access where it is critically lacking. The system employs a tech An ontologically advanced way of amplifying weak cellular signals and redistribute them via a Wi-Fi network, intended for simplicity, portability, and sustainability with renewable energy sources or battery power. Initial tests showcase significant Signal improvements, reaching as high as 4-bar strength in remote locations. The Such solution supports rural development, disaster re Lief, and on-the-go connectivity, driving inclusion to progress in underserved areas.

title: CoDA: Color Distribution Probing for Efficient and Generalizable AI-Generated Image Detection
authors: Zexi Jia, Zhiqiang Yuan, Xiaoyue Duan, Jinchao Zhang, Jie Zhou, Anil K. Jain
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: AI-generated image detection faces a persistent trade-off between generalization and efficiency: lightweight artifact-based methods often degrade on unseen generators or domains, whereas more robust large-scale models are computationally expensive. Meanwhile, existing benchmarks mainly focus on cross-model evaluation in photorealistic settings, leaving cross-domain robustness underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce FakeForm, a large-scale benchmark with approximately 370,000 images across 62 diverse domains for both cross-model and cross-domain evaluation. Motivated by this broader setting, we revisit color-distribution probing as an efficient complementary cue for AI-generated image detection. We observe that, especially for photographic content, real photographs tend to exhibit smoother and more stable color patterns, whereas synthetic images often show characteristic color imbalances introduced by neural generation. Based on this observation, we propose CoDA, a compact 1.48M-parameter detector built on a Noise-Quantization Probe, together with a theoretical analysis linking probe responses to color non-uniformity. Experiments show that CoDA achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks and the best results on the challenging cross-domain evaluation of FakeForm, while remaining highly competitive in cross-model photorealistic settings. These results suggest that persistent generative artifacts can provide a practical foundation for efficient and robust AI-generated image detection. The models and FakeForm benchmark will be made publicly available.

title: MMA++: Effective Multi-Modal Adaptation for Vision-Language Models_supp1-3691448.pdf
authors: Xiaohua Xie
publish: N/A
abstract: Large scale pre-trained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have shown good generalization capabilities across diverse downstream tasks. However, adapting such large-scale models to few-shot generalization scenarios remains challenging due to the trade-off between preserving general knowledge and incorporating task-specific information. In this paper, we propose MMA++, an advanced and effective Multi-Modal Adapter framework for parameter-efficient VLM adaptation. Unlike prior works that independently inject adapters into each modality or uniformly across layers, MMA++ performs a dataset-level analysis to identify discriminative and generalizable features, and selectively applies adapters to the higher layers of both vision and text encoders. To bridge the modality gap, we further propose a shared feature projection space that enhances alignment between modalities. Beyond architecture design, we identify the fusion scale $\alpha$—which controls the strength of adapter integration—as a key factor in few-shot generalization. We empirically and theoretically demonstrate that $\alpha$ should not be static, but adapted based on training data size. To reduce the effort of tuning this value across different datasets, we propose the $\alpha$-consistency framework, consisting of: (1) a consistency training strategy under varying fusion scales; and (2) an $\alpha$-decoupling strategy that uses a larger fusion scale during training and a smaller one at inference to account for sample size mismatch. We evaluate MMA++ on a wide range of few-shot generalization tasks, including base-to-novel generalization, cross-dataset transfer, and domain generalization. Our method consistently achieves leading performance.

title: Beamforming Algorithm and System Optimization Based on True-Time-Delay and Phase Cooperative Control for Broadband Optically Controlled Phased Arrays
authors: Ting An, Limin Liu, Yunfeng Jiang, Yunjuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: To address the contradiction between hardware non-idealities and broadband beam squint effects in microwave photonic beamforming systems, this paper proposes a beamforming algorithm framework based on true-time-delay and phase cooperative control. In this paper, we decouple and analyze the discrete tuning characteristics of optical true-time delay lines and the continuous phase tuning capability of electro-optic phase modulators, a mapping model from device constraints to system performance is established. A three-layer algorithm architecture is proposed, including discrete true-time delay solving, improved delay-and-sum algorithm, and robust MVDR algorithm. On this basis, a true-time-delay and phase cooperative control strategy is constructed, utilizing true-time delay to eliminate beam squint and electro-optic phase modulator to compensate for time-delay quantization residual errors. Simulation results show that the cooperative control reduces the beam pointing error from 0.86° to 0.06°, suppresses the sidelobe level to −31.5 dB, and achieves a squint coefficient lower than 0.01 ° /GHz. This study provides theoretical and algorithmic support for high-precision, low sidelobe, wide-angle scanning of broadband optically controlled phased array systems.

title: Optical Remote Sensing Image Super-Resolution via Adaptive High-Pass Filtering and Window Attention Mechanism
authors: Qikai Zhou, Baoxiang Huang, He Gao, Milena Radenkovic, Ge Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote sensing image super-resolution (SR) aims to enhance image resolution, critical for various applications. Conventional convolutional neural networks (CNNs) often struggle to recover fine textures and sharp edges, and are limited in adaptively combining local and global features. To address this, we propose Adaptive High-Pass Spatial Gate Transformer Network (AHGTNet), a novel cascade network for optical remote sensing image SR. AHGTNet integrates an Adaptive High-Pass Enhancement Block (AHEB) and a Residual Gate Window Attention Blocks (RGWB). The AHEB dynamically extracts and emphasizes high-frequency features, crucial for detailed textures and edges, by generating adaptive filtering weights and applying grouped high-pass operations. Subsequently, the RGWB efficiently models both local details and long-range dependencies, facilitating a comprehensive integration of contextual features. Extensive experiments on three optical remote sensing datasets demonstrate AHGTNet's effectiveness. Compared with state-of-the-art methods, our model consistently achieves competitive, and often leading, reconstruction quality in terms of PSNR and SSIM. Furthermore, AHGTNet shows favorable performance on real-world SR tasks, adeptly recovering detailed textures while preserving overall scene coherence.

title: New Products
authors: Robert M. Goldberg
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine
abstract: Ahead of the final ratification of the IEEE standard, Rohde & Schwarz has implemented the first Wi-Fi 8 RF tests in signaling mode into its CMX500 one-box signaling tester. Validating a prototype next-generation WLAN device from Broadcom, the CMX500 verifies new Wi-Fi 8 physical layer features designed to shift the focus from sheer speed towards ultra-high reliability (UHR) performance of tomorrow's wireless connectivity.

title: UAV-Assisted-IRS-Aided-UE: Analysis of Ergodic Capacity, Outage Probability, and Diversity Order
authors: Nikita Goel, Pankaj Kumar, Rohit Singh, Sandesh Jain, Manoj Tolani, Sam Darshi
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-assisted transmission provides adaptive and flexible coverage, however, it is constrained by limited transmission range and data rate. Complementing these limitations, intelligent reflecting surface (IRS) assistance emerges as a promising solution. Despite dynamic topology and dynamic environment, existing works are largely based on the ideal or fixed Rician channels, often overlooking the impact of altitude. In light of this, the letter proposes a UAV-assisted-IRS (UA-IRS)–aided communication framework among ground users (GUs) to enhance overall connectivity under practical channel constraints and altitude considerations. Specifically, a comprehensive mathematical framework is developed by modeling the air-to-ground (A2G) link as a height-dependent Rician fading channel. Closed-form expressions for the upper bound on the ergodic capacity are derived, along with an accurate approximation of the outage probability. Furthermore, simplified asymptotic expressions are obtained, and results are provided to validate the theoretical analysis. The study also offers important theoretical insights, including the impact of the IRS order on diversity gain and the influence of height-dependent channel realization on the achievable coding gain.

title: Innovation-Domain Decision-Directed Phase Tracking for Wiener Phase Noise in Fast Rayleigh Fading
authors: Ura Klongklaew, Nithiroth Pornsuwancharoen, Phichai Youplao
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This letter proposes an innovation-domain decision-directed phase tracking (ID-DDPT) architecture for coherent detection over Rayleigh fading channels with temporally correlated phase evolution and Wiener phase noise. By reformulating phase tracking into the innovation domain, replacing the unbounded cumulative phase with its stationary increments, the proposed method converts a non-stationary estimation problem into a stable low-complexity filtering problem. A closed-form expression for the steady-state residual phase error variance is derived under the locked-regime assumption, along with an analytical optimal smoothing factor. Modeling the residual phase distortion as an effective signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) attenuation yields a tractable bit error rate (BER) approximation for BPSK over Rayleigh fading. A first-order error-propagation analysis further characterizes the impact of decision errors and provides insight into the onset of cycle slips. Simulation results demonstrate that ID-DDPT outperforms DBPSK and a complexity-equivalent scalar Kalman tracker (SKT), achieving near-coherent performance with O(1) per-symbol complexity and minimal pilot overhead.

title: Performance of X -bar Control Chart Pattern Recognition Scheme with Regulated Monitoring and Ensemble Recognizers
authors: Adnan Hassan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: An effective control chart pattern recognition (CCPR) scheme is vital for detecting process abnormalities and identifying possible root causes in modern manufacturing systems. While most prior studies emphasize fully developed signals, limited attention has been given to partially developed and noise-contaminated patterns encountered in practice. This study revisits the online recognition of X-bar control chart patterns using classical multilayer perceptron (MLP). The focus is on the methodological soundness of regulated monitoring and the comparison of the performance of alternative schemes using generalized and specialized recognizers, and ensemble fusion. The results show that the summation-based ensemble with regulated monitoring of Nelson’s Run Rules and cumulative sum (Scheme C) achieved superior performance, with 91.3% correct classification for stable processes, and between 91.1% and 98.7% for unstable processes. The scheme scored ARL0 (269), ARL1(1 to 15), Type I error (8.7%), and Type II error (< 1%, in most cases). The scheme preserves the simplicity of classical networks while being robust against noisy data streams. This study proposes practical recommendations for the effective ranges of drift parameters.

title: Adaptive Structured Proximal Policy Optimization-Based Compensation for PID control of Folding-Wing UAVs under Disturbances
authors: Ying Liu, Tingrui Li, Guangyou Fang, Xiaojun Liu, Yicai Ji, Shun Wang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Conventional PID control exhibits limited robustness when applied to folding-wing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) operating under realistic flight conditions, where strong nonlinearities, model uncertainties, and external disturbances are present. In particular, the coupled lateral-directional dynamics make reliable attitude regulation challenging. This paper presents a structured neural compensation approach that augments a baseline PID controller with a bounded auxiliary compensator learned via Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO). Instead of directly generating control commands, the policy operates in a low-dimensional residual space, preserving the classical control structure while improving robustness to disturbances and uncertainties. The proposed framework is evaluated via software-in-the-loop simulations on a high-fidelity folding-wing UAV model. Comparative results show improved tracking performance and disturbance rejection over conventional PID control and unstructured reinforcement learning-based controllers.

title: Ultra-low-power Monostatic Backscatter Platform with Phase-Aware Channel Estimation and System-Level Validation
authors: Hanyeol Ryu, Sangkil Kim
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This paper presents a novel phase-aware channel-estimation (CE) method and an experimentally validated hardware–software platform for an ultra-low-power monostatic backscatter link. Conventional phase-stationary least squares (LS) and linear minimum mean square error (LMMSE) estimators incur deterministic bias when residual phase-slope is present across pilot symbols, a backscatter-specific impairment that is particularly consequential in monostatic configurations. To address this limitation, this paper introduces a lifted pilot model augmented with a linearized residual-phase-slope regressor, yielding closed-form phase-aware LS and LMMSE estimators with minimal additional computation. A double-Rician fading model characterizes the cascaded forward and backscatter hops, and a resource-optimal pilot allocation rule is analytically derived to balance CE accuracy against payload efficiency. The prototype integrates a semi-passive tag, a software-defined radio (SDR) reader, and a 2×1 planar Yagi–Uda array providing 7 dBi gain and over 30 dB isolation at 2.4–2.5 GHz. The receiver combines pilot-aided carrier frequency offset (CFO) compensation, the proposed lifted estimators, and zero-forcing (ZF) equalization for symbol recovery over the cascaded backscatter channel. It achieves 500 kbps at 1 m, with tag-side power consumption of 10 μW for the radio-frequency (RF) switch and 11.2 mW including the general-purpose microcontroller unit (MCU), corresponding to 20 pJ/bit and 22.4 nJ/bit, respectively. Measured error vector magnitudes (EVMs) are 2.97% for on-off keying (OOK) and 4.02% for binary phase-shift keying (BPSK). Bit error rate (BER) measurements and over-the-air (OTA) full-color image reconstruction confirm reliable multimedia-capable operation and provide practical codesign guidance for future Internet of Things (IoT) deployments.

title: Structure from Noise: Confirmation Bias in Particle Picking in Structural Biology
authors: Amnon Balanov, Alon Zabatani, Tamir Bendory
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: The computational pipelines of single-particle cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM) and cryo-electron tomography (cryo-ET) include an early particle-picking stage, in which a micrograph or tomogram is scanned to extract candidate particles, typically via template matching or deep-learning-based techniques. The extracted particles are then passed to downstream tasks such as classification and 3D reconstruction. Although it is well understood empirically that particle picking can be sensitive to the choice of templates or learned priors, a quantitative theory of the bias introduced by this stage has been lacking. Here, we develop a mathematical framework for analyzing bias in template matching-based detection with concrete applications to cryo-EM and cryo-ET. We study this bias through two downstream tasks: (i) maximum-likelihood estimation of class means in a Gaussian mixture model and (ii) 3D volume reconstruction from the extracted particle stack. We show that when template matching is applied to pure noise, then under broad noise models, the resulting maximum-likelihood estimates converge asymptotically to deterministic, noise-dependent transforms of the user-specified templates, yielding a structure from noise effect. We further characterize how the resulting bias depends on the noise statistics, sample size, dimension, and detection threshold. Finally, controlled experiments using standard cryo-EM software corroborate the theory, demonstrating reproducible structure from noise artifacts in low-SNR data.

title: Using Transformer Encoders for Light Curve Classification in Telescope Pipelines
authors: Avni Bansal, Dhruv Roongta
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Modern time-domain surveys such as the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) and the upcoming Vera Rubin Observatory generate millions of light curves per night. Identifying and flagging the scientifically interesting observations for follow-up is a time-sensitive and non-trivial problem. Traditional models, including tree-based classifiers and convolutional neural networks (CNNs), are widely used in this context. However, they have limited success, and they often struggle with the irregular sampling, multi-band structure, and long-range dependencies inherent in astronomical time series. They also often systematically miss transient and semi-periodic sources. In this work, we implement and benchmark a Transformer-based architecture for sequence-to-label classification of light curves using the PLAsTiCC dataset. Our Transformer model takes raw light curve data as inputs without the need for resampling or handengineered features, and outperforms both a baseline CNN and a hybrid CNN+Transformer model in overall accuracy and perclass recall. In particular, the Transformer does well on rare and irregular classes such as kilonovae, M-dwarf flares, and active galactic nuclei, where traditional models often fail. We also find that the CNN+Transformer hybrid counterintuitively performs poorly, due to information loss in the convolutional front-end. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of selfattention mechanisms for irregular time-series classification and suggest that Transformer-based models offer a scalable and generalizable approach for next-generation telescope pipelines.

title: Image Enhancement for Classifying the Diabetic Retinopathy in Retinal Fundus Images
authors: Mahesh M R, Rohith S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The fundus images are improvised by introducing a complete framework for retinal image enhancement that addresses typical problems including uneven lighting and poor contrast. This system improves images by creative de-hazing methods and contrast augmentation, making subsequent analytic jobs more accurate. Images of the retinal fundus are essential for the diagnosis of several conditions affecting the eyes, such as hypertension and diabetic retinopathy. These issues are fully addressed by the retinal image enhancement architecture presented in the research. It uses cutting-edge methods to improve fundus picture clarity, including as contrast augmentation and de-hazing. By correcting non-uniform illumination, de-hazing techniques successfully lessen the effects of uneven lighting situations. By restoring the photos original sharpness, this restoration improves their suitability for analysis.

title: Secured Cloud Data Storage using Blockchain
authors: A Bhuvanesh, Arpita Belure, Cheemala Lavanya Reddy, Yellati Jyothi, Bharathi M, Ambika S R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Cloud computing provides scalable on-demand resources but faces significant security challenges, including reliance on centralized third-party identity providers, data breaches, and single points of failure. To address these vulnerabilities, this paper proposes a Blockchain-based Secure Cloud Identity and Access Management (BSCIAM) framework that integrates decentralized identity verification with secure cloud data storage. The proposed architecture utilizes Ethereum smart contracts to manage user authentication and access control tokens, eliminating the need for centralized intermediaries. Furthermore, the system incorporates the Interplanetary File System (IPFS) for decentralized file storage, ensuring high data availability and integrity. A key feature of this framework is the implementation of client-side encryption using the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) 256-bit algorithm, which ensures that data is encrypted before transmission and storage, thereby preserving strict confidentiality. The system was implemented using the Sepolia test network for blockchain interactions and a React-based frontend for user operations. Experimental results and performance analysis demonstrate that the framework successfully mitigates centralized risks, optimizes gas consumption for smart contract execution, and achieves low latency in encrypted file retrieval, providing a robust solution for privacy-preserving cloud computing.

title: Joint Beam Scheduling and Passive Beamforming for IRS-Assisted Hybrid-Field SWIPT
authors: Chaoying Huang, Wen Chen, Qiong Wu, Jinhong Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper investigates an intelligent reflecting surface (IRS)-assisted simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) system under the hybrid-field model, where the IRS operates in the near-field region of the base station, while one information user (IU) and multiple energy users (EUs) are located in the far-field of the IRS. Specifically, we establish a hybrid-field model and derive a closed-form expression for the element-wise combined channel gain. Based on this, we jointly optimize beam scheduling, power allocation, and IRS phase shift with the quality-of-service (QoS) constraint, enabling dynamic beam steering to maximize the total harvested energy of EUs while maintaining a balanced trade-off between information transfer and energy harvesting. An alternating optimization algorithm is developed to solve the non-convex problem by applying the Lagrange duality method, semidefinite relaxation (SDR), and successive convex approximation (SCA). Our results reveal that the beam is always directed toward the IU to guarantee its QoS and is further adaptively steered toward the EU with the highest energy-harvesting priority in hybrid-field SWIPT. Numerical results validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme.

title: Optimized Recognition of Dermal Lesion Analysis Based on CNN and Vision Transformer
authors: P. Arivubrakan, C. Arun Kumar, Peram Venkata Naga Durga Prasad
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In recent years, skin disease has played an important role in humans, to diagnose the disease based on the inputs. With the increasing demand on the automation diagnosis, deep learning methods are very much familiar nowadays. Skin cancer is the most common and rapidly enhancing forms with variety of cancer in today's world, with millions of humans are identified every day. The performance metric analysis for skin cancer, which is in the dataset by integrating the two important models of deep learning, such as Convolution Neural Networks (CNN) and Vision Transformer (ViT) is used. The ViT model achieved an accuracy of 87 %, while the ensemble method of CNN+ ViT, which obtained 91 % accuracy. The analysis emphasises the importance of choosing the right model selection based on the available dataset characteristics, and also it provides insights into designing an effective dermal lesion classification system.

title: Smart Kinetic Envelope: Modeling and Empirical Testing of IoT Systems for Tropical Climates : A Low-Cost Innovation Approach to Making the Global South More Energy Efficient
authors: M. Amsaveni, Nikitha, Adarsh, Shree Harsha, Yashwanth R, Govardhan Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Tropical cities face acute energy challenges driven by rapid urbanisation. Buildings globally consume over 40% of total energy, with air conditioning systems responsible for the dominant share in hot-humid climates such as those prevalent across India and the wider Global South. Conventional static building envelopes are fundamentally incapable of adapting to dynamic solar geometry, leading to chronic over-consumption of 40–50% beyond theoretical optima. This paper presents the complete design, mathematical modelling, prototype fabrication, and empirical validation of a Smart Kinetic Envelope (SKE) system that integrates IoT intelligence with origami-inspired panel mechanics. The system employs a Waterbomb Base tessellation to enable mechanically efficient, low-torque modulation of solar heat gain and natural ventilation.Control architecture is realised using ATmega328P microcontrollers, LM393 light-dependent resistor sensor modules, and MG996R metal-gear servo motors arranged in a fault-tolerant decentralised I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C cluster topology. Maekawa’s Theorem and Kawasaki’s Theorem guarantee flat-foldability and single-degree-of-freedom actuation, while a dynamic thermal model couples the fold angle θ to both the effective U-value and the Solar Heat Gain Coefficient. EnergyPlus simulation of a 100 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> office zone in Chennai and physical wind-tunnel testing of a 1:1 prototype demonstrate a 28% reduction in annual cooling energy, a 31.7% reduction in peak cooling load, a 75% improvement in daylight glare control, and structural stability at gusts up to 60 km/h. Total installed cost is approximately USD 65 per m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, representing a 90% reduction relative to premium kinetic facade benchmarks.

title: Investigating the Suitability of High-Performance Linux-Based System-on-Modules for Lunar Applications
authors: L. Coïc, G. Giuffrida, E. Le Goulven, S. Yjjou, A. Marino, P. Garcia, L. Turchi, G. Magistrati, A. Cowley
publish: 2025 IEEE Radiation Effects Data Workshop (REDW) (in conjunction with 2025 NSREC)
abstract: Characterization campaigns conducted on two System-on-Modules destined for lunar surface applications are presented. Their TID and SEE sensitivities were investigated under irradiation. Outgassing, Offgassing and Lifetest campaigns were also performed in this ESA project.

title: Study and Analysis of Cyber Security Threats in Smart Home Technology
authors: Divya Kaushal, Aniza Flaquer
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Smart home technology enhances convenience but introduces significant cybersecurity risks due to interconnected devices. This study explores common threats, including unauthorized access, data breaches, and malware attacks, while assessing the effectiveness of current security measures. By analyzing case studies and emerging challenges, the research emphasizes the need for robust security frameworks and user awareness to ensure the safety of smart home ecosystems.

title: ICPEA 2026 Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)

title: Automated Screening of Diabetic Retinopathy from Retinal Images
authors: G.Prabhakar, Jukanti Sneha, K.Maneesha, Kadari Rachana, Kandala Harini, Jalli Nikhitha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is still one of the most serious challenges to vision worldwide, but early diagnosis is often obstructed by a lack of advanced eye care resources and the insidious presentation of early disease. Although Artificial Intelligence has achieved great success in medical imaging, most current models are still challenged to effectively combine the strengths of deep feature learning with the capability to identify very small, multi-scale lesions such as microaneurysms. In this paper, we propose a Hybrid Multi-Scale Fusion Framework that is specifically tailored to address the gap between high-performance research models and more practical clinical applications. Our proposed model combines the unique strengths of the InceptionV3 model’s diverse receptive fields and the powerful residual learning of ResNet50. To further break through the limitations of most current models, we also developed a Customized Concatenation Mechanism and a specialized SW (Swish-based) Activation Layer, which maintains essential spatial information that is usually ignored by most current models.

title: Thermoelectric Energy Generators for Harnessing Excess Thermal Energy: Thermoelectric Energy Sources for Sustainable Usage
authors: Varun Akella, Amogh Basker, Camryn Evans, Kyuri Lim, Abigail Mokrzycki, Dhruhi Patel, Victoria Rough, Gideon Sun, Paul Belony
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Sustainable thermoelectric energy generation has the prospect of addressing current and future energy crises by utilizing the waste heat emitted by everyday appliances and equipment. The vast majority of literature has been directed towards semiconductor doping to achieve high figures of thermoelectric merit (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$Z T$</tex>) for efficient energy conversion. By contrast, there is far less research on practical thermoelectric systems that are affordable, especially for underprivileged communities that cannot afford costly-yet-efficient devices such as solar panels. To address this, we present an alternative energy device employing the Seebeck and Peltier effects. Using thermoelectric generator modules (TEGs) configured as a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3 \times 3$</tex> array of nine thermoelectric coolers (TECs) set between two <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.24 ~\text{cm} \times 15.24 ~\text{cm}$</tex> copper plates (overall module dimensions are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$15.4 ~\text{cm} \times 15.4 ~\text{cm} \times$</tex> 15.0 cm, and temperature differences between hot and cold tap water, we achieved a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\Delta \mathrm{T}=66 ~\mathrm{K}$</tex> and a peak open-circuit voltage of 3.5 V across the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.0238 ~\mathrm{m}^{2}$</tex> module surface area. This work offers insights into the design and development of this low-cost TEG and its promise for improving household energy efficiency in underpriveledged communities.

title: Reviving Ancient Immunity: Harnessing Molecular De-Extinction and Scalable Algal Biofactories of Computationally-Mined Antimicrobial Peptides to Eradicate Livestock Antibiotic Resistance
authors: Hosamulhaq Brula
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: The overconsumption of antibiotics in livestock drives bacterial resistance, threatening global food security and human health. This unchecked trend imposes escalating economic and sustainability burdens with prevalent mortality and diminishing feed efficiency. In the absence of antibiotic discovery for decades, antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) offer broad-spectrum, membranedisruptive mechanisms but face emerging resistance and prohibitive synthesis costs. This study presents a molecular de-extinction strategy employing the APEX deep-learning pipeline to mine 10.3 million peptides reconstructed from extinct proteomes. Three lead candidates (Elephasin-1.5, Mylodonin-2, and Staph-2) were validated, exhibiting minimum inhibitory concentrations (MICs) of 2-8 μM against Clostridium perfringens, 1-4 μM against Enterococcus cecorum, and 16 μM against avian pathogenic Escherichia coli (APEC), with no hemolysis up to 64 μM. By accessing peptide diversity beyond modern pathogenic evolutionary exposure, these de-extinct AMPs circumvent prevailing resistance mechanisms. For scalable, costeffective deployment, Chlamydomonas reinhardtii chloroplasts were engineered to function simultaneously as photosynthetic biofactories and oral delivery vehicles. Algae-produced AMPs can be freeze-dried into feed, protecting peptides from gastric proteolysis and ensuring targeted intestinal release while preserving MIC potency. This green bioprocess leverages phototrophic cultivation to obviate complex chemical synthesis and sustain circular bioeconomics. Collectively, this end-to-end platform, from extinctome-inspired discovery to algal biomanufacturing, mitigates livestock-driven antibiotic resistance, thereby safeguarding human health and preserving a $1.3 trillion food industry. Moreover, it establishes a blueprint to harness this vast, untapped sequence space for rapid discovery and delivery of novel therapeutics across various disorders.

title: KubeOrch: Visual Template-Based Kubernetes Configuration Management
authors: Aruna S, Mohit Nagaraj, Kshitij N. Kulkarni, Rishiraj Chirchi, Naman B. Parlecha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: KubeOrch is a visual platform that replaces manual YAML authoring for Kubernetes with a template-based, drag-and-drop workflow composed of four subsystems: a Visual Composer, a Template Engine, a Parameter Inference Engine, and a Validation Engine. A user study with 20 participants compared KubeOrch against raw YAML and Helm on five deployment tasks. KubeOrch reduced average completion time by 84.6% versus YAML, lowered configuration errors from 8.0 to 1.1 per session, achieved a 99.1% validation success rate, and reduced NASA-TLX cognitive load by 78.4%.

title: Woman-in-the-Loop: AI-Driven Mentorship Matching System
authors: Angela Santos, Nina Klimenkova, Roee Shraga
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Despite the proven impact of good quality mentorship, the process of finding the right mentor remains an underexplored challenge. This is especially evident for underrepresented groups such as women. To address this issue, we propose an AI-driven mentorship matching system that is refined iteratively via Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) feedback to enable personalized and equitable pairings. We designed a survey-based data collection process, followed by automated filtering to validate completeness and quality. To address lack of data, we generated synthetic data profiles based on survey structure. We evaluated the quality of generated datasets, comparing the realism, diversity, naturalness, and coherence. We employ a natural language processing (NLP) model, BERT, to assess mentor-mentee compatibility using both classification and regression. Future work will include obtaining and utilizing larger, more diverse datasets, integrating more HITL, developing matching algorithms, and launching the official survey.

title: Fourier-Denoising for Noisy Hamiltonian Systems: Enhancing Symplectic Dynamics Learning
authors: Chloe Ho
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Hamiltonian Neural Networks (HNNs) allow for modeling physical and dynamical systems, but their performance deteriorates when trained on noisy real-world data. We demonstrate that such noise disrupts the symplectic structure HNNs are designed to preserve, leading to unstable trajectories and energy drift. Through error analysis, we quantify the impact of noise on learned Hamiltonian gradients and propose a low-pass Fourier filtering approach to remove high-frequency artifacts prior to training, reducing the mismatch between the true and learned dynamics. Our preliminary findings indicate that this frequencydomain preprocessing substantially enhances HNN robustness, yielding both short-horizon accuracy gains and long-horizon stability. In this position paper, we argue that such denoising strategies are paramount for bridging the gap between idealized Hamiltonian models and noisy, real-world data, ensuring reliable, physically meaningful performance in practical applications.

title: Melody-Centric Cryptography: A Human-Centered Framework for Symmetric Key Generation
authors: Julia Lu, Qiaoyan Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Traditional methods for symmetric key generation rely on random alphanumeric strings, biometric data, or hardware tokens. While secure, these approaches often suffer from poor memorability, limited interpretability, and reduced accessibility in resource-constrained settings. In this paper, we propose a novel human-centered method for generating Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) keys from musical melodies. The pitch sequence of a user-selected melody is encoded into a structured string and then hashed using SHA-256 to produce a 128-bit or 256-bit AES key. To demonstrate the feasibility of this approach, we developed a web-based prototype that allows users to upload or input a melody, then perform AES encryption and decryption of text messages using the derived key. Encrypted messages can be easily copied and sent via email, enabling lightweight and portable secure communication. This system bridges humanperceivable artistic input with algorithmic cryptography, providing a cross-modal pathway for secure interaction. Preliminary use cases suggest that melody-based key derivation offers a promising direction for designing culturally adaptive, memoryfriendly symmetric encryption schemes. Future work will be focused on developing a mobile version to support real-time, on-device secure messaging.

title: SPARE: Asymmetric Proof-of-Work–Based DoS Mitigation for IoT Devices
authors: Amirhosein Yari, Majid Khabbazian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: Battery-powered IoT nodes are vulnerable to signature-verification denial-of-service (DoS): an adversary can flood a device with well-formed but invalid messages, forcing expensive digital-signature verifications. A standard mitigation is to require proof-of-work (PoW) per message, but this symmetrically burdens honest and dishonest senders alike. We address this shortcoming with an asymmetric PoW-based mitigation that makes attackers pay while sparing honest parties. A designated Bitcoin miner embeds a request commitment in its coinbase transaction; any non-winning block header whose hash falls below an application-chosen threshold serves as PoW. The sender appends this header and a logarithmic-size Merkle proof; the IoT device first validates this PoW—just a handful of hash evaluations—before attempting the costly signature verification. Because proofs are bound to the miner’s payout address, adversaries cannot piggy-back on recycled work: they must grind fresh headers (or effectively mine on the designated miner’s behalf), preserving a large resource gap in the defender’s favor. We prototype the scheme on an ESP32 MCU and show that PoW verification takes 0.8 ms versus 260 ms for ECDSA (>300× speed-up); proof packages remain < 1 kB, and end-to-end latency is dominated by network RTT even with a moderate-capacity miner; power measurements likewise confirm that hash-based verifications cost orders of magnitude less energy than signatures. The mechanism requires no blockchain modifications, scales to thousands of devices per miner, and immediately hardens firmware updates, certificate rotation, and other unsolicited IoT traffic against signature-verification DoS attacks.

title: Decoupled Cross-Modal Fusion and Knowledge Injection for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis
authors: Aoqiang Zhu, Min Hu, Xiaohua Wang, Yan Xing, Fuji Ren
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Multimodal Sentiment Analysis (MSA) aims to integrate multiple modalities for a more comprehensive analysis of sentiment tendencies. However, existing approaches often couple joint representations during fusion, weakening unimodal properties and reducing the discriminative power of the model. To address this, we propose Decoupled Cross-Modal Fusion and Knowledge Injection (DecFI). Specifically, we design Decoupled Cross-Modal Fusion (DCMF), which optimizes cross-attention and self-attention to establish a decoupled cross-self-attention mechanism. Furthermore, to mitigate the potential weakening of modality-specific features during multi-layer iterative interactions in DCMF, we introduce Decoupled Knowledge Injection (DKI). Leveraging the decoupling properties of DCMF, DKI injects unimodal features into the corresponding unimodal regions of the multimodal representation. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of DecFI, which consistently outperforms strong baselines with average performance improvements of 2.03%, 2.37%, 2.48%, and 2.53% across all evaluation metrics on MOSI, MOSEI, SIMS, and CHERMA, respectively. These results validate the effectiveness and necessity of decoupled cross-modal fusion in capturing both modality specificities and commonalities.

title: Multi-Slot Distributed Frequency Control for Power Systems With Heterogeneous Devices
authors: Xiemin Mo, Tao Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a distributed frequency control framework for power systems with high renewable penetration. The goal is to restore nominal frequency by achieving multi-slot optimal allocation of heterogeneous controllable devices, including synchronous generators, energy storage units, and controllable loads. To this end, we embed the optimality conditions of a multi-slot economic dispatch problem into a projected primal-dual gradient dynamics (PDGD) controller, which enables a fully distributed implementation and guarantees device capacity constraints throughout transients and at the steady state. The resulting closed-loop system, formed by the projected controller dynamics and the structure-preserving network model with first-order turbine-governor dynamics, is nonlinear and discontinuous, rendering existing PDGD-based stability results inapplicable. For step-changed net loads, sufficient conditions for asymptotic stability are derived, which also provide practical controller tuning guidelines and robustness against uncertainties in generator damping and load-frequency sensitivity coefficients. For time-varying net loads, input-to-state stability is established, providing quantitative robustness guarantees under bounded disturbances. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method improves frequency regulation performance, strictly enforces device capacity limits, and effectively exploits the temporal flexibility of energy storage compared with conventional single-slot PDGD-based methods.

title: Collision-Free Coordination of Heterogeneous Multiagent Systems Under Independent DoS Attacks
authors: Yuan-Cheng Sun, Yunlong Feng, Liwei Chen, Lina Yao, An-Yang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: In this article, the problem of collision avoidance coordination for heterogeneous multiagent systems (MASs) subject to independent denial-of-service (DoS) attacks is investigated. Unlike the traditional attack model, the challenge posed by independent DoS attacks lies in their ability to disrupt communication channels individually, thereby causing each agent to perceive the states of its neighbors in an unsynchronized and inconsistent manner, which may lead to inaccurate or delayed collision avoidance decisions. To deal with the information interruption on collision avoidance coordination, a predictor-based event-triggered communication strategy is developed to manage the network communication between agents. In addition, a hierarchical control architecture with a dynamic compensator incorporating a barrier function is designed to address collision avoidance in multiagent formations. Finally, a simulation on unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) demonstrates that agents successfully reach leader-following coordination and maintain interformation collision avoidance, even under DoS attacks.

title: Current Sensor Detection and Remedial Strategy for Repeated Faults in Dual Three-Phase PMSMs With Multiple-Branch Current Sampling
authors: Yunjiang Wu, Wenxiang Zhao, Tao Tao, Yuxuan Du, Yifan Chen, Wenjing Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: For dual three-phase permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs), the accurate acquisition of phase current information is critical. Incorrect current information caused by sensor failure will degrade motor performance or lead to drive collapse. This work proposes a strategy that leverages multiple-branch current sampling to broaden current sampling paths. This redundancy permits the detection and localization of current sensor faults through combinational logic circuits. Moreover, the redundant current information supports effective remedial strategy against all four types of sensor faults. Even when faults occur repeatedly, the strategy maintains excellent remediation performance. In contrast to conventional observe/model-based methods, the proposed strategy eliminates the need for an accurate parameterized model. Its enhanced simplicity and reliability are well-suited for real-time motor drives with limited computational resources. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy under repeated sensor faults.

title: Risk-Sensitive Stochastic Control for Robust UAV-Assisted Free-Space Quantum Key Distribution under Atmospheric Turbulence
authors: Mohd. Asim Sayeed, Mohd. Abuzar Sayeed, Tanveer Ahmed, Anuj Kumar Bharti, Rohit Verma
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) enable rapidly deployable secure communication, while quantum key distribution (QKD) ensures information-theoretic security over free-space optical channels. In UAV-assisted QKD, however, atmospheric turbulence and mobility-induced misalignment introduce stochastic channel fluctuations that cause intermittent secrecy degradation. Existing trajectory optimization methods are largely deterministic or risk-neutral and fail to address rare but severe deep-fading events. We develop a risk-sensitive stochastic control framework for robust UAV-assisted free-space QKD under atmospheric uncertainty. The UAV motion is modeled as a continuous-time stochastic dynamical system, and the quantum channel transmissivity is characterized by mobility-dependent log-normal fading, yielding a stochastic secret key rate process. The exponential performance functional simultaneously penalizes the propulsion energy and secrecy outage, resulting in a nonlinear risk-sensitive Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation. Simulation results show that there is a significant reduction in secrecy outage probability and key rate variation when atmospheric turbulence exists, but there is only a relatively minor rise in the energy consumption for propulsion, achieving a robustness-efficiency tradeoff for secure aerial quantum communication.

title: Smart Parking for Medical Cities via Passenger-Aware Allocation and Fast Grouping–Permutation Heuristics
authors: Mahdi Jemmali, Loai Kayed B Melhim, Adel Ammar, Thippa Reddy Gadekallu, Wadii Boulila
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Computer Society
abstract: Medical cities serve large populations and experience high parking demand due to heterogeneous visitor profiles and time constraints. This paper addresses the parking allocation problem in such environments by proposing a grouping-based optimization framework that allocates spaces according to the total number of passengers per vehicle rather than vehicle count alone. The proposed system integrates passenger-count information and real-time occupancy data through a coordinated architecture involving booking interfaces, IoT-based sensing, and a centralized allocation engine. Six novel algorithms are developed to achieve equitable load distribution across parking zones. Their performance is evaluated using nine instance classes and 2,790 test cases. Experimental results demonstrate that the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$GVF$</tex-math></inline-formula> algorithm achieves the highest best-solution percentage (68.2%) and the lowest average gap among the proposed algorithms, confirming its scalability and robustness in large-scale medical city environments

title: Resource Allocation Optimization in Process Petri Nets Based on Bottleneck Identification
authors: Weijie Shi, Jianbin Qiu, Shan Luo, Zhou He, Carla Seatzu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Resource allocation systems are considered fundamental to many practical applications, where the challenge is often posed by the need to optimize resource usage under budget constraints to improve system performance. In this paper, a systematic approach to resource allocation in Petri net-based systems is presented. First, a method for system bottleneck identification is analyzed to reveal the structural limitations affecting throughput. Based on this, the minimum number of resources required to maximize system throughput without budget constraints is determined. Subsequently, a heuristic iterative resource allocation strategy is developed, in which mixed-integer linear programming is employed to guide the allocation process considering budget levels. The effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated through comparative experiments under different resource constraints.

title: GaussianFusion_AI:Urban 3D Reconstruction and Label-Efficient Segmentation with Gaussian-Based Splatting
authors: Thiago J. M. Baldivieso, Fabio Suim Chagas, Luiz C. P. R. Velho, Paulo F. F. Rosa
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: This paper presents GaussianFusion_AI, a modular pipeline that integrates UAV RGB, thermal, and LiDAR data for three-dimensional urban reconstruction, latent-space segmentation, and continuous rendering via Gaussian-based splatting. In order to achieve dense multimodal point clouds and continuous views, the architecture combines multi-view photogrammetry, consistent multisensor registration, Gaussian-weighted radio-metric interpolation, and self-supervised latent encoding with anisotropic Gaussian-based splatting as its continuous rendering and representation primitive. The pipeline is evaluated on urban datasets from the city of Rio de Janeiro (Outeiro da Gloria and Quinta da Boa Vista park) and on the public USGS Darby benchmark. Quantitative results indicate stable label-efficient segmentations and average geometric errors of less than 0.6 m, assessed using silhouette coefficients, Davies-Bouldin index, and visual diagnostics. The proposed method, compared to a PointBERT baseline, produces coherent latent manifolds, demonstrates better compatibility with density-based clustering, and achieves competitive execution time. Furthermore, the Gaussian representations obtained showed preservation of spatial, spectral, and thermal gradients. This allows for differentiation in interpreting urban structures and suggests the operational viability of 3D multimodal analysis in dense urban environments.

title: Hardware-in-the-Loop Testbed for Advanced Distribution Management Systems
authors: Konrad Schmitt, Rajendra Shrestha, Larissa Souto, Pedro Eisenkraemer, Manohar Chamana, Meisam Mahdavi, Stephen Bayne, Luciane Canha
publish: IEEE Canadian Journal of Electrical and Computer Engineering
abstract: Advanced distribution management systems (ADMSs) embrace several online and offline monitoring and control features to ensure power delivery with reliability, continuity, and quality. By having data about the system’s topology, ADMS can obtain real-time field measurements to perform further analysis on the network and automatically respond to events or support operators’ decisions. Academia has been advancing the current state of the art of ADMS by proposing more sophisticated solutions that aim to address the ongoing changes in active distribution networks. However, such solutions are commonly tested in offline environments, neglecting the dynamics and challenges related to real-world field equipment and communication protocols. The most faithful power systems solution can get tested before actual deployment is through hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing, where electrical systems are simulated in real-time digital simulators, and physical equipment is integrated into the simulator through analog hardwiring and communication protocols. Considering the need for more realistic testing and validation of proposed solutions to facilitate real-world deployment, this research proposes and presents an HIL testbed for ADMS’s testing purposes that may guide future studies in the area. Along with the testbed, an automatic outage restoration ADMS feature is formulated and tested on the proposed HIL system, where the benefits of pushing validation to the most faithful testbed are highlighted.

title: Enhancing End-to-End Delay Satisfiability for IEEE 802.1Qav
authors: Cheng Long, Zonghui Li, Shuxian Jin, Kang G. Shin, Daquan Zhang, Zixiao Wang, Zhibo Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems
abstract: IEEE standard 802.1Qav evolves from Ethernet Audio Video Bridging (AVB) to support real-time transmission by performing Credit-Based Shaping (CBS) of flows at each hop. Prior work primarily focuses on achieving a tighter upper bound of the End-to-End (E2E) delay using network calculus, given the CBS configuration of flows. However, it is important to adjust the CBS configuration at each hop to meet the deadlines for various data flows. To this end, we address the CBS scheduling problem to enhance the satisfiability of E2E delay requirements. First, we formulate a linear constraint model and propose a Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN) reinforcement learning method, called CBS-DDQN, to solve the model. Second, to improve model training and scheduling, we present a fast method using equivalence reduction to calculate the E2E upper bounds of data flows for the evaluation of E2E delay satisfiability. Finally, we conduct an extensive evaluation of CBS-DDQN, demonstrating its ability to efficiently schedule thousands of flows and increase the number of flows meeting the delay requirements by 18–26% over prior methods.

title: AMPGLDA: Predicting LncRNA-Disease Associations Based on Adaptive Meta-Path Generation and Multi-Layer Perceptron
authors: Xuehui Zhang, Dengju Yao, Xiaojuan Zhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Biology and Bioinformatics
abstract: Increased evidence suggests that long non-coding RNAs (lncRNAs) hold a vital position in intricate human diseases. Nonetheless, the current pool of identified lncRNAs linked to diseases remains restricted. Hence, a reliable and cost-effective computational method is needed to predict probable correlations between lncRNAs and diseases, which could help reveal underlying mechanisms and foster the development of novel treatments. In this study, we propose a novel approach for predicting the associations between lncRNAs and diseases, which relies on the adaptive meta-path generation and multi-layer perceptron (AMPGLDA). Firstly, we integrate information about lncRNA, diseases, and miRNAs to construct a heterogeneous graph. Then, we utilize principal component analysis to extract global features from nodes. Based on this heterogeneous graph, AMPGLDA adaptively generates multiple meta-path graph structures and uses a graph convolutional neural network to learn the semantic feature representations of lncRNA and disease from the meta-path. Ultimately, AMPGLDA utilizes a deep neural network classifier to accurately predict the association between lncRNA and disease. Cross-validation experiments revealed that AMPGLDA outperformed 5 other cutting-edge prediction methods. Moreover, ablation studies and case studies confirmed its technical contributions and its capability to identify disease related lncRNAs.

title: Insulator Defect Detection Algorithm Based on Optimized YOLOv8-Transformer
authors: Ying Wang, Pengfei Cui
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Long-term operation of transmission lines causes changes in the internal stress of insulators, leading to damage. This can lead to insulator breakage, string drop, and fracture, potentially causing power outages and threatening the safe operation of the power system. However, existing insulator defect detection algorithms face challenges such as interference from rain and fog, background interference from power towers and buildings, a scarcity of insulator defect images, and difficulty extracting subtle defect features. Therefore, we propose an insulator defect detection algorithm based on an optimized YOLOv8-Transformer. First, we design a novel YOLOv8 object detector. This network incorporates an improved BiFPN structure, which effectively utilizes both local and global information in the feature map during feature extraction. Next, to address the problem of extracting weak defect features, we construct a small object detection layer to enhance the expressiveness of the feature map and enable the extraction of weak features. Furthermore, to obtain multi-scale insulator feature information, we propose a feature extractor based on the Transformer architecture, thereby obtaining hierarchical features for insulators of different modalities. Furthermore, to capture the coupling relationship between feature information from different modalities, the paper uses a CNN to model the complementary relationship between the insulator defect saliency information implicit in the cross-modal information. This enables interaction and feature fusion between defect information from different modalities, effectively addressing the issue of decreased defect recognition accuracy caused by the loss of information from a particular modality. Finally, we conducted performance comparison experiments, which showed that our method achieved recognition accuracies of 95.1% and 93.2% in terms of precision and mean average precision (mAP), significantly outperforming other mainstream object detection algorithms.

title: Interactive Support of Frequency and DC Voltage for Receiving-End Network with Offshore Wind VSC-HVDC Integration
authors: Peijun Liu, Yuan Fu, Wenbin Ci, Quan Wang, Yongzhi Su, Chunming Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: To ensure frequency stability of the receiving-end power netwok for large-scale offshore wind farms connected via VSC-HVDC, this paper first analyzes the system's support requirements during frequency fluctuations. Based on the energy conversion relationship between DC capacitor storage and rotational kinetic energy, the quantitative relationship between DC capacitance and system inertia is investigated. Subsequently, a coupling control mechanism among receiving-end netwok's frequency, DC voltage, and offshore wind farm frequency is developed, and a frequency support control strategy is proposed. Finally, a simulation platform is built to verify the effectiveness of the strategy. The results demonstrate that the system can rapidly respond to frequency changes, meet system safety requirements, and provide effective dynamic frequency support.

title: The xPU-athalon: Quantifying the Competition of AI Acceleration
authors: Alicia Golden, Carole-Jean Wu, Gu-Yeon Wei, David Brooks
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on Performance Analysis of Systems and Software (ISPASS)
abstract: The push for greater efficiency in AI computation has given rise to an array of accelerator architectures that increasingly challenge the GPU’s long-standing dominance. In this work, we provide a quantitative view of this evolving landscape of AI accelerators, including the Cerebras CS-3, SambaNova SN-40, Groq, Gaudi, and TPUv5e platforms, and compare against both NVIDIA (A100, H100) and AMD (MI-300X) GPUs. We evaluate key trade-offs in latency, throughput, power consumption, and energy-efficiency across both (i) end-to-end workloads and (ii) benchmarks of individual computational primitives. Notably, we find the optimal hardware platform varies across batch size, sequence length, and model size, revealing a large underlying optimization space. Our analysis includes detailed power measurements across the prefill and decode phases of LLM inference, as well as quantification of the energy cost of communication. We additionally find that Cerebras, SambaNova, and Gaudi have 10-60 % higher idle power than NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, emphasizing the importance of high utilization in order to realize promised efficiency gains. Finally, we assess programmability across platforms based on our experiments with real profiled workloads, comparing the compilation times and software stack maturity required to achieve promised performance.

title: Wavelet-Decoupled Representation with Rotation-Domain Prior Gating for PolSAR Ship Detection
authors: Shibo Chang, Haoying Feng, Ping Lang, Hao Chang, Jian Dong, Xiongjun Fu
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Polarimetric synthetic aperture radar (PolSAR) provides multi-channel scattering responses, offering richer cues for ship detection. However, existing data-driven methods primarily focus on spatial-domain feature modeling and underutilize physically motivated priors embedded in polarimetric scattering mechanisms. To address this, we propose a Wavelet-based Rotation-Prior Gated Network (WRPGN). First, via equivalent polarization-basis rotation, we construct a rotation-domain polarimetric prior map. This map exploits polarimetric information to enhance signal-to-noise ratio, yielding reliable spatial confidence for subsequent gating. Second, we introduce a High-Frequency Prior-Gated (HFPG) block into the feature pyramid. It decomposes features into low-frequency structural components and high-frequency details, and maps the rotation-domain prior to learnable gating weights that selectively modulate the details. These modulated details are then residually fused with the structural components to reconstruct multi-scale representations that are both physically interpretable and highly discriminative. Experiments on the FPSD dataset demonstrate the effectiveness and competitive performance of the proposed method.

title: Spatiotemporal Ensemble Deep Learning for Sea Surface Temperature Forecasting
authors: Lalita Chaudhary, Shakti Sharma, Gaurav Kumar Dashondhi, Sudipan Saha, Arnab Muhuri
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Sea Surface Temperature (SST) in the Bay of Bengal exhibits rapid variability driven by freshwater discharge, monsoon winds, and frequent atmospheric disturbances. Accurate short-range forecasting of this field is important for marine operations and climate-related studies over the region. This letter presents an ensemble deep learning framework that integrates a Long Short-Term Memory network, a Gated Recurrent Unit, and a transformer-based TimeSformer branch. All three components are trained using satellite-derived SST fields from the SEVIRI Indian Ocean product to capture complementary temporal and spatial characteristics of the signal. The individual forecasts are combined using a weighted fusion strategy to generate SST predictions for a fourteen-day horizon. The proposed approach is evaluated over the Bay of Bengal and compared against several widely used deep learning models, including CNN-Long Short Term Memory (LSTM), ConvLSTM, Bi-LSTM, Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU), ViT-LSTM, TimeSformer, and the earlier DualSeq model. The ensemble framework achieves the lowest prediction error, with a root mean square error of 0.55°C, a mean absolute error of 0.42°C, and an R-squared value of 0.96. Compared with DualSeq, the root mean square error is reduced by approximately eleven percent, indicating improved skill in short-range SST forecasting for this dynamically evolving region.

title: SCD-Net: A Semantic-Contour-Disparity Triadic Collaborative Network for 3D Building Reconstruction from Stereo Satellite Imagery
authors: Maozhen Zou, Liwei Li, Lingfeng Mei, Jianjun Sha, Jiajun Yu, Jianwei Meng
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: For the task of 3D building reconstruction from stereo satellite imagery, existing methods often treat semantic segmentation, contour regularization, and disparity estimation as independent or loosely coupled tasks, failing to fully exploit their geometric correlations. To address this, this letter proposes the Semantic-Contour-Disparity Triadic Collaborative Network (SCD-Net). This network establishes a deeply coupled computational framework that extracts multi-scale features through a shared encoder, subsequently routing them to dedicated branches. Specifically, we design a Multi-Scale Edge Aggregation (MEA) module that significantly enhances the refined representation of building edges, and also propose a Semantic-Contour-Disparity Up-sampling (SCDU) module, which leverages dual geometric priors from semantic categories and building contours to guide disparity optimization, effectively resolving the issue of blurred building boundaries in disparity map. Furthermore, a novel building contour loss function is introduced, strengthening multi-task constraints by quantifying the geometric consistency between disparity edges and semantic contours. Experiments on the US3D and WHU datasets demonstrate that the SCD-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance on key metrics for stereo matching and building semantic segmentation.

title: Prioritizing Expert-Like Transitions for Reward-Free Offline Imitation Learning
authors: Zhiliang Lin, Zhuangzhuang Chen, Guanming Zhu, Li Wang, Jianqiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Offline imitation learning (IL) enables embodied agents of AI-driven automation control systems to acquire policies from demonstrations without interacting with the environment. However, collecting extensive expert demonstrations that rely on a manually designed reward function is labor-intensive, and even impractical for dynamic real-world settings. To mitigate the reliance on large amounts of reward-annotated expert data, previous works have made great efforts by leveraging both expert and suboptimal demonstrations. In this paper, we reveal that existing methods still suffer from suboptimal results for two reasons: (i) reward signals in suboptimal demonstrations are not reliable, due to these rewards derived from inexperienced annotators, thus can not reflect the actual rewards, and (ii) those suboptimal demonstrations, which are significantly distant from expert behaviors, result in low reconstructed rewards. Moreover, motivated by the recent diffusion-based foundation model, we propose Expert-Like Transition Imitation Learning (ELTIL), a novel generative trajectory augmentation method that effectively leverages diffusion models. ELTIL re-designs the reward function based on expert proximity, and then proposes amplified return conditioning for achieving high-quality trajectory generation. More specifically, instead of relying on environment-provided rewards or manually designed rewards, ELTIL first serves as a good reward labeler for transitions by measuring their distance to expert states. These re-designed rewards then serve as guidance in the diffusion process, with amplified returns contributing as conditioning values to bias generation toward expert-consistent behaviors. Extensive experiments on diverse D4RL benchmarks demonstrate that ELTIL consistently improves the performance and stability of reward learning and offline IL methods, maintaining reliability under varying noise levels, reward sparsity, and dataset distributions. These characteristics make ELTIL well-suited for deployment in robust and reliable automated control systems such as robotic manipulation and autonomous driving.

title: Foreword
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE 6th International Conference in Power Engineering Applications (ICPEA)
