title: Automated Fish Feeding System for Aquaculture
authors: S. Sharmila Devi, P. Srinath, S. Monish, K.N.V. Vivinrakul
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: A new fish-feeding system is proposed here, incorporating an intelligent and automatic fish-feeding mechanism using Internet of Things, along with real-time monitoring and live streaming. In this proposed design, an ESP32-CAM is utilized to watch over fish activities round the clock, streaming live images to both a computer and a smartphone interface. The fish-feeding process is further performed by a servo motor, which feeds fish only when they demonstrate adequate activities. This design has adopted IoT connectivity, which allows remote control and observation using a smartphone application. Additionally, remote monitoring using live streaming has also been incorporated. The power management is carried out with a help of a transformer, a bridge rectifier, a capacitor, along with voltage regulators.

title: Mitigating String Current Deficit in Future-Generation 3D NAND through a Co-Design Strategy for Verify Level and Bitline Voltage
authors: Wei Cao, Xiang Yang, Henry Chin, Deepanshu Dutta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: The scaling of modern 3D NAND has been driven by the rapidly increasing number of wordline layers to achieve higher bit density and lower bit cost. One expected challenge of this scaling approach is the string current deficit as the poly-Si channel length is growing while electron mobility is difficult to improve at the same pace. Increasing bitline voltage is a straightforward knob. However, it is found in this work that simply increasing bitline voltage does not increase string current as much as expected, and moreover, it can even decrease string current. To address this issue, we propose and demonstrate a verify level and bitline voltage co-design rule to recover the string current deficit for future 3D NAND.

title: Enhancing SONOS-Type Flash Memory for Nonvolatile Analog Computing-in-Memory via Precise Multi-Level Weight Control and Improved Retention
authors: T. Kobayashi, Y. Taniguchi, H. Ikegaya, T. Tamatsu, M. Yamaguchi, K. Yanagisawa, Y. Uji, T. Yabe, S. Yoshida, Y. Kawashima, T. Shimozato, H. Kobori, T. Kato, F. Owada, S. Noda, H. Yoneda, K. Okuyama, T. Hosomi, K. Oto, S. Koshina, T. Morie
publish: 2026 IEEE International Memory Workshop (IMW)
abstract: This paper demonstrates the advantages of SONOS-type flash memory for non-volatile analog computing-in-memory (nvACiM) applications. For nvACiM, high-density weight states per cell and precise weight control are essential. We implement an iterative multi-step programming sequence in SONOS memory to achieve 256 -level weight control, validated through both simulations and single-bit cell measurements. To address the critical requirement of weight stability, we introduce a combination of band control thermal treatment during the ONO formation process and shallow trap charge reduction during the programming sequence; this approach significantly enhances the thermal retention of weights. These techniques were applied to nvACiM test chips, demonstrating 32-level weight programming with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-\text{nA}$</tex> spacing in the subthreshold region of memory MOSFETs, enabling extremely low power operation. Furthermore, linear multiply-accumulate (MAC) operations were successfully verified.

title: CFACO-Net: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework with Dual-Backbone Transformer Fusion and Metaheuristic Feature Selection for Aluminum Wheel Defect Detection
authors: K. Rajavadhani, B.Sheela Rani
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In modern automotive manufacturing, ensuring the structural integrity of aluminium wheels is critical for safety and quality. Traditional inspection methods are slow and error-prone, while existing automated systems struggle with defect localization, generalization, and efficiency. To overcome these issues, this study proposes CFACO-Net, a novel hybrid Deep Learning (DL) model for accurate and real-time aluminium wheel defect detection. The model integrates ViGo-YOLO for fast rim detection, DualNeXt-SwinCF with DCS-TNN for rich local-global feature extraction, and Chaotic-Fuzzy Ant Colony Optimization (CFACO) technique for optimal feature selection. Moreover, a lightweight MLP is used for achieving efficient classification. In addition, the efficiency of the devised model is tested on the CAWDEC dataset, the CFACO-Net system achieves better performance than existing models by achieving 99.55 % accuracy and 99.35 % precision while delivering a dependable and adaptable solution that enables intelligent quality control in smart manufacturing environments. Overall, the evaluation results demonstrate that CFACO-Net effectively improves quality control procedures while it decreases inspection mistakes and enhances productivity in smart manufacturing systems.

title: Linear Economic MPC With Nonlinear Friction Feedforward for Built-in Direct-Drive Wave Energy Converters
authors: Guodong Fan, Weimin Wu, Yichuan Wu, Liang Yuan, Frede Blaabjerg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Built-in direct-drive wave energy converters (BD-WECs) offer a promising solution for the autonomous power supply of marine data buoys. However, existing energy extraction schemes often suffer from limited energy-harvesting performance. To address this issue, this article proposes a linear economic model predictive control (LEMPC) strategy with nonlinear friction feedforward (NFF). First, a nonlinear dynamic model of the BD-WEC is established through physics-based structure analysis, and the key friction-related parameters are identified via forced oscillation tests. Based on the identified friction characteristics, NFF is introduced to compensate for the nonlinear friction effect, thereby improving the applicability of the model to quadratic-programming-based online optimization. Furthermore, an auto-regressive model is employed for online prediction of tilt-angle excitation. Experimental results obtained on a physical prototype demonstrate that the proposed NFF-assisted LEMPC strategy can operate robustly in real-time under both regular and irregular wave conditions. The results also show that the system achieves an energy conversion efficiency of over 60%, representing a clear improvement in the output power compared with the existing method.

title: A sub-1V, 82ppm/K Cryo-CMOS Voltage Reference based on ΔVth in 22nm FDSOI
authors: Matthias Eberlein, Johannes Weber
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: We present a precise CMOS reference in 22nm FDSOI for low-voltage cryogenic applications. The simple structure uses a self-biased amplifier whose differential input employs transistors with different threshold voltages. A resistive feedback loop scales the systematic offset from the threshold-delta, yielding a buffered output Vref. The temperature slope under cryogenic conditions is tuned by the sizing/current ratio of the input pair, which introduces a PTAT or CTAT component. Measurements from 4–75 K demonstrate operation down to VDD = 0.66 V and a stability of 82 ppm/K. With an active area of 594 μm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, the approach provides robust reference generation suitable for densely integrated cryogenic SoCs.

title: Mechanism Analysis of the Impact of Distributed Photovoltaic Integration on Distribution Network Overcurrent Protection*
authors: Runze Yin, Feng Zhang, Junshui Hu, Jingxuan Zhang, Huahua Jiang, Kunlin Shu
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: With the continuous increase of distributed photovoltaic (PV) penetration in distribution networks, the fault characteristics of distribution networks have undergone significant changes, and traditional overcurrent protection faces severe challenges. This paper systematically investigates the impact mechanism of distributed PV integration on distribution network protection. Firstly, based on the different control strategies of Class A and Class B inverters, fault steady-state calculation models for distributed PV are established, revealing their controlled current source characteristics that are fundamentally different from synchronous generators. Secondly, three types of impact mechanisms of PV integration on overcurrent protection are deeply analyzed: the infeed effect causes unintended expansion of the protection range of the outgoing line section I, leading to the risk of mal-tripping; the outfeed effect results in reduced fault current detected by upstream protection, decreasing sensitivity or even causing failure to operate; the reverse overcurrent effect may cause mal-operation of downstream branch protection. On this basis, we quantify the system reliability risk under different PV penetration levels, discuss feasible engineering mitigation schemes, and verify the scalability of the conclusions in a standard IEEE test feeder. Finally, the influence patterns of these three effects under different PV capacities are verified through PSCAD simulations. Research shows that the weak-infeed characteristics and controlled phaseangle characteristics of PV fault currents are key factors affecting protection performance, providing a theoretical basis for subsequent protection adaptability assessment, risk management, and engineering optimization.

title: An Automated Brain Stroke Classification from Computed Tomography Scans using a Deep Denoising Autoencoder Network with Feature Extraction Technique
authors: Ramachandiran R, R. Karunia Krishnapriya, G. Sharmila, V. Kavitha, A. Sariga, S. T. Arokkiya Mary
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: A brain stroke is a severe health condition resulting from a lack of blood flow. The affected brain area loses normal function after a stroke, making early detection significant. Computed tomography (CT) plays a key role in detecting suspected stroke patients, but is currently prone to significant intra- and interobserver variation. CT are extensively applied for classifying abnormal brain tissue after haemorrhage and infarct in stroke. Manual lesion representation is now the conventional method, but it is either operator-dependent or time-consuming. Primary techniques for automatically identifying brain stroke on CT scans using deep learning (DL) are presented. The DL advances a computational method with multi-layered processing to gradually learn from raw input. Many DL frameworks are widely used to address image processing challenges. This paper proposes an Automated Brain Stroke Classification Using Deep Denoising Autoencoder with Feature Extraction (ABSCDDAEFE) model from CT scans. The aim is to detect brain stroke from CT images to support timely analysis and treatment planning. Initially, the adaptive Kalman filter (AKF) method is used for image pre-processing. Next, the EfficientNet-B5 method is employed for feature extraction. To detect and classify brain stroke, the stacked denoising autoencoder (SDAE) model is employed. Finally, the parameter selection of SDAE is implemented by using the cuckoo search (CS) approach. The comparison analysis showed a superior accuracy of 98.80 % over existing models on the brain stroke CT image dataset.

title: Aero-Engine Remaining Useful Life Prediction Based on CNN-BiLSTM Bidirectional Temporal Modeling
authors: Jingzhi Luo, Lu Yang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Accurate prediction of the remaining useful life (RUL) of aero-engines is crucial for ensuring flight safety and enabling predictive maintenance. To address the coexistence of local degradation features and long-term temporal dependencies in sensor monitoring data, this paper proposes a hybrid prediction model that integrates a convolutional neural network with a bidirectional long short-term memory network (CNN-BiLSTM). The model extracts local spatial patterns from sensor sequences through the CNN layer and captures bidirectional long-term dependencies during the equipment performance degradation process via the BiLSTM layer, thereby achieving comprehensive modeling of degradation dynamics. Experimental results on the NASA C-MAPSS benchmark dataset demonstrate that the hybrid deep learning model achieves excellent performance on the FD001 subset, with a coefficient of determination <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$({R}^{{2}})$</tex> of 0.8641 and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 10.27, significantly outperforming other models used for comparison. This work provides an effective technical approach for the intelligent health management of aeroengines.

title: Early Detection of Heart and Stroke Analysis Facial Bio Marks and Location Based Medical Analysis
authors: Rakshana P, Padmapriya B, R Mohandas, Kandavel N, M Vinodhini, Karthikeyan P
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Stroke and heart attacks cause over 15 million deaths annually, with treatment delays often exceeding the critical “golden hour” in disadvantaged communities. This paper proposes a smartphone-based early detection system using facial video analysis with personalized baseline comparison. The system extracts 468 facial landmarks from 5 -second video recordings using MediaPipe Face Mesh, measuring facial asymmetry, drooping, abnormal eye movements, and skin color changes. Unlike population-based models, this approach compares each user's current facial features against their healthy baseline recorded during registration, enabling personalized risk assessment. An ensemble machine learning model combining Random Forest, XGBoost, and CNN analyzes facial features alongside medical parameters including blood pressure, cholesterol, and blood sugar levels. The 10MB TensorFlow Lite model operates offline on entry-level Android devices with 37 ms inference time, achieving 92% accuracy, 91% precision, and 89.7% recall. An adaptive triage system categorizes users into three color-coded risk levels: GREEN (healthy-personalized health advice), YELLOW (moderate risk-symptom questionnaire), and RED (emergency-automatic geolocationbased hospital routing with one-tap emergency calling). Simulation studies demonstrate up to 80% reduction in treatment delays compared to conventional diagnostic pathways. This accessible solution enables early cardiovascular risk detection without requiring internet connectivity, wearable devices, or laboratory infrastructure, making it particularly suitable for resource-limited and rural communities where timely access to emergency medical services remains challenging.

title: Structure-Aware Mamba Sequence Modeling for Image Super-Resolution
authors: Jian Xu, Jiaqi Liu, Xiaoli Meng, Jingang Shi
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: This paper introduces a structure-aware Mamba sequence modeling approach for image super-resolution (SR). While Mamba has shown success in NLP with linear complexity and long-sequence modeling, its application to visual tasks faces challenges due to the two-dimensional structural constraints of images. To address this, we propose a lightweight structure-aware prior module that quantifies regional importance using gradient and local variance cues. Based on this prior, we design a dualbranch Mamba block (DCMB) that jointly models local structural details and global contextual dependencies, fused via an adaptive gating mechanism. Furthermore, an adaptive scanning path strategy is introduced to improve structural continuity and reduce computational redundancy. Experiments on Urban100 demonstrate that our method achieves a 0.34 dB PSNR gain with only 0.84 M parameters and improves inference speed by approximately 32%, validating the effectiveness of structureaware sequence modeling for efficient visual reconstruction.

title: Redefining AI Architecture with NAND Flash
authors: Hao-Zhi Lee, Ming-Hui Wang, Yu-Hao Wang, Chia-Ming Hsu, Yi-Juan Du, An-Cin Li, Tso-Yuan Chen, Kai-Wei Tsou, Shiu-Ming Tsao, Jian-Ping Syu, Wei Lin
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: As Agentic AI accelerates data demands, Phison aiDAPTIV utilizes high-endurance NAND Flash as a memory expansion layer to optimize training, inference, and RAG applications. To overcome PCIe bandwidth bottlenecks, we introduce Computational Storage Devices (CSD), which offload tasks like weight updates and vector searches directly to the storage level. These cases demonstrate that CSD is a pivotal evolution for scaling efficient, AI-centric infrastructure.

title: A Non-autoregressive based Multi-role Speech Synthesis Method
authors: DingYun-tao
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: Non-autoregressive speech synthesis methods offer short synthesis time and high speech fidelity. Based on the Transformer speech synthesis model, this paper employs the Montreal Forced Alignment (MFA) technique for text and audio corpus, adds an enhancement module to strengthen prosodic feature representation, and achieves efficient and highly natural speech synthesis. The experimental results show that the method proposed in this paper performs better than the Transformer speech synthesis model, with the synthesized speech being closer to natural speech in terms of naturalness, clarity, and stylistic quality.

title: Comparative Analysis of Regression Algorithms for Crop Yield Prediction
authors: Khushali Gupta, Rashi Bhati, Ayush Pandey, Roshi Saxena, Parneeta Dhaliwal
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Crop yield prediction plays an important role in agricultural planning, food security, and the efficient use of resources. With the availability of large amounts of agricultural and environmental data, it has become possible to estimate crop production more accurately. This study compares several regression-based models to predict crop yield using factors such as rainfall, temperature, soil type, crop type, fertilizer use, irrigation, and weather conditions. The dataset was prepared through data cleaning, encoding, and feature scaling before training different models, including linear regression, ridge regression, LASSO, decision tree, random forest, gradient boosting, XGBoost, and LightGBM. The models were evaluated using standard performance measures such as Mean Absolute Error (MAE), Root mean square error (RMSE), and R-squared. The results show that linear and boosting-based models provided the best performance, achieving an R-squared value of around 0.91. These findings indicate that the proposed approach can effectively support better decision-making in agriculture, helping improve crop productivity, sustainability, and overall yield for farmers.

title: Deep Learning-based Automated Defect Detection Approaches for Quality Assessment in Smart Manufacturing Industries: A Comprehensive Review
authors: G. R. Bharathi, Salini Suresh
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: In quality control, defect detection is an essential feature in industrial processes. Standard manual control techniques are costly, incompetent, and vulnerable to human errors. With current developments in computer vision (CV) and deep learning (DL), intellectual visual examination models present an assuring solution for automatically detecting defects. In the advanced industry, defect detection in manufacturing systems is among the major applications of artificial intelligence (AI). By using AI models, like DL, intellectual production methods have the potential to examine videos and images of production procedures, identify deviations, detect issues promptly, enhance product quality, and forecast maintenance requirements. This study presents a detailed investigation of DL based models for automated defect detection in smart manufacturing industries. All-inclusive reviews of existing machine learning (ML) and DL models are presented for detecting defects in industrial production systems. Additionally, a broad overview of industrial defect detection and the challenges involved in automated inspection is presented. Furthermore, experimental results reported in the surveyed studies are discussed to highlight the effectiveness and limitations of various methods. Finally, a conclusion on DL-based automatic defect detection in innovative manufacturing industries is presented.

title: High-Frequency Performance Analysis of a Compact Multi-Port Planar Antenna for Future 6G Networks
authors: S.Kavi Priya, V. Nagaraja, V.Sai Sampath Kumar Reddy, T. Revathi, C.Sai Prakash
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: The fast development of the wireless communication systems to sixth-generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(6 \mathrm{G})$</tex> networks has boosted the need to have a compact and efficient antenna that can work at a high frequency and its multi-port performance is reliable. A small multi-port planar antenna is introduced in this work and its properties at high frequencies are discussed in the case of future 6G wireless services. The antenna suggested in this paper makes use of orthogonal planar arrangement, which minimizes mutual coupling but ensures stable radiation characteristics throughout the operating range. The main performance parameters, such as impedance matching, gain, radiation properties, and envelope correlation coefficient (ECC), are explored with the help of complete electromagnetic full-wave simulations. The antenna is shown to exhibit good impedance behavior, that is, with reflection coefficients less than -10 dB, a peak achievable gain of about 7.4 dB, and low ECC values across the board, which is an indicator of great diversity performance. The results obtained suggest that the designed compact planar antenna architecture has a potential to be a prospective solution in high-frequency multi-port systems in the next-generation 6G communication devices.

title: Fast Dual Path Hybrid FIR-IIR Filter Algorithm for Multi Target Separation in Array Signal Processing
authors: Wei Sun, Santuan Qin
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Natural Language Processing (ICNLP)
abstract: In array signal processing applications such as audio and radar telemetry, multi-target separation and direction of arrival (DOA) estimation are critical tasks. Conventional methods typically employ a multi-stage FIR filter structure: signals of multiple targets at different frequencies are first separated to the baseband via Digital Down Conversion (DDC), then each target signal undergoes multi-stage FIR filtering to suppress interference from other targets, before final DOA estimation. However, multi-channel, multi-stage FIR filters consume substantial resources in FPGA implementations. To address this, in this paper, a dual-path hybrid FIR-IIR filtering algorithm is proposed. Leveraging the advantage of IIR filters achieving comparable filtering performance with fewer resources, the proposed algorithm replaces the final several stage in the multi-stage filter structure with an IIR filter. This approach significantly reduces FPGA resource utilization while maintaining the overall link performance. To tackle the issue of phase nonlinearity in IIR filters when handling wideband signals, the proposed dual-path method decouples the tap position for DOA estimation from the signal synthesis point. Specifically, DOA estimation is performed after IIR filtering, whereas signal synthesis is conducted before the IIR filtering stage. This design effectively prevents the synthesized signal from suffering from phase nonlinearity distortion, which can ensure accurate demodulation by subsequent receiver stages. The effectiveness of the proposed algorithm was verified through simulations involving the separation of four targets within a 200 MHz bandwidth, using a hybrid architecture of 3 FIR stages and 3 IIR stages. The results demonstrate that the proposed dualpath algorithm achieves target separation performance and DOA estimation accuracy comparable to the traditional method, while saves more than half of the DSP slices, underscoring its significant value for engineering applications.

title: LUCID: An Explainable Multi-Stage Framework for Lung CT Image Analysis and Clinical Outcome Interpretation
authors: Rachana.S, Kokila Paramanandam
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Lung cancer continues to be one of the most common causes of death globally, where timely detection and individualized treatment are key to better outcomes. The majority of the current medical image analysis platforms offer black-box predictions or correlations only. To address this, we introduce a multistage analytical pipeline that integrates lung CT segmentation, explainable AI (XAI), clinical data causal inference, and a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) chatbot to provide explainable and patient-specific explanations. We utilize two complementary datasets: the NSCLC Radiogenomics Archive (TCIA) for patient-specific clinical and survival data to facilitate causal modeling, and the Medical Segmentation Decathlon (Task 6) for expert-annotated lung CT scans to facilitate robust segmentation and interpretability. Our SE-Attention UNet segmentation pipeline achieved a Precision of 0.9144, a recall of 0.8647, an F1-score and Dice coefficient of 0.8888, and an IoU of 0.7999.Grad-CAM++ and attention maps improve interpretability by identifying clinically relevant tumor areas. Contrary to standard reports that only summarizes associations, causal inference gives actionable cause–effect knowledge e.g - smoking status highly increases the risk of mortality and recurrence, and radiation therapy and adjuvant therapy have protective roles. The relationships differ between subgroups, requiring the need for individualized care. Lastly, a domain-expert RAG chatbot combines segmented image outputs, causal results, and filtered medical knowledge to provide context-aware explanations for clinicians and patients. It integrates the outputs of imaging, causal reasoning, and interactive knowledge retrieval, this model extends existing models to provide a new, interpretable, and clinically meaningful basis for lung cancer diagnostic analysis
