title: A Clinical Feature-Guided Deep Learning Framework for Robust Dementia Severity Assessment using the Clock Drawing Test
authors: R.K. Resheka, R. K. Vigneshwar, P. Aurchana, Nithiya Baskaran
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Early identification of cognitive impairment using the analysis of the Clock Drawing Test is critical for timely dementia intervention, yet manual scoring remains insensitive to subtle spatial disorientations. Most of the existing automated systems still rely purely on convolutional neural networks, which overlook the clinically meaningful patterns that neurologists use for interpretation. These convolutional approaches often misclassify moderate and severe impairment when drawings contain digit misplacement, distortions, or hand- angle deviation. To address these challenges, the proposed work presents a clinically-guided hybrid framework that combines deep visual embedding with more than 25 handcrafted clinically-inspired features capturing stroke structure, digit morphology, and radial symmetry, which are fused and classified using an ensemble of logistic regression and random forest models. For this work, the clinical data were taken from the Shulman dataset, which contains drawings classified based on the Shulman scale (0-5). The proposed hybrid model classifies the data into three classes based on severity, namely mild/ normal, moderate, and severe dementia. The hybrid model showed a remarkable improvement over the convolution method, achieving an overall accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6 \%}$</tex> and an AUC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 8 4 - 0. 9 8}$</tex>. This hybrid approach not only enhances performance but also provides interpretable insights into the clinical reasoning, making it suitable for real-world cognitive screening. This work demonstrates the achievability of fusing clinical knowledge with modern feature learning to improve dementia classification.

title: Superior Performance of Online Learning in Breast Cancer Classification: An AI-Driven Approach for Diagnosis and Prognosis
authors: Umme Haani A Haliyal, R. Kokila
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Breast cancer is a tumor that arises in the breast that is commonly overlooked and has resulted in the death of thousands of women. Artificial intelligence has simplified the process of diagnosis and prognosis, which is further categorized into machine learning. This advancement has made the detection process quicker than traditional methods, which can potentially increase the risk of fatalities. Approximately 15 algorithms were trained and evaluated using the Wisconsin Breast Cancer Dataset (WBCD) to assess their accuracy, precision, recall, and F1 score. The dataset includes 569 instances and 30 features, such as the radius, texture, and concavity. It also has a split of 62.7 % benign and 37.3% of malignant cases. The Stratified 10-Fold Cross-Validation were employed to ensure reliable performance estimation and quality assurance. Logistic Regression achieved the highest mean cross-validation accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$97.54 \% \pm 1.95$</tex>, while the Passive Aggressive Classifier achieved a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$96.67 \% \pm 2.41$</tex> and consistent high precision, recall and F1score. While batch learning models are performed well on static datasets, the Passive Aggressive Classifier has the advantage of online learning capability and low computational cost making it a better option for real time clinical decision support systems.

title: Proposal of a 3D Position Estimation Method by Region-based Matching of Stereo Images using a Two-Pass Scan Clustering Method
authors: Kazuma Kato, Hideki Toda
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Mechatronics, Control and Robotics (ICMCR)
abstract: Stereo matching is essential for obstacle detection and understanding road conditions in autonomous driving; however, matching between stereo images can be computationally expensive and sensitive to brightness changes. In this study, we perform stereo matching using regional features to reduce the matching load, and we propose an image segmentation method that mitigates the influence of brightness changes. We present results on stereo image pairs with complex backgrounds, showing that the proposed approach can detect corresponding points robustly in the presence of shadows and gloss, while suggesting efficient processing with reduced computational cost. As future work, we will further discuss improvements to the algorithm and its applications.

title: Areca Nut Recognition Using Machine Learning
authors: Pooja Bharamagoudra, Geetha R Bharamagoudar, Manjula
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Accurate identification and grading of Areca nuts are essential for maintaining quality standards and improving efficiency in agricultural practices. Traditional manual inspection techniques are often inefficient, require significant human effort, and are susceptible to errors. To overcome these limitations, this study proposes an automated Areca nut recognition system based on machine learning approaches. The developed framework employs effective feature extraction techniques combined with supervised classification algorithms to differentiate between high-quality and defective Areca nuts. Performance evaluation is conducted using multiple machine learning models, including Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Support Vector Machines (SVM), and Random Forest classifiers, with assessment based on accuracy, precision, and computational efficiency. The proposed system supports farmers and agro-processing industries by minimizing manual intervention, improving quality evaluation, and optimizing the overall Areca nut processing pipeline.

title: Intelligent Laptop Assistance Chatbot using Retrieval Augmented Generation
authors: Varsha R, Manasa, Prarthana G, Rashmi S G, Shilpa K N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The growth of e-commerce has led to an increasing demand for smart and scalable customer support systems. Traditional chatbots remain less effective since they are more prone to giving incorrect responses due to poor contextual understanding and brittle rule-based reasoning. This paper proposes the use of a Retrieval-Augmented-Generation based laptop assistance chatbot that effectively combines semantic search with large language models powers to provide smart, context-sensitive responses to troubleshoot laptops of top few brands . Product guides and support documents are chunked, processed, and stored in a vector database to enable real-time retrieval. Given a customer's query, the chatbot retrieves the most relevant chunks of documents and leverages a large language model to generate human-like answers. Moreover, the system supports smart recommendations of YouTube videos relevant to the user's question, which enhances guidance over troubleshooting and product usage. The proposed model was evaluated with the RAGAS metric, achieving a faithfulness of 0.81, answer relevance of 0.76, and context precision and recall of 0.74, to show strong factual grounding and contextual reliability. Overall, the system greatly improves response accuracy, reduces human-agent load, and enhances customer experience in resolving laptop related issues.

title: IoT for Social Safety: A Cloud-Based Fire Detection and Alert Solution
authors: Lohith C, Channaveerayya B H, G V Pradeep, Deepak T, Aditya Mahajan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Fire incidents remain one of the most frequent hazards in residential, commercial, and institutional indoor environments, often causing substantial property loss and risk to human life due to delayed detection, limited warning mechanisms, and inadequate emergency response systems. Traditional fire alarm systems rely heavily on isolated smoke or heat triggers and local audible notifications, which are ineffective when occupants are away or unable to respond. With advancements in Internet of Things (IoT) technologies, opportunities now exist for developing distributed, intelligent detection platforms offering real-time alerts and automated safety actions. This paper presents an integrated IoT-enabled fire detection and alert solution incorporating flame and temperature sensors, multi-parameter threshold analysis, secure ID-based authentication, and automated emergency response via cloud-connected SMS and voice notifications using the ThingSpeak API. The system employs an ESP32 microcontroller for real-time sensing, dual-sensor fusion to minimize false alarms, and relay-based appliance shutdown to mitigate incident escalation. Extensive testing across 50 controlled scenarios demonstrated an overall detection accuracy of 94.1%, mean cloud alert delivery time between 2.1–5.2 seconds, and complete elimination of false alarms under healthy operating conditions. The proposed solution offers a reliable, scalable, and costeffective fire safety framework suitable for smart homes, offices, warehouses, laboratories, and educational institutions, enabling continuous environmental monitoring and rapid automated emergency response.

title: SAATH:Smart Alert and Tracking Hardware
authors: Savitha MM, Supriya G, Varun Kumar G, Varun Kumar K A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This project presents a Smart ID Card system designed to enhance personal safety and asset tracking using RFID, GPS, and ESP32 microcontrollers. The Smart ID Card includes an RFID tag for entry and exit logging, and a GPS module for continuous location tracking. One ESP32 controller within the card collects and transmits data, while another serves as a gateway to send information securely to the cloud via Wi-Fi. The system uses the Telegram messaging platform to deliver real-time alerts when an individual moves outside predefined safe zones or interacts with an RFID reader. This enables instant communication with guardians or security personnel. The proposed solution demonstrates how low-cost hardware and widely used communication tools can be combined to create a reliable, efficient, and user-friendly safety monitoring system.

title: ShadowGPT: Linguistic Fingerprinting for Robust Detection of AI-Generated Phishing Emails
authors: Yousef Abu Dayeh
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: This paper presents ShadowGPT, a novel machine learning system for detecting ChatGPT-generated phishing emails through linguistic fingerprinting. As ChatGPT-3.5 become increasingly accessible, malicious actors exploit them to create sophisticated phishing campaigns that evade traditional detection methods. Our approach extracts 4 4 distinctive linguistic features encompassing stylometric patterns, readability metrics, vocabulary diversity, syntactic structures, and semantic indicators. We developed a robust gradient boosting classifier trained on 6,749 emails comprising 831 ChatGPT-generated phishing samples, 1,660 human-written phishing emails, and 4,258 legitimate communications. The training process incorporated noise augmentation to ensure real-world resilience. ShadowGPT achieves 99.6% accuracy with a 95% confidence interval of 99.2% to 99.8% in binary classification while maintaining an exceptionally low 0.14% false positive rate. The system demonstrates remarkable robustness, maintaining 99% accuracy under 20% feature perturbation. Our analysis reveals that ChatGPT-generated phishing exhibits consistent patterns including moderate urgency scores (0.98 ± 0.73), elevated trust language (0.70 ± 0.81), complex readability (Flesch: 23.9 ± 26.1), and low vocabulary diversity (0.684). These findings demonstrate that ChatGPT-3.5-generated phishing possesses detectable linguistic fingerprints, enabling effective defense against this emerging threat.

title: An Energy Balancing Fuzzy based Routing Algorithm for Energy-Efficient IoT Networks
authors: Shilpa A N, Jayant Nandwalkar, P. Susheelkumar Sreedharan, Viji K, Rajeswari P, Sridhar N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: We have seen the wide proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in every-day that has bought to the fore-front concerns regarding optimizing energy utilization and routing paths along with scalability factors in a constrained environment. In the world of IoT, with multitudes of autonomous sensing nodes continuously communicating, it’s a great challenge to maintain balanced energy consumption with reliable data communication path. It is observed that under dynamic conditions, the existing traditional routing cannot adapt to the uneven load distributions and cannot be relied on. Hence, we propose an enhanced fuzzy based multi-criteria-based routing algorithm F-EVM++ which is energy efficient and provides for stable IoT communication. In the proposed approach, we have integrated the Fuzzy MAIRCA (Multi-Attribute Ideal–Real Comparative Analysis) and Fuzzy Eigenvector approaches to evaluate the decision criteria such as residual energy, distance to the sink under varied network conditions. Further we have incorporated a dynamic cluster formation algorithm called as Adaptive DBSCAN (Density-Based Spatial Clustering of Applications with Noise) algorithm which takes into account the balance energy and the spatial distribution of sensing nodes. To facilitate the above algorithm, we make use of an Energy Balancing Feedback (EBF) mechanism that continuously monitors the residual energy and ensures a controlled re-clustering process to effect minimal node deaths and even-out the load distribution from time to time. The collaborative mechanism of all the three algorithms enables intelligent re-clustering and minimum-cost path selection for data movement in real time. We have implemented the above approach using MATLAB@2024b in a simulation network of 100 sensing nodes employing random distribution. The simulation results are quite encouraging and suggests enhanced energy utilization and a prolonged stable network lifetime in comparison to the existing approaches namely F-EVM, DECR and MT-MAC protocols.

title: K-FDE: Kurtosis and Frequency Domain Enhanced Adaptive Feature Learning Framework for Intelligent Fault Diagnosis
authors: Huan Wang, Xiaoyu Luo, Jiale Liu, Junpeng Huang, Xinmeng He, Min Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Intelligent Fault Diagnosis (IFD) has achieved notable advancements through deep learning technologies, yet it continues to confront significant challenges in complex industrial environments. Current methodologies exhibit limited frequency perception capabilities, which impedes the comprehensive capture of critical frequency components within fault signals, thereby affecting diagnostic accuracy. Furthermore, existing IFD approaches lack interpretability, making it challenging to correlate selected features with physical fault phenomena effectively, thereby diminishing the practical applicability of diagnostic outcomes. To address these issues, this paper presents an adaptive feature learning framework that integrates kurtosis-based explainable attention and frequency domain encoding(K-FDE) to enhance both the interpretability and accuracy of fault feature selection. The proposed framework incorporates a kurtosis-based interpretable fault feature selection module and an Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) based frequency domain encoding module, which together dynamically capture fault-relevant features while improving the interpretability of feature selection. Additionally, this framework introduces a Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT)-driven frequency decomposition module and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-driven feature learning module, facilitating detailed frequency decomposition from coarse to fine and multi-resolution frequency feature learning. Empirical evaluations on high-speed aerospace bearings and motor bearings datasets validate that the proposed method demonstrates exceptional noise robustness and superior frequency perception capabilities.

title: Near-Field Beam Training Design for Stacked Intelligent Metasurface Assisted Communication Systems
authors: An Wei, Zhipeng Wang, Kun Fang, Jiaqi Zhang, Yinuo Dong, Liang Yang, Qingchao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The technique of stacked intelligent metasurfaces (SIMs) have emerged as a promising solution to enable advanced wave-domain processing with reduced hardware complexity. However, existing SIM research works are mainly based on the assumption of perfect channel state information (CSI), ignoring the critical challenge of channel acquisition. To address this problem, this paper proposes a novel near-field beam training framework for SIM-assisted systems. Specifically, we first establish an end-to-end near-field channel model of the SIM-assisted systems. Based on this model, we design a DFT based beam training scheme that performs a matched-filter search over distance, elevation, and azimuth to localize the user equipment (UE). The obtained position estimate is then utilized to construct the equivalent channel, which serves as the foundation of the subsequent beamforming algorithm by refining the digital combiner and the SIM phase shifts alternatively. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed SIM-aided near-field beam training framework significantly improves spectral efficiency compared to the conventional schemes without SIMs.

title: Spatiotemporal Drought Analysis Using Hybrid Evolutionary Algorithm-Optimized Deep Learning Architecture
authors: Vandana Sardar, Surabhi B P, Varshitha D, Harshitha B M, Likitha J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: The agricultural community in the semi-arid region of Bagalkot faces significant economic hazards due to the unpredictable occurrence of drought. The current system of monitoring is inefficient due to the use of global thresholds, which are inappropriate for classifying vegetation stress in the region. This paper proposes a region-specific decision support system based on a hybrid deep learning model. The core model combines a one-dimensional Convolutional Neural Network (1D-CNN) to identify short-term meteorological variations with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units to identify seasonal variations. Instead of using trial-and-error methods for hyperparameter selection, the paper proposes a hybrid optimization technique that combines Whale Optimization and Grey Wolf Optimization (WOA-GWO). The model is tested on new data and achieves an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 90.74% and an RMSE of 0.0296. Moreover, the severity levels were revised to show that a Vegetation Health Index (VHI) value below 36.10 represents severe drought in Bagalkot, while the global value is 20. This revised and optimized tool provides a localized approach for accurate agricultural risk assessment.

title: In Vitro Micro-Propagation of Chrysanthemum (Poornima) Using Apical Meristem
authors: Ashwini D.S, Santosh Kumar Singh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This Chrysanthemum, a decorative flower belonging to the Asteraceae family, has emerged as an important commercial crop for numerous farmers in Karnataka because of its strong market demand. However, the supply of high-quality plants has been challenging, complicating the mass production of genetically pure lines. To tackle this issue, we implemented tissue culture technology. We sterilized shoot tips as explants using various agents, including Bavistin, Streptomycin sulfate, Cetrimide, 2% sodium hypochlorite, 70% ethanol, and 0.1% HgCl2. The basal MS medium was enriched with different combinations of auxins and cytokinin’s to optimize shoot and root formation. The findings indicated that the medium containing MS medium along with 2 mg/L BAP, 0.2 mg/L NAA, and 50 mg/L ADS, resulted in the highest number of healthy shoots and an improved survival rate. Additionally, the medium with ½ strength MS and 1.0 mg/L IBA proved to be the most suitable for developing a healthy and viable root system. Acclimatization, which gradually introduces tissue culture plants to the natural environment, used a mixture of red soil, vermicompost, and coco peat in a 1:1:1 ratio. The micro propagated plants were assessed by using different DNA markers, and it was observed that the plantlets had no genetic variations.

title: OpenVerse: An Intelligent Recommendation Platform for Google Summer of Code Preparation
authors: Sharvani G S, Prajwal Mundargi, Ramya G, Sahana Adiga V, Samarth G, Prarthana V Gunakimath
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: <copy979-8-3315-4777-6/26/$31.00 ©2026 IEEE>OpenVerse is an intelligent web-based platform designed to simplify and enhance the preparation process for open-source contributions, particularly for Google Summer of Code (GSoC) aspirants. The methodology integrates data analytics, predictive modeling, and personalized recommendation systems to guide users toward suitable organizations and projects. Historical GSoC datasets are collected and analyzed to identify trends such as recurring organizations, project domains, and commonly used technology stacks. Predictive analytics techniques are employed to forecast potential organization participation in future GSoC editions. The platform is implemented using a Flask-based backend with a Neon PostgreSQL database for efficient data storage and processing, while a React-based frontend provides an interactive and responsive user interface. OpenVerse further integrates GitHub analytics by displaying organization-level metrics and highlighting "Best First Issues," enabling users to initiate contributions directly. Performance evaluation using Google Lighthouse reports a performance score of approximately 78, accessibility of 95, best practices score of 92, and SEO score of 82, demonstrating a reliable, accessible, and user-friendly web experience.

title: A High-Performance Modular Linear Actuator With Enhanced Compensation of Cogging Torque and Friction
authors: Junyang Wang, Jinhong Dong, Xueai Li, Fenglei Ni, Kui Sun, Jinjun Xia, Baoshi Cao, Hong Liu
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: This article presents a compact linear actuator featuring a high force-to-weight ratio enabled by integrating all the drive, transmission, and sensing control modules. A four-guide-rod design enhances bending and torsional stiffness with low mass. An iterative method collects and compensates cogging torque of the linear actuator, ensuring reduced speed fluctuations, improved motion stability, and rapid convergence. Position-dependent friction is addressed by a hybrid Stribeck-neural network strategy, which captures residual effects to enhance low-speed smoothness and tracking accuracy. The integrated framework combines model-based and data-driven strengths to deliver precise, robust, and high-performance linear actuation and effectively mitigate dynamic disturbances.

title: Detection of Health Conditions and Disease of Multi-Crops Using Deep Learning and Machine Learning Models
authors: Hamsa G R, Geeta Budni, Harshith Kolluru, Akarshak Sharma, Nalini N, P. N. Tengli
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: Plant diseases pose a significant challenge to agricultural productivity and global food security, particularly in regions with limited access to expert diagnosis. This paper presents a lightweight and deployment-oriented convolutional neural networks for multi-crop plant disease classification, designed to operate efficiently in low-resource environments. The proposed approach utilizes a compact CNN architecture to classify leaf images into 38 healthy and diseased categories across various crops. The model is trained on the PlantVillage dataset using TensorFlow and Keras, with images resized to 224×224 pixels and processed in batches of 32. Regularization techniques and early stopping are employed to improve generalization and reduce overfitting. Experimental results demonstrate a validation accuracy of 91%, with consistent class-wise precision, recall, and F1-score values. The lightweight design enables fast inference and reduced memory usage, making the system appropriate for deployment on edge and mobile devices. The primary contribution of this work lies in system-level optimization, efficient training strategies, and deployment feasibility rather than architectural complexity.

title: Lifecycle-Aware Multi-Temporal InSAR: Synergistic Optical-Radar Monitoring of Evolving Infrastructure
authors: Boxuan Du, Lei Zhang, Hyung-Sup Jung, Guoqiang Shi, Daqing Ge, Hongyu Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Conventional Multi-temporal InSAR (MT-InSAR) requires temporal coherence persistence throughout the observation period, limiting its effectiveness in dynamic environments where urbanization causes frequent surface changes. We propose a Lifecycle-Aware Multi-Temporal InSAR (LA-MTInSAR) framework that leverages optical imagery to synergistically monitor evolving infrastructure and its associated discontinuous coherent scatterers. The method integrates three components: (1) initializing scatterer lifecycles via detection of surface transition epochs using the Pruned Exact Linear Time (PELT) algorithm on Sentinel-2 time series, (2) refining the onset of radar signal stability through cascaded search to eliminate the time lag between optical changes and radar coherence establishment, and (3) reconstructing deformation fields via dynamic network construction and Least Squares QR (LSQR) sparse inversion. Applied to Hong Kong International Airport's Third Runway reclamation, LA-MTInSAR corrects an average 42-day physical time lag and increases monitoring point density by 1.7× overall and 28× (416 to 11,669 points) in the reclamation zone, capturing subsidence rates of -80 mm/year. Validation against continuous Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) observations at six sites yields RMSE values of 4.27–6.43 mm. By explicitly accounting for scatterer lifecycle heterogeneity, LA-MTInSAR extends time-series InSAR monitoring capability from static environments to actively evolving construction zones.

title: Enabling Fast Polar SC Decoding with IR-HARQ
authors: Marwan Jalaleddine, Mohamad Ali Jarkas, Jiajie Li, Warren J. Gross
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: To extend the applications of polar codes within next-generation wireless communication systems, it is essential to incorporate support for Incremental Redundancy (IR) Hybrid Automatic Repeat Request (HARQ) schemes. For very high-throughput applications, Successive Cancellation (SC) decoding is particularly appealing for polar codes owing to its high area efficiency. In this paper, we propose modifications to SC decoders that employ special nodes to accelerate decoding. Our modifications enable the use of polar IR-HARQ with SC decoding for high throughput applications. Compared to the unmodified SC IR-HARQ scheme, our proposed approach allows us to achieve up to 66% reduction in node traversals with polar codes. Simulation results confirm that the proposed special node modifications do not cause any degradation in FER performance compared to the unmodified state-of-the art special nodes.

title: Design of a 3D Braiding Online Tension Monitoring Spindle Based on Multi-Sensor Fusion
authors: Haowen Jiao, Keze Xu, Yujing Zhang, Zhuo Meng
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In view of the problems of insufficient measurement accuracy, large system size, and delayed data acquisition in traditional yarn tension monitoring methods during 3D braiding, a tension monitoring system based on multi-sensor fusion technology and combined with the original slider-type spindle is designed. An adaptive tension fusion algorithm based on vibration perception is also proposed. Based on the stm32 main processor and BLE wireless transmission, various sensors such as laser ranging sensors, micro pressure sensors, and inertial modules are deployed to achieve online acquisition of spindle yarn tension data during 3D braiding. The Sigmoid function is used to continuously reconstruct the dynamic model of spindle pulley displacement-yarn tension mapping, eliminating the singularity of the observation matrix. Considering the inertial impact on the micro pressure sensor during spindle trajectory changes, an adaptive weight function is utilized to dynamically adjust the noise covariance in the AKF algorithm. The system is applied to an inner-loop 3D braiding machine, and tension is selected as the observed quantity for offline and online experiments. The results show that this tension monitoring system can accurately and real-time output yarn tension changes, achieving higher measurement accuracy and better robustness compared to single-sensor measurement systems and other measurement methods.

title: A Real-Time Heuristic 3D Bin Packing Algorithm for Robotic Mixed Palletizing with Kinematic and Stability Constraints
authors: Yongyong Sun, Kezheng Sun, Wu Yan, Yisong Cai, Zhaoyang Liao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: Mixed palletizing of multi-sized boxes is a critical operation in modern flexible manufacturing and smart logistics. However, traditional 3D Bin Packing Problem (3D-BPP) algorithms often fail in physical deployment because they prioritize theoretical volume utilization while neglecting the mechanical and kinematic realities of robotic execution. This paper proposes a real-time heuristic 3D-BPP algorithm specifically tailored for industrial robotic palletizing systems. By integrating a Guillotine Split space management strategy with strict physical guardrails-specifically, a minimum <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 5 {\%}}$</tex> bottom support area constraint to prevent collapse, and a top-down kinematic clearance check to avoid end-effector collisions-the proposed algorithm effectively eliminates dangerous trajectories during the packing sequence. Furthermore, the algorithm is encapsulated within a highly extensible software framework, enabling millisecond-level inference on standard industrial PCs without requiring heavy GPU acceleration. Real-world implementation demonstrates that while the theoretical space utilization is marginally compromised, the algorithm achieves a near-perfect 99% industrial executability rate, successfully bridging the “Sim-to-Real” gap in robotic automation.

title: AI-Driven Agricultural Portal for Optimized Crop Management
authors: Ms.A.P. Narmadha, Pindi Jaya Ramireddy, A V Pranay Kumar
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Modern agriculture faces challenges in maximizing productivity and ensuring sustainable practices, which necessitate the integration of advanced technologies for real-time decision support. Climate uncertainties, pests, soil erosion and fluctuating markets are increasingly having impacts on agricultural production and require informed decisions by farmers. This paper proposes a solution to all these challenges by introducing a unified platform that integrates machine learning-based analytics, secure data management, and digital trading services. The suggested system is based on supervised machine learning models, which help in crop selection, yield forecast, rainfall forecast, and fertilizer prescription using historical yield data, soil parameters, and weather data. Moreover, pest and disease identification by the use of images helps farmers to detect and intervene on time. To guarantee the privacy and integrity of the data, the platform will store and transmit data via Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) and the integrity of the data will be ensured with the help of Secure Hash Algorithm (SHA-256), which prevents any manipulations with data by any third party. A trading module based on blockchains enables transparent and direct farmer-to-consumer processes, eliminating the need for intermediaries to ensure better profitability. The experimental analysis proves that the suggested system has about <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 - 1 8 \%}$</tex> higher predictive accuracy and operational efficiency than the traditional methods. The findings illustrate that incorporating intelligent prediction models into secure data infrastructure is feasible in supporting sustainable and data-driven agricultural decision-making.

title: Enabling Logic-in-Memory (LiM) Functionality with Junctionless FeFETs
authors: Neeraj Samant, Aarya Ameet Agavekar, Dhruv Ganesh, Rohit Kumar Nirala, Manish Gupta, Abhinav Kranti
publish: 2026 International VLSI Symposium on Technology, Systems and Applications (VLSI TSA)
abstract: Implementing logic (NOR/NAND) by utilizing the polarization of HZO ferroelectric (Fe) layer in field effect transistors (FETs) opens new possibilities for realizing logic operations within memory. Despite the relatively simpler fabrication process, junctionless (JL) Fe FETs exhibit a degraded State 1 and are unsuitable for implementing logic-in-memory (LiM). The drawback is addressed through a co-optimization of channel doping (10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">18</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> instead of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">19</sup> cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup>) and gate workfunction (midgap 4.7 eV rather than 5 eV) which enables NOR/NAND operations in JL FeFETs and their adaptation in LiM modules.

title: Adaptive NeuroDefense: Graph Neural Network and Reinforcement Learning-based Self-Healing Cybersecurity Architecture
authors: Manoj Kumar R, Srinivasan K, Rohidh S, Sibirajan D, Sakthivel G, Iniavan G
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Adaptive NeuroDefense is a cybersecurity architecture that uses artificial intelligence to predict and mitigate threats in a smart fashion combining Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). The system represents the network behavior by means of problematic attack patterns as interlocked graphs. A counter-agent based on Deep Q-Network (DQN) makes independent choices of the best counter-measures. The self-healing mechanisms isolate the affected nodes and restore the services with minimal human intervention. It would enable real-time data streaming and automated incident response because it would be based on Kafka and Flask microservices. Broad comparison of NSL-KDD, CICIDS-2017 and UNSW-NB15 has an accuracy of 98.2 % and an average response time of 45 ms, both significantly better than the conventional methods. It is a dynamic, expandable solution to up-and-coming network-based cybersecurity problems.

title: Smart Sidewalks: Exploring Piezoelectric Energy Harvesting for Sustainable Urban Transport Systems
authors: Dominic Bien M. Aquino, Margareth S. Barber, Mervin Anthony S. Millamina, Dante L. Silva, Jocelyn S. Buluran, Christ John L. Marcos
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This study investigates the performance of a piezoelectric tile prototype in converting footstep-induced mechanical energy into electrical energy within an urban pedestrian setting. A three-day experimental setup was deployed at the Mapúa University exit, combining real-time voltage monitoring with manual foot traffic counting during peak and offpeak hours. The results demonstrated that the tile system successfully generated voltage in response to varying pedestrian loads, with higher outputs recorded during peak times. Statistical analysis using four t-tests confirmed a significant relationship between foot traffic intensity and voltage output. Although the harvested energy was insufficient for high-power applications, it proved suitable for low-energy functions, such as pathway lighting and pedestrian sensors. Environmental impact assessment revealed modest but meaningful non-monetary benefits, including reduced grid dependency and increased awareness of sustainable practices. These findings support the potential of piezoelectric sidewalks as a supplemental power source and a step toward more intelligent, sustainable transportation infrastructure.

title: Adaptive Multi-Resolution Attention Networks for Speech Emotion Recognition with Temporal Consistency Regularization
authors: M. V. Rajesh, Nalla Manasa Surya Subrahmanayeswari, Doddi Hari Sri Priyanka, Kommu Ratna Chandra, Kolli Leela Prasad, Illa Chandravirat
publish: 2026 Fourth International Conference on Augmented Intelligence and Sustainable Systems (ICAISS)
abstract: Research Objective: Contemporary SER architectures exhibit two fundamental limitations: the application of uniform temporal resolutions across emotionally diverse utterances, and the absence of mechanisms ensuring prediction stability across temporal segments. This work introduces AMRAN (Adaptive Multi-Resolution Attention Network), a framework that dynamically determines optimal temporal granularity based on emotion-specific acoustic characteristics while enforcing cross-segment prediction coherence. The proposed architecture employs learnable gating mechanisms that assign differential weights to three concurrent CNN pathways, each processing WavLM-Large representations at distinct temporal scales (kernels: 3, 7, 15). We then apply stacked bidirectional LSTMs and multi-head attention mechanisms. A novel temporal consistency loss function discourages abrupt shifts in predictions between adjacent segments. The system was rigorously tested through 5-fold speaker-independent cross-validation across 14,169 combined utterances from RAVDESS, TESS, and IEMOCAP datasets. Experiments show three key findings. The model delivered a mean accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$64.97 \% \pm 9.66 \%$</tex>, with the strongest fold reaching 81.99%. The results reveal temporal preferences specific to emotions: high-arousal emotions such as anger and happiness gravitate toward fine-grained features (42% weight on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{k}=3)$</tex>, while low-arousal states such as sadness and neutrality showed a clear preference for coarse patterns (41% weight on <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{k}=15)$</tex>. This finding validates our central hypothesis about adaptive resolution. The temporal consistency regularization mechanism successfully reduced prediction variance through smoother emotion transitions, contributing to an F1-score of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.6385 \pm 0.0984$</tex>. Notably, all three resolutions played substantial roles (Fine: 33.4%, Medium: 33.5%, Coarse: 33.1%), showing that fixed-scale architectures cannot capture the full diversity of emotion-specific temporal characteristics.

title: A Comparative Study of Backbone Architectures for Document Analysis with DINO
authors: Liyakhanya Tabata, Clement N. Nyirenda
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This study evaluates the integration of the DINO transformer-based detector with four modern backbones: ResNet-50, EfficientNetV2-S, Swin-T and MobileNetV3-L, within a document analysis pipeline. Models are benchmarked on a high-resolution synthetic dataset simulating real-world paperwork with diverse layouts, handwriting and fine-grained elements. The aim is to assess how backbone choice affects detection accuracy, efficiency and performance under resource constraints and limited data. All models are stress-tested using document artefact augmentations to reflect deployment conditions. Results show EfficientNetV2-S leads in accuracy with a high computational overhead. MobileNetV3-L performs well on small objects with a comparable computational performance. ResNet-50 and Swin-T offer the fastest inference. ResNet-50 is balanced for low-resource setups, while Swin-T delivers high throughput but struggles under noise. These findings highlight the role of backbone choice in real-world document detection.

title: Prognostic Impact of Hypertension and Diabetes on PRKAA1, MAP4K4, RBP4, and ZFHX4 Expression in Endometrial Cancer
authors: Aditya Shah, Ishaan Anand, Katie Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Endometrial Cancer (EC) remains the most common gynecological malignancy and often co-occurs with metabolic disorders, including hypertension and diabetes. Although these associations are well established, their molecular and genetic understanding is much less established. In this study, we focus on four metabolism- and cancer-related genes in EC patients. Using TCGA data of 175 primary EC patients, we conducted KaplanMeier survival analyses on gene expression cohorts divided at median expression levels in UCSC Xena Browser. Overall, high PRKAA1 expression was associated with worse survival in the full cohort (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$p<0.01$</tex>), despite its well known role as an AMPKmediated tumor suppressor, and its effect was also amplified in comorbid EC/hypertension patients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}<0.05$</tex>) - highlighting a novel insight that PRKAA1 may instead mark metabolic stress in EC rather than protect. MAP4K4, a kinase gene implicated in inflammation and glucose dysregulation, was significantly associated with poorer outcomes only in hypertensive patients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p} =0.0155$</tex>), but not in other subgroups. On the other hand, RBP4 showed no prognostic impact in any of the cohorts, besides in diabetic patients <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p=0.0766)$</tex>, while the ZFHX4 gene was the only one to approach significance in metabolically healthy patients (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p} =0.0653$</tex>). Together, our findings reveal a context-dependent interplay between gene expression and comorbid metabolic states in EC prognosis, implicating precision medicine treatments.

title: Benchmarking Machine Learning Models for Cloud Resource Forecasting: LSTM vs. SVR, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Prophet
authors: Lusani Mamushiane, Mduduzi C. Hlophe, Hlabishi Kobo, Albert A. Lysko, Joyce Mwangama
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Effective cloud resource forecasting is essential for optimizing virtual machine (VM) provisioning in increasingly dynamic and resource-constrained environments, especially now with the advent of 5G/6G networks. This paper evaluates a set of machine learning and deep learning models (LSTM, SVM, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Prophet) for VM workload prediction using real-world data. The analysis emphasizes the trade-off between forecasting accuracy and computational complexity, two critical aspects for real-time and scalable deployment. Results show that while LSTM achieves superior accuracy, it incurs significant training overhead. Random Forest provides a more balanced trade-off, maintaining competitive accuracy with lower computational cost. These findings inform model selection in contexts like 5G/6G base station association, network slicing orchestration, multi-access edge computing, intelligent handover decisions, and dynamic service function chaining, where both efficiency and precision are vital for maintaining service-level guarantees under constrained resources.

title: Effects of Grid Impedance and Load on Transformer Frequency Response via in situ Measurement
authors: Stevan Engelbrecht, Johannes Cornelius Bekker, Fredrick Mukundi Mwaniki
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: This paper investigates, through simulation, the effects of grid and load impedance on the frequency response of a wideband model of a 16 kVA, 22 kV / 240 V distribution transformer. The aim is to provide insight into how network impedance influences in situ measurements for transformer condition monitoring. A Pseudo Random Impulse Sequence (PRIS) perturbation source delivers broadband excitation (1 - 100 kHz) while presenting high impedance at 50 Hz to protect the source from the grid. Case studies vary the Thevenin-equivalent grid impedance to represent strong, weak, and open-circuit conditions, and apply loadings up to 75% of rated capacity. Results show that grid impedance significantly affects frequency response, shifting the first resonance to lower frequencies as grid reactance increases. Load impedance has negligible effect but reduces signal-to-noise ratio at high-frequency resonances. The findings highlight the effect of network impedance in wideband transformer diagnostics.

title: Scalable Short-Term Solar PV Forecasting Using Hybrid Transformer-ELM Models under Heterogeneous Data Conditions
authors: Seyyed Kasra Mortazavi, Witesyavwirwa Vianney Kambale, Hussein Ahmad Ahmad, Kyandoghere Kyamakya
publish: 2025 IEEE AFRICON
abstract: Accurate short-term photovoltaic forecasting is crucial for maintaining grid stability, but real-world datasets vary from clean, regularly sampled series to noisy, irregular records with numerous missing values. We propose a scalable hybrid framework that combines transformer-based temporal encoders (PatchTST and TS-BERT) with ridge-regularized Extreme Learning Machine (ELM) regressors. Notably, PatchTST+ELM performs best on clean, structured data, while TS-BERT+ELM is more robust for irregular and noisy data. Across five European national-scale PV datasets with meteorological covariates, the hybrid models reduce mean absolute error by 12–18% versus LSTM, GRU, and statistical baselines, gain a further 7–12% from weather features, provide 80% prediction intervals with 90–94% coverage, and achieve sub-3 ms inference with 5–7× faster updates than full retraining.

title: Leveraging Large Language Models to Predict Long-Term Language Outcomes in Late Talkers
authors: Saketh Sundar
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Early identification of children likely to experience persistent language delays remains a gap in both research and clinical practice. Late talkers (LTs) are toddlers with delayed expressive language but otherwise typical development that show highly variable outcomes, with some catching up and others experiencing long-term difficulties. This exploratory study investigates whether large language models (LLMs) can classify early language delay as LT or typically developing (TD) and predict outcomes at school-age (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5; 6$</tex>) among LTs using spontaneous speech transcripts collected at ages 2;6 and 3;6. Using transcript data from the Ellis Weismer Corpus in CHILDES, we evaluate a transformer-based model against a traditional machine learning baseline (Random Forest) trained on common linguistic features used by clinicians such as mean length of utterance and lexical diversity. The LLM transformer model DistilBERT slightly outperformed the baseline, achieving moderately high accuracy in distinguishing LTs from typically developing peers and moderate success in forecasting long-term outcomes. The findings show that early child speech data contains features that transformer models can leverage to make accurate predictions, suggesting these models offer a promising but still preliminary path to automating developmental risk evaluation for late talkers.

title: A Table of Contents-Based Novel Architecture for Compute-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Harshit Kondle, Ruhaan Singh, Sai Satheshkumar, Saketh Nandam, Yuvaan Chandra, Anvit Koppella, John McKee, Mihai Boicu, Kamaljeet Sanghera
publish: 2025 IEEE MIT Undergraduate Research Technology Conference (URTC)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems often embed entire corpora upfront, which is computationally expensive in constrained environments such as military deployments. We introduce a novel architecture, TOC-Based Load-Time Section Filtering (TLSF), which parses structured documents like DAFMAN 36-2664 using a hierarchical TOC and routes queries to relevant sections before embedding. Our pipeline comprises three modules: TOC parsing, probabilistic keyword-based TOC indexing, and lazy embedding. We evaluate TLSF across latency, compute cost, retrieval precision, and memory usage. Our results demonstrate 3.15x latency reduction and significant compute savings over brute-force RAG, while increasing retrieval precision by 19%. This approach is promising for low-resource natural language processing applications in policy-intensive domains.

title: Experimental Benchmarking: CAN and I2C Protocols for EV BMS Communication
authors: Pavithra G, Anjana Joshi, Monica K M, M C Prabhavathi, Sharavani Math, Bhavyashree S Bhat
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Knowledge Engineering and Communication Systems (ICKECS)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated analysis of battery behavior under dynamic load conditions with a focus on state-of-charge (SoC) transitions, thermal response, and communication efficiency using CAN and I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C protocols in electric-vehicle battery management systems (BMS). The study models realistic charging and discharging profiles, illustrating SoC rise under partial recovery, voltage stabilization, and decline during sustained load. Complementary temperature-response observations capture heating during high-current discharge and gradual cooling as load decreases, reflecting thermal dynamics critical for safety and performance. Simulink-based implementations of CAN and I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C communication layers demonstrate comparative data-handling capability, latency, and robustness under varying operating conditions. Results indicate that CAN provides superior noise immunity and bandwidth for real-time monitoring, while I<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>C offers compact integration suitable for low-power sensing modules. The combined modeling framework supports improved BMS design, enabling enhanced estimation accuracy, fault detection reliability, and overall system efficiency in EV applications.

title: Discrete-Time Sliding Mode Control for Constrained Mechatronic Systems via an $r$-Order Difference Observer
authors: Shuyi Zhu, Yuhao Ji, Jiayi Mao, Jinliao Yan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This paper proposes a discrete-time sliding mode control (DSMC) strategy based on a finite-memory <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex>-order difference observer to address the ubiquitous issues of actuator input saturation and mismatched external disturbances in mechatronic servo systems. Traditional disturbance observers are highly susceptible to nonlinear coupling when facing actuator input truncation, which consequently leads to amplified steady-state errors or even the windup phenomenon. To circumvent this, an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r$</tex>-order difference disturbance observer is first designed based on a single-step physical measurement basis. Incorporating a cold-start data truncation mechanism, the observer explicitly derives the algebraic amplification upper bound of high-frequency measurement noise during the difference extrapolation process under the worst-case scenario, strictly adhering to the boundedinput bounded-output (BIBO) stability principle. Subsequently, by integrating the orthogonal decomposition of disturbances, a discrete sliding mode control law with mismatched disturbance feedforward compensation is formulated, where the physical saturation deviation is treated as a lumped bounded disturbance and suppressed via a robust reaching gain. Theoretical derivations confirm that the observation error dynamics are structurally decoupled from the control law, thereby ensuring the global uniform ultimate boundedness (UUB) of the closed-loop system. Subsequent simulations on a normalized mechatronic servo joint validate the robustness of this scheme across different noise levels and observer configurations.

title: Weak Magnetic Detection Method of Steel Wire Rope Based on the Principle of Spatial Magnetic Field Vector Synthesis
authors: Longfei Wu, Tong Wu, Cunlong Zheng, Lei Yi, Dapeng Huang, Guan Wang
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: This study introduces a novel weak magnetic detection approach for steel wire ropes based on the principle of spatial magnetic field vector synthesis. A circumferential fourloop excitation scheme is adopted. Simulation analyses are conducted on the leakage magnetic field characteristics of wire rope defects. The accuracy of the simulation model is verified through analytical calculation and physical experiment comparison. Furthermore, a complex-valued convolutional neural network (CVCNN) model is constructed to achieve defect localization and type identification. A complete physical experimental platform is built and a labeled dataset of actual defect signals is constructed. Experimental findings confirm that the proposed method effectively suppresses background interference noise, clearly characterizes defect-related information, and achieves higher detection precision and classification accuracy than existing mainstream comparison models. The minimum detectable defect size is clarified to guide engineering applications. Consequently, it provides an efficient and stable technical solution for the nondestructive testing of steel wire ropes in engineering scenarios.

title: A Global Vision-Based Mobile Obstacle Perception and Global Path Optimization Method for Unmanned Vehicles
authors: Shuang Yu, Liangxi Xie, Ming Xiao, Zihao Guo
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mechatronics Technology and Intelligent Manufacturing (ICMTIM)
abstract: In dynamic environments, unmanned vehicles are required to achieve safe and efficient autonomous navigation in the presence of dynamic obstacles such as workers and transport vehicles. This paper proposes a risk-aware global path planning algorithm based on the historical trajectories of dynamic obstacles under a global perspective. By collecting multiple historical motion trajectories of dynamic obstacles, the algorithm infers the probability of their possible motion routes according to the current states of dynamic obstacles, which is then applied to the global path planning of unmanned vehicles. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can generate effective dynamic obstacle avoidance trajectories and reduce potential collision risks. Compared with traditional methods, the method presented in this paper significantly improve safety of the planned paths.

title: Interference-resistant Radar Reception with Microwave Photonic Time-frequency Matched Signal Selection and Amplification
authors: Gengze Wu, Fangzheng Zhang, Xiaoyue Yu, Hao Wang, Chenchang Zeng, Zhenyu Xu, Shilong Pan, Kai Wang
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Traditional radar receivers are confronted with severe challenges posed by strong in-band interference. Such interference signals often drive the low-noise amplifier (LNA)-based receiver into saturation, resulting in spectral regrowth and the generation of intermodulation distortion products (IMPs). To address this critical issue, an interference-resistant radar reception method based on microwave photonic time-frequency matched signal selection and amplification (TFM-SSA) is proposed in this paper. The core foundation of this method lies in a Dynamic sideband amplification injection locking (SAIL) mechanism in an optically injected semiconductor laser (OISL) system. By properly controlling the current of the slave laser (SL), the narrowband gain spectrum (GS) generated by period-one (P1) oscillation can be designed to dynamically sweep to match the instantaneous frequency of the signal of interest (SOI). Based on the Dynamic SAIL mechanism and considering the distinct time-frequency characteristics of the SOI and in-band interference, the proposed TFM-SSA method selectively amplifies the SOI that matches the designed GS in time-frequency domain, while effectively suppressing the strong interference before the receiver enters saturation. In the experiment, the properties of Dynamic SAIL are investigated, and the reception of a 4-GHz bandwidth linear frequency-modulated (LFM) signal interfered by parallel interference and cross interference signals is demonstrated, respectively. Experimental results verify the effectiveness of the Dynamic SAIL-based TFM-SSA method in reducing the noise floor and improving the target peak-to-floor ratio (PFR). This not only eliminates the generation of false targets but also enhances the radar's detection sensitivity under high-power interference conditions. Therefore, the proposed TFM-SSA method can provide a powerful solution for addressing the in-band interference encountered by current radar receivers.

title: Flexible Floating-Gate Optoelectronic Synaptic Transistor for Neuromorphic Visual Processing
authors: Qingxuan Li, Bin Cui, Siwei Wang, Qingqing Sun, David Wei Zhang, Lin Chen
publish: IEEE Electron Device Letters
abstract: Neuromorphic visual systems inspired by biological vision have demonstrated great potential in visual perception and information processing. With the rapid development of wearable electronics, there is an increasing demand for flexible neuromorphic visual systems. However, most reported devices are fabricated on rigid substrates, which fail to mimic the elastic structure of the human retina and limit their applicability in flexible scenarios. Here, we present a flexible floating-gate phototransistor (FGPT) based on CsPbBr<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> quantum dots and an organic semiconductor. Benefiting from the high photosensitivity of the organic semiconductor and the electrical properties of zero-dimensional quantum dots, the device exhibits a large memory window of 20 V and multilevel storage capability. In addition, under combined electrical and optical modulation, the device demonstrates nonlinear memory decay and learning-experience behavior. Notably, even under a small bending radius (R = 5 mm), the device maintains continuously tunable conductance modulation. Furthermore, the flexible FGPT enables image preprocessing, achieving a low mean squared error (MSE) of 5.51×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-3</sup> for the combined edge detection result. This work provides an effective strategy for visual information preprocessing in next-generation flexible neuromorphic systems.

title: Supervised Feature Selection Guided by Granular-Ball Computing: A Two-Stage Geometry-Driven Framework_supp1-3695768.pdf
authors: Jin Qian
publish: N/A
abstract: Feature selection is a crucial data preprocessing technique aimed at enhancing robustness against boundary uncertainty and complex geometric distributions and improving model interpretability by removing irrelevant or redundant features. Most filter-based feature selection methods employ a single evaluation metric and often fail to explore the underlying geometric distribution structure of the data, which holds significant guiding information for feature selection. In view of this, this paper proposes a Supervised Feature Selection framework guided by granular-ball computing (SFS-GBC). The framework aims to identify superior feature subset candidates for downstream classification tasks by leveraging geometric insights derived from granular balls. Meanwhile, the proposed algorithm introduces a unique two-stage evaluation process designed to synergize coarse-grained efficiency with fine-grained geometric precision, adapting to data of varying dimensionality. Spectifically First, Granularity Consistency (GC) is employed to rapidly rank all features, effectively pre-screening for candidates highly relevant to the decision classes. More innovatively, the subsequent stage departs from traditional statistical metrics by proposing a novel evaluation measure based on the geometric distribution of granular balls, combining Intra-class Compactness (ICC) and Inter-class Discrimination (ICD) to accurately assess feature subsets within a forward search procedure. Furthermore, by adjusting a granulation parameter, the algorithm can explore the data structure from a multi-granularity perspective, potentially yielding superior feature subsets. Experimental results on public benchmark datasets demonstrate the superiority and effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A High-Precision Dual-Domain Signal Denoising and Lightweight Phase Picking Method for Seismic Edge Devices
authors: Ruyun Tian, Yuyang Chen, Hongyu Wang, Yuxing Zhang, Yihan Cao
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Aiming at the issues of poor real-time performance in phase picking under high background noise, low accuracy in low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) data , and limited deployment on edge devices, the paper proposes an edge computing (EC)-based seismic phase picking (SPP) method. Firstly, to achieve efficient data denoising, a dual-domain seismic signal denoising algorithm based on deep neural networks (DNNs) is designed. The time-domain network extracts waveform features for denoising, while the frequency-domain network corrects spectral features. The results from both domains are dynamically and adaptively fused based on real-time SNR estimation. Additionally, to address the resource constraints of edge devices, a lightweight SPP algorithm based on time-domain convolutional networks (TCNs) is designed. It combines the innovative inverted residual blocks (IRBs) with TCN to model temporal dependencies, while also introducing a channel attention mechanism to dynamically enhance key features. Particularly, through structured pruning and mixed-precision quantization, the model is compressed, enabling significantly reduced computational complexity while providing high-precision P-wave and S-wave picking in real-time at the edge. The results demonstrated that the F1 scores of P-wave and S-wave phase picking reached 92.48% and 90.05% before noise reduction. Especially under low SNR data, the F1 scores were significantly improved by 3.42% and 9.34%. The model was deployed to the edge device RK3566, verifying the real-time performance of local data processing and the accuracy of phase picking method.

title: Transient Modulation of the Nucleus Accumbens by Transcranial Temporal Interference Stimulation: An fMRI Study in Healthy Adults
authors: Kexu Zhang, Chenchen Zhang, Xiaochi Yuan, Rui Li, Cuiyuan Fu, Aixin Jia, Zhiqian Liu, Liju Qian, Cong Zhou, Meng Zhang, Kun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: The nucleus accumbens (NAcc) plays a central role in motivation and reward processing, and its dysfunction is implicated in various psychiatric disorders. While invasive neuromodulation of the NAcc has shown therapeutic benefits, current noninvasive techniques cannot effectively reach this deep target. Recently, transcranial temporal interference stimulation (tTIS) has emerged as a promising approach to noninvasively modulate deep brain regions. Here, we explored whether tTIS could noninvasively modulate NAcc activity and its associated networks in humans. Fifteen healthy adults received a single 30-minute tTIS session targeting the right NAcc; resting-state fMRI was acquired immediately before stimulation (T0), immediately after (T1), and again 30 minutes later (T2). Analyses focused on local spontaneous activity, functional connectivity (FC), and effective connectivity (EC) using ROI-based linear mixed models and voxel-wise whole-brain contrasts. Electric-field simulations confirmed focal targeting of the NAcc with minimal current spread. Although no significant change in the amplitude of spontaneous activity was observed, local activity coherence within the NAcc significantly increased immediately after stimulation. FC between the NAcc and the limbic network and caudate, as well as EC from the NAcc to the default mode network and limbic network, were significantly enhanced at T1. These effects largely returned to baseline by T2, indicating transient short-term modulation. Collectively, these findings show that a single tTIS session can modulate both local NAcc dynamics and large-scale network interactions, supporting the feasibility of noninvasive deep-brain modulation of the NAcc and highlighting its potential as a neuromodulatory approach for disorders involving dysregulated reward and affective processing.

title: Arrayed triaxial force plate system using high-speed line-scan camera and sampling moiré method for jumping spider measurement
authors: Yukitake Nakahara, Toshihiro Shiratori, Hidetoshi Takahashi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Among small animals, jumping spiders are known for their outstanding agility, and the quantitative measurement of ground reaction forces (GRFs) is essential for understanding their jumping mechanisms. However, existing force measurement systems face limitations in simultaneously achieving high temporal resolution, multi-axis force sensing, and array integration. In this study, we propose an arrayed triaxial force plate system based on the sampling moiré (SM) method using a high-speed line-scan camera with 4096 pixels and a 4 kHz sampling rate. The system consists of six spring-supported glass plates (2 mm × 6 mm each) with tilted patterns, which are imaged simultaneously through a prism. Triaxial forces are independently calculated for each plate by analyzing the one-dimensional strip signals via the SM method. Calibration experiments demonstrated linear force responses for each axis and a triaxial force resolution of approximately 15 μN. The fabricated force plate array was used to measure the GRFs generated by a jumping spider (Hasarius adansoni) during takeoff. Each pair of legs was found to contact a different force plate, resulting in the forces generated by each pair being measured independently. The experimental results suggested that the fourth leg pair generated a forward force, whereas the third leg exhibited two noticeable peaks in vertical force. Therefore, the proposed system enables multi-axis GRF measurements for jumping spiders.

title: Bayesian Learning-Based Spectrum Mapping with UAV Path Dynamic Optimization Under Three-dimensional Unknown Environments
authors: Jie Wang, Qiuming Zhu, Yuanjin Zheng, Zhipeng Lin, Qihui Wu, Kai-kuang Ma, Qianhao Gao, Yiran Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Spectrum mapping (SM) visualizes spectrum information across a geographical area, constructing radio environment maps (REMs), which serve as a foundation for spectrum monitoring, management, and security. Most existing SM schemes rely on spatially distributed sensors or vehicle-mounted equipment, and assume prior environmental knowledge, limiting their applicability in dynamic or unknown 3D environments. In this paper, we propose a Bayesian learning-based three-dimensional (3D) SM framework that enables accurate REM construction through adaptive UAV sampling in complex and unknown environments. First, a mutual-information-driven UAV path planner is designed by integrating an enhanced sampling-based optimization scheme, enabling efficient data collection according to the maximum mutual information criterion and recent sensing data. Second, a semi-deterministic channel dictionary, refined with sampled field data, is established to model the correlation between observed spectrum values and environmental features. Based on this dictionary, a Bayesian learning-based recovery algorithm reconstructs the spectrum distribution at unsampled positions, producing the corresponding 3D REM. Experimental results on open simulated and measured datasets demonstrate that the proposed framework reduces the mean absolute error by over 60% compared with CS-based methods and by 35% with data-driven interpolation. It also improves sampling efficiency by up to 70% for a given recovery accuracy, highlighting the effectiveness in unknown 3D environments.

title: Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Method Based on Similar-Day Clustering and Attention-Based Multi-Scale Transformer
authors: Yang Wang, Xiaochen Zou
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: With the continuous development of renewable energy, photovoltaic (PV) power generation has taken on an increasingly significant role within power systems. However, the output of PV power generation is considerably influenced by weather conditions, which gives rise to notable randomness as well as fluctuation. In order to enhance the accuracy of forecasting, this paper puts forward a PV power prediction method that combines similar-day clustering and deep learning. Pearson correlation is employed to pick out the key meteorological features, and K-means clustering based on global horizontal irradiance (GHI) is utilized to categorize weather types. Afterwards, a prediction model that integrates multi-scale convolution and Transformer attention is constructed, which can capture both short-term fluctuations and long-term dependencies. The experimental results indicate that the proposed method surpasses several baseline models and possesses practical value for power grid scheduling and renewable energy integration.

title: A Method for Short-Term Wind Power Forecasting Based on Multi-Scenario Segmentation and Error Correction
authors: Fuhan Liu
publish: 2026 5th International Conference on Green Energy and Power Systems (ICGEPS)
abstract: Accurate wind power forecasting is of great importance to the operation of power grid systems. To address the strong volatility of wind power output and the difficulty of a single model adapting to all operating conditions, this paper proposes a short-term power forecasting method based on scenario classification and error correction. First, statistical methods are employed to remove outliers, and Principal Component Analysis is used for feature dimensionality reduction. Second, low-, medium-, and high-output scenarios are classified according to power levels, and Support Vector Machine, Long Short-Term Memory network, and Back Propagation neural network are adopted for differentiated modeling under different scenarios. Finally, Random Forest is used to correct the forecasting residuals. Validation using actual data shows that, compared with traditional methods, the proposed method reduces the Root Mean Square Error by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 9 5 \% - 3. 2 2 \%}$</tex> and the Mean Absolute Error by 1.68%3.92%. These results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively capture the fluctuation characteristics under different operating conditions, and its forecasting accuracy is significantly superior to that of a single model.

title: Use of What-if Scenarios to Help Explain Artificial Intelligence Models for Neonatal Health
authors: Abdullah Mamun, Lawrence D. Devoe, Mark I. Evans, David W. Britt, Judith Klein-Seetharaman, Hassan Ghasemzadeh
